documents.searchText: to provide consistent guidelines for use, documentation, and reconciliation of buprenix. this procedure is applicable for the department of animal services (das) veterinarians and registered veterinary technicians (rvt). the supervising registered veterinary technician (srvt) or rvt designee controls issuance; the balanced final log is submitted to the chief or srvt designee. srvt supervising registered veterinary technician rvt registered veterinary technician chain of custody upon receipt of the buprenix order, the supervising registered veterinary technician (srvt) or registered veterinary technician (rvt) designee will log each bottle in the safe. {"step": "number each bottle in the following format:", "sub": ["17-01: year and bottle number for the riverside shelter only.", "sj17-01: shelter location, year, and bottle number for the san jacinto shelter only.", "tp17-01: shelter location, year, and bottle number for the cvac shelter."]} the srvt or rvt designee shall issue one box or five (5) bottles at a time to the designated premise license area (receiving, clinic, etc.). refer to policy 100-08. administration - desktop chameleon enter each dose administered by the rvt in chameleon accurately. if a treatment window does not exist that matches the medication date, create a new treatment window (medical screen). enter buprenix in the medication field, including dosage and the correct format of the bottle number. if the round of the medication is not completed and the dose is changed, cancel the remaining todos. create a new medication protocol from the date changed forward. note: the treatment window must match the medication date. administration - webchameleon tap on the check icon to complete the task. enter the bottle number in the correct format. tap on enter. if the dose being administered is different than what is shown on the todo list, the rvt shall create a new task by tapping on the animal record and choosing create new task. it is recommended to use desktop chameleon if possible. enter the new task (medication) and dose (description) with the applicable time the medication was supposed to be administered. save. repeat the new-task steps for the second dose of the same medication. tap on the home menu icon at the top left corner. tap on kennel, then daily tasks. {"step": "when prompted, enter the following:", "sub": ["kennel number: enter the beginning kennel letter and first number. for example, if performing follow-up treatments in pod 200, enter r 2.", "tap on ok. this will allow the user to view a refreshed todo list that shall include the new medication task."]} repeat the task completion steps (check icon, bottle number, enter). reconciliation on a daily basis, at the end of the day, the closing rvt will balance use based on the bottle number. print the balanced buprenix drug log. place the printed log in the designated drug folder. once a buprenix bottle is empty, repeat the daily balancing, printing, and filing steps above. submit the balanced final buprenix log. sign. submit to the chief or srvt designee. das policy 100-08, controlled substances chain of custody

documents.title: Bad Check Procedure NSF Receipt in Chameleon
documents.purpose: To establish consistent procedures when processing a Bad Check receipt for collections within the Department of Animal Services.
documents.authority: CA Penal Code 476(a) g-h: a sheriff's department, police department, or other law enforcement agency may collect a fee from the defendant for investigation, collection, and processing of checks referred to their agency for investigation of alleged violations of this section or section 476. The amount of the fee shall not exceed twenty-five dollars ($25) for each bad check.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to any member of the Department of Animal Services.
documents.supersedes: SOP Bad Check Procedure, effective September 12, 2019 (revised from 12/06/2017 and 08/25/2015)
references: CA Penal Code 476(a) g-h
DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information
SOP: Uploading Pictures or Documents to Chameleon
SOP: Renewing Pet Licenses

definitions.def: Non Sufficient Fund (returned check)
Green Chameleon icon signifying a person has written Animal Services a check that was returned by the bank; must be accompanied by an Accounting memo

sections.heading: Notification of a Non Sufficient Fund (NSF) Check
steps: A designee from Accounts Receivable will notify the collections division that a check has been returned as NSF. He or she will put the Important Account Notice from Union Bank (Attachment A), along with the original Chameleon receipt (Attachment B) and the check, in the inbox of the collections division.

sections.heading: Look Up the Receipt in Chameleon
steps: Once you have obtained the paperwork, look it up in Chameleon. Refer to DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information, for log in instructions.
Once logged in, select Windows from the main menu screen, then select Receipt, then Receipt again. The receipt window will be displayed.
Place the cursor in the Receipt No field and type in the original receipt number from the paperwork (example: R15-040781).
Press the function key F12 to find the record.
Verify the name, address, and amount on the check match the information from the Chameleon receipt window.

sections.heading: Check the Receipt for License Fees
steps: If there are no license fees on the receipt, skip this section and go straight to initiating the Bad Check receipt.
If the receipt has license fees, go to the tag window and update the status of the license to Temporary. From the receipt screen choose Windows, then Tag.
Once you are in the tag window the PID should automatically be filled out. Find (F12) the record.
Select the correct license record to update. Refer to the original receipt (Attachment B) to choose the correct license record.
Double click on the license record to bring it up in the tag window.
Place your cursor in the Status box, highlight that field, and type the word Temporary.

Tab over and type in the applicable problem code: LM for late money or M for money. Pay special attention to the tag date; if that date is 30 or more days late, you must choose LM as the problem code. Refer to the SOP on Renewing Pet Licenses for a complete list of problem codes.
Place your cursor in the Price field and change the price to $0.00.
Update (F8) the record.
Repeat these steps for each additional license record.
Once completed, place your cursor in the receipt no field and popup (F4).

sections.heading: Initiating the Bad Check (NSF) Receipt
steps: Once you have verified the information, initiate the BAD CHECK process in Chameleon. The bad check process requires you to have access; see the Chameleon administrator for access to the bad check process.

If the check was processed as a credit in Chameleon, the receipt has to be JE'd by the original clerk. Contact them via email. After the original receipt has been JE'd, have the clerk restore the receipt as a check. Once restored, you can continue to the bad check process.
Keep the original receipt number open in Chameleon and select Procedures.
Click on BAD CHECK.
A message will appear on your screen. Click OK.
Another window will appear that reads Process Complete. Click OK to print the receipt, or click Cancel if you do not need to print the receipt.
Chameleon will automatically store a receipt number starting with the letter B. This B number will be displayed in the receipt field in the main transaction window.
A green snake icon will appear to signify a Bad Check. This must be accompanied with an Accounting memo.

sections.heading: Storing the Bad Check Accounting Memo
steps: After completing the receipt, the designee will initiate a Bad Check memo in Chameleon. This memo signifies that DAS will no longer accept checks from the constituent. Refer to DAS Policy 000-01 for more information on the memo window.
While in the receipt window, click on the notepad icon to pop up the memo screen.
Write the word Accounting in the Type field.
The memo ID is the current receipt number, the ID type is RECEIPT_No, and the date field should be today's date.
In the template field, popup (F4) and select the Bad Check template. Fill in the blanks on the template.
When finished writing the memo, store (F9) the record. Chameleon will automatically store a Memo No.
Hit the green check button to go back to the receipt window.

sections.heading: Storing the Billing Accounting Memo
steps: After completing the Bad Check memo, the designee will store a Billing memo. This memo signifies which fees the constituent owes the department and will include a $25 Bad Check Fee.
While in the receipt window, click on the notepad icon to pop up the memo screen.
Write the word Accounting in the Type field.
The memo ID is the current receipt number, the ID type is RECEIPT_No, and the date field should be today's date.
In the template field, popup (F4) and select the Billing template. Fill in the blanks on the template. You can erase the codes that do not pertain to the stored receipt line items.
When finished writing the memo, store (F9) the record. Chameleon will automatically store a Memo No.
Select the green check button to go back to the receipt window.

sections.heading: Placing the Snake Icon on the Person Window
steps: Once the Bad Check memo is complete, place your cursor in the PID field in the receipt window and popup (F4). Place a Y in the bad check field at the bottom of the screen.
Update (F8) the record.
A little green snake will appear on the PID record. This snake icon shall not be removed.
Hit the green check icon to go back to the receipt window.

sections.heading: Printing the NSF Letter
steps: Once the memos are stored and the receipt has been created, print the NSF letter to send to the customer.
While in the receipt screen, place your cursor on the Receipt No field and type in the newly created B receipt number (example: B15-040781).
Press the function key F12.
Leave your cursor in the Receipt No field.
Select Reports from the menu, then Run a Report.
From the drop down menu select NSF Notice and click OK.
A table will appear; enter the password NSF and type in the newly created B number (example: B15-040781).
Click OK. A letter will appear.
Review the letter to make sure it has the correct information, then print one copy.
Save the document to your computer so you can upload it.

sections.heading: Scanning the NSF Letter into Chameleon
steps: After printing the NSF letter, scan it into the PID. For a more detailed explanation on how to scan documents, refer to the SOP on Uploading Pictures and Documents to Chameleon.
Place the NSF letter face down in the scanner and turn it on.
Place your cursor in the PID field of the receipt window. Make sure that you still have the current receipt open.
Select Extras from the menu screen, then select CHAM CAM.
Place your cursor on the documents box and check it.
Make sure the image ID has the correct PID number in it.
Select the correct icon on the load section, then hit the save button.
Put the NSF letter in an envelope to mail out to the customer.

sections.heading: Applying a Payment for the Bad Check
steps: A payment via cash, money order, cashier's check, or credit card can be received via mail, phone, or in person. You cannot accept a check as payment from the constituent.
To apply a payment you must be in the main receipt window of Chameleon. Select Windows, then Receipt, then Receipt again. The Receipt window will be displayed.
Type in the PID number of the person you are taking the payment from.
Perform a Debt Lookup: select Procedures, then select Debt Lookup.

Remove any outstanding debt not associated with the Bad Check: click on the line item you want to remove, then hit the minus (-) button. Repeat this step until you are left with only the two Bad Check line items.
Both line items must be in the receipt window. Do not separate the line items or the debt may be erased.
Input the payment amount in the appropriate field.
Store (F9) the record.
Click OK to print the receipt. Give the customer the receipt or email it to them.

Once the receipt is stored, store an accounting memo: from the main transaction window in Chameleon, make sure the current receipt is open, click on the notepad icon to pop up the memo screen, and write the word Accounting in the Type field.
The memo ID is the current receipt number, the ID type is RECEIPT_No, and the date field should be today's date.
Tab over to the Templates field and popup (F4). Select the Payment Plan BAL template.
Fill in the blanks and make sure to include the fees from the Bad Check receipt to conclude this memo.
Store (F9) the record.

related.name: Uploading Pictures or Documents to Chameleon
Receipt window, Tag window, Memo window, Person (PID) window, CHAM CAM
Employee acknowledgment form (retained in personnel file)

appendices: Appendix: Attachment A, Important Account Notice from bank with check (attached separately)
Appendix: Attachment B, Original receipt from Chameleon (attached separately)

documents.title: Billing for Unredeemed Owner Known Animals and Foster No Returns
documents.purpose: This creates a billing cycle for animals fostered, transferred, or euthanized, whose owner did not surrender their pet to the shelter or whose owner or custodian abandoned their pet at the shelter.
documents.authority: Designated staff in charge of initiating invoices on behalf of the department.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to designated staff in charge of initiating invoices on behalf of the department.
documents.supersedes: SOP Billing for Unredeemed Owner Known Animals and Foster No Returns, effective May 24, 2017 (revised from September 29, 2014 and August 6, 2012)
references: Food and Agricultural Code 31253, Payment by Owner
Food and Agricultural Code 31254, Nonpayment: Abandonment
Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window
SOP Animal Redemption
SOP Animal Adoption
Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department
SOP Owner Notification Procedure
SOP How to Process Unredeemed Owned Animals Using the Chameleon Kennel Window

sections.heading: Verification of Data
steps: Log into Chameleon. Pay attention to the shelter location that the bill will be generated from.
From the Reports menu, choose the Billing report.
Choose the shelter location for which records will be reviewed for billing.
Preview or export to Word or Excel for easy reference.
Do not bill any unknowns, pet owner, adopter, resident, or records that do not have a disposition yet from the report.
You must be in the Chameleon kennel window to begin the review.
Place the cursor in the Search Back field.
Enter 999 as the default.
Enter the animal's 7-digit ID number in the AID field and find [F12] the record(s).

steps.step: Pop-up [F4] the memo window to verify that the record used as the basis for billing has the following information:
sub: Memo type: OWNER INFO or O NOTIFIED.
Memo extra1 field: Billing.

steps.step: Using the Animal ID, find the impound record of the animal and verify the following:
sub: The information in the Person To field is not the person who adopted the animal.
Mouse click on the memo icon to bring up the memo window. The memo window must have a memo type of Owner Info or O Notified and Billing in the memo extra1 field.
Check the Receipt window to ensure that the pet owner or custodian did not pay RTO fees or adoption fees.
Check all memo windows for any information pertinent to the owner and the animal's impound.
Check the treatment window to ensure that no additional veterinary services codes are owed.

steps.step: Once the information is verified:
sub: Go to Procedures.
Choose Preferences.
Click on Receipt.
Remove the X from the box that states No Positive Balance.
Make sure the debt script field is not blank.
Click OK to exit.

sections.heading: Billing
steps: From the kennel window, place the cursor in the receipt field on the intake section of the second kennel record.
Pop-up [F4] to bring up the receipt window.

Chameleon will automatically populate the debt owed. You must change the existing codes to collection line items. Double click on the line item you want to change. Once the line item is in the item code box, pop-up [F4] and select the collection line item of the same code. Then hit the + and then - to get rid of the original line item.
Refer to SOP Animal Redemption or the SOP for adoption for additional line item codes that are to be billed to the owner.
Include the payment plan line item.
Store [F9] the receipt.
Print one (1) copy to mail to the customer. For follow-up, scan one of the copies to the collections department at the Riverside Shelter via email.

steps.step: Click on the memo receipt icon and store [F9] the following information:
sub: Memo type: Accounting.
Templates: Billing.
Fill in the correct information on the items indicated.

sections.heading: Updating the Memo Record Containing Memo Type Owner Info or O Notified
steps: Remove Billing from the extra1 field of the applicable memo window to remove the record from the Billing report.
Update [F8] the record.

sections.heading: Updating the Kennel Record
steps: From the receipt window, press enter to go back to the kennel window.
Enter the original receipt number in the receipt field.
Update [F8] the record.

related.name: SOP How to Process Unredeemed Owned Animals Using the Chameleon Kennel Window
Kennel Window; Memo Window; Receipt Window; Billing Report

appendices: Appendix A: Sample Billing Prep Report (attached separately; contains customer records and is based on memo subtype of Billing)

documents.title: Chain of Command
documents.purpose: Institute a department-wide chain of command so that staff at all levels have a supervisor to whom they may ask questions or report problems, while creating efficiency at the same time.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Department of Animal Services employees
documents.supersedes: Chain of Command memorandum to All Staff, dated February 24, 2017
references: Acknowledgment of Employee Concern Form (included with original memorandum)

sections.heading: Chain of Command Procedure
steps: Bring any issue that warrants attention first to the attention of your immediate supervisor or lead.
If the issue remains unresolved, bring the issue to the attention of your manager, once you have notified your immediate supervisor of the same.
In the event the issue is still not resolved, bring the issue to the attention of the Executive level or division leader once you have notified your manager.
Accept the decision made at the Executive level with regard to the issue as final.

sections.heading: Exceptions to the Chain of Command
steps: Recognize that special circumstances may require ignoring or bypassing the chain of command. Examples include sexual harassment, discrimination, and hostile work environment.
Treat the chain of command procedure above as applying to issues more routine in nature.

sections.heading: Director's Open Door
steps: Use your supervisor, lead, or manager within the chain of command as your first-line support for each question or problem that arises.
In the case of chain of command failure or lack of response to your employment or job concerns, contact the Director's office directly.

sections.heading: Acknowledgment and Documentation
steps: Sign and date the memorandum acknowledgment when the directive is issued.

Complete the Acknowledgment of Employee Concern Form when meeting with a supervisory position within the chain of command about a concern, recording the supervisory position met with, the date, and the concern discussed.
Keep the completed Acknowledgment of Employee Concern Form for your own documentation.

appendices: Appendix A: Acknowledgment of Employee Concern Form

documents.searchText: institute a department-wide chain of command so that staff at all levels have a supervisor to whom they may ask questions or report problems, while creating efficiency at the same time. all department of animal services employees director of animal services chain of command procedure bring any issue that warrants attention first to the attention of your immediate supervisor or lead. if the issue remains unresolved, bring the issue to the attention of your manager, once you have notified your immediate supervisor of the same. in the event the issue is still not resolved, bring the issue to the attention of the executive level or division leader once you have notified your manager. accept the decision made at the executive level with regard to the issue as final. exceptions to the chain of command recognize that special circumstances may require ignoring or bypassing the chain of command. examples include sexual harassment, discrimination, and hostile work environment. treat the chain of command procedure above as applying to issues more routine in nature. director's open door use your supervisor, lead, or manager within the chain of command as your first-line support for each question or problem that arises. in the case of chain of command failure or lack of response to your employment or job concerns, contact the director's office directly. acknowledgment and documentation sign and date the memorandum acknowledgment when the directive is issued. complete the acknowledgment of employee concern form when meeting with a supervisory position within the chain of command about a concern, recording the supervisory position met with, the date, and the concern discussed. keep the completed acknowledgment of employee concern form for your own documentation. acknowledgment of employee concern form (included with original memorandum)

documents.title: Challenge Coins
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform guideline for the issuance of DAS Challenge Coins.
documents.authority: Approved by the Director of Animal Services. Deviation from the awarding guidelines must have Director or Deputy Director approval.
documents.scope: DAS employees and DAS volunteers; the Stronger as a Pack Coin also covers non-DAS county employees, employees of allied agencies, and the public.
documents.supersedes: SOP Challenge Coins, effective May 1, 2022

definitions.def: A coin used to instill unity among team members and to recognize excellent work; a tradition drawn from the military, law enforcement, and first responder communities
Riverside County volunteer emergency animal response organization referenced in the coin award process

sections.heading: General
steps: Award a challenge coin to a DAS staff member or volunteer as a moment to celebrate their hard work and achievement.

Whenever appropriate, present coins during briefings or other gatherings of the recipient's peers. However, traditionally, challenge coins are not distributed with a ceremony; coins are passed via a handshake with a palmed coin.
After awarding a coin, supervisors and managers should notify the department's PIO, which gives them the opportunity to highlight the achievement.
Recipients tend to carry the coins or display them someplace important. When asked about a coin, the owner should be able to tell the story of how they earned it.
A challenge coin is a thing of honor and should be treated like one.

sections.heading: Coin Supply, Custody, and Tracking
steps: The department has purchased a limited supply of multiple coins. Each coin represents a different achievement.

Each Operations Chief and Lieutenant will be issued a small cache of the Mission Statement Coin; the Enforcement, Emergency, and Disaster Response Coin; and the Stronger as a Pack Coin. Maintain coins in a secure place.
Award coins only based on the attached guidelines. Deviation from these guidelines must have Director or Deputy Director approval.
Track the issuance of each coin. Each division is responsible for maintaining records of when, to whom, and the circumstances of how the coin was earned for each coin awarded.
Scan and email logs to the Field Services Administrative Lieutenant quarterly.

The Field Services Administrative Lieutenant will be responsible for maintaining a department-wide log of all coins awarded. This department-wide log will be presented to the Director of Animal Services for review annually.

sections.heading: Mission Statement Coin (Design 1)
steps: Award the Mission Statement Coin to those who have gone above and beyond to support the department's mission of Working together to improve Riverside County for people and animals.
Description: front, badge with Riverside County Department of Animal Services; back, Animal Services logo and mission statement. Diameter 1.75 inches.
Eligibility: DAS employees and DAS volunteers.

steps.step: Guidelines for awarding the coin:
sub: Assisting in the preparation for or operation of department events beyond that of normal assigned duties (for example, special adoption events).
Providing significant assistance to another division inside the department.
Providing significant assistance to an outside agency beyond normal assigned duties.
Teaching or providing training to staff beyond normal assigned duties.
Extraordinary performance of duties well beyond expectations.
Employee of the quarter or similar award.
Director's discretion.

steps.step: Process for awarding the coin:
sub: Field Services: Lieutenant or above.
All other divisions: Supervisor or above with the approval of their Operations Chief.
Volunteers: shelter volunteers, awarded by the Volunteer Services Manager; REARS, Lieutenant or Commander.
All divisions: Director or Deputy Director.

sections.heading: Enforcement, Emergency and Disaster Response Coin (Design 3)
steps: Present the Enforcement, Emergency, and Disaster Response Coin for outstanding contribution to DAS's role in emergency or disaster mitigation, preparedness, response, or recovery. This coin may also be awarded in recognition of exceptional investigative work.
Description: front, badge with Riverside County Department of Animal Services; back, handcuffs, fire evacuation, DAS logo, and caduceus with Protect, Rescue, Adopt, Care. Diameter 1.75 inches.
Eligibility: DAS employees and DAS volunteers.

steps.step: Guidelines for awarding the coin:
sub: Significant response to a disaster or emergency outside of the employee's normal duties.
Mutual aid deployment outside of our County.
Investigating Officer after adjudication of a felony criminal case (may be awarded after adjudication for a significant misdemeanor case at the discretion of a Lieutenant or Commander).
Significant deployment to a disaster by a DAS volunteer (for example, REARS).
Director's discretion.

steps.step: Process for awarding the coin:
sub: Field Services: Lieutenant or above.
All other divisions: Supervisor or above with the approval of their Operations Chief.
Volunteers: shelter volunteers, awarded by the Volunteer Services Manager; REARS, Lieutenant or Commander.
All divisions: Director or Deputy Director.

sections.heading: Exceptional Service Coin (Design 2)
steps: Award the Exceptional Service Coin in recognition of the highest level of service to the community and animals. This honor is reserved for employees whose actions have brought great credit to themselves and to the department.

Description: front, badge with Riverside County Department of Animal Services; back, dog paw with American background and Exceptional Service To The People And Animals Of Our Community. Diameter 1.75 inches.
Eligibility: DAS employees.

steps.step: Guidelines for awarding the coin:
sub: Providing lifesaving aid to another person.
Protecting another person or an animal from an imminent threat of danger.
Taking essential action to protect another at a moment of clear and obvious danger to themselves.
Annual Director's Award or similar annual award.
Director's discretion.

steps.step: Process for awarding the coin:
sub: Staff nominated for the Exceptional Service Award will be reviewed by Field Services Command Staff. Nominees receiving a majority approval vote from Lieutenants will be awarded the coin. Tie votes will be decided by the Commander of Field Services.

Nominations are made by emailing Field Services' Administrative Lieutenant and the Commander of Field Services. The Administrative Lieutenant is responsible for presenting all nominations to Command Staff for voting.
At any time, the Director or Deputy Director may bypass the nomination process and award an Exceptional Service Coin at their discretion.

sections.heading: Stronger As A Pack Coin (Design 4)
steps: Give the Stronger as a Pack Coin in recognition of a non-DAS employee's aid to the department in achieving its mission.
Description: front, badge with Riverside County Department of Animal Services; back, dog pack with Stronger As A Pack, Thank You For Your Support. Diameter 1.75 inches.
Eligibility: volunteers, non-DAS county employees, employees of allied agencies, and the public.

steps.step: Guidelines for awarding the coin:
sub: A non-DAS employee who provided notable assistance to the department (for example, a deputy assisting DAS in executing a warrant).
An allied agency responding to DAS's request for mutual aid.
An outside trainer coming in to provide training to DAS staff.
Director's discretion.

steps.step: Process for awarding the coin:
sub: Field Services: Sergeant or above.
All other divisions: Supervisor or above with the approval of their Operations Chief.
Volunteers: shelter volunteers, awarded by the Volunteer Services Manager; REARS, Lieutenant or Commander.
All divisions: Director or Deputy Director.

related.name: Challenge Coin Log (date, name, coin, reason)

appendices: Appendix: Challenge Coin Log form (attached separately)

documents.title: Change Fund Audit Procedure
documents.purpose: To conduct quarterly spot checks of all change funds that have been issued to the Department of Animal Services (DAS) Fiscal Unit and confirm that the money is accounted for and safely secured.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to all DAS Fiscal - Cash Reporting Unit staff. For Fiscal use only.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-26, Change Fund Audit, effective September 29, 2009 (revised from June 14, 2007; formerly Policy 211); absorbs undated redline revision (000-26-rev by ndaudert) updating CHA-Fiscal references to DAS-Fiscal

definitions.def: A fund established to provide front counter and event staff with necessary change for cash transactions that occur within the Department of Animal Services.

sections.heading: Audits
steps: Conduct change fund audits unannounced, using the Change Fund Audit Form (Appendix A). The Accounting Technician I performs the audit. Spot checks confirm that accountability for government funds is appropriately monitored.

The Accounting Technician I counts the change fund with the Lead or Supervisor on staff. Both parties sign and date the Change Fund Audit Form (Appendix A) and submit it to the Administrative Services Division Director.
Conduct a change fund audit on each change fund issued to the Department of Animal Services, per the Fiscal List of Responsible Change Fund Holders (Appendix B).

sections.heading: Procedure
steps: The amount authorized at a unit or site should remain intact at all times. It is never to be over or short. Report any overages or shortages in a day's transactions on the Daily Deposit Distribution Memo (DDDM) (Appendix C) and on the Shortage/Overage Form AR-3 (Appendix D).
Maintain small bill denominations regularly in the Change Fund to achieve maximum availability.

Keep the Change Fund in safe handling and place it in the safe for security purposes. File cabinets with common locks are not acceptable. Take every precaution in control of the Change Fund lock box key and secured area to prevent the risk of loss or theft.

sections.heading: Filing of Audit Reports
steps: File all audit reports with the Accounting Technician I in a binder to provide to the Auditor-Controller's Office in the event of an external audit.

appendices: Appendix A: DAS Change Fund Audit Form (attached separately)
Appendix B: Fiscal List of Responsible Change Fund Holders (attached separately)
Appendix C: Daily Deposit Distribution Memo (DDDM) (attached separately)
Appendix D: Shortage/Overage Form AR-3 (attached separately)

documents.title: Compiling Monthly Statistical Reports
documents.purpose: To define what reports are submitted and their due date in compliance with city contracts.
documents.authority: Reports are submitted to designated staff in the Administrative Contracts Billing Unit and to contract city contacts.
documents.scope: The Crystal Report prime (Chameleon administrator) or backup is responsible for performing this task.
documents.supersedes: SOP Compiling Monthly Statistical Reports, effective February 7, 2017 (revised from 08/16/2016 and 08/01/2009)
references: City animal services contracts (Desert Hot Springs, La Quinta, Menifee)
Shelter Animals Count data matrix

sections.heading: City Billings - Report Requirements
steps: At the beginning of the new month, reconcile the following reports to ensure reporting accuracy.
From the Chameleon reports drop down menu, choose Activity Discrepancy Report.
Export the data as PDF and save the report for each Field Services area with the following format: 2017jan_western_activitydiscrepancyreport.
Email the field sergeant and field lieutenant for each area with the email title Activity - Kennel Discrepancy Report and the standard message.
Reports are due on the 5th of each month.
If a report is in Excel or Crystal format, convert the report to PDF format.

steps.step: Run Intake Statistics for the month:
sub: Compare the department total with the intake statistics report from the Chameleon reports dropdown menu.
Subtract intake type EUTH REQ from the department totals to make sure that the total intake numbers match.
Export to PDF format beginning with By Shelter Impounds to the last page of By City Impounds.
Save as: MMMM YYYY Intake Statistics by shelter and by city.pdf.

steps.step: Run Outcome Statistics for the month:
sub: Compare the department total with the outcome statistics report from the Chameleon reports dropdown menu.
Subtract outcome type EUTHANIZED - OWNER REQ from the department totals to make sure that the total outcome numbers match.
Export to PDF format beginning with By Shelter Outcomes to the last page of By City Outcomes.
Save as: MMMM YYYY Outcome Statistics by shelter and by city.pdf.

steps: Submit to designated staff in the Administrative Contracts Billing Unit in electronic (email) format.

sections.heading: City of Desert Hot Springs Monthly Report
steps: Due on the 5th of each month.
Email to the City of Desert Hot Springs contact and to the designated RCDAS staff (see Appendix A for the 2017 recipient addresses).
Convert the spreadsheet to PDF.

steps.step: Run the following Chameleon reports:
sub: DHS Field Services Activity Report (month).
City of DHS License Report.
Intake Statistics for dogs, cats, and others (green): Owner Turn-In is intake type OWN SUR; Stray is intake type STRAY; Dead on arrival is intake types DISP REQ plus STRAY-DEAD.

steps.step: Compile the final report:
sub: Open the Excel file MMMM DHS City Contract Monthly reports.xls.
Right click on the previous month's tab and choose Move or Copy.
From the move/copy options, place a check mark on Create a Copy.
In the Before Sheet box, scroll down and choose Move to End.
Rename the duplicate tab to the reporting month.

Based on the reports run or sent, enter the corresponding numbers for: Total Canines Impounded [Intake Statistics]; Total Felines Impounded [Intake Statistics]; Total Others Impounded [Intake Statistics]; Number of Requests for Services [DHS Field Services Activity Report total]; Number of Bite Calls [DHS Field Services Activity Report, INV Bite]; Number of Sick/Injured Animal Calls [DHS Field Services Activity Report: Stray Injured, Stray Sick, Wild Injured]; Vicious Animal (imminent danger) [DHS Field Services Activity Report, Stray Aggrs]; Cruelty/Neglect Calls [DHS Field Services Activity Report, INV 597]; Total Citations [DHS Field Services Activity Report, CITE]; Total Licenses [City of DHS License Report]; Number of Nuisance Complaints [DHS Field Services Activity Report, INV Bark].
Save the report as previous month, year, DHS City Contract Monthly Statistics (example: Aug 2009 DHS City Contract Monthly Statistics.xls).

sections.heading: City of La Quinta Monthly Report
steps: Due on the 5th of each month.
Email to the City of La Quinta contact and to the designated RCDAS staff (see Appendix A for the 2017 recipient addresses).
Convert the spreadsheet to PDF.

steps.step: Run the following Chameleon reports:
sub: License Records.
Veterinary Vaccert Summary.
LQ licensesIssuedRenewed_asof (date).

steps.step: Email the report as attachments covering the contractual items:
sub: A report to include but not limited to specific data such as the name of the licensee, date of issuance, date of expiration/renewal, description of the dog, and serial number of the license issued (the LQ license report).

A list of non-compliant veterinarians. Note from the original: a master list of all veterinarians in the county is not available, so only the list of who complied can be provided (the Veterinary Vaccert Summary).
Total licenses issued year to date (the LQ licensesIssuedRenewed report).
Licenses due for renewal (the LQ licensesIssuedRenewed report).

sections.heading: City of Menifee Monthly Report
steps: Due on the 5th of each month.
Email to the City of Menifee contact and to the Chief of the San Jacinto Animal Campus (see Appendix A for the 2017 recipient addresses).
Convert the spreadsheet to PDF.

steps.step: Run the following Chameleon reports:
sub: Intake Statistics for dogs, cats, and others (green): Owner Turn-In is intake type OWN SUR; Stray is intake type STRAY; Confiscate is intake type CONFISCATE.

Outcome Statistics for dogs, cats, and others (green): Adoption is outcome type ADOPTION; Transfer to Adoption Partners is outcome type TRANSFER ADOPTION PARTNER; Transfer to Contract Shelters is outcome type TRANSFER TO OTHER AGENCY; Reunited with Owners is outcome type RETURNED TO OWNER; Foster is outcome type FOSTERED OUT; Euthanized Untreatable is outcome type EUTHANIZED UNTREATABLE; Euthanized Treatable is outcome type EUTHANIZED TREATABLE; Euthanized Adoptable is outcome type EUTHANIZED ADOPTABLE.

steps.step: Compile the final report:
sub: Open the Excel file MMYYYY_Menifee_intakeOutcome.xlsx.
Update the dates for the reporting period.
Based on the reports run or sent, enter the corresponding numbers for Live Impounds (Intake Statistics by City) and Live Outcomes (Outcome Statistics by City).
Save the report as previous month, year, Menifee_intakeOutcome (example: MMYYYY_Menifee_intakeOutcome.xlsx).

sections.heading: Shelter Animal Count Data Matrix
steps: This data matrix requires tracking of impounded dogs and cats by species and age. The determining age for puppies and kittens for this report is under five months of age.

Beginning and ending shelter counts are the physical count of the animals presently at the shelter at the beginning and ending of the month, including animals in foster care and in the care of a veterinary hospital.
The object of this report is to make sure that the beginning shelter count plus total live intake matches the ending shelter count plus total outcomes.

steps.step: Run the following reports:
sub: Daily Average Kennel Population by Month.
Intake Statistics.
Outcome Statistics.

steps.step: Compile the report:
sub: Open the previous reporting period Excel spreadsheet titled DataMatrix_MMYYYY.xlsx.
Delete the previous month's data.
Save the spreadsheet for the reporting period (example: DataMatrix_jan2017).

Enter the corresponding numbers: A. Beginning Animal Count [daily average population report plus foster outcome]; B. All Strays [intake statistics] minus DOA; C. Relinquished by Owner [OWN SUR, intake statistics]; D. Owner Intended Euthanasia [EUTH REQ, intake statistics]; E. Transferred In from Agency [transfer from other agency, intake statistics]; F. Other Intakes [confiscates minus DOA, intake statistics]; H. Adoption [adoption, outcome statistics]; I. Returned to Owner [return to owner front counter, outcome statistics]; J. Transferred to Another Agency [add transfer adoption agency and transfer to other agency, outcome statistics]; K. Returned to Field [return to owner field, outcome statistics]; L. Other Live Outcome [normally 0]; N. Died in Care [died in shelter, outcome statistics]; O. Lost in Care [missing, outcome statistics]; P. Shelter Euthanasia [add euth treatable, euth untreatable, and euth adoptable, outcome statistics]; Q. Owner Intended Euthanasia [euthanized owner request, outcome statistics].
Once the numbers balance, save the spreadsheet.
From the menu bar, click on File, select Share, choose Email, and select Send as PDF.
Email to the program coordinator.

related.name: Reports drop down menu (Activity Discrepancy Report, Intake Statistics, Outcome Statistics, license reports)

appendices: Appendix A: 2017 report recipients

transfer to other agency; reunited with owners is outcome type returned to owner; foster is outcome type fostered out; euthanized untreatable is outcome type euthanized untreatable; euthanized mmyyyy_menifee_intakeoutcome.xlsx.", "update the dates for the reporting period.", "based on the reports run or sent, enter the corresponding numbers for live impounds (intake statistics by city) and live outcomes (outcome statistics by city).", "save the report as previous month, year, menifee_intakeoutcome (example: mmyyyy_menifee_intakeoutcome.xlsx)."]} shelter animal count data matrix this data matrix requires tracking of impounded dogs and cats by species and age. the determining age for puppies and kittens for this report is under five months of age. beginning and ending shelter counts are the physical count of the animals presently at the shelter at the beginning and ending of the month, including animals in foster care and in the care of a veterinary hospital. the object of statistics]; i. returned to owner [return to owner front counter, outcome statistics]; j. transferred to another agency [add transfer adoption agency and transfer to other agency, outcome statistics]; documents.title: Contract Billing - City of Riverside
documents.purpose: To ensure consistency and accuracy in billing practices.
documents.authority: The Contracts Billing Supervisor verifies and approves the completed billing packet; CHA-Fiscal provides final verification and approval before submittal to the City.
documents.scope: Staff responsible for preparing the monthly City of Riverside contract billing.
documents.supersedes: SOP Contract Billing - City of Riverside, effective May 15, 2009
references: City of Riverside animal services contract (FY08/09 terms, including the performance matrix)
PeopleSoft HRMS Labor Distribution Report

definitions.def: A payroll summary extracted from PeopleSoft HRMS to identify actual hours worked and time billed (vacation, sick, comp, etc.) per employee; generated per pay period and obtained from CHA-Fiscal
Community Outreach and Education division

sections.heading: Staffing Hours

steps.step: Prepare the Labor Distribution Report (Attachment A) for each pay period:
sub: Sort the document by Class and Dept ID to obtain the staff hours for the Riverside Shelter (class code 6601-).
Copy the report into the City of Riverside Excel billing file on the worksheet titled PPXXXX.
Retitle the worksheet PPXXXX to identify the specific pay period (for pay period 7 in 2008, the worksheet should be titled PP0708).

For most months there are only two (2) pay periods; however, twice a year there are three (3) pay periods. When this occurs, add an additional worksheet for the third pay period following the guidelines above.

steps.step: Using the information on the Labor Distribution Report, complete the Riverside Shelter Billable Hours report (Attachment B) located on the Payroll Register worksheet in the monthly billing file. Only staff identified by Class 6601- are to be extracted for this report:
sub: Change all month and year references to reflect the billing cycle that the report is being created for.

In columns D and E, enter the payroll end dates and the pay period number in the column titles. This information is found on the Labor Distribution Report. Note: when there are three (3) pay periods during a specific month, an additional column must be added to the right of the second pay period column.

Copy employee information into the report based on the operational division in which they work. Each operational division is broken down by Department ID in the Dept ID or Charge Dept ID columns: Veterinary Services 4200600100; Field Services 4200600200; Shelter Services 4200600300; Community Outreach and Education 4200600500.
Employee Name: enter the name of each employee under the specific division for which they work.
Position: enter the employee's title.
Pay Period: enter the total regular, overtime, and comp time hours worked. These hours are identified in the TRC column as REG (regular), OVT (overtime), and CTW (comp).
TPI Code: enter the code listed in the TskProfID column.
Division: enter the division name.
DEPT ID: enter the department ID.

steps.step: Under the COE Staffing (Deducted) section, enter the same information for the following positions within the Community Outreach and Education Division: Staff Analyst, Community Connections Chief, Senior Behaviorist, Chief Volunteer Coordinator, CHA Program Coordinator, Adoption Chief, Staff Analyst, CHA Program Chief, and Senior Public Information Specialist.
sub: Staff filling these positions are based at the Riverside shelter; however, they service all four (4) DAS shelters. Therefore, staffing hours for these positions should be subtracted from the COE total hours and not be included in the calculation of the City's monthly bill.
Confirm that the sub-totals and totals for each of the divisions are correct.

steps: Link the cumulative payroll hours total for each of the divisions to the corresponding Actual Hours Worked box on the Billing Summary worksheet (Attachment C).

sections.heading: Animal Intake Percentage
steps: Based upon agreement with the City, the Riverside shelter live animal intake percentage for July 2008 (the beginning of FY08/09) shall be used consistently throughout the fiscal year to calculate the shelter cost paid by the City. In July 2008, 45% of the live animal intakes were for City of Riverside animals and 55% were for residents of the unincorporated county area.
Confirm that 45% is entered in the Animal Intake % box on the Billing Summary worksheet (Attachment C).

sections.heading: Performance Matrix

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Shelter 1 (monthly reports to the City):
sub: The Department submits the monthly reports to the City via email and copies the Billing Clerk.
Enter a 1 in the # of Reports Due column.
Confirm that the Customer Service Survey report (Attachment E) was included in the email to the City.

If it was: enter a 1 in the # of Reports Received column; enter the email date in the Date Reports Received column; the report is due by the 25th of the following month, and if sent after this date, enter the number of days late; the Penalty column will automatically calculate the result.
If it was not, contact the staff member tasked with submitting these documents to request that they be forwarded to the City immediately, then follow the steps above.

steps: Performance Measurement - Shelter 2: leave blank.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Shelter 3 (returns within 90 days), using the Intake/Outcome report (Attachment F):
sub: Enter the number of animals adopted to City residents.
Enter the number of animals returned to the shelter within the past 90 days.
The % of Animals Returned within 90 Days column will automatically calculate the result.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Shelter 4 (adoptable euthanasia), using the Intake/Outcome report (Attachment F):
sub: Enter the number of adoptable animals euthanized.
The Penalty column will automatically calculate the penalty, if any, due for the month.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Shelter 5 (customer service surveys), using the Customer Service Survey report (Attachment E):
sub: Enter the number of surveys collected for the month.
Enter the cumulative total of surveys that were given an outstanding, good, or average overall grade.
The % of Average or Above Surveys column will automatically calculate the result and penalty, if any.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Shelter 6 (volunteer hours), using the Volunteer Hours report (Attachment G):
sub: Enter the total volunteer hours for the Riverside shelter for FY07/08.
Enter the total volunteer hours for the Riverside shelter for FY08/09.
The Increase/Decrease column will automatically calculate the result.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Shelter 7 (owner-known returns):
sub: Using the Intake/Outcome report for FY07/08 (Attachment H), enter the number of owner-known returns for the particular month during FY07/08.
Using the Intake/Outcome report for FY08/09 (Attachment F), enter the number of owner-known returns for the particular month.
The % of Increase or Decrease column will automatically calculate the result.

steps: Performance Measurement - Shelter 8: leave blank.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Field 1, using the Field Services Statistics & Response Times report (Attachment I):
sub: Enter the number of Priority 1 calls not responded to within one (1) hour.
Enter the number of Priority 2 calls not responded to within one (1) hour.
The Total and Penalty columns will calculate the result automatically.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Field 2, using the Field Services Statistics & Response Times report (Attachment I):
sub: Enter the number of Priority 3 calls not responded to within 12 hours.
Enter the number of Priority 4 calls not responded to within 12 hours.
The Total and Penalty columns will calculate the result automatically.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Field 3, using the Field Services Statistics & Response Times report (Attachment I):
sub: Enter the number of Priority 5 calls not responded to within 24 hours.
The Total and Penalty columns will calculate the result automatically.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Field 4 (complaint calls from City officials):
sub: Contact the Executive Assistant for the Director to obtain information regarding complaint calls received for the month from City officials.
Enter the number of complaint calls that were received from City officials for the month.
Enter the number that were responded to within two (2) days.
Enter the number that were responded to in three (3) days or longer.
The Penalty column will calculate the result automatically.

steps.step: Performance Measurement - Field 5 (monthly field reports to the City):
sub: The Department submits the monthly reports to the City via email and copies the Billing Clerk.
Enter a 3 in the # of Reports Due column.

Confirm that the following monthly reports were included in the email to the City: Intake/Outcome Report (Attachment H), Field Services Statistics and Response Time Report (Attachment I), and Field Services Impound Report (Attachment J).

If they were: enter a 3 in the # of Reports Received column; enter the email date in the Date Reports Received column; the reports are due by the 25th of the following month, and if sent after that date, enter the number of days late; the Penalty column will calculate the result automatically.
If they were not, contact the staff member tasked with submitting these documents to request that they be forwarded to the City immediately, then follow the steps above.

steps: The Performance Measurement formulas are set up to automatically calculate; however, double-check to make sure that all penalties were calculated correctly and accounted for.

sections.heading: Performance Matrix Summary
steps: The Performance Matrix penalties are automatically set to populate the Performance Matrix Summary (Attachment K); however, double-check to make sure that the formulas are working and that all penalties were accounted for.
Link the cumulative total for the Shelter Performance Measures (1-8) to the corresponding box on the Billing Summary sheet (Attachment C).
Link the cumulative total for the Field Performance Measures (1-5) to the corresponding box on the Billing Summary sheet (Attachment C).

sections.heading: Live Animal Impound Summary
steps: Using the Shelter Live Impounds report (Attachment L), enter the cumulative total of stray, owner surrendered, and confiscated animals for the Riverside shelter in the Total Animal Population field on the Animal % Summary tab (Attachment M) of the City billing.
Using the City of Riverside Intake Statistics Report (Attachment N), enter the cumulative total of stray, owner surrendered, and confiscated animals for the City in the City of Riverside field.
The percentage of City and County live animal intakes will automatically calculate.

sections.heading: Billing Summary
steps: Confirm that all fields have been populated and that all formulas are correct.

sections.heading: Billing Verification
steps: Print a complete copy of the billing to include: Billing Summary (Attachment C); Performance Matrix Summary (Attachment K); Performance Matrix (Attachment D); Animal % Summary (Attachment M); Shelter Live Impounds report (Attachment L); City of Riverside Intake Statistics Report (Attachment N); Riverside Shelter Billable Hours report (Attachment B); and the Labor Distribution Report (Attachment A) for each pay period referenced.
Submit the completed billing packet to the Contracts Billing Supervisor for verification and approval. He or she will sign and date the Billing Summary (Attachment C) when approved.
Submit the approved billing packet, along with an electronic copy, to CHA-Fiscal for verification and approval.
If errors are found on the billing, CHA-Fiscal notifies the Department so that corrections can be made.
Once approved, CHA-Fiscal creates a coversheet (Attachment O) to accompany the billing packet and returns the entire packet to the Contracts Billing Supervisor for submittal to the City.

documents.title: Crystal Report Writing Standards
documents.purpose: To define a consistent format for writing Crystal Reports so that all reports produced and published are similar in format and easier to read. This standard also applies to existing reports.
documents.authority: Chameleon Administrator/Support developed these standards.
documents.scope: All Crystal Reports produced and published by the department, including existing reports.
documents.supersedes: SOP Crystal Report Writing Standards, effective August 1, 2009

sections.heading: Standard Font
steps: Use ARIAL font, Western script, REGULAR font style, with the exceptions noted in the sections below.

sections.heading: Report Header

steps.step: Format the title as three lines, centered on the page.
sub: Line 1: DEPARTMENT OF ANIMAL SERVICES, bolded in caps, font size 14.
Line 2: The report title, bolded in mixed case (for example, Current Licenses - Missing Person.Jurisdiction), font size 12.
Line 3: The report description, bolded in mixed case (for example, Fiscal Year 1999 or Receipts Dated 99/99/99 through 99/99/99), font size 10.

steps: Place the County logo in the upper left corner on all reports. Crystal Report prime has the logo and will include it on the report when the report is moved from test to production status.
Line 4: Place the date/time in the top left corner of the report under the logo (for example, 09/02/00 08:15 AM), bolded, font size 10.

sections.heading: Page Header
steps: Center column headers over the columns, bolded, font size 10.

sections.heading: Details
steps: Format the body of the report not bolded, font size 10.

sections.heading: Page Footer
steps: Include a report description with author, creation date, filename and location, report comments, and modification date in the lower right corner, not bolded, font size 8.
Place page numbering in the lower right hand corner, as Page x of y when possible, not bolded, font size 10.

sections.heading: Orientation
steps: Set orientation to portrait or landscape, as appropriate.

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2009 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.searchText: to define a consistent format for writing crystal reports so that all reports produced and published are similar in format and easier to read. this standard also applies to existing reports. all crystal reports produced and published by the department, including existing reports. chameleon administrator/support developed these standards. standard font use arial font, western script, regular font style, with the exceptions noted in the sections below. report header {"step": "format the title as three lines, centered on the page.", "sub": ["line 1: department of animal services, bolded in caps, font size 14.", "line 2: the report title, bolded in mixed case (for example, current licenses - missing person.jurisdiction), font size 12.", "line 3: the report description, bolded in mixed case (for example, fiscal year 1999 or receipts dated 99/99/99 through 99/99/99), font size 10."]} place the county logo in the upper left corner on all reports. crystal report prime has the logo and will include it on the report when the report is moved from test to production status. line 4: place the date/time in the top left corner of the report under the logo (for example, 09/02/00 08:15 am), bolded, font size 10. page header center column headers over the columns, bolded, font size 10. details format the body of the report not bolded, font size 10. page footer include a report description with author, creation date, filename and location, report comments, and modification date in the lower right corner, not bolded, font size 8. place page numbering in the lower right hand corner, as page x of y when possible, not bolded, font size 10. orientation set orientation to portrait or landscape, as appropriate.

documents.title: Daily Deposit Procedure
documents.purpose: To establish a deposit method with a step by step process.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to all Fiscal - Cash Reporting Unit staff that handle the day to day deposits for the Department of Animal Services. For Fiscal use only.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-25, Daily Deposit Procedure, effective September 29, 2009 (revised from June 14, 2007)

sections.heading: Receipt and Revenue Code Balancing
steps: Arrange receipts in numerical order, keeping each clerk's receipts and money separated.
Verify the correct address and revenue codes on receipts. Make corrections if needed.

Run a calculator tape for each clerk's total receipts, cash, checks, and credit card totals. Label each tape with the clerk's initials and date, one for city and one for county. Run another calculator tape of each clerk's combined city and county totals and label it the same way.
Verify each clerk's total against the Cash Box Closing Report (Appendix A).

sections.heading: Preparing Receipts for Deposit
steps: Organize all receipts in numerical order, keeping city and county receipts separated.
Run a calculator tape of all city receipts. Do the same for county receipts.
Print a copy of the Chameleon Account Code Report for the city (Appendix B) and another for the county (Appendix C).
Match the total from the city report to the city receipts total. If out of balance, find the incorrect codes, make the needed corrections, and run a new report. Do the same for the county.
Verify all checks have a current date and the designated signature.
Verify all checks are stamped with the correct bank stamp.
Make photocopies of checks.
Arrange cash and checks in numerical order.
Input cash and checks into the Excel Cash Form (Appendix D).
Subtract the total amount of credit cards from the total amount of receipts. Make sure that total matches the total on the Excel Cash Form (Appendix D). Print the spreadsheet, sign, and date.
Assign another person from the fiscal department to verify cash and checks. That person runs a calculator tape of cash and checks, then initials and dates the tape and the spreadsheet.

sections.heading: Preparing Cash and Checks for Armored Transport Deposit: City
steps: Prepare the designated Bank of America bank bag.

steps.step: Prepare the three-part Bank of America deposit slip (Appendix E) and distribute the copies as follows:
sub: Attach the white copy to the spreadsheet along with the cash and checks.
Attach the pink copy to the shelter report.
Attach the yellow copy to the fiscal report.

steps: Fill out the Armored Transport slip (Appendix F).
Attach all money, checks, and the Bank of America deposit slip (Appendix E) to the Armored Transport slip (Appendix F). Place them in the bank bag and seal the bag.

sections.heading: Preparing Cash and Checks for Armored Transport Deposit: County
steps: Prepare the designated Union Bank bank bag. Checks are to be placed in the bottom bag.

steps.step: Prepare the three-part Union Bank deposit slip (Appendix G) and distribute the copies as follows:
sub: Photocopy the Union Bank deposit slip.
Attach the white copy of the deposit slip to the Daily Deposit Distribution Memo (DDDM) (Appendix H) along with the checks.
Attach the pink copy of the deposit slip to a copy of the DDDM and the cash.
Attach a photocopy of the deposit slip to the DDDM for Fiscal.
Attach the yellow copy of the deposit slip to the shelter report.

steps: Place checks in the bottom pocket of the bank bag and cash in the main pocket of the bag.
Fill out the Armored Transport slip (Appendix F).
Rubber band all logs to the county deposit.
Lock both city and county bags in the overhead cabinet until Armored Transport arrives for pickup.

sections.heading: Armored Transport Pick-Up
steps: When armored transport arrives, verify that the employee's name and picture are on the list of authorized drivers provided by the Armored Transport Company.

Once the driver's identity is verified, unlock the cabinet, remove the deposit from the cabinet, and hand it to the driver. Verify that the driver has signed the deposit book before relinquishing the deposit.

Appendix B: Chameleon Account Code Report for City (attached separately)
Appendix C: Chameleon Account Code Report for County (attached separately)
Appendix D: Excel Cash Form (attached separately)
Appendix E: Bank of America Deposit Slip (attached separately)
Appendix F: Armored Transport Slip (attached separately)
Appendix G: Union Bank Deposit Slip (attached separately)
Appendix H: Daily Deposit Distribution Memo (attached separately)

department of animal services. for fiscal use only. director of animal services receipt and revenue code balancing arrange receipts in numerical order, keeping each clerk's receipts and money separated. verify the correct address and revenue codes on receipts. make corrections if needed. run a calculator tape for each clerk's total receipts, cash, checks, and credit card totals. label each the designated signature. verify all checks are stamped with the correct bank stamp. make photocopies of checks. arrange cash and checks in numerical order. input cash and checks into the excel cash and date. assign another person from the fiscal department to verify cash and checks. that person runs a calculator tape of cash and checks, then initials and dates the tape and the spreadsheet. documents.title: Desert Cities Billing
documents.purpose: To prepare and submit monthly billings to the desert cities serviced by the Coachella Valley Animal Campus (CVAC): Cathedral City, Coachella Valley, Desert Hot Springs, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Palm Desert, and Rancho Mirage. Includes the monthly trap rental billing for the City of Palm Desert.
documents.authority: Billings are approved and signed by the supervisor before submission.
documents.scope: Clerical staff responsible for preparing monthly city billings for the desert cities serviced by the CVAC shelter.
documents.supersedes: DESERT CITY BILLINGS - STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES and PALM DESERT MONTHLY CITY BILLING - TRAP RENTALS, dated 10/2009
references: SOP Dog Licensing For City Monthly Billings

sections.heading: Monthly Desert City Billings
steps: At the beginning of each month, receive an email from the CVAC Shelter ACO Supervisor with monthly mileage and hours for the desert cities serviced by the CVAC Shelter. Save this to the Q Drive for each desert city.
Run the license report for each city (see Dog Licensing For City Monthly Billings SOP).
Print the license report.
Print the monthly mileage and hour list.
Enter the information from the license report and the mileage and hour list on the Monthly Billing sheet for the appropriate city (Q Drive/Anmshlt Admin/Fiscal/Contracts & MOUs/City of XXX/Billings).
Print the billing sheet.
Update the Summary and print the Summary.
Attach all printed forms together and turn in to the supervisor for approval and signature.
Make two copies of the billing: one copy for the billing file, and a second copy for the supervisor's billing binder.
Email the billing files for each desert city in the Q Drive to the designated fiscal staff contact at CHA Fiscal.
Hand deliver the original signed billing to the designated fiscal staff contact at CHA Fiscal.

sections.heading: Palm Desert Monthly City Billing: Trap Rentals

steps.step: Run a Revenue Report from Chameleon.
sub: Open Chameleon Reports.
Choose Run A Report.
Click Account Code Any Date.
Enter the dates for the month.
Click on the location for CVAC.
Arrow over to the right.
Scroll down and click OK.
Export to Excel.

steps.step: Review the report in the Excel worksheet.
sub: Scroll down to the bottom of the report.
Look for Trap Rent Cat in Column B.
Delete everything else on the report.

steps: Save the file to the Q Drive, City of Palm Desert folder.

steps.step: Prepare the Trap Rent Cat information.
sub: Delete all lines except for the ones listed as .00.
Verify any $2.00 amounts. Verify that the receipt is not associated with a Palm Desert address (these receipts usually are not Palm Desert addresses, but they should be verified anyway).

steps: Format the information as a trap rental summary for the city, listing date, receipt number, name, item (TRAP RENT CAT), quantity, amount, and trap number, with a total quantity at the bottom (see source document for sample layout).

campusVariations.variation: This procedure applies only to billings for the desert cities serviced by the CVAC shelter.

documents.title: Dog Issues Telephone Protocol
documents.purpose: To guide phone operators and front desk staff in assessing and responding to citizen calls regarding dogs, including bites, medical services, owner relinquishment, and found or stray dogs. Riverside County is committed to educating citizens on keeping animals safely indoors and at home and returning pets to their original owner.
documents.authority: Riverside County Animal Services is a statutory agency and needs legislative or contract authority to provide services to the citizens of Riverside County.
documents.scope: Phone operators and front desk staff handling dog-related calls.
documents.supersedes: DogIssuesprotocal (undated working document)

sections.heading: Assessing the Call
steps: When a call comes in from a citizen wanting assistance with a found or loose dog issue in their area, first assess the full extent of the citizen's need.

sections.heading: Dog Bite
steps: If a dog has bitten a human being and the citizen has it confined, Riverside County will impound the dog for 10 days from the date of bite for rabies observation.
At the discretion of Riverside County, impoundment may also be provided by an authorized veterinary hospital, at home, or at the shelter.
Citizens can bring the dog into the shelter, or an officer can be dispatched to the scene.

sections.heading: Owned Dogs: Medical Services
steps: Encourage all dog owners to spay or neuter their dogs and keep them indoors, properly vaccinate dogs against rabies and other communicable diseases, microchip their dogs, and affix a rabies and identification tag to their dogs' collar. Dog owners should also purchase a dog license.

Riverside County offers its residents low-cost, or in some cases free, services for spay or neuter, vaccination, and licensing for their dog. Other spay/neuter programs are also available. Refer the customer to the website where they can learn more about the services and make an appointment.

Ill or injured dogs: Riverside County provides euthanasia service to owners requesting to have their dog euthanized due to illness, injury, or age-related problems. Field pick up service is not provided. The dog must be brought to the shelter after an appointment is made online and a telemedicine appointment occurs. There is a $25 fee for euthanasia, along with a $25 fee for disposal.

sections.heading: Owned Dogs: Unwanted by Owners
steps: Encourage owners to consider relinquishment to the shelter as a last resort.

Moving: Suggest an initial inquiry regarding whether new housing accepts pets. There are many properties that allow pets; they can be found with patience. Also, if a pet owner can get an emotional support designation for their pet, most housing management companies will allow it.

Unwanted litter of puppies: Riverside County provides spay/neuter services to owners relinquishing litters of puppies and the moms. Encourage these owners to keep the litters until they are weaned from the mother to enhance the chances of the offspring being adopted. Riverside County can assist with resources (bottles, etc.) if needed.

Riverside County does not provide field service pick up for unwanted owned dogs unless the animal has been part of a bite case, is aggressive, or is part of a cruelty situation. To turn an animal in to the shelter, an owner surrender appointment must be made on the website.
Behavioral issues: Refer the caller to the website for more behavioral support.

Pregnancy, childbirth, or allergy: These are common reasons why dogs are given up, often on the advice of ill-informed doctors. Urge the citizen to contact their doctor for allergy medication and to contact their veterinarian for information on products and procedures available. At a minimum, encourage them to educate themselves before relinquishing a dog.
Abandoned in a home: Make an appointment to bring the animal into the shelter or send a field officer to pick up.

sections.heading: Homeless, Lost, or Stray Tame Dogs
steps: Statistically speaking, most dogs that are found are owned or cared for by a neighbor. Encourage the public to assist in finding the owner of a stray dog prior to bringing it into the shelter.

If the finder chooses to take the dog into their home, instruct them to file a found report on the website and follow the other guidelines listed on the site, including posting on local social media sites and scanning for a microchip.

If the finder wants to turn the dog in to the shelter, ask the person to make an appointment on the website to bring the animal in. If a person shows up at the shelter without an appointment, make them an immediate appointment. The animal will be held for the legal hold period.

Dogs will be picked up in the field if they are involved in a bite or animal cruelty case, pose a real threat to a person or to animals, or on a case by case basis after prevention and lost and found options are discussed.

If picked up by a field officer, the officer will scan the animal in the truck and attempt to locate the owner and return the animal in the field. If unable to locate the owner, impound the dog and put notes in the system about the attempt.

related.name: RCDAS website (found reports, owner surrender appointments, spay/neuter and licensing services)

documents.title: Evictions and Foreclosures Call Entry
documents.purpose: Change current practices related to evictions and foreclosures, effective immediately, in an effort to abide by all State and local laws, and standardize how staff enter eviction and foreclosure calls in Chameleon.
documents.authority: Operations Division, Department of Animal Services
documents.scope: All staff creating new Activities in Chameleon
documents.supersedes: Memorandum, Evictions and Foreclosures, dated June 24, 2015, and the prior practice of instructing owners or banks to hold animals for 15 days under Civil Code 1983
references: California Civil Code Section 1981
California Civil Code Section 1983
Penal Code Section 597 (referenced by Chameleon subtype POSS 597)

sections.heading: Background: Animals as Personal Property
steps: Understand the prior practice: when property owners or banks called to inform the Department of evictions or foreclosures, staff instructed the owner or bank to maintain the animals left behind for a period of 15 days as noted in Civil Code 1983, based on the understanding that animals are considered personal property.
Understand the basis for the change: current law as it relates to evictions and foreclosures, Civil Code 1981, states that personal property does not apply to animals.

sections.heading: Entering Eviction and Foreclosure Calls
steps: When receiving a call regarding a posted eviction or foreclosure, enter the call in Chameleon with Type INV and Subtype POSS 597, with a priority level of 3.
Add either the property owner's information or the bank representative's information in the comments line.

sections.heading: Abandoned Animal Calls That Are Not Evictions or Foreclosures
steps: Make no changes when entering calls regarding potentially abandoned animals that are not evictions or foreclosures; enter the activity in Chameleon the same way as before.
Note that these calls are handled slightly differently in the field and require some investigation.
Contact your supervisor with any questions related to this procedure.
