documents.title: Fix Duplicate Records
documents.purpose: To clear the database of duplicate animal, person, and group records throughout the Chameleon Animal Management System and to assure all valid transactions are properly recorded.
documents.authority: Chameleon administrators/support with delete privileges in the Chameleon Animal Management System.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to Chameleon administrators/support assigned to complete the fix duplicate procedure with delete privileges in the Chameleon Animal Management System.
documents.supersedes: SOP Fix Duplicate Records, effective May 27, 2009
references: Food and Agricultural Code 32003, Recordkeeping
Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window

definitions.def: Person Identification.
Animal Identification.
Two or more records having the same information, such as address, last name or first name, phone number, or license animal record.

sections.heading: Before You Begin
steps: Understand that this process eliminates duplicate Person IDs, Animal IDs, and Group IDs. It does not merge the information from one record to the other. The first step is to accurately identify the correct record and the duplicate record.

Look carefully at each record and decide which is most complete. For an animal record, choose the one that has the markings field completed. For a person record or group record, choose the one that has both telephone numbers and the ID filled out. Once the update process has been completed, the incorrect animal, group, or person record is deleted.

Confirm you have delete rights. If someone without delete rights completes the procedure, it will transfer all the data but not delete the duplicate, which leaves a Person ID or Animal ID floating in the database.
Do not run this procedure during normal business hours, as it may take several minutes to complete and can cause lockout conditions.

sections.heading: Entering Records in the Fix Duplicate Window
steps: Go to Procedures > Supervisor > Fix Duplicate ID.
Select either Group ID, Animal ID, or Person ID.
Leave New selected.
In the Correct ID field, enter the number for the correct person or animal.
In the Duplicate ID field, enter the number for the duplicate person, group, or animal.
Click the NEW button on the left side.

Verify there is one row of information in the list box. This row will contain both the correct and duplicate IDs and a brief description of each. If there are several to enter, continue with the above steps until all have been completed.

sections.heading: Processing the Duplicates

steps.step: Depending on the records being fixed, use the option of selecting either of the two check boxes at the bottom of the screen.
sub: The Extra Tables (Person Only) check is needed only when there are duplicate IDs for staff personnel. It is highly unlikely that a citizen would be assigned to activities as an officer.

The Store Tag can be used either for people or for animals. This will store an additional tag record for any animal or person that has a tag linking the original Animal ID, Group ID, or Person ID with A000000, G000000, or P000000. The Duplicate ID is placed into the tag number field with a tag type of DUP GROUP ID, DUP ANIMAL ID, or DUP PERSON ID.

steps: Select the rows to be processed by double-clicking on the box to the left of the correct ID, or select all rows by clicking the SELECT ALL button.
Click the FIX SELECTED DUPLICATES button.
Click OK to the message advising that this may be time consuming.
Once completed, a message box will advise how many records were either completed or not.

steps.step: The system will perform the following:
sub: Update Kennel record(s): changes duplicate Animal ID to correct Animal ID.
Update Activity record(s): changes duplicate Animal ID to correct Animal ID.
Update Animal Profile record(s): changes duplicate Animal ID to correct Animal ID.
Update Treatment record(s): changes duplicate Animal ID to correct Animal ID.
Update Tag record(s): changes duplicate Animal ID to correct Animal ID.
Update Memo record(s): changes duplicate Animal ID to correct Animal ID.
Deletes the duplicate Person, Group, or Animal record.

steps: Note the three radio buttons on the upper right. The New button is the default and should be used only when adding records to the list box. The Failed button will fill the list box with those records that were not successful in being fixed. The Successful button will list all those that were corrected.

sections.heading: If a Record Fails
steps: Click on the Failed button. The list box will fill with the record(s) that failed. The Result column has the code that gives clues on where to look to correct a record. These codes indicate the table (window) that caused the failure. See the attached list of Result Codes.
Correct the situation indicated by the result code.
Return to the Fix Duplicate ID window.
Click on the Failed button.
Find the record in the list box and click the FIX SELECTED DUPLICATES button.
Click OK.

sections.heading: Completing the Process
steps: Verify there are no items in the Failed box.
Click on the Successful box and review all the items that were fixed.
Click the SELECT ALL button and then click DELETE FROM LIST. This will remove them from the list box but will not delete the recently corrected items from the database.

appendices: Appendix A: Screenshots - New, Failed, and Successful Duplicate Submissions (attached separately)
Appendix B: Result Codes list (attached separately)

"update memo record(s): changes duplicate animal id to correct animal id.", "deletes the duplicate person, group, or animal record."]} note the three radio buttons on the upper right. the new button is the default and should be used only when adding records to the list box. the failed button will fill the list box with those records that were not successful in being fixed. the successful button will list all those that were corrected. if a record fails click on the failed button. the list box will fill with the record(s) that failed. the result column has the code that gives clues on where to look to correct a record. these codes indicate the table (window) that caused the failure. see the attached list of result codes. correct the situation indicated by the result code. return to the fix duplicate id window. click on the failed button. find the record in the list box and click the fix selected duplicates button. click ok. completing the process verify there are no items in the failed box. click on the successful box and review all the items that were fixed. click the select all button and then click delete from list. this will remove them from the list box but will not delete the recently corrected items from the database. food and agricultural code 32003, recordkeeping policy 000-03, chameleon kennel window

documents.title: How to Process Unredeemed Owned Animals Using the Chameleon Kennel Window
documents.purpose: To provide a procedure that is consistent and accurate for DAS staff to follow while maintaining the integrity of the animal's impound record. This process supports the seamless review and billing of impounded animals unclaimed by the owner or custodian.
documents.authority: Any DAS staff who reviews the Chameleon kennel record and deems that the animal is abandoned.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to DAS staff who is involved in the transporting of animals between DAS shelters.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to process Unredeemed Owned Animals using the Chameleon Kennel Window, effective May 24, 2017 (revised from September 28, 2014)
references: Food and Agricultural Code 31254, Nonpayment: Abandonment
Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window
SOP Animal Redemption
Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department
SOP Owner Notification Procedure

definitions.def: Animals impounded by the Department of Animal Services (DAS) that have verifiable identification of ownership, have a KNOWN WAIT status, and in accordance with Food and Agricultural Code 31254 are deemed abandoned in spite of reasonable attempts to contact the owner or custodian.

sections.heading: Method
steps: Any DAS staff who reviews the Chameleon kennel record and deems that the animal is abandoned shall place BILLING in the extra1 field of the memo window containing memo type O NOTIFIED or OWNER INFO. This ensures the record is included in the billing report initiated by the billing and collection department.
The staff member who adds the BILLING extra1 field is responsible for outcoming the original intake record and storing a new or second intake record by following the steps below.

sections.heading: Original Impound Record
steps: Confirm the outcome person-to Person ID is the identified owner or custodian of the impounded animal.
Enter RTO in the outcome type field.
Pop-up [F4] from the outcome subtype field to bring up a list and choose ABANDONMNT.
Press the function key [F5] in the outcome date field so Chameleon enters the current date.
Press the function key [F5] in the outcome time field so Chameleon enters the current time.
Enter the staff member's user ID in the outcome by field.
Save or update [F8] the kennel record.

sections.heading: Second Impound Record
steps: Place the cursor in the impound number of the original kennel window and erase the kennel number [KYY].
Erase the PID in the intake person from field and replace it with the PID of the outcome person to field (the owner) from the original impound record.
Erase the original intake type and replace it with RTO.
In the intake subtype field, pop-up [F4] to bring up a list and choose ABANDONMNT.
Change the date, time, and due out to the same date and time as the outcome date and time of the original impound record.
Do not change the impound location or jurisdiction.
Enter the staff member's user ID in the intake by field.
Delete all outcome information except for the weight and medical grade.
Store [F9] the new impound record.

sections.heading: Reviewing That the Procedure Was Successful
steps: Clear [F11] the Chameleon kennel window.
Enter the Animal ID or press [F6] to choose the most recent Animal ID record.
Find or press [F12] to find the records.
Verify the kennel table grid displays available records and the sequence of each impound record.

related.name: Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department

documents.title: Initiating a Bite Activity Procedure
documents.purpose: The rabies control desk or call center will initiate the bite activity, and the field officer will create the written report. This procedure addresses the lack of information on the activity screen versus faxed or mailed reports, and access to the victim after a bite incident. It is intended to eliminate duplicate bite reports and limit misplaced bite reports during transport from the rabies desk or call center to Field Services.
documents.authority: Rabies control desk and call center staff initiate the activity; Field Services Animal Control Officers complete the official Rabies Control Investigation Report.
documents.scope: Rabies control desk and call center staff receiving bite or scratch reports by telephone, fax, or mail. This procedure does not apply to admissions staff, who will continue to initiate bite reports on animals impounded or surrendered over the counter, nor to kennel staff initiating bite reports involving stray animals currently housed at RCDAS.
documents.supersedes: SOP Initiating a Bite Activity Procedure, effective June 24, 2022 (revised from January 2, 2014)
references: DAS Policy 000-04, The Chameleon Activity Window
DAS Policy 100-11, Duties of the Rabies Control Clerk, Office Assistant III

sections.heading: Background
steps: The activity window includes fields for most of the information that might be faxed (see attachment, Bite Report for Public Use). If a report is phoned in, the person receiving the report will gather as much information as possible for the investigation, completing the attachment to ensure the data necessary to complete the report is received.
In the event of a minor victim, a PID will be created from the faxed or mailed report to preserve any information given.

A memo template is used in the memo window of the activity to capture any details of the bite given by caller, fax, or mail that do not have a specific field on the activity screen. When a note is created, SEE MEMO is added to the comment field of the activity to alert dispatch of additional information for the responding officer.

sections.heading: Bite or Scratch Incident Reported by Telephone
steps: Complete the attachment Bite Report for Public Use as a guideline.

steps.step: Rabies desk or call center will initiate an INV-BITE activity with the following information:
sub: Activity type: INV.
Activity subtype: BITE.
Priority: 5.
Animal type: press the F4 key or pop-up to bring up a list of animals involved in the possible exposure.
Activity phone: enter the phone number of the adult victim, minor's parent or guardian, witness, or rabies desk phone number.
Caller ID: the Person ID of the adult victim, minor's parent or guardian, witness, or minor victim if a guardian is not available, or the rabies desk PID.
Activity address: enter the address of the dog owner or the current location of the biting or scratching animal. If the dog owner is not available, enter the address of the victim.

Comment: shall contain the date of bite; time of bite; victim name if a minor or if the person calling is different; location of the bite or scratch wound; how the bite or scratch happened (circumstances of the bite); where the report originated from if faxed from a doctor or hospital (for example, report faxed from DRMC); and See Memo for additional information.
Animal description: description of the biting or scratching animal.
Owner ID: the Person ID of the owner or custodian of the biting animal, if available.
Store or press F9 so Chameleon assigns an activity number.

steps.step: Click on the memo icon from the activity window.
sub: Memo ID must display the activity number assigned to the bite or scratch report.
In the template field, type BITE and pop-up [F4] to bring up options.
Choose Bite Details.
Complete the memo template.

steps: Field Services Animal Control Officers will initiate and complete the official Rabies Control Investigation Report according to policy based on the activity.

related.name: DAS Policy 000-04, The Chameleon Activity Window
DAS Policy 100-11, Duties of the Rabies Control Clerk, Office Assistant III
Activity Window; Memo Window (Bite Details template)

appendices: Appendix A: Sample Human vs. Animal Activity Screenshots (minor and adult victims) (attached separately)
Appendix B: Bite Report for Public Use (attached separately)

documents.title: Inventory of License Tags and Numbered Assets
documents.purpose: To provide consistent guidelines and ensure pet license tags and numbered forms (citation books) are accounted for and recorded in an accurate and timely manner. Monitoring of liquid assets is required to ensure proper accounting of all controlled items and that all transactions are recorded. Per County accounting procedures, periodic spot-checks should be performed by designated staff other than the user of the items, and the findings documented.
documents.authority: The supervising office assistant I, licensing inspector lead, or designee of the veterinary services licensing division.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable only to the designated DAS employees charged with numbered forms and pet license tag inventory. Access to the Chameleon certification book is restricted to specific employees.
documents.supersedes: SOP Inventory of License Tags and Numbered Assets, effective August 18, 2015
references: County accounting procedures

definitions.def: License tags, official County receipt books, citation books.

sections.heading: Pet License Tags: Recording
steps: The supervising office assistant I or designee of the veterinary services licensing division is responsible for ordering the department's license tags directly or through contracted cities and recording the delivery of pet license tags in the Chameleon cert book.
Log in to Chameleon.
From the menu tab, click on Windows.
Choose Certification Book from the drop-down menu.

Enter OPEN in the book status field if all boxes have been issued to staff. If previous supplies of tags have not yet been fully issued, then enter CLOSED (note: this status will be changed to OPEN once the status of the previous order is closed).
Press F5 to enter the current date.
Tab to the Group ID field.
Press function key 4 (F4) to bring up the Group window.

steps.step: In the group name field, type in the following:
sub: County: for County of Riverside tags.

City: choose the applicable city tag (City of Desert Hot Springs Tags; City of La Quinta Tags 1 Year; City of La Quinta Tags 2 Year; City of La Quinta Tags 3 Year; City of Rancho Mirage Tags; City of Riverside Tags; City of Coachella Valley Tags; City of Cathedral City Tags; City of Palm Desert Tags).

steps: Press the enter key to bring the group name back to the certification window.
Enter the beginning number in the start seq field. This is typically the starting number of the order received.
Enter the ending number in the end seq field. This is the last sequence number of the order received.
Enter the number 1 in the issue seq field, as one box is assigned for each user.
Store (F9).

sections.heading: Pet License Tags: Issuing
steps: Pet license tags are stored in a secured area and issued to staff by the box (100 tags) on an as-needed basis through the Chameleon certification book.
Log in to Chameleon.
From the menu tab, click on Windows.
Choose Certification Book from the drop-down menu.
Tab to the Group ID field.

steps.step: Press function key 4 (F4) to bring up the Group window.
sub: In the group name field, use the wildcard (%) key along with the name of the employee. For example, Alison% or %Alison%.
Press function key 4 (F4) to bring up a list based on the employee's name.
If the employee does not exist, create a new group record by following the format: fname, lname, dash, and type of city tag, complete with work address, telephone number, and jurisdiction.
Choose the applicable employee name for the type of tag.
Press OK once the choice is made.
Press the enter key to bring the group name back to the certification window.

steps: Perform a search using function key 12 (F12). If previous records exist, double mouse-click on the record with an OPEN status. If the record is new, skip the next step.
Change the status to CLOSED, press F5 in the complete date field, and update the record (F8).

steps.step: Create a new sequence book for the new box of tags.
sub: Enter the beginning number in the start seq field. This is typically the starting number of the order received.
Enter the ending number in the end seq field. This is the last sequence number of the order received.
Enter the number 1 in the issue seq field, as one pet license tag is issued for each dog.
Enter the leading characters of the current license prefix. For example, L15.

steps: Store (F9).

sections.heading: Citation Books: Recording
steps: The supervising office assistant I, licensing inspector lead, or designee is responsible for recording the delivery of official numbered forms (citations) in the Chameleon cert book.
Log in to Chameleon.
From the menu tab, click on Windows.
Choose Certification Book from the drop-down menu.

Enter OPEN in the book status field if all boxes have been issued to staff. If previous supplies have not yet been fully issued, then enter CLOSED (note: this status will be changed to OPEN once the status of the previous order is closed).
Press F5 to enter the current date.
Tab to the Group ID field.

steps.step: Press function key 4 (F4) to bring up the Group window and choose the applicable group:
sub: County (covers Indio, Eastvale, and Jurupa Valley): choose County of Riverside Citations - License Inspectors, or County of Riverside Citations - Field Services.
City: choose City of Riverside Citations - License Inspectors, or City of Riverside Citations - Field Services.

steps: Press the enter key to bring the group name back to the certification window.
Enter the beginning number in the start seq field. This is typically the starting number of the order received.
Enter the ending number in the end seq field. This is the last sequence number of the order received.
Enter the number 1 in the issue seq field, as one box is assigned for each user.
Store (F9).

sections.heading: Citation Books: Issuing
steps: Citations are stored in a secured area and issued to staff by books (20 pages) on an as-needed basis through the Chameleon certification book.
Log in to Chameleon.
From the menu tab, click on Windows.
Choose Certification Book from the drop-down menu.
Tab to the Group ID field.

steps.step: Press function key 4 (F4) to bring up the Group window.
sub: In the group name field, use the wildcard (%) key along with the name of the employee. For example, David% or %David%.
Press function key 4 (F4) to bring up a list based on the employee's name.
If the employee does not exist, create a new group record by following the format: fname, lname, dash, and type of city tag, complete with work address, telephone number, and jurisdiction.

steps: Choose the applicable employee name for the type of citation book issued.
Press OK once the choice is made.
Press the enter key to bring the group name back to the certification window.
Perform a search using function key 12 (F12). If previous records exist, double mouse-click on the record with an OPEN status. If the record is new, skip the next step.
Change the status to CLOSED, press F5 in the complete date field, and update the record (F8).

steps.step: Create a new sequence book for issuing citation books.
sub: Enter the beginning number in the start seq field. This is typically the starting number of the order received.
Enter the ending number in the end seq field. This is the last sequence number of the order received.
Enter the number 1 in the issue seq field, as one page is issued.
Enter the leading characters of the current citation book prefix. For example, A for county and RA for city.

steps: Store (F9).

steps.step: Periodic review shall include:
sub: Running and printing the License Tag and Numbered Forms inventory report and the User Inventory of Tags and Numbered Forms report.
Following the tag number or citation number through the Chameleon database system to the Receipt window.
Documenting the review in the box on the report with the date reviewed and the initials of the reviewer.
Documenting any discrepancies in the memo window of the group name and forwarding them to the Chameleon administrator for follow-up.
Investigating tag numbers or citations not recorded in Chameleon to determine if they are to be issued at a future date or were issued to another user.

related.name: Certification Book; Group Window; Receipt Window
County accounting procedures for controlled items

documents.title: Issuing a New Pet License or Tag
documents.purpose: To establish uniform and consistent guidelines when processing new pet license transactions using the Chameleon Tag Window.
documents.authority: Licensing staff; see the Licensing Supervisor for issuing a special (service) license tag.
documents.scope: Staff processing new pet license transactions at the front counter, by mail, through the lockbox (web licensing), at shot clinics, or generated by citation.
documents.supersedes: SOP Issuing New Licenses, effective October 29, 2019 (revised from June 26, 2019; November 7, 2018; December 6, 2017; June 29, 2016; April 27, 2016; April 1, 2009)
references: Title 17 California Code of Regulations, Section 2606.4
Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6
Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information
California Public Health Compendium of approved rabies vaccines

definitions.def: A dog owner has complied with the license requirements needed to have a valid license: a CA-approved rabies vaccination that will cover the licensing term, a microchip, and the license fee.
A tag number beginning with a U, automatically generated by Chameleon for a pending (temporary) license record. A U number is not a license number.
Defined by state law and county ordinance as 30 days from the renewal date or creation/purchase date. The due date does not change because of the TEMPORARY status.

sections.heading: Issuing a New License: Points to Remember
steps: In accordance with Title 17 California Code of Regulations Section 2606.4(a).3, a license is required 30 days after the dog is four months of age.

steps.step: The license expiration date will be 12 months from the vaccination date or from when the dog attains the age of four months, whichever comes first.
sub: Example 1: if the dog was vaccinated for the first time on 1/1/2019 and the owner complies with licensing on 6/1/2019, the license will expire on 1/1/2020.

Example 2: if the dog is licensed for the first time on 4/1/2019, does not have existing rabies vaccination history, and presents a rabies vaccination certificate good for three years given on 1/1/2019, the license will expire on 1/1/2020.

Example 3: if the dog owner pays for a license on 1/1/2019 and produces proof of a rabies vaccination with a vaccination date of 4/1/2019 and it is a first-time license, then the license expires on 4/1/2020.

steps.step: A new license means that a dog owner has complied with the license requirements needed to have a valid license: a CA-approved rabies vaccination that will cover the licensing term, a microchip, and the license fee.
sub: If paying the discounted spay or neuter fee, the owner must produce a copy of the pet's spay or neuter certificate.
If paying the senior discounted fee, the owner must produce a copy of the owner's identification showing date of birth and proof that the dog is spayed or neutered.

steps: A PERMANENT license tag is issued at the front counter or mailed with a license confirmation letter.
A license CERTIFICATE postcard is mailed.
If the dog owner submitted a license application along with all other documents, these documents are scanned to the license record.

sections.heading: Issuing a New License: Chameleon Process
steps: Create and store a Person ID and an Animal ID before a license may be issued. Check to see if the owner exists in Chameleon (see Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information). If the pet owner is not in the system, create and store the Person ID and Animal ID. If several licenses will be issued, set the computer's preferences; refer to General Chameleon Procedures.
Receive all license requirements prior to issuing a new license.

Place the cursor in the Tag No box and enter the tag number (issued by the box to each user) preceded by L and the last two digits of the current year (for example, tag #000123, year 2000: enter L00-000123).

steps.step: Tab over to the Tag Type field and enter the type of license being processed. Choose from one of the following:
sub: LIC: this will be the most commonly used type.
LIC SN: use this only if there is verification that the dog has been sterilized.
LIC SR: use this only if there is verification of sterility and senior citizen status.
LIC SERV: use this only if documentation has been provided showing that the animal is a certified guide dog. See the Licensing Supervisor for issuing a special license tag.

steps: Enter today's date in the Tag Date field. If the tag record has been created, the tag date shall stay the same regardless of the date when the owner complied.
Enter 12 months as the term of the license.
Enter the license expiration date: one year from the vaccination date or from when the dog attains the age of four months, whichever comes first (see the examples under Points to Remember).
Enter the status as Current, unless the license being processed is incomplete, in which case the status will be Temporary (refer to the New Incomplete Pet License section).
Vacc Date: enter the date the rabies vaccination was administered.
Term: if the rabies vaccination was administered to the dog or cat for the first time, enter 12. If the rabies vaccination is a booster, enter 36.

steps.step: Enter the rabies vaccination information:
sub: Vaccine: enter the product name based on the California Public Health Compendium approved for use in California. For example, IMRAB3.
Cert No: enter the rabies tag number from the rabies certificate, if provided.
Lot No: enter the lot number of the rabies vaccine, if provided.
Lot Exp: enter the rabies vaccine lot expiration date, if provided.

Vet ID: enter the name of the clinic if the veterinarian who administered the vaccine is a member of a clinic. Pop-up (F4) to open the group window; perform a wildcard search by entering a percent (%) sign before and after a few letters of the clinic name in the agency/group field (for example, %BUTC%); press F4 to view the list of choices; choose the most complete group record, then press F12; press Enter to bring the information back to the tag window.

steps: Enter the total amount of money collected in the price field.

Store the information by clicking on the plus icon or pressing F9. The Person and Animal will be linked under the Tag/Link window as one record; therefore it is extremely important to store the information before leaving this window. The upper left corner will display the message Store OK.

steps.step: Store a receipt:
sub: Pop-up (F4) the receipt window.
Make sure the Person ID and Animal ID are correct.
Below the item code, the Tag Type (LIC, LIC SN, etc.) will appear.
If not, close the receipt window and store (F9) the tag window.
Pop-up (F4) the receipt window and verify the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price are displayed.
Click on the green ADD button.
Enter the total amount of monies received and store (F9) the information.
A window will appear; print 1 page and 1 copy only unless at the front counter.

sections.heading: New Incomplete Pet License: General
steps: In accordance with California Code of Regulations, Title 17, 2606.4, a license is only valid if all the requirements to license a dog or cat are provided at the time of the purchase. The Department of Animal Services will accept the payment in good faith provided the missing documents are provided within 30 days from when the license was due.
The DUE DATE is defined by state law and county ordinance as 30 days from the renewal date or creation/purchase date. The due date does not change because of the TEMPORARY status.

Create a pending (TEMPORARY tag status) license record to allow the customer to submit the missing documents. The dog is not considered licensed until all requirements are met. Following the steps below ensures that the Pending License Notification program output is correct.

steps.step: The following field entries are common to all incomplete license scenarios below:
sub: Tag subtype: Counter (used primarily for front counter licensing transactions); Mail (used for payments received through mail); HLP Web (used primarily for lockbox licensing transactions); Shot Clini (used for any licensing transactions during a shot clinic event); or Cite Field (signifies that the license transaction was generated because of an administrative citation issued to the owner).
Jurisdiction: make sure that the tag jurisdiction matches the jurisdiction in the person window of the owner.
Status: enter TEMPORARY or TMP.
Tag date: enter today's date.
Problem codes can be combined with other problem codes (refer to Appendix B).

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Missing Proof of Spay or Neuter Certificate (Problem Code A)
steps: Tag No: leave blank and Chameleon will automatically generate a U number. A tag number beginning with a U is not a license number.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.

Problem code: enter A to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that proof of spay or neuter is required or the balance from a regular unaltered license fee is due, except for female dogs 12 years or older and male dogs 10 years or older in the unincorporated areas of Riverside County.
Term: the term of the license will always remain 12 months.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date unless the rabies vaccination expires sooner than the 12 months.
Vacc Date and rabies term: enter the vaccination date; enter 12 for a first vaccination or 36 for a booster.
Enter the rabies vaccination information (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID) as described in the Chameleon Process section.
Price: enter the amount paid or received.
Store (F9) the record.

steps.step: Press F3 to access reports and press T or scroll down to Temporary License Notice.
sub: Mouse-click OK to display the notice.
Review the notice and make sure the instructions match the missing requirement.

Create an Animal ID memo using the memo templates Weblicensing A (for lockbox transactions) or Temporary A (for mail or front counter licensing transactions). The memo templates can be combined with other problem code memo templates. The text must match what is missing or needed.
The due date in the memo must match the due date on the temporary notice.
Print the notice and mail.

steps.step: Create a receipt:
sub: Pop-up (F4) the receipt window and make sure the Person ID and Animal ID are correct.
Double click on the item code and change to the spay/neuter tag type applicable for the jurisdiction. For example, LIC SN 1YR.
Pop-up (F4) the receipt window; the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price must be displayed.
Click on the green ADD button. Enter the total amount of monies received and store (F9) the information.
A window will appear. Print one page only for the deposit.

steps.step: To clear problem code A:
sub: Once proof of a spay or neuter certificate is received, pop-up (F4) the animal window from the Animal ID field: enter the spay/neuter completed date in the spay/neuter field, place a Y in the proof field, enter the Vet ID (clinic) using the group window wildcard search, then update (F8) the animal record and press Enter to go back to the tag window.
Type over the U number and enter a license number in the tag no field (refer to the tag number format in the Chameleon Process section).
Change the tag type to the applicable spay/neuter tag type. For example, LIC SN 1YR.
S/N field: enter S for female spayed or N for male neutered. This field is only used when proof is provided after payment was made.
Status: change from TEMPORARY to CURRENT.
Remove problem code A.
Update (F8) the license record.
Scan the document to the license number through Chamcam.

Press F3 to access reports and press L or scroll down to License Certificate: enter the password; verify the license information and animal description match the tag window; print one copy; staple the tag number to the folded license certificate and mail.

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Pending Rabies Vaccination Deferment Request (Problem Code V)
steps: If any letter is submitted, this is not acceptable but needs to be legibly scanned to the tag record. The exemption request is the required form as approved by the state and must be completed and submitted by the pet owner.
Tag No: use the same license number except for the prefix year.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.
Problem code: enter V to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that proof of a new or current rabies certificate is required.
Term: the term of the license is dependent on the type of license the customer paid for.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date.
Vacc Date, rabies term, and all rabies vaccination information fields (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID): leave blank.
Price: enter the amount paid or received.
Store (F9) the record.

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Missing Proof of Current or New Rabies Vaccination Certificate (Problem Code V)
steps: Tag No: leave blank and Chameleon will automatically generate a U number.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.
Problem code: enter V to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that proof of a new or current rabies certificate is required.
Term: the term of the license will always remain 12 months.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date.
Vacc Date, rabies term, and all rabies vaccination information fields: leave blank.
Price: enter the amount paid or received.
Store (F9) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press T or scroll down to Temporary License Notice. Review the notice, create an Animal ID memo using the Weblicensing V or Temporary V template, verify the due date in the memo matches the temporary notice, and print and mail the notice.

Create a receipt: pop-up (F4) the receipt window, verify the Person ID and Animal ID, verify the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price are displayed, click the green ADD button, enter the total amount of monies received, and store (F9). Print one page only for the deposit.

steps.step: To clear problem code V:
sub: Once proof of a current or new rabies vaccination certificate that will cover the entire licensing term is received, type over the U number and enter a license number in the tag no field.
Status: change from TEMPORARY to CURRENT.
Remove problem code V.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date unless the rabies vaccination expires sooner than the 12 months.
Vacc Date: enter the date the rabies vaccination was administered.
Term: enter 12 for a first vaccination or 36 for a booster.
Enter the rabies vaccination information (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID) as described in the Chameleon Process section.
Update (F8) the license record.
Scan the document to the license number through Chamcam.

Press F3 to access reports and press L or scroll down to License Certificate: enter the password; verify the license information and animal description match the tag window; print one copy; staple the tag number to the folded license certificate and mail.

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Missing Late Fee or Late Fee Owed (Problem Code L)
steps: A license is delinquent, according to Title 17, 2606.4, when a person has owned a dog more than 30 days that is four months old or older and is rabies vaccinated.
Tag No: leave blank and Chameleon will automatically generate a U number.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.
Problem code: enter L to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that a late fee is owed.
Term: the term of the license will always remain 12 months.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date unless the rabies vaccination expires sooner than the 12 months.
Vacc Date and rabies term: enter the vaccination date; enter 12 for a first vaccination or 36 for a booster.
Enter the rabies vaccination information (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID) as described in the Chameleon Process section.
Price: enter the amount paid or received.
Store (F9) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press T or scroll down to Temporary License Notice. Review the notice, create an Animal ID memo using the Weblicensing L or Temporary L template, verify the text and balance match what is missing or needed, verify the due date in the memo matches the temporary notice, and print and mail the notice.

Create a receipt: pop-up (F4) the receipt window, verify the Person ID and Animal ID, verify the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price are displayed, click the green ADD button, enter the total amount of monies received, and store (F9). Print one page only for the deposit.

steps.step: To clear problem code L:
sub: Once payment of the late fee is received, type over the U number and enter a license number in the tag no field.
Status: change TEMPORARY to CURRENT.
Remove problem code L.
Add the late fee to the license fee in the price field.
Update (F8) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press L or scroll down to License Certificate: enter the password; verify the license information and animal description match the tag window; print one copy; staple the tag number to the folded license certificate and mail.
Create a receipt for the late fee payment and print one page only for the deposit.

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Missing License Fee or Partial License Fee Owed (Problem Code M)
steps: Tag No: leave blank and Chameleon will automatically generate a U number.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.
Problem code: enter M to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that a license fee is owed.
Term: the term of the license will always remain 12 months.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date unless the rabies vaccination expires sooner than the 12 months.
Vacc Date and rabies term: enter the vaccination date; enter 12 for a first vaccination or 36 for a booster.
Enter the rabies vaccination information (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID) as described in the Chameleon Process section.
Price: enter the actual payment received, or $0 if payment was not received.
Store (F9) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press T or scroll down to Temporary License Notice. Review the notice, create an Animal ID memo using the Weblicensing M or Temporary M template, verify the text and balance match what is missing or needed, verify the due date in the memo matches the temporary notice, and print and mail the notice.

Create a receipt: if payment was not received, leave blank. If partial payment was received, pop-up (F4) the receipt window, verify the Person ID and Animal ID, verify the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price are displayed, click the green ADD button, enter the total amount of monies received, and store (F9). Print one page only for the deposit.

steps.step: To clear problem code M:
sub: Once the license fee balance owed is received, type over the U number and enter a license number in the tag no field.
Status: change TEMPORARY to CURRENT.
Remove problem code M.
Add the balance owed to the license fee in the price field.
Update (F8) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press L or scroll down to License Certificate: enter the password; verify the license information and animal description match the tag window; print one copy; staple the tag number to the folded license certificate and mail.
Create a receipt for the balance payment and print one page only for the deposit.

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Missing Proof of Senior Age for Owner, 60 Years Old (Problem Code S)
steps: Tag No: leave blank and Chameleon will automatically generate a U number.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.
Problem code: enter S to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that the owner must send proof of age.
Term: the term of the license will always remain 12 months.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date unless the rabies vaccination expires sooner than the 12 months.
Vacc Date and rabies term: enter the vaccination date; enter 12 for a first vaccination or 36 for a booster.
Enter the rabies vaccination information (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID) as described in the Chameleon Process section.
Price: enter the actual payment received.
Store (F9) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press T or scroll down to Temporary License Notice. Review the notice, create an Animal ID memo using the Weblicensing S or Temporary S template, verify the text and balance match what is missing or needed, verify the due date in the memo matches the temporary notice, and print and mail the notice.

Create a receipt: pop-up (F4) the receipt window, verify the Person ID and Animal ID, verify the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price are displayed, click the green ADD button, enter the total amount of monies received, and store (F9). Print one page only for the deposit.

steps.step: To clear problem code S:
sub: Once proof of senior age for the owner is received, place the cursor on the Person ID field and press F4 to bring up the person window: enter the birthdate in the dob field, enter SENIOR in the status field of the person window, update (F8) the person window, and press Enter to go back to the tag window.
Type over the U number and enter a license number in the tag no field.
Change the tag type to the appropriate senior tag type code based on jurisdiction. For example, LIC SR 1YR.
Status: change TEMPORARY to CURRENT.
Remove problem code S.
Update (F8) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press L or scroll down to License Certificate: enter the password; verify the license information and animal description match the tag window; print one copy; staple the tag number to the folded license certificate and mail.

sections.heading: Incomplete License: Missing Proof of Microchip (Problem Code C)
steps: This applies to unincorporated areas and contract cities that adopted Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6.
Dogs 10 years or older are exempt for the City of Riverside.
Tag No: leave blank and Chameleon will automatically generate a U number.
Tag type: enter the applicable tag type based on the tag jurisdiction for an unaltered license. For example, LIC 1.
Enter the tag subtype, jurisdiction, and status per the common field entries above.
Problem code: enter C to generate an instruction in the Pending License Notification that proof of microchip is required.
Term: the term of the license will always remain 12 months.
Tag Exp: enter the license expiration date. The expiration date is from the last renewal date or 12 months from the tag date.
Vacc Date and rabies term: enter the vaccination date; enter 12 for a first vaccination or 36 for a booster.
Enter the rabies vaccination information (Vaccine, Cert No, Lot No, Lot Exp, Vet ID) as described in the Chameleon Process section.
Price: enter the amount paid or received.
Store (F9) the record.

Press F3 to access reports and press T or scroll down to Temporary License Notice. Review the notice, create an Animal ID memo using the Weblicensing C or Temporary C template, verify the text matches what is missing or needed, verify the due date in the memo matches the temporary notice, and print and mail the notice.

Create a receipt: pop-up (F4) the receipt window, verify the Person ID and Animal ID, verify the item code, license number, Animal ID, department number, fee type, and price are displayed, click the green ADD button, enter the total amount of monies received, and store (F9). Print one page only for the deposit.

steps.step: To clear problem code C:
sub: Once proof of the microchip is received, using the same PID and AID, store a microchip record: Tag no: enter the microchip number; Tag type: enter MCHIP-PUB; Subtype: PUA (Public-AVID), PUI (Public-ISO chip), PUH (Public-HOMEAGAIN), or PUR (Public-RESQ chip); Status: CURRENT; Tag date: F5 for today's date; Term: 99, for permanent until the animal dies; Tag_exp: automatically calculated by Chameleon based on tag date and term; store (F9) the microchip record.
Double click on the temporary license record for the animal, type over the U number, and enter a license number in the tag no field.
Status: change from TEMPORARY to CURRENT.
Remove problem code C.
Update (F8) the license record.
Scan the document to the license number through Chamcam.

Press F3 to access reports and press L or scroll down to License Certificate: enter the password; verify the license information and animal description match the tag window; print one copy; staple the tag number to the folded license certificate and mail.

sections.heading: Licensing and Administrative Citations (New: No Existing License or Vaccert in the Tag/Link Window)
steps: Store (F9) a Person ID and Animal ID. Refer to DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Procedures.
Leave the tag number field blank.
Enter the applicable tag type based on jurisdiction, gender of dog, etc. Refer to Appendix A.
Enter the applicable tag subtype.
Verify the tag jurisdiction applies to the tag type used.
Enter TEMPORARY in the tag status field.
Refer to Appendix B for the correct codes to enter in the problem code field.
Enter today's date in the Tag Date field.
Enter 12 months as the term of the license.
Leave the tag_exp field blank. It is normally calculated automatically by Chameleon unless the rabies vaccination will not cover the entire licensing period.
Source of information: a rabies certificate (VACCERT) is received from a vet, web licensing, adoptions, RTOs, or a shot clinic, or a CITATION is issued by a license inspector.

The owner has 30 days from when the VACCERT was stored (tag date) or when the LIC was due (tag exp) to purchase or renew without penalty. If the owner pays and is still missing license requirements, the due date does not change (30 days from tag date for Vaccert/New or 30 days from tag exp).
30 days after, the first delinquent notice is sent by email or mail and includes the license penalty.
30 days after that, a final delinquent notice is sent by email or mail that includes the license penalty and notice of pending citation penalties.

30 days after that, an administrative citation is mailed to the pet owner that includes licensing fees, penalties, violation fees, and the citation fee. If the offense status is not changed to HOLD, VOID, or CLEARED within 30 days, the violation status is changed to collection.

90 days after, if compliance is not met, administrative citation 2 is mailed to the pet owner. If the offense status is not changed to HOLD, VOID, or CLEARED within 30 days, the violation status is changed to collection.

120 days after, if the owner does not make any attempt to comply, administrative citation 3 is mailed to the pet owner. If the offense status is not changed to HOLD, VOID, or CLEARED within 30 days, the violation status is changed to collection.

includes licensing fees, penalties, violation fees, and the citation fee. if the offense status is not changed to hold, void, or cleared within 30 days, the violation status is changed to collection. 90 days after, if compliance is not met, administrative citation 2 is mailed to the pet owner. if the offense status is not changed to hold, void, or cleared within 30 days, the violation status is changed to collection. 120 days after, if the owner does not make any attempt to comply, administrative citation 3 is mailed to the pet owner. if the offense status is not changed to hold, void, or cleared within 30 days, the violation status is changed to collection. title 17 california code of regulations, section 2606.4 riverside county ordinance, title 6 policy 000-01, general chameleon information california public health compendium of approved rabies vaccines

documents.title: Mandatory Spay and Neuter Compliance Process
documents.purpose: To describe the compliance process for impounded unneutered or non-spayed dogs or cats reclaimed by their owners under Riverside County Code Title 6, Section 6.08.120, Mandatory Spaying and Neutering.
documents.authority: Hearings are conducted by the DAS Director's designee. Deferment appeals go to the Director or Chief of Veterinary Services.
documents.scope: Applies to impounded unaltered dogs and cats reclaimed by their owners or custodians, and to staff processing those redemptions, licenses, and appeals.
documents.supersedes: Mandatory S/N Compliance Process flowchart, June 23, 2014
references: Riverside County Code Title 6, Section 6.08.120 (Mandatory Spaying and Neutering)

sections.heading: Reclaim Paths
steps: Determine the reclaim path when an owner redeems an impounded unaltered dog or cat under Section 6.08.120.

steps.step: Reclaim with intent to comply: the animal will be spayed or neutered at DAS.
sub: Have the owner sign the Intent to Comply.

steps.step: Reclaim with proof from the owner's private veterinarian: the owner will send proof of sterility from their own veterinarian.
sub: Allow ten (10) days to submit a proof of sterility certificate or documentation that the animal is incapable of breeding.

steps: Reclaim without intent to comply: issue an administrative citation for failure to license and violation of Section 6.08.120 (dog or cat), and issue a Notice of Intent to Revoke License for an unaltered dog, sent by mail.

sections.heading: Deferment Appeal
steps: The owner may make an informal appeal to the Director or Chief of Veterinary Services for deferment of spay or neuter.
If approved, document the approval in a Chameleon memo and have the owner sign the Intent to Comply.

sections.heading: Hearing on Denial or Revocation
steps: The owner may request a hearing to appeal the denial or revocation.
The request must be made in writing within ten (10) days after the notice is mailed. Failure to submit in a timely manner is deemed a waiver of appeal.
The hearing shall be conducted by the DAS Director's designee.
The hearing officer mails a written notice of the date, time, and place of the hearing.
The hearing will be informal and rules of evidence will not be strictly observed.
The hearing officer issues a written decision to the owner or custodian within ten (10) days after the hearing.
The decision of the hearing officer shall be the final administrative decision.

steps.step: If the license is denied:
sub: The owner may re-apply with proof of compliance.
The Department will refund one-half of the license fee.
The owner pays the full fee upon re-application.

steps.step: If the license is revoked:
sub: The owner may re-apply with proof of requirements after 30 days.
No part of the license fee is refundable.
The owner pays the full fee upon re-application.

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2014 version; flowchart converted to narrative steps; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Official County Receipt Tracking Policy
documents.purpose: To establish tracking procedures for Official County Receipt Books used by Department of Animal Services employees.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This policy is applicable to the Fiscal Division. For Fiscal use only.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-27, Official County Receipt Tracking Policy, effective January 19, 2017 (revised from September 29, 2009); absorbs duplicate Word draft of the same 2017 version
references: DAS Policy 000-42, Records and Information Management
SOP: How to Input OCR Receipts in Chameleon

definitions.def: Official County Receipt. Pre-numbered receipt books issued to individual employees and tracked by the Fiscal Division.

sections.heading: OCR Books
steps: The DAS Fiscal Designee distributes OCR books to all applicable personnel. Store all unused OCR books in the DAS Fiscal Unit and check them out to each employee using the OCR Custody Form.
Issue each employee one OCR book plus one waiting to be used. Store the unused book in the Fiscal Unit.
Use OCR books within four (4) months, or turn them in for a new book before then.
Do not share OCR books or leave them unattended; they are assigned to the individual employee. Failure to comply with this process will result in disciplinary action.
Store OCR books in an employee locker or secured cabinet every night.
The Fiscal Division is responsible for tracking and reconciling OCR books. Tracking and reconciling occur through the Chameleon system.

sections.heading: Issuing the OCR Book

steps.step: Prior to issuing the OCR book to the employee, the Fiscal Designee verifies that all receipts, in numeric order, are included in the book being checked out.
sub: If a receipt is missing, the Fiscal Designee notes the OCR number on the OCR Custody Form and the employee initials to confirm agreement.

steps: Complete the OCR Custody Form and issue the OCR book to the employee.

sections.heading: Entering the OCR Book in Chameleon
steps: Log in to the Chameleon system and access the Windows menu on the toolbar.
Select Certification Book and tab to Group ID.
Pop up (F4) and select the person to whom you are issuing the OCR book.
OCR books are assigned in Chameleon by Group ID (the employee name). All books that are currently checked out show as Open in the Book Status line.

Enter the Start Sequence, which is the starting number of the receipt book located on the front cover of the book or on the first receipt. End Sequence is the last number of the book. Issue Sequence is 1.
Enter the current date or press F5 to automatically enter the date.
Store (F9) the record.

sections.heading: Completed OCR Books
steps: Once an employee has used all of the receipts in their OCR book or has had the book for four (4) months, they return the book to the Fiscal Designee for reconciliation.
The Fiscal Designee looks up the employee's issued book in Chameleon and presses F5 to enter the return date in the Complete field.
Type Closed in the Book Status field.

steps.step: Type in the end sequence number of the last completed receipt.
sub: Every receipt issued with that particular book should be accounted for or voided out. See the SOP on How to Input OCR Receipts in Chameleon for more information on voiding out receipts.

steps: Update (F8) the record.
If needed, issue another OCR book to the employee.

sections.heading: Reconciling the OCR Book

steps.step: The Fiscal Designee reconciles the OCR book with the OCR Custody Form and the Chameleon OCR Reconciliation Report:
sub: Select Reports from the toolbar.
Run a report.
Choose OCR Reconciliation Reports.
Select OK.
Fill in the OCR starting and ending sequence numbers and the employee name.
Select OK. The report will appear.

steps: If all three (3) reports balance, the Fiscal Designee and employee sign on the appropriate line of the OCR Custody Form to verify that all receipts were received and the book is completed.
The Fiscal Designee files the completed OCR book, the OCR Custody Form, and the Chameleon OCR Reconciliation Report.
Retain these records for the fiscal year plus seven (FY+7) years, per DAS Policy 000-42, Records and Information Management.
If all three (3) reports do not balance, the Fiscal Designee reviews all documentation pertaining to the OCR book, from issuance to turn-in, to locate the discrepancy.

Appendix B: OCR Custody Form (attached separately)
Appendix C: Chameleon OCR Reconciliation Report sample (attached separately)

assigned in chameleon by group id (the employee name). all books that are currently checked out show as open in the book status line. enter the start sequence, which is the starting number of the receipt book located on the front cover of the book or on the first receipt. end sequence is the last number of the book. issue sequence is 1. enter the current date or press f5 to automatically enter the date. store (f9) the record. completed ocr books once an employee has used all of the receipts in their ocr book or has had the book for four (4) months, they return the book to the fiscal designee for reconciliation. the fiscal designee looks up the employee's issued book in chameleon and presses f5 to enter the return date in the complete field. type closed in the book status field. {"step": "type in the end sequence number of the last completed receipt.", "sub": ["every receipt issued with that particular book should be accounted for or voided out. see the sop on how to input ocr receipts in chameleon for more information on voiding out receipts."]} update (f8) the record. if needed, issue another ocr book to the employee. reconciling the ocr book {"step": "the fiscal designee reconciles the ocr book with the ocr custody form and the chameleon ocr reconciliation report:", "sub": ["select reports from the toolbar.", "run a report.", "choose ocr reconciliation reports.", "select ok.", "fill in the ocr starting and ending sequence numbers and the employee name.", "select ok. the report will appear."]} if all three (3) reports balance, the fiscal designee and employee sign on the appropriate line of the ocr custody form to verify that all receipts were received and the book is completed. the fiscal designee files the completed ocr book, the ocr custody form, and the chameleon ocr reconciliation report. retain these records for the fiscal year plus seven (fy+7) years, per das policy 000-42, records and information management. if all three (3) reports do not balance, the fiscal designee reviews all documentation pertaining to the ocr book, from issuance to turn-in, to locate the discrepancy. das policy 000-42, records and information management sop: how to input ocr receipts in chameleon

documents.title: Owner Notification Procedure
documents.purpose: To comply with Riverside County Ordinance Title 6, Section 6.08.110(D), Impounded Dogs, Cats and Service Fees, for unincorporated areas of Riverside County, and with City of Riverside Animal Protection Ordinance Title 8, Section 8.14.020, Notification of Owner and Redemption Fees, for City of Riverside residents, and to establish consistency in notifying pet owners of their animal's impoundment at the Department of Animal Services (DAS).
documents.authority: Not stated in source document; to be confirmed in review.
documents.scope: Designated staff responsible for notifying owners of impounded animals, including customer service, veterinary staff, animal care technicians, and field services staff.
documents.supersedes: Owner Notification Procedure SOP, effective August 27, 2020; prior revisions dated September 30, 2019, September 17, 2019, August 27, 2015, October 21, 2014, and November 17, 2011
references: Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6, Section 6.08.110(D), Impounded Dogs, Cats and Service Fees
City of Riverside Animal Protection Ordinance, Title 8, Section 8.14.020, Notification of Owner, Redemption Fees
Riverside County Fee Ordinance 630.13
California Food and Agricultural Code 31107, Impounded Dog: Notice to Owner Before Disposal
California Food and Agricultural Code 31108(c) and 31752(c), Impound Holding Period for Stray Dogs or Cats
California Food and Agricultural Code 31252, County Charge
California Food and Agricultural Code 31253, Payment by Owner
California Food and Agricultural Code 31254, Nonpayment: Abandonment
DAS Administrative Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department
DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information

sections.heading: Points to Remember
steps: Notify the owner within 24 hours from the impound date.
Mail the letter and fee sheet by first class postage.
Designated staff must have all letters at the mailbox prior to the USPS scheduled pickup.
Set the Chameleon kennel window kennel status to KNOWN WAIT.

Set the due out date field of the kennel window to ten (10) days from the mailing date of the Notice of Impoundment, or ten (10) days from the impound date if the owner personally comes to the shelter and confirms ownership of the impounded animal. Place a Y on the right side of the screen with the description Hold Notify.
Keep a Stray Wait status for animals wearing a tag with only a name (for example, Goofy), because the information on the tag does not provide owner information.

If a microchip is found during an intake examination by veterinary staff, veterinary staff or animal care technicians must enter a note in Chameleon with the subtype Owner Info to ensure the animal is registered in the Owner Known List, and change the kennel status to KNOWN WAIT. Otherwise, the report is not able to retrieve the information from the medical screen.
Prior to printing the Notice of Impoundment, make sure the due out date field displays the last day and final hour the owner is allowed to reclaim their pet.
After the letter is printed, change the due out date to display the day and time when the impounded animal is available to the public, then scan the letter in Chameleon.
The person notifying pet owners is responsible for creating a microchip, tag, or rabies tag record linking the owner (PID) and animal (AID) in the tag/link window.
If an animal has been impounded on more than one occasion, the memo should reflect the impound number to alert staff (for example, second impound, third impound).
If multiple animal IDs have been created, send an email to management to have all animal IDs merged to ensure proper record keeping.
If a Notice of Impoundment is returned in the mail, create a memo noting the date it was returned and a brief explanation of why it was returned.
Once the owner identifies and confirms ownership of an impounded animal, the owner has been notified and must reclaim the pet the same day.

steps.step: Animals wearing County or contract city license tags:
sub: Verify that the animal description on the kennel window matches the licensing information.
Verify that the tag status is current.
If the information does not match, verify the tag number on the kennel window memo against the tag the animal is wearing.

Note on the Animal ID memo: the date the memo was created, that the animal description does not match the licensing information, the owner information including name, address, and phone number, and the initials of the staff member who verified the information.
Update the Animal ID memo with memo type Owner Info.

steps.step: Animal ID memo with address only:
sub: Using the tag/link window, verify owner information based on the licensing address.
If no information is found, search an online white pages directory for possible owner information.
Note in the Animal ID memo: the owner information including name, address, and phone number, the date the memo was created, and the initials of the person who located the owner information.
Update the Animal ID memo with memo type Owner Info.

steps.step: Owner identification tag with telephone number:
sub: Using the tag/link window or an online white pages directory, research the telephone number to obtain the owner information.

Update the Animal ID memo with: the date the memo was created, the owner information including name, address, and phone number, verification that the memo type is Owner Info, and the initials of the staff member who entered the information.

steps.step: Microchip:
sub: Determine what kind of microchip it is (for example, AVID, HomeAgain).
Contact the microchip company, whether information exists in Chameleon or not, to ensure that owner information is up to date.
Using the tag/link window, type the microchip number in the tag number field to locate the record.
Update Chameleon if the information provided by the microchip company is more current than Chameleon. Document in Chameleon when the information was last updated by the microchip company.

Update the Animal ID memo with: the date the memo was created, the owner information including name, address, and phone number, verification that the memo type is Owner Info, and the initials of the staff member who entered the information.

steps.step: Out of jurisdiction or non-contract city license:
sub: Contact the applicable agency or veterinarian by telephone and obtain the owner and animal information, including rabies expiration date, name of animal, and gender.

If the applicable agency or veterinarian is not available, update the Animal ID memo with: the date the memo was created, the name of the agency or veterinarian being contacted, how the agency or veterinarian is being contacted, a note that a message was left if applicable, and the initials of the person who made the contact.

When information is obtained, update the Animal ID memo with: the date the memo was created, the owner information including name, address, and phone number, the source of the information (name of person and agency or veterinary office), a description of the animal, verification that the memo type is Owner Info, and the initials of the staff member who entered the information.
Pop up (F4) the Animal ID and update the information on the impounded animal.

sections.heading: Notification to Owner by Telephone
steps: Designated staff shall notify owners by telephone of their animal's impoundment at the shelter.

Make three (3) call attempts on a daily basis in sequence until contact is made with the legal owner or custodian of the animal, or the animal has been held the legally required number of days (refer to Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department). Phone ringing and busy signals are not considered attempts.
Prior to contacting the owner, print and review the Owner Known List report.
Verify the due out date and time in the kennel window (last day and time to redeem).

steps.step: Update the kennel window with the following:
sub: Type Y under the Hold field and update the window.
Once a Hold icon appears, click on the icon to view the memo screen.
Verify that the memo ID is the same as the Animal ID.

Set the memo type to O Notified if ownership is verified, or Owner Info if the owner has not been notified personally. There should not be an Owner Info and an O Notified memo type within the same impound period.

Type the memo text to include: date notified; time notified; how the owner was notified (for example, MM for message machine, LM for left message with someone other than the owner, texted, emailed, or NA for no answer); last day of redemption; schedule of fees; and the initials of the person notifying.
If it is a quarantine impound, type in the quarantine release date and the last day to redeem.
Store or update the Animal ID memo.

steps: In the kennel window screen, change the due out date and time to the available date and time (refer to Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department).

If the third or final day of attempting contact by telephone is not successful, enter COMPLETED in the memo subtype field. This removes the record from the Owner Known List. This is only applicable with the memo type of Owner Info.

sections.heading: Notification to Owner by Email or Text
steps: Obtain the customer's email or cell phone number and cell phone carrier. Enter the cell phone carrier in the field next to the cell phone number in the person window.
Designated staff shall print the Notice of Impoundment in PDF format based on the displayed kennel window record and save it to a designated shared folder by animal ID.
For kennel records with an Owner Info memo type, follow each computer prompt to generate the letter.
Send a text via MMS email with attachment, or SMS without attachment. Refer to Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information, person window section, for the texting format.

The text will contain the subject line Notice of Impoundment with the Animal ID, and a body notifying the recipient that their pet is impounded, at which shelter and address, the intake date, the last day and time to claim the pet, that current personal identification (driver's license or other government issued ID) is required for pickup, a request to reply to the message, and the statement that failure to redeem the pet will not release the debt owed to the County or City of Riverside.
Attach the letter to the email, to the text if applicable, and to the impound number through ChamCam.
Attach the fee schedule, when applicable, to the email and text message.

steps.step: Update the kennel window with the following:
sub: Type Y under the Hold field and update the window.
Once a Hold icon appears, click on the icon to view the memo screen.
Verify that the memo ID is the same as the Animal ID.
Set the memo type to O Notified if ownership is verified, or Owner Info if the owner has not been notified personally.

Type the memo text to include: date notified, time notified, how the owner was notified, what letter was printed, the fee schedule attachment, the last day and time of redemption, and the initials of the person notifying.
Store or update the Animal ID memo.

sections.heading: Notification to Owner by Mail
steps: Designated staff shall print the Notice of Impoundment based on the displayed kennel window record.
Scan and attach the letter to the impound number through ChamCam.
For kennel records with an Owner Info memo type, follow each computer prompt to generate the letter.

steps.step: Update the kennel window with the following:
sub: Type Y under the Hold field and update the window.
Once a Hold icon appears, click on the icon to view the memo screen.
Verify that the memo ID is the same as the Animal ID.
Set the memo type to O Notified if ownership is verified, or Owner Info if the owner has not been notified personally.

Type the memo text to include: date notified, time notified, how the owner was notified, what letter was printed, the fee schedule attachment, the last day and time of redemption, and the initials of the person notifying.
Store or update the Animal ID memo.

steps: In the kennel window screen, change the due out date and time to the available date and time (refer to Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department).
Attach the impound fee sheet to the letter.
Mail the letter.

sections.heading: Notification at Counter
steps: Designated staff shall print the Notice of Impoundment based on the displayed kennel window record.
Scan and attach the letter to the impound number through ChamCam.
For kennel records with an Owner Info memo type, follow each computer prompt to generate the letter.

steps.step: Update the kennel window with the following:
sub: Type Y under the Hold field and update the window.
Once a Hold icon appears, click on the icon to view the memo screen.
Verify that the memo ID is the same as the Animal ID.
Set the memo type to O Notified. Include the return-to-owner fees in the memo, along with details of the conversation held with the owner.

Type the memo text to include: date notified, time notified, how the owner was notified, what letter was printed, the fee schedule attachment, the last day and time of redemption, and the initials of the person notifying.
Store or update the Animal ID memo.

steps: In the kennel window screen, change the due out date and time to the available date and time (refer to Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department).
If the owner or custodian is at the front counter and confirms ownership, the owner must reclaim the impounded animal that day. No additional days will be given.

If an owner or custodian comes in and identifies their pet but states that they are unable to reclaim the pet that day, set the due out date to reflect a two calendar day extension from when the owner came in.
Attach the impound fee sheet to the letter.
Hand the letter to the owner.

sections.heading: Notification for Confiscates
steps: Designated staff shall print the Notice of Impoundment based on the displayed kennel window record.
Scan and attach the letter to the impound number through ChamCam.
For kennel records with an Owner Info memo type, follow each computer prompt to generate the letter.

steps.step: Update the kennel window with the following:
sub: Type Y under the Hold field and update the window.
Once a Hold icon appears, click on the icon to view the memo screen.
Verify that the memo ID is the same as the Animal ID.
Set the memo type to O Notified. Include the return-to-owner fees in the memo, along with details of the conversation held with the owner.

Type the memo text to include: date notified, time notified, how the owner was notified, what letter was printed, the fee schedule attachment, the last day and time of redemption, and the initials of the person notifying.
Store or update the Animal ID memo.

steps: In the kennel window screen, change the due out date and time to the available date and time (refer to Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department).
Field staff may extend the due out date for confiscated animals for a hearing if necessary.
Attach the impound fee sheet to the letter.
Mail the letter.
Phone notification is not necessary for a confiscated animal.

sections.heading: Field Services Owner Notification
steps: On the third attempt of telephone notification, generate a field services activity for an animal control officer visit to the address. Refer to the Activity Window policy.
Set the activity type to ASSIST.
Set the activity subtype to ONOTIFY.
Enter the staff member initiating the activity as the Caller ID.
Enter the address of the dog owner as the activity address.
Enter the first main street in the cross street low field.
Enter the second main street in the cross street high field.
In the comment field, note to contact the owner because previous attempts have been exhausted.
Enter the applicable GEO, jurisdiction, and map page information.
Enter the Animal ID that matches the kennel Animal ID.
Enter the Owner ID (person ID) that matches the person PID from the kennel window.
Press F5 in the call field.
Press F5 in the new field.
Press F9 to store.

appendices: Appendix A: Notice of Impoundment (attached separately)
Appendix B: Impound Fee Sheet (attached separately)
Appendix C: Owner Known List (attached separately)
Appendix D: Fee quotation memo template
Appendix E: Employee acknowledgment form (signature page; retained in employee's personnel file)
