documents.title: Completion and Routing of the Rabies Control Investigation Report
documents.purpose: To establish procedures for the initiation, completion, and routing of the Rabies Control Investigation Report (Animal Bite/Scratch Report) when a human has been bitten or scratched by a wild or domestic mammal (excluding rabbits and rodents), and when a domestic animal has been exposed to a wild animal.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. Case closure, veterinary review, and quarantine location exceptions are authorized by the Chief Veterinarian (Chief of Veterinary Services), Clinic Veterinarian, or Rabies Control Desk as specified below.
documents.scope: All DAS personnel who have knowledge of an animal biting or scratching a person, and all staff responsible for placing domestic animals under quarantine that have been bitten or scratched by a wild animal.
documents.supersedes: Policy 100-04, Completion and routing of the Rabies Control Investigation Report for Animal vs. Wildlife, effective September 16, 2014 (revised from October 8, 2009 and May 28, 2007); absorbs Policy 100-05, Completion and Routing of the Rabies Control Investigation Report for a Human vs. Animal, effective November 16, 2016 (revised from March 14, 2016; December 2, 2015; December 16, 2014; October 8, 2009; May 28, 2007)
references: California Code of Regulations, 2606 (c)
Food and Agriculture Code 2606 (b) 2
DAS P&P 313-01 (approved rabies vaccinations)
DAS P&P 700.12 (estimating animal age)

definitions.def: The multi-copy Animal Bite/Scratch Report form designed to record all applicable information needed by Public Health authorities to evaluate human exposures from animal bites or scratches, determine the need for rabies prophylactic treatment, and report exposures of pet animals to known or suspected rabid animals.
Fluorescent rabies antibody laboratory examination of a specimen submitted for rabies testing.
Unable to Quarantine; disposition used when the biting animal or animal owner could not be found, or the quarantine could not be completed.
In domestic vs. wild animal reports, the domestic animal is the victim and the wild animal is the biting animal.

steps.step: Rabies is a fatal disease, communicable to both animals and humans. Take preventive measures when humans or domestic animals come in contact with a rabies suspect wild animal by bites, scratches, or other types of exposure. Such measures include, but are not limited to:
sub: Rabies vaccination of all dogs four months of age or older.
Confinement of all dogs less than four months of age.
Establishment of ownership and rabies vaccination status of all dogs by licensing.
Elimination of stray animals from the environment.
Thorough investigation of all reported bites and scratches inflicted by warm-blooded mammals, and proper isolation and observation of these warm-blooded mammals.
Thorough investigation of contacts between domestic pets and wild mammals, proper isolation and observation of these domestic pets, and laboratory examination of the wild animals.
Public education regarding rabies prevention.

sections.heading: Human vs. Animal: Submission of Report
steps: Complete the form and submit it to: Riverside County Department of Animal Services, 6851 Van Buren Blvd, Jurupa Valley CA 92509.
Agencies other than DAS should submit the second copy on the day the investigation begins and the working copy when the investigation is completed.

In cases where the behavior of the animal may indicate rabies, or in which the victim received a face, head, or neck exposure by a wild, stray, or rabies suspect animal, immediately telephone a report of the incident to DAS at (951) 358-7387. Also fax the completed report to DAS at (951) 358-7738, or deliver it in person.

sections.heading: Human vs. Animal: Distribution of Copies

steps.step: White (Original): This is the official form and should successively be in the possession of each person taking action related to the incident.
sub: At the conclusion of an investigation, file the original at DAS.

ALL signatures (report writer, investigators, owner, person placing animal in quarantine, person releasing from quarantine, veterinarian reviewing case, physician or veterinarian taking other action or closing the case, and the clerks making the necessary notification) MUST be on this copy.

steps.step: Yellow (Second Copy): This is the notification copy.
sub: If agencies other than DAS are retaining the white copy to investigate the case, observe, isolate, or release the animal from quarantine, mail this yellow copy to DAS on the day the bite report is initiated. This notifies the Chief Veterinarian that a possible exposure has occurred and that action is being taken.
Fill out the original as completely as possible; do not retain the yellow form while an effort is made to complete the investigation.

steps.step: Goldenrod: This is the FRA test copy.
sub: Submit the report with the specimen to the Lab.
The lab scans this report and returns a signed copy with the FRA test results.

steps: Pink: This is the owner's Notice to Quarantine form. The owner agrees to adhere to the quarantine imposed on their pet.

steps.step: White (Supplemental): Reports and records actions and results during the course of the investigation.
sub: The supplemental is to remain, and be filed, with the original copy of the bite report.
Date and initial or sign (first initial, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31 and the supplemental.

sections.heading: General Instructions for Completing the Report
steps: Treat the report as a legal document that should be easily read and understood. Brevity, completeness, accuracy, and clearness are required.
Box print using a BLACK ballpoint pen. Police/radio codes are not acceptable.
Abbreviations familiar to a layperson are acceptable. 24-hour (military) time is acceptable.
Enter all applicable information on the report. If information is not applicable, enter "N/A". If the information is unknown, leave blank.
Mark boxes with an X, not a check mark.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Victim and Incident (Lines 1a-17)
steps: 1a. IMP #: DAS Animal ID entered by personnel (if applicable).
1b. CASE #: Bite report number assigned to the case (created by Rabies Control).
1c. ACT #: DAS Activity number assigned to the case (if applicable).
2a. Name: The name of the victim, last name first, followed by first name and middle initial (if applicable). If the victim is unknown, leave blank and write details in section 31.
2b. Victim (Last Name): The victim's last name.
3. Sex: Mark the appropriate box.
4. Age: The victim's age. If under one year, enter the number of months followed by "mos." If the victim is a minor, complete line 7 (Name of Parent/Guardian) and line 8 (address if different).
5. Telephone: Home and work telephone numbers of the victim.
6. Address: Residence number, direction (N, S, E, W), prefix (Via, Calle, etc.), street name, apartment or space number, city, state, and zip code.
7. Parent/Guardian (if victim is under 18 years of age): Last name, first name, and middle initial (if applicable).
8. Address of Parent or Guardian: Use only if different from the victim's address.
9. Source of Information: Required. Mark the appropriate box. If "other," indicate the source and telephone number.
10. Report Received By: First initial, last name, and badge number (if applicable) of the person initiating the report, plus the date and time the report was received. Required.
11. Place of Attack: The exact location of the bite/scratch. Corresponding numbers from the report (for example, in the street in front of #33) may be used.
12a. Attack Date/Time: The numerical date of the bite/scratch and the time of day it occurred.
12b. Date of Bite: The date of the bite/scratch.

13. Circumstances: Exact information regarding the circumstances surrounding the bite/scratch. Mark the appropriate box for "Was the bite through material?" Report only factual knowledge, not what an animal was thinking or why an animal bit or scratched.

steps.step: 14. Wound Location:
sub: The exact part of the body bitten/scratched, together with right or left as appropriate (for example, left calf, right hand, right thumb).
Description of the wound as a scratch, puncture, or laceration (for example, multiple punctures, 3 inch laceration).
If the bite/scratch did not cause a break in the skin, enter "no skin break". Indicate whether the wound resulted from the animal's teeth or claws if known.

steps.step: 15. Skin Break:
sub: Mark the box and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable), if there is a bite/scratch that caused a break in the skin.
If no skin break, write "No" and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable).
Skin breaks reported by a physician or hospital do not need to be verified.

steps: 16. Wound Treatment: The date the victim received treatment, including the details of the treatment. If no treatment was given, advise the victim (parent/guardian) to consult a doctor regarding wound care.
17. Treated By: Mark the appropriate box. If a physician rendered treatment, enter the physician's name (or secondarily the hospital/facility) and the telephone number.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Veterinary, Laboratory, and Closure (Lines 19-30 and Non-Numbered Items)
steps: 19. Anti-Rabies Treatment: For use by the Riverside County Chief Veterinarian or Clinic Veterinarian only. Mark the appropriate box and enter "recommended".
20. Veterinary Review: For use by the Riverside County Chief Veterinarian or Clinic Veterinarian only. Enter responses on applicable lines.
21-26. Laboratory Exam (FRA Test): For laboratory use only. Enter responses on applicable lines.

steps.step: 27. Case Closed: For use by the Rabies Control Desk. The Chief Veterinarian or designee will review the report and will mark the appropriate boxes, date, and sign the report in the following cases:
sub: When a stray biting animal was never located.
When laboratory exam results are positive for rabies.
When the victim or biting animal is located outside of Riverside County.
When there are unusual circumstances in the report and the Chief Veterinarian needs to review before closure.

steps: 28. Notification: For use by the Rabies Desk Clerk. Mark the appropriate box, date, and sign.

29. QV Visit Date: Quarantine visits will be conducted on all animals that do not have a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite: five (5) days for dogs and cats and seven (7) days for all other animals.

30. QR Date: The biting animal's quarantine release date. A telephone release may be allowed for any animal that had a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite or any animal quarantined at a veterinary hospital. All other releases will be a field release, indicated by "FR" placed in the box by the correcting sergeant or Rabies Control Desk.
Map Ref: The Riverside County Thomas Guide Street Guide and Directory reference for both victim and owner (if applicable).

steps.step: QT Fee (Quarantine Fee): Mark the appropriate box.
sub: Fee collected: write the receipt number in the space provided.
Fee not collected: if not applicable, mark "NO". If the fee is to be billed, write "billed" in the receipt number section.

steps: Supplemental: Mark this box when the supplemental (attached to the bite report) is initiated. The person initiating the supplemental will fill out the victim and owner information at the top of the page along with boxes 2b, 12b, and 32b (Victim, Date of Bite, and Owner) along the side of the page.

steps.step: 31. Miscellaneous:
sub: Use the spaces in line 31 to record pertinent information about the investigation.
Line 31 may also be used to complete any other line on the report when additional space is needed.
Do not use this space for extraneous information such as "no one home"; write that type of information on the supplemental Investigation Report.
Date and initial or sign (first name, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Biting Animal (Lines 32a-45 and Signature)

steps.step: 32a. Owner (Custodian):
sub: Line out the non-applicable word.
Enter the name of the owner or custodian (last, first, middle).
If the name previously entered is different from the actual owner of the animal, enter the owner's name, address, and phone number on line 31.
If the animal is a stray, enter the word "stray".
Enter both home and work (if applicable) telephone numbers.

steps: 32b. Owner: Enter the owner's last name. If the animal is a stray, leave blank; the Rabies Control Desk will enter "STRAY" before filing the report.
33. Address: Enter the address where the animal is to be quarantined. If the address previously entered is different from the animal owner's actual address, enter the owner's address on line 31.

34. Animal Type: Mark the appropriate box (if other, enter the type of animal). Enter the breed, the animal's name, and the sex (write an "N" above the appropriate box if the animal is verified as having been altered). Enter the age according to P&P 700.12; if unknown, enter an estimated age. If under one year, enter the number of months or weeks followed by "mos." or "wks."

35. Description: Enter the color(s) of the biting animal. If the animal is a stray, enter all other distinguishing characteristics (long hair, short hair, large, small, pointed ears) that could be used to identify the animal.

36. License: Enter the current license number and the license jurisdiction (if Riverside County, mark the box) and the license expiration date. If the license is expired, or if the dog should be licensed and is not, issue a citation and enter the citation number in the space provided.

37. Behavior: A live animal must be observed before it can be quarantined. Upon quarantine, mark the appropriate box, "normal" or "abnormal". If the animal's behavior is abnormal, enter the specifics in section 31. If the animal is deceased or stray, mark the box "unknown".

38. Travels: Ascertain whether the animal has been outside Riverside County in the past 6 months and outside the USA in the past year; mark the appropriate boxes. If yes, indicate when and where. If a detailed explanation is needed, enter "supp" and make notations on the supplemental.

39. Vaccination: Mark the appropriate box. Enter the kind of rabies vaccine given, the date given, and the expiration date, if available. For Given By, enter the name of the veterinarian or veterinary clinic that vaccinated the animal, if available, or indicate "per comp." if the information was obtained by a computer records check through dispatch.

steps.step: 40. Quarantine:
sub: Mark the appropriate box describing the place of quarantine. "#33" refers to the address listed on line 33.

"Kennel #" is used for animals quarantined at the Riverside County Animal Shelter. A kennel number is required for live animals; for deceased animals write "R Morgue" and place the animal in the FRA Test section.
"Impound Tag" is the other ID assigned to the impounded animal.
"Microchip #" is used to enter the microchip number when the impounded animal is scanned.
The "other" box is used when the animal is quarantined at any other location; list the exact address in line 31.
If the animal is quarantined at the victim's residence, write "#6" or the actual address in the space provided.

Quarantine imposed (required): enter the date quarantine is imposed and the first initial, last name, and badge number of the officer imposing quarantine. Riverside County officers mark the agency box; city agencies mark the box labeled "City of" and write in the city name.

steps: 41. Observed OK: Enter the date, initials, and badge number (if applicable) when the animal is observed healthy. Use this line for quarantine visits (including daily shelter visits by vet staff), stray bite patrols, and when an animal is observed OK during the quarantine period and at the time of quarantine release.

42. Not Observed: Enter the date and the initials and badge number when the animal is not observed. Use this line for stray bite patrols and when an animal is not observed before quarantine is imposed. Note: an animal must be observed before quarantine can be imposed.

43. Release: Mark the appropriate box (if applicable) describing how the animal was released, "by telephone" or "upon inspection". Enter the date the animal is released from quarantine and the first initial, last name, and badge number of the person releasing the animal. Riverside County officers mark the agency box; city agencies mark "City of" and write in the city name.
44. Cause of Death: Enter (if applicable) the date the death of the biting animal occurred and mark the appropriate box or boxes describing the cause.

45. Veterinarian/Symptoms: Mark (if applicable) the appropriate box indicating whether a veterinarian observed the animal prior to the animal's death, and the box relating to symptoms as obtained by the observing veterinarian.

Agreement for Quarantine Compliance and Signature: Enter the date quarantine is imposed and a driver's license or ID number of the animal owner or custodian (indicate the state of issuance if other than California). Obtain the signature of the animal owner, and of the custodian (if applicable), who will be responsible for the animal during the quarantine.

sections.heading: Veterinarian or Home Quarantines
steps: With the approval of the Chief of Veterinary Services, Clinic Veterinarian, or Rabies Control Desk, any severely sick, injured, or elderly animal requiring medical treatment may be quarantined at an outside private veterinarian or at the owner's home. These quarantines may also be approved in unusual or abnormal situations by the same approvers.

Indicate any quarantine so approved in section 31 or on the supplemental of the Bite-Scratch Report, including who is approving the quarantine and the circumstances necessitating quarantine somewhere other than DAS.

All quarantines at an outside private veterinarian shall be imposed and released by an Animal Control Officer. When imposing quarantine, the officer will direct the veterinarian to sign the bite report as the custodian. No fee will be collected for these quarantines. All quarantines at an outside private veterinarian may be released via telephone.

sections.heading: Shelter Quarantines

steps.step: In accordance with Food and Agriculture Code 2606 (b) 2, shelter quarantine will be imposed at the Department of Animal Services. The basis for shelter quarantine will be the following:
sub: When the dog bites above the shoulders.
Severity of the bite.
When the owner releases the dog for euthanasia.
A prior bite history to any part of the body by the dog.
An unprovoked bite to a child.
The dog is unvaccinated.
Any medical index of suspicion that the dog may be rabid.

steps: The quarantine period shall be 10 days from the date of the bite.
The animal may be subjected to euthanasia and the head submitted for rabies FRA.

sections.heading: Chameleon Bite Window Entry (Human vs. Animal)
steps: Once the official bite report is completed (the biting animal was released from quarantine or the report was closed and signed by the Chief Veterinarian), enter a human vs. animal bite report in Chameleon. Accurate entry ensures accuracy of the statistical report submitted to the State for Local Rabies Control Activities.
Click on Windows > Activity > Bite Investigation.

steps.step: Bite Type: select the applicable type:
sub: DOG10: dog vs human.
CAT10: cat vs human.
BATVH: bat vs human, possible or verified exposure.
COYOTEVH: coyote vs human.
FERRETVH: ferret vs human.
FOXVH: fox vs human.
HORSEVH: horse vs human.
OPOSSUMVH: opossum vs human.
OTHERVH: other than what is listed vs human.
RACCOONVH: raccoon vs human.
SKUNKVH: skunk vs human.

steps: Bite Date and Time: enter information based on line 12a of the official bite report.
Report Date: enter information based on line 10 of the official bite report.
Activity No: based on line 1c, enter the activity number, pop up (F4) the activity window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Tag No: enter information based on line 36 of the official bite report and search for the current license of the biting animal.

steps.step: Status: pop up (F4) and select based on the current status of the report:
sub: YELLOW: second copy of report received by Rabies Desk.
FRA: completed report, waiting for FRA result.
PENDING APR: completed UTQ report, pending approval.
RTN FIELD/VET/OTHER: incomplete, returned to the appropriate area for completion.
CLOSED: completed report, case closed.

steps: Owner ID: based on line 32a, pop up (F4) the person window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Animal ID: based on line 34, pop up (F4) the animal window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Prior Bites: by searching based on the owner's address and owner's ID, find prior bite numbers and enter the number of prior bites for the animal.
Valid Vac: based on line 39 of the official bite report, enter "Y" for yes or "N" for no.

steps.step: Controlled By (only applicable for bite type DOG10 or CAT10):
sub: STRAY = no owner information.
LIC = owned, current license and vaccination.
VACC = owned, current rabies vaccine but no license.
NOVAC = owned but no rabies vaccine or license.

steps: Incident Location: refer to line 11 of the official bite report. May be a street address, cross-streets, or landmark. Pop up (F4), enter the house number and first few letters of the street name with a wildcard (%), and hit OK to search.
Jurisdiction: refer to the jurisdiction of the activity.
Geo: refer to the geocode of the activity.
Price: for home quarantine billing purposes.
Receipt No: if the officer collected a quarantine fee, enter the receipt number.
Victim ID: pop up (F4) to bring up the person window and press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window. Refer to line 2a of the official bite report.
Age: refer to line 4 of the official bite report.

steps.step: Relation:
sub: If the owner of the dog is also the victim, enter SELF.
If the victim lives in the same area as the owner, enter NEIGHBOR.
If the victim is visiting the dog owner, enter FRIEND.
If no owner, enter STRAY.

steps: Location: general location of the bite or scratch. Add specifics to the synopsis.
Severity: enter puncture, scratch, scrape, or laceration.
Circumstance: enter "See Synops" and refer to line 13 of the official bite report.
Guardian: if the victim is a minor (0-17 years old), refer to line 7 of the official bite report.
Witness: enter witness information whether the biting animal is located or not, or if victim or owner information does not exist. Refer to line 9 of the official bite report.
Treatment By: enter DOCTOR if treated by hospital or doctor, PARENT if treated by parent, or SELF if treated by self. Refer to line 17 of the official bite report.
Treatment Date: refer to line 16 of the official bite report.
Desc: refer to line 16 (details) of the official bite report.
Quarantine Begin Date: refer to line 40 of the official bite report.
By: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. Enter the Chameleon ID or first name initial and last name of the ACO, RVT, or VET.
Location: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. Enter "SHELTER" if the quarantined animal is impounded at a DAS shelter, or "HOME" if quarantine was imposed by DAS at home.
Abated By: refer to line 43 of the official bite report. Enter the Chameleon ID or first name initial and last name of the officer, vet, or RVT who released the animal from quarantine.
End Date: refer to line 43 of the official bite report. Enter the date the animal was released from quarantine.
Euth By: enter the Chameleon ID or first name initial and last name of the officer, vet, or RVT who euthanized the animal before the QTR date.
Euth Date: refer to line 44 of the official bite report. Enter the date the animal was euthanized before the quarantine release date.
Notified By (Rabies Desk only): refer to line 28 of the official bite report; enter first name initial and last name.
Notify Date: refer to line 28 of the official bite report. Enter the date the victim was notified that the biting animal did not have rabies.
Print Letter (quarantine): click on Reports, Run a Report, Quarantine Observed Notification, if the animal was quarantined.
ToLab Date: refer to the DLVR activity. Enter the date the specimen was delivered to the lab.
Exam Date: same date as ToLab Date.
Head Date: refer to the head results fax accession date. Enter the accession date as shown on the results fax.
Specimen No: refer to line 22 or the accession number on the results fax. Enter the numerical code given by the lab.
Disposition: enter HEAD TEST if the biting animal is submitted for FRA.
Head Result: refer to line 23 of the official bite report. Enter NEGATIVE, POSITIVE, or UNSAT.
Print Letter (FRA): click on Reports, Run a Report, FRA Negative Notification, if the animal was tested.

steps.step: Unable to Quarantine (UTQ) transactions:
sub: Status: if the disposition is UTQ, enter PENDING APR until reviewed. Once reviewed and signed by the Chief Veterinarian, enter CLOSED.

Disposition: enter UTQ if the biting animal was not quarantined because the biting animal or animal owner could not be found; UTQ-EUTH if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal was euthanized (does not include FRA); UTQ-DIED if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal died.
Print Letter: click on Reports, Run a Report, UTQ Notification in Jurisdiction, if the animal was not quarantined.

sections.heading: Animal vs. Wildlife: Policy and Quarantine Periods
steps: According to California Code of Regulations, 2606 (c), when a domestic animal has been bitten or a portion of its body has made contact with a wild mammal, the domestic animal must be quarantined for a period of six months, or destroyed and its brain tested for rabies.
All vaccinations and re-vaccinations stated within DAS P&P 313-01 must be California approved rabies vaccinations.
Dogs and cats rabies vaccinated less than 30 days prior to exposure will not be re-vaccinated and will be quarantined for 6 months from the date of the exposure.
Dogs and cats re-vaccinated less than 30 days before exposure will be quarantined for 30 days from the date of exposure.
Dogs and cats not rabies vaccinated will be placed under quarantine, vaccinated within 48 hours, and remain quarantined for six (6) months from the date of exposure.
Dogs under four (4) months of age and cats under three (3) months of age will be placed under quarantine for six (6) months from the date of exposure.
Initiate a Notice to Comply to have the animal vaccinated for rabies within 48 hours after a dog becomes four (4) months of age or a cat three (3) months of age.

steps.step: Exceptions:
sub: Dogs and cats that are currently rabies vaccinated at least thirty (30) days prior to exposure will be placed under quarantine, re-vaccinated within 48 hours, and remain quarantined for thirty (30) days from the date of re-vaccination.
Exposure to any rodents, rabbits, or hares will not require quarantine unless unusual circumstances exist as determined by the Chief Veterinarian.

sections.heading: Animal vs. Wildlife: Quarantine Visits and Releases
steps: Conduct a quarantine visit every month after the exposure date for a six (6) month quarantine. Conduct a field release at the end of the six (6) month quarantine.
Conduct a quarantine visit fourteen (14) days after the revaccination date for a thirty (30) day quarantine. Conduct a field release at the end of the thirty (30) day quarantine.

sections.heading: Animal vs. Wildlife: Distribution of Copies
steps: Refer to the Human vs. Animal distribution of copies section above (formerly DAS Policy 100-05).
Submit the goldenrod to clinic personnel completed with the Animal ID. Place the animal in a receiving cage for regular or after hours, or tag and place in the morgue section if deceased.
The ACO will initiate a follow-up notice giving the owner 48 hours to obtain a rabies vaccination.
Give the yellow copy to Rabies Control immediately once the bite report is initiated.

sections.heading: Animal vs. Wildlife: Completion of Report
steps: In domestic vs. wild animal reports, the domestic animal is the "victim" and the wild animal is the "biting animal".
1a. Imp #: RAC Animal ID number generated by Chameleon. 1b. Case #: bite number. 1c. Act #: activity number.
2a. Name: enter the last name of the owner, "Pet" and the type of domestic animal (for example, pet dog, pet cat), and the name of the pet if more than one.
2b. Victim (last name): enter the domestic animal owner's last name (for example, pet dog Smith).
3. Sex: the sex of the victim animal. Place an "N" or "S" in the box if the animal is altered.
4. Age: the age of the victim animal.
5. Telephone: home and work numbers for the animal owner.
6. Address: the address from where the animal is most likely to be found and quarantined.
7. Parent/Guardian: last name first, first name, middle initial of the animal owner.
8. Address: where the person listed in line 7 lives, if different from line 6.
9. Source: mark the "other" box and list the person and his or her telephone number. If the source is the dog owner, list "#7" and "same" for the telephone number.
10. Report Received By: no change from the human vs. animal instructions.
11. Place of Attack: although there is no attack, list the place where the animal was discovered. BE SPECIFIC.
12a. Attack Date/Time: line out "attack". Enter the date and time the animal was exposed to wildlife.
12b. Date of Bite: enter the date of exposure.
13. Circumstances: no change. "Through material?" will not apply in most cases; animal clothing is considered "material".
14. Wound Location: the exact part of the body that was bitten or scratched.

15. Skin Break: there is no need to verify skin break to impose quarantine. Puncture wounds on animals are sometimes difficult to locate; therefore, quarantine is necessary whenever there is intimate contact between a domestic animal and a rabies suspect wild animal. If there is a skin break, mark the box and initial the line.
16-20. Wound Treatment, Treated By, Anti-Rabies Treatment, Veterinary Review: no change.
21-28. Laboratory Exam, Case Closed, Notification: no change.
29. QV Date and 30. QR Date: list the correct dates per the Animal vs. Wildlife quarantine visits and releases section.

steps.step: 31. Miscellaneous: use the space in line 31 to record information about the victim domestic animal, including:
sub: The domestic animal's sex, breed, color, age, and name.
Rabies vaccination, type, and expiration date.
Number of the Notice to Comply issued to have the animal rabies vaccinated.
Disposition of the rabies suspect wild animal.

steps: 32a. Owner (Custodian): of the rabies suspect animal. Generally enter "stray" because there is usually no owner. If there is an owner, enter last name first, first name, then middle initial.
32b. Owner: of the rabies suspect animal. Enter only if there is a known owner; otherwise the Rabies Clerk will complete.
33. Address: of the rabies suspect animal owner. Leave blank if there is no owner.
34-35. Animal and Description: pertaining to the rabies suspect wild animal. No change.
Insert a heavy vertical line between the end of line 35 and the number 36. The remaining lines (36-46) will relate to the domestic "victim" animal.
36-38. License Number, Behavior, Travels: no change.
39. Vaccination: refers to the re-vaccination date given to comply with California Code of Regulations, 2606 (c).
40. Animal Quarantined At: no change.
43. Release: see the Animal vs. Wildlife quarantine visits and releases section.
44-45. Cause of Death, Veterinarian/Symptoms: if the domestic animal died, no change.
Signature: no change.
Non-numbered items: Map Ref: no change. QT Fee: mark the "no" box and write "N/A" in the space; no fee is collected. Supplemental: no change.

related.name: Rabies Control Investigation Report (Animal Bite/Scratch Report)

appendices: Appendix A: Pet vs. Wildlife decision flowchart (attached separately)
Appendix B: Human vs. Animal decision flowchart (attached separately)
Appendix C: Sample completed Rabies Control Investigation Reports (attached separately)
Appendix D: Chameleon notification letter templates (Quarantine Observed, FRA Negative, UTQ) (attached separately)

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template, combining Policy 100-05 (2016) as the base with the Animal vs. Wildlife content of Policy 100-04 (2014). Content unchanged from source versions; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.searchText: to establish procedures for the initiation, completion, and routing of the rabies control investigation report (animal bite/scratch report) when a human has been bitten or scratched by a wild or domestic mammal (excluding rabbits and rodents), and when a domestic animal has been exposed to a wild animal. all das personnel who have knowledge of an animal biting or scratching a person, and all staff responsible for placing domestic animals under quarantine that have been bitten or scratched by a wild animal. director of animal services. case closure, veterinary review, and quarantine location exceptions are authorized by the chief veterinarian (chief of veterinary services), clinic veterinarian, or rabies control desk as specified below. rabies control investigation report the multi-copy animal bite/scratch report form designed to record all applicable information needed by public health authorities to evaluate human exposures from animal bites or scratches, determine the need for rabies prophylactic treatment, and report exposures of pet animals to known or suspected rabid animals. fra test fluorescent rabies antibody laboratory examination of a specimen submitted for rabies testing. utq unable to quarantine; disposition used when the biting animal or animal owner could not be found, or the quarantine could not be completed. victim (animal vs. wildlife reports) in domestic vs. wild animal reports, the domestic animal is the victim and the wild animal is the biting animal. policy: rabies prevention measures {"step": "rabies is a fatal disease, communicable to both animals and humans. take preventive measures when humans or domestic animals come in contact with a rabies suspect wild animal by bites, scratches, or other types of exposure. such measures include, but are not limited to:", "sub": ["rabies vaccination of all dogs four months of age or older.", "confinement of all dogs less than four months of age.", "establishment of ownership and rabies vaccination status of all dogs by licensing.", "elimination of stray animals from the environment.", "thorough investigation of all reported bites and scratches inflicted by warm-blooded mammals, and proper isolation and observation of these warm-blooded mammals.", "thorough investigation of contacts between domestic pets and wild mammals, proper isolation and observation of these domestic pets, and laboratory examination of the wild animals.", "public education regarding rabies prevention."]} human vs. animal: submission of report complete the form and submit it to: riverside county department of animal services, 6851 van buren blvd, jurupa valley ca 92509. agencies other than das should submit the second copy on the day the investigation begins and the working copy when the investigation is completed. in cases where the behavior of the animal may indicate rabies, or in which the victim received a face, head, or neck exposure by a wild, stray, or rabies suspect animal, immediately telephone a report of the incident to das at (951) 358-7387. also fax the completed report to das at (951) 358-7738, or deliver it in person. human vs. animal: distribution of copies {"step": "white (original): this is the official form and should successively be in the possession of each person taking action related to the incident.", "sub": ["at the conclusion of an investigation, file the original at das.", "all signatures (report writer, investigators, owner, person placing animal in quarantine, person releasing from quarantine, veterinarian reviewing case, physician or veterinarian taking other action or closing the case, and the clerks making the necessary notification) must be on this copy."]} {"step": "yellow (second copy): this is the notification copy.", "sub": ["if agencies other than das are retaining the white copy to investigate the case, observe, isolate, or release the animal from quarantine, mail this yellow copy to das on the day the bite report is initiated. this notifies the chief veterinarian that a possible exposure has occurred and that action is being taken.", "fill out the original as completely as possible; do not retain the yellow form while an effort is made to complete the investigation."]} {"step": "goldenrod: this is the fra test copy.", "sub": ["submit the report with the specimen to the lab.", "the lab scans this report and returns a signed copy with the fra test results."]} pink: this is the owner's notice to quarantine form. the owner agrees to adhere to the quarantine imposed on their pet. {"step": "white (supplemental): reports and records actions and results during the course of the investigation.", "sub": ["the supplemental is to remain, and be filed, with the original copy of the bite report.", "date and initial or sign (first initial, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31 and the supplemental."]} general instructions for completing the report treat the report as a legal document that should be easily read and understood. brevity, completeness, accuracy, and clearness are required. box print using a black ballpoint pen. police/radio codes are not acceptable. abbreviations familiar to a layperson are acceptable. 24-hour (military) time is acceptable. enter all applicable information on the report. if information is not applicable, enter "n/a". if the information is unknown, leave blank. mark boxes with an x, not a check mark. specific instructions: victim and incident (lines 1a-17) 1a. imp #: das animal id entered by personnel (if applicable). 1b. case #: bite report number assigned to the case (created by rabies control). 1c. act #: das activity number assigned to the case (if applicable). 2a. name: the name of the victim, last name first, followed by first name and middle initial (if applicable). if the victim is unknown, leave blank and write details in section 31. 2b. victim (last name): the victim's last name. 3. sex: mark the appropriate box. 4. age: the victim's age. if under one year, enter the number of months followed by "mos." if the victim is a minor, complete line 7 (name of parent/guardian) and line 8 (address if different). 5. telephone: home and work telephone numbers of the victim. 6. address: residence number, direction (n, s, e, w), prefix (via, calle, etc.), street name, apartment or space number, city, state, and zip code. 7. parent/guardian (if victim is under 18 years of age): last name, first name, and middle initial (if applicable). 8. address of parent or guardian: use only if different from the victim's address. 9. source of information: required. mark the appropriate box. if "other," indicate the source and telephone number. 10. report received by: first initial, last name, and badge number (if applicable) of the person initiating the report, plus the date and time the report was received. required. 11. place of attack: the exact location of the bite/scratch. corresponding numbers from the report (for example, in the street in front of #33) may be used. 12a. attack date/time: the numerical date of the bite/scratch and the time of day it occurred. 12b. date of bite: the date of the bite/scratch. 13. circumstances: exact information regarding the circumstances surrounding the bite/scratch. mark the appropriate box for "was the bite through material?" report only factual knowledge, not what an animal was thinking or why an animal bit or scratched. {"step": "14. wound location:", "sub": ["the exact part of the body bitten/scratched, together with right or left as appropriate (for example, left calf, right hand, right thumb).", "description of the wound as a scratch, puncture, or laceration (for example, multiple punctures, 3 inch laceration).", "if the bite/scratch did not cause a break in the skin, enter \"no skin break\". indicate whether the wound resulted from the animal's teeth or claws if known."]} {"step": "15. skin break:", "sub": ["mark the box and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable), if there is a bite/scratch that caused a break in the skin.", "if no skin break, write \"no\" and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable).", "skin breaks reported by a physician or hospital do not need to be verified."]} 16. wound treatment: the date the victim received treatment, including the details of the treatment. if no treatment was given, advise the victim (parent/guardian) to consult a doctor regarding wound care. 17. treated by: mark the appropriate box. if a physician rendered treatment, enter the physician's name (or secondarily the hospital/facility) and the telephone number. specific instructions: veterinary, laboratory, and closure (lines 19-30 and non-numbered items) 19. anti-rabies treatment: for use by the riverside county chief veterinarian or clinic veterinarian only. mark the appropriate box and enter "recommended". 20. veterinary review: for use by the riverside county chief veterinarian or clinic veterinarian only. enter responses on applicable lines. 21-26. laboratory exam (fra test): for laboratory use only. enter responses on applicable lines. {"step": "27. case closed: for use by the rabies control desk. the chief veterinarian or designee will review the report and will mark the appropriate boxes, date, and sign the report in the following cases:", "sub": ["when a stray biting animal was never located.", "when laboratory exam results are positive for rabies.", "when the victim or biting animal is located outside of riverside county.", "when there are unusual circumstances in the report and the chief veterinarian needs to review before closure."]} 28. notification: for use by the rabies desk clerk. mark the appropriate box, date, and sign. 29. qv visit date: quarantine visits will be conducted on all animals that do not have a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite: five (5) days for dogs and cats and seven (7) days for all other animals. 30. qr date: the biting animal's quarantine release date. a telephone release may be allowed for any animal that had a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite or any animal quarantined at a veterinary hospital. all other releases will be a field release, indicated by "fr" placed in the box by the correcting sergeant or rabies control desk. map ref: the riverside county thomas guide street guide and directory reference for both victim and owner (if applicable). {"step": "qt fee (quarantine fee): mark the appropriate box.", "sub": ["fee collected: write the receipt number in the space provided.", "fee not collected: if not applicable, mark \"no\". if the fee is to be billed, write \"billed\" in the receipt number section."]} supplemental: mark this box when the supplemental (attached to the bite report) is initiated. the person initiating the supplemental will fill out the victim and owner information at the top of the page along with boxes 2b, 12b, and 32b (victim, date of bite, and owner) along the side of the page. {"step": "31. miscellaneous:", "sub": ["use the spaces in line 31 to record pertinent information about the investigation.", "line 31 may also be used to complete any other line on the report when additional space is needed.", "do not use this space for extraneous information such as \"no one home\"; write that type of information on the supplemental investigation report.", "date and initial or sign (first name, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31."]} specific instructions: biting animal (lines 32a-45 and signature) {"step": "32a. owner (custodian):", "sub": ["line out the non-applicable word.", "enter the name of the owner or custodian (last, first, middle).", "if the name previously entered is different from the actual owner of the animal, enter the owner's name, address, and phone number on line 31.", "if the animal is a stray, enter the word \"stray\".", "enter both home and work (if applicable) telephone numbers."]} 32b. owner: enter the owner's last name. if the animal is a stray, leave blank; the rabies control desk will enter "stray" before filing the report. 33. address: enter the address where the animal is to be quarantined. if the address previously entered is different from the animal owner's actual address, enter the owner's address on line 31. 34. animal type: mark the appropriate box (if other, enter the type of animal). enter the breed, the animal's name, and the sex (write an "n" above the appropriate box if the animal is verified as having been altered). enter the age according to p&p 700.12; if unknown, enter an estimated age. if under one year, enter the number of months or weeks followed by "mos." or "wks." 35. description: enter the color(s) of the biting animal. if the animal is a stray, enter all other distinguishing characteristics (long hair, short hair, large, small, pointed ears) that could be used to identify the animal. 36. license: enter the current license number and the license jurisdiction (if riverside county, mark the box) and the license expiration date. if the license is expired, or if the dog should be licensed and is not, issue a citation and enter the citation number in the space provided. 37. behavior: a live animal must be observed before it can be quarantined. upon quarantine, mark the appropriate box, "normal" or "abnormal". if the animal's behavior is abnormal, enter the specifics in section 31. if the animal is deceased or stray, mark the box "unknown". 38. travels: ascertain whether the animal has been outside riverside county in the past 6 months and outside the usa in the past year; mark the appropriate boxes. if yes, indicate when and where. if a detailed explanation is needed, enter "supp" and make notations on the supplemental. 39. vaccination: mark the appropriate box. enter the kind of rabies vaccine given, the date given, and the expiration date, if available. for given by, enter the name of the veterinarian or veterinary clinic that vaccinated the animal, if available, or indicate "per comp." if the information was obtained by a computer records check through dispatch. {"step": "40. quarantine:", "sub": ["mark the appropriate box describing the place of quarantine. \"#33\" refers to the address listed on line 33.", "\"kennel #\" is used for animals quarantined at the riverside county animal shelter. a kennel number is required for live animals; for deceased animals write \"r morgue\" and place the animal in the fra test section.", "\"impound tag\" is the other id assigned to the impounded animal.", "\"microchip #\" is used to enter the microchip number when the impounded animal is scanned.", "the \"other\" box is used when the animal is quarantined at any other location; list the exact address in line 31.", "if the animal is quarantined at the victim's residence, write \"#6\" or the actual address in the space provided.", "quarantine imposed (required): enter the date quarantine is imposed and the first initial, last name, and badge number of the officer imposing quarantine. riverside county officers mark the agency box; city agencies mark the box labeled \"city of\" and write in the city name."]} 41. observed ok: enter the date, initials, and badge number (if applicable) when the animal is observed healthy. use this line for quarantine visits (including daily shelter visits by vet staff), stray bite patrols, and when an animal is observed ok during the quarantine period and at the time of quarantine release. 42. not observed: enter the date and the initials and badge number when the animal is not observed. use this line for stray bite patrols and when an animal is not observed before quarantine is imposed. note: an animal must be observed before quarantine can be imposed. 43. release: mark the appropriate box (if applicable) describing how the animal was released, "by telephone" or "upon inspection". enter the date the animal is released from quarantine and the first initial, last name, and badge number of the person releasing the animal. riverside county officers mark the agency box; city agencies mark "city of" and write in the city name. 44. cause of death: enter (if applicable) the date the death of the biting animal occurred and mark the appropriate box or boxes describing the cause. 45. veterinarian/symptoms: mark (if applicable) the appropriate box indicating whether a veterinarian observed the animal prior to the animal's death, and the box relating to symptoms as obtained by the observing veterinarian. agreement for quarantine compliance and signature: enter the date quarantine is imposed and a driver's license or id number of the animal owner or custodian (indicate the state of issuance if other than california). obtain the signature of the animal owner, and of the custodian (if applicable), who will be responsible for the animal during the quarantine. veterinarian or home quarantines with the approval of the chief of veterinary services, clinic veterinarian, or rabies control desk, any severely sick, injured, or elderly animal requiring medical treatment may be quarantined at an outside private veterinarian or at the owner's home. these quarantines may also be approved in unusual or abnormal situations by the same approvers. indicate any quarantine so approved in section 31 or on the supplemental of the bite-scratch report, including who is approving the quarantine and the circumstances necessitating quarantine somewhere other than das. all quarantines at an outside private veterinarian shall be imposed and released by an animal control officer. when imposing quarantine, the officer will direct the veterinarian to sign the bite report as the custodian. no fee will be collected for these quarantines. all quarantines at an outside private veterinarian may be released via telephone. shelter quarantines {"step": "in accordance with food and agriculture code 2606 (b) 2, shelter quarantine will be imposed at the department of animal services. the basis for shelter quarantine will be the following:", "sub": ["when the dog bites above the shoulders.", "severity of the bite.", "when the owner releases the dog for euthanasia.", "a prior bite history to any part of the body by the dog.", "an unprovoked bite to a child.", "the dog is unvaccinated.", "any medical index of suspicion that the dog may be rabid."]} the quarantine period shall be 10 days from the date of the bite. the animal may be subjected to euthanasia and the head submitted for rabies fra. chameleon bite window entry (human vs. animal) once the official bite report is completed (the biting animal was released from quarantine or the report was closed and signed by the chief veterinarian), enter a human vs. animal bite report in chameleon. accurate entry ensures accuracy of the statistical report submitted to the state for local rabies control activities. click on windows > activity > bite investigation. {"step": "bite type: select the applicable type:", "sub": ["dog10: dog vs human.", "cat10: cat vs human.", "batvh: bat vs human, possible or verified exposure.", "coyotevh: coyote vs human.", "ferretvh: ferret vs human.", "foxvh: fox vs human.", "horsevh: horse vs human.", "opossumvh: opossum vs human.", "othervh: other than what is listed vs human.", "raccoonvh: raccoon vs human.", "skunkvh: skunk vs human."]} bite date and time: enter information based on line 12a of the official bite report. report date: enter information based on line 10 of the official bite report. activity no: based on line 1c, enter the activity number, pop up (f4) the activity window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window. tag no: enter information based on line 36 of the official bite report and search for the current license of the biting animal. {"step": "status: pop up (f4) and select based on the current status of the report:", "sub": ["yellow: second copy of report received by rabies desk.", "fra: completed report, waiting for fra result.", "pending apr: completed utq report, pending approval.", "rtn field/vet/other: incomplete, returned to the appropriate area for completion.", "closed: completed report, case closed."]} owner id: based on line 32a, pop up (f4) the person window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window. animal id: based on line 34, pop up (f4) the animal window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window. prior bites: by searching based on the owner's address and owner's id, find prior bite numbers and enter the number of prior bites for the animal. valid vac: based on line 39 of the official bite report, enter "y" for yes or "n" for no. {"step": "controlled by (only applicable for bite type dog10 or cat10):", "sub": ["stray = no owner information.", "lic = owned, current license and vaccination.", "vacc = owned, current rabies vaccine but no license.", "novac = owned but no rabies vaccine or license."]} incident location: refer to line 11 of the official bite report. may be a street address, cross-streets, or landmark. pop up (f4), enter the house number and first few letters of the street name with a wildcard (%), and hit ok to search. jurisdiction: refer to the jurisdiction of the activity. geo: refer to the geocode of the activity. price: for home quarantine billing purposes. receipt no: if the officer collected a quarantine fee, enter the receipt number. victim id: pop up (f4) to bring up the person window and press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window. refer to line 2a of the official bite report. age: refer to line 4 of the official bite report. {"step": "relation:", "sub": ["if the owner of the dog is also the victim, enter self.", "if the victim lives in the same area as the owner, enter neighbor.", "if the victim is visiting the dog owner, enter friend.", "if no owner, enter stray."]} location: general location of the bite or scratch. add specifics to the synopsis. severity: enter puncture, scratch, scrape, or laceration. circumstance: enter "see synops" and refer to line 13 of the official bite report. guardian: if the victim is a minor (0-17 years old), refer to line 7 of the official bite report. witness: enter witness information whether the biting animal is located or not, or if victim or owner information does not exist. refer to line 9 of the official bite report. treatment by: enter doctor if treated by hospital or doctor, parent if treated by parent, or self if treated by self. refer to line 17 of the official bite report. treatment date: refer to line 16 of the official bite report. desc: refer to line 16 (details) of the official bite report. quarantine begin date: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. by: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. enter the chameleon id or first name initial and last name of the aco, rvt, or vet. location: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. enter "shelter" if the quarantined animal is impounded at a das shelter, or "home" if quarantine was imposed by das at home. abated by: refer to line 43 of the official bite report. enter the chameleon id or first name initial and last name of the officer, vet, or rvt who released the animal from quarantine. end date: refer to line 43 of the official bite report. enter the date the animal was released from quarantine. euth by: enter the chameleon id or first name initial and last name of the officer, vet, or rvt who euthanized the animal before the qtr date. euth date: refer to line 44 of the official bite report. enter the date the animal was euthanized before the quarantine release date. notified by (rabies desk only): refer to line 28 of the official bite report; enter first name initial and last name. notify date: refer to line 28 of the official bite report. enter the date the victim was notified that the biting animal did not have rabies. print letter (quarantine): click on reports, run a report, quarantine observed notification, if the animal was quarantined. tolab date: refer to the dlvr activity. enter the date the specimen was delivered to the lab. exam date: same date as tolab date. head date: refer to the head results fax accession date. enter the accession date as shown on the results fax. specimen no: refer to line 22 or the accession number on the results fax. enter the numerical code given by the lab. disposition: enter head test if the biting animal is submitted for fra. head result: refer to line 23 of the official bite report. enter negative, positive, or unsat. print letter (fra): click on reports, run a report, fra negative notification, if the animal was tested. {"step": "unable to quarantine (utq) transactions:", "sub": ["status: if the disposition is utq, enter pending apr until reviewed. once reviewed and signed by the chief veterinarian, enter closed.", "disposition: enter utq if the biting animal was not quarantined because the biting animal or animal owner could not be found; utq-euth if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal was euthanized (does not include fra); utq-died if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal died.", "print letter: click on reports, run a report, utq notification in jurisdiction, if the animal was not quarantined."]} animal vs. wildlife: policy and quarantine periods according to california code of regulations, 2606 (c), when a domestic animal has been bitten or a portion of its body has made contact with a wild mammal, the domestic animal must be quarantined for a period of six months, or destroyed and its brain tested for rabies. all vaccinations and re-vaccinations stated within das p&p 313-01 must be california approved rabies vaccinations. dogs and cats rabies vaccinated less than 30 days prior to exposure will not be re-vaccinated and will be quarantined for 6 months from the date of the exposure. dogs and cats re-vaccinated less than 30 days before exposure will be quarantined for 30 days from the date of exposure. dogs and cats not rabies vaccinated will be placed under quarantine, vaccinated within 48 hours, and remain quarantined for six (6) months from the date of exposure. dogs under four (4) months of age and cats under three (3) months of age will be placed under quarantine for six (6) months from the date of exposure. initiate a notice to comply to have the animal vaccinated for rabies within 48 hours after a dog becomes four (4) months of age or a cat three (3) months of age. {"step": "exceptions:", "sub": ["dogs and cats that are currently rabies vaccinated at least thirty (30) days prior to exposure will be placed under quarantine, re-vaccinated within 48 hours, and remain quarantined for thirty (30) days from the date of re-vaccination.", "exposure to any rodents, rabbits, or hares will not require quarantine unless unusual circumstances exist as determined by the chief veterinarian."]} animal vs. wildlife: quarantine visits and releases conduct a quarantine visit every month after the exposure date for a six (6) month quarantine. conduct a field release at the end of the six (6) month quarantine. conduct a quarantine visit fourteen (14) days after the revaccination date for a thirty (30) day quarantine. conduct a field release at the end of the thirty (30) day quarantine. animal vs. wildlife: distribution of copies refer to the human vs. animal distribution of copies section above (formerly das policy 100-05). submit the goldenrod to clinic personnel completed with the animal id. place the animal in a receiving cage for regular or after hours, or tag and place in the morgue section if deceased. the aco will initiate a follow-up notice giving the owner 48 hours to obtain a rabies vaccination. give the yellow copy to rabies control immediately once the bite report is initiated. animal vs. wildlife: completion of report in domestic vs. wild animal reports, the domestic animal is the "victim" and the wild animal is the "biting animal". 1a. imp #: rac animal id number generated by chameleon. 1b. case #: bite number. 1c. act #: activity number. 2a. name: enter the last name of the owner, "pet" and the type of domestic animal (for example, pet dog, pet cat), and the name of the pet if more than one. 2b. victim (last name): enter the domestic animal owner's last name (for example, pet dog smith). 3. sex: the sex of the victim animal. place an "n" or "s" in the box if the animal is altered. 4. age: the age of the victim animal. 5. telephone: home and work numbers for the animal owner. 6. address: the address from where the animal is most likely to be found and quarantined. 7. parent/guardian: last name first, first name, middle initial of the animal owner. 8. address: where the person listed in line 7 lives, if different from line 6. 9. source: mark the "other" box and list the person and his or her telephone number. if the source is the dog owner, list "#7" and "same" for the telephone number. 10. report received by: no change from the human vs. animal instructions. 11. place of attack: although there is no attack, list the place where the animal was discovered. be specific. 12a. attack date/time: line out "attack". enter the date and time the animal was exposed to wildlife. 12b. date of bite: enter the date of exposure. 13. circumstances: no change. "through material?" will not apply in most cases; animal clothing is considered "material". 14. wound location: the exact part of the body that was bitten or scratched. 15. skin break: there is no need to verify skin break to impose quarantine. puncture wounds on animals are sometimes difficult to locate; therefore, quarantine is necessary whenever there is intimate contact between a domestic animal and a rabies suspect wild animal. if there is a skin break, mark the box and initial the line. 16-20. wound treatment, treated by, anti-rabies treatment, veterinary review: no change. 21-28. laboratory exam, case closed, notification: no change. 29. qv date and 30. qr date: list the correct dates per the animal vs. wildlife quarantine visits and releases section. {"step": "31. miscellaneous: use the space in line 31 to record information about the victim domestic animal, including:", "sub": ["the domestic animal's sex, breed, color, age, and name.", "rabies vaccination, type, and expiration date.", "number of the notice to comply issued to have the animal rabies vaccinated.", "disposition of the rabies suspect wild animal."]} 32a. owner (custodian): of the rabies suspect animal. generally enter "stray" because there is usually no owner. if there is an owner, enter last name first, first name, then middle initial. 32b. owner: of the rabies suspect animal. enter only if there is a known owner; otherwise the rabies clerk will complete. 33. address: of the rabies suspect animal owner. leave blank if there is no owner. 34-35. animal and description: pertaining to the rabies suspect wild animal. no change. insert a heavy vertical line between the end of line 35 and the number 36. the remaining lines (36-46) will relate to the domestic "victim" animal. 36-38. license number, behavior, travels: no change. 39. vaccination: refers to the re-vaccination date given to comply with california code of regulations, 2606 (c). 40. animal quarantined at: no change. 43. release: see the animal vs. wildlife quarantine visits and releases section. 44-45. cause of death, veterinarian/symptoms: if the domestic animal died, no change. signature: no change. non-numbered items: map ref: no change. qt fee: mark the "no" box and write "n/a" in the space; no fee is collected. supplemental: no change. california code of regulations, 2606 (c) food and agriculture code 2606 (b) 2 das p&p 313-01 (approved rabies vaccinations) das p&p 700.12 (estimating animal age)

documents.title: Issuance of Administrative Citations
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform guideline for the issuing and processing of administrative citations.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. California Government Code 53069.4 (a)(1) authorizes the local agency to make any violation of its ordinances subject to an administrative fine or penalty; the administrative fine or penalty shall not exceed the maximum fine or penalty amounts for infractions set forth in Government Code 25132 (b) and 36900 (b).
documents.scope: Applicable to all Department of Animal Services staff responsible for issuing and handling administrative citations.
documents.supersedes: Policy 100-13, effective September 17, 2019 (revised from August 11, 2014 and July 22, 2014)
references: California Government Code 53069.4 (a)(1)
California Government Code 25132 (b) and 36900 (b) (maximum fine amounts for infractions)
California Code of Regulations, Title 17, 2606.4.a (3)
Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6, Section 6.08.220, Administrative citations
Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6, Section 6.08.120, Mandatory spaying or neutering
Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6, Section 6.08.130, Mandatory microchipping of dogs and cats
Riverside County Ordinance, Title 6, Section 6.08.020, Mandatory dog licensing and vaccination

definitions.def: A non-criminal ticket issued to an individual who does not correct violations of state mandates and local ordinances. It can be issued whether or not the individual is present, and may be posted on the property, served, or signed.
A violation that can be dismissed by showing proof of compliance or correction within 20 calendar days from the date the citation was issued.
A violation for which all penalties and administrative fees are due upon issue of the citation.

Per California Code of Regulations, Title 17, 2606.4.a (3), the expiration date from when the previous license was issued; a new license shall be procured no later than 30 days after the dog attains the age of four months.

sections.heading: Policy: When to Use
steps: Issue administrative citations as non-criminal tickets to individuals who do not correct violations of state mandates and local ordinances.
This type of citation can be issued whether or not the individual is present. Citations can be posted on the property, served, or signed.

Administrative citations are also issued to any person responsible for the violation whenever an enforcement officer or DAS employee, charged with the enforcement of any provision of state mandates and local ordinances, determines that a violation of that provision has occurred.
Citations are pre-numbered and must be accounted for in Chameleon, whether issued or voided.

steps.step: Citations are usually issued for animal related violations such as:
sub: Correctable violations, which can be dismissed by showing proof of compliance or correction within 20 calendar days from the date the citation was issued: (a) Mandatory Dog License; (b) Mandatory Rabies Vaccination; (c) Mandatory Microchipping; (d) Mandatory Spay/Neuter, which is added secondary to a primary violation, including but not limited to those listed in (a) through (c).
Non-correctable violations, which require all penalties and administrative fees to be due upon issue of the citation: (a) Permitting Dog to Run at Large; (b) Kennel/Cattery violations.

sections.heading: Completion of an Administrative Citation Form
steps: Use a black ballpoint pen.
Block print in all sections except the witness, issuing officer signature, and received by sections.

Place the hard cover of the citation book under the last copy of the citation being written in order to prevent marking other blank citations, as they are made with NCR (non-carbon reproduction) paper.
Date of Violation: enter the date the citation is written showing the month, day, and year in number form (for example, 4/1/13).
Time of Violation: enter the time the citation was issued using conventional standard time references (for example, 11:20 a.m.).
Day of Week: circle the day of the week the citation was issued.
Identification of the Violator: enter the full name and address of the defendant. If the citation is posted, enter the property owner.

steps.step: Personal Identification of the Violator:
sub: Driver's License Number and State: enter the violator's official government identification number and applicable state if the violator is present.
Class: enter the driver's license class type of the violator.
Age.
Birthdate (for example, 7/19/87).
Phone Number: enter the violator's telephone number with area code.
Sex (M or F).
Hair color, eye color, height, weight.
Race (W - White, B - Black, H - Hispanic, O - Other).
Other description.

steps: Location of Violation(s): if the violation occurred at the address indicated in the Address section of the violator, enter Same as Above. Otherwise, enter the complete address where the violation occurred.

steps.step: Description of Animal(s): enter the description of each animal involved:
sub: Name: if unknown, enter DOG1 up to DOG4.
Gender (for example, F, M, Unk).
Age: enter the approximate age.
Color.
Breed.

steps.step: Violation of Riverside County Ordinance Number:
sub: Correctable? (yes or no).
Counts: if only one animal is involved, enter 1; otherwise enter the total number of animals involved.
Circle the amounts based on whether the citation is a 1st, 2nd, or 3rd offense.
Total: enter the total amount for each violation item multiplied by the counts of animals involved.
Mandatory administrative processing fee per citation: County $25; City of Riverside $21.

steps: Enter the total amount owed by adding each subtotal for every violation, including the mandatory administrative processing fee.
Witness: the witness will sign his or her name and date.
Signature: the issuing officer will sign his or her name (first name initial and last full name) and enter his or her badge number.
Received By: the subject of the violation will sign his or her name after the citation is explained to him or her. If the citation is posted, enter posted or served.

sections.heading: Distribution of the Form
steps: White copy: retained by the officer on file until the citation goes to collection.
Yellow copy: either posted on the property or given to the defendant.
Pink copy: retained by the officer for filing and kept as long as the staff sees fit.
Goldenrod: the officer's copy.

sections.heading: Issuing a Chameleon Administrative Citation
steps: Start from the Chameleon citation window.
Date: press the F5 key to date and time stamp when the citation was issued in the date field.
Violation Type: if it is the first citation for a specific violation within one year, enter ADM CITE1. Otherwise, enter ADM CITE2 or ADM CITE3.
Defendant: enter the PID of the defendant who will be receiving the citation.
Issued By: enter P0287142 in the Issued By field.
Location of Violation: press the F4 key and enter the street number and the street name of the defendant, then press OK. This will populate both the location and jurisdiction.
Jurisdiction: automatically populated by the location of violation.
Store (F9) the record. A V number must be assigned by Chameleon before offenses can be added.

steps.step: Offense: enter the specific code based on ordinance or municipal code:
sub: 6.08.020A: Mandatory Dog License.
6.08.020B: Mandatory Rabies Vaccination.
6.08.130: Mandatory Microchipping.
6.08.120: Mandatory Spay/Neuter.
8.12.010: License Required.
8.16.010: Mandatory Vaccination of Dogs.
8.21.030: Microchipping - Dogs and Cats.
8.21.010: Spay or Neuter.
18.12.010: License Required.
18.16.010: Mandatory Vaccination of Dogs.
18.21.030: Microchipping - Dogs and Cats.
18.21.010: Spay or Neuter.
10.12.010: License Required.
10.16.010: Mandatory Vaccination of Dogs.
10.21.030: Microchipping - Dogs and Cats.
10.21.010: Spay or Neuter.

steps: Quantity: enter one (1).
Animal ID: enter the Chameleon issued animal ID of the dog that is the subject of the citation.
Animal Description: automatically populated from the animal window.
Price: automatically populated based on the violation type.
Status: choose NOTICE.
Click on the PLUS icon to add the offense.
Repeat the offense entry steps when adding additional offenses to the same animal and if there is more than one animal involved in the citation.
Print the administrative citation and issue it to the defendant.
