documents.title: Initiating State Spay-Neuter Violation Administrative Citations
documents.purpose: To provide a consistent process for initiating, following up on, and administering an administrative citation due to non-compliance with mandatory spay or neuter of an adopted dog or cat.
documents.authority: Not stated in source; procedure is executed by the Supervising Office Assistant or Lead Office Assistant.
documents.scope: Supervising Office Assistants and Lead Office Assistants who initiate, follow up on, and administer state spay/neuter violation administrative citations. Applies to any jurisdiction regardless of where the dog will be residing.
documents.supersedes: None identified. Effective date of source: July 27, 2016.
references: California Food and Agricultural Code Sections 30503, 30804.7, 31751.3(a)(1)
Riverside County Ordinance 630.16, Section 22
Riverside County Code Title 6, Section 6.08.220 (Administrative citations and penalties), section C.1.3
SOP on Initiating Administrative Citations

definitions.def: In compliance with Food and Agricultural Code Sections 30503, 30804.7, and 31751.3(a)(1), no public animal control agency or shelter, society for the prevention of cruelty to animals (SPCA), humane society shelter, or rescue group can sell or give away to a new owner any cat or dog that has not been spayed or neutered, unless the veterinary staff certifies that the animal is too sick or injured to be spayed or neutered, or that it would otherwise be detrimental to the health of the animal. The adopter or purchaser must present proof of spaying or neutering within 30 business days.

sections.heading: Authority
steps: Initiate this procedure in compliance with Food and Agricultural Code Sections 30503, 30804.7, and 31751.3(a)(1) when an adopter or purchaser has not presented proof of spay or neuter within 30 business days.

steps.step: Base the citation on:
sub: Tag Window S/N field is blank.
Outcome type is ADOPTION.
Outcome subtype is CONTRACT.

steps: Apply this procedure to any jurisdiction regardless of where the dog will be residing.

sections.heading: Prior to Initiating an Administrative Citation

steps.step: Apply the general guidelines:
sub: Does the tag/link window S/N field show S or N?
Do any of the memos provide documentation that a new and approved arrangement has been made?
Is there an active or pending administrative citation under the person owner ID record?
The location of the violation is the shelter where the animal was impounded.
The appeal process will be within the location of the shelter where the animal was impounded.
In accordance with Title 6, 6.08.220, Administrative citations and penalties, section C.1.3, the letter mailed will be sent via return receipt.

In accordance with Ordinance 630.16, Section 22(b), Administrative citations and penalties, the failure of the citation to set forth all required contents shall not affect the validity of the proceedings.
The administrative citation fee of $25 (for all jurisdictions except Riverside) or $21 (City of Riverside) will be due once the cite is mailed, regardless of compliance.
The administrative citation will be generated on the 31st day from the adoption-contract outcome date.

sections.heading: Storing a Violation Record Within Our Jurisdiction

steps.step: Open the Violation Window:
sub: Click on Windows.
From the drop down menu, choose Activity.
Choose Citation.

steps.step: If an administrative citation for S/N Violation does not exist, create an ADM CITE1 violation record (refer to the SOP on Administrative Citations); otherwise, choose ADM CITE2 or ADM CITE3:
sub: Press [F5] in the violation date field.
Tab to the Type field and choose ADM CITE1.
Tab to the Defendant ID field and enter the person ID of the adopter or purchaser.

Tab to the Issued By field and enter: P0287142 for the Riverside Shelter; P0377476 for the Coachella Valley Animal Campus; P0770819 for the Blythe Animal Shelter; P0649511 for the San Jacinto Shelter.

Tab to the location of violation and pop up [F4]. Type in the address of the shelter and hit OK. Chameleon will automatically show a list of options if the address checker fails to meet a match. Select the street number, street, and jurisdiction, and then press the OK button.
Once you select the address, the jurisdiction will automatically show up in the jurisdiction field.

Tab over to the Synopsis field and enter a note in the following format: date and time, then: non-compliance with state mandate F&A Code 30503, 30804.7, 31751.3(a)(1), failure to present proof of spay/neuter within 30 business days. (Your initials.)
Tab over to the Outcome Date field and press [F5] for today's date.
Tab over to the outcome status field, pop up [F4], and select MAILED.
Store the record [F9]. Chameleon will assign the Violation_No [VYY-######].

steps.step: Assign the offense. A violation offense cannot be added until Chameleon assigns the V number:
sub: Tab to the offense field and type in 30804.7. This will populate the field with FAILURE TO SPAY/NEUTER.
Tab to the animal ID field and enter the kennel animal ID.

Tab to the price field and enter the following amounts: 100.00 if the violation type is ADM CITE1; 200.00 if the violation type is ADM CITE2; 500.00 if the violation type is ADM CITE3. (Please note: if this is for an admin cite 2 or above and within our licensing jurisdiction, then you must cite for license violation separately. Refer to the SOP on Administrative Citations.)
Tab to the status field, pop up [F4], and choose Mailed.
Click on the plus green icon to add the offense to the grid.
If more than one animal was redeemed, repeat the offense assignment steps for each animal.

steps.step: Print the citation. This report will only print from the Violation Window:
sub: From the menu tab, choose Reports or press [F3].
Scroll to Vet Services Mgmnt Reports, choose Intent to Comply Citation, and hit OK.
Print the citation.
Mail to the owner or custodian using return receipt mail.

sections.heading: Clearing a Citation
steps: If the constituent provides proof of spay or neuter within 30 days, $25 is due and the citation will be cleared.

steps.step: In the animal window, in the Spay/Neuter area, enter:
sub: Completed: enter the spay/neuter date.
Place a Y in the proof field.
Pop up [F4] in the Vet ID field to first perform a search on the vet or clinic. If the vet or clinic exists, choose the information and press enter.

steps: Scan the proof of spay or neuter to the tag/link window.
Double-click on the offense, change the offense status to CLEARED, and click on the icon below the red minus icon to update the offense.
Enter the violation outcome status of CLEARED and press F5 on the outcome date field for Chameleon to generate the current date.
If the constituent paid the F&A Code state deposit fee of $75 for dogs or $40 for cats and provides proof of spay or neuter after 30 business days, then no refund is due.

sections.heading: Initiating Collections
steps: Send the citation into collection status on the 31st day from the violation issue date if the owner or custodian does not comply with the spay or neuter violation. (Refer to the SOP on Administrative Citations.)
Place the cursor in the outcome status field, pop up [F4], and select COLLECTION.
Place the cursor in the Outcome Date field and hit [F5] for today's date.
Update [F8] the record.
Tab over to the receipt field to generate a receipt for the violations.
Add the payment plan fee once you have initiated a collections receipt.
Go to the Chameleon violation window and initiate the next level of admin citation within the year from the violation date.

Acknowledgment of SOP (retained in employee personnel file)

documents.title: Processing Service Dog Applications and Issuing Service Dog Licenses
documents.purpose: Provides consistent guidelines in handling requests for service dog applications, documenting service dog applications in Chameleon, processing approved and denied service dog applications, issuance of service dog tags, and maintenance of applications.
documents.authority: Applications are reviewed by the Chief of Veterinary Services; processing is handled by the Animal Services Supervisor in charge of licensing.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to Department of Animal Services (DAS) staff who receive requests for service dog applications and to the Animal Services Supervisor in charge of licensing who processes and maintains all service dog applications.
documents.supersedes: None identified. Effective date of source: March 18, 2018.
references: Title II and Title III of the American Disabilities Act (ADA), as cited in the source document
County of Riverside Ordinance 630.17
ADA National Network service animals booklet: https://adata.org/publication/service-animals-booklet

definitions.def: Any dog that is individually trained to do work or perform tasks for the benefit of an individual with a disability, including a physical, sensory, psychiatric, intellectual, or other mental disability. Tasks performed can include, among other things, pulling a wheelchair, retrieving dropped items, alerting a person to a sound, reminding a person to take medication, or pressing an elevator button.

definitions.term: Animals that are not service animals
definitions.def: Emotional support animals, comfort animals, and therapy dogs are NOT service animals under Title II and Title III of the ADA. Other species of animals, whether wild or domestic, trained or untrained, are not considered service animals. The work or tasks performed by a service animal must be directly related to the individual's disability. A doctor's letter stating that a person has a disability and needs the animal for emotional support does not turn an animal into a service animal.

sections.heading: License Prerequisite
steps: Require a current valid dog license to submit a service dog application, effective March 30, 2018, in accordance with County of Riverside Ordinance 630.17, Section 2, subsection g. This does not apply to active working police dogs involved in enforcement.

sections.heading: Requests
steps: Front counters: notify the licensing supervisor by email or by telephone of a request for a service dog application.
By email: forward the email to the licensing supervisor for response.
By telephone: provide the direct desk number of the licensing supervisor, 951-358-7342.
Reminder: referring a customer to the licensing supervisor for a service dog application DOES NOT constitute an automatic approval.

steps.step: Upon receipt of a request for the service dog application, the licensing supervisor or designee will:
sub: Create a Chameleon tag/link window record, making sure that SuperSearch is performed on the pet owner or applicant to avoid creating duplicate IDs for the owner or animal, and generate the service dog application from Chameleon.
Tag type: apply the applicable tag type based on the jurisdiction.
Tag_status: TEMPORARY with a problem code M. Until the application is received by the licensing supervisor, this allows the system to generate a notice for licensing compliance.
Tag_date: today, or press F5.
Enter rabies vaccination information in the appropriate fields.
Tag_price: $0.00.
Store (F9) the record.
Press F3 to open the reports drop down menu.
Scroll down to Licensing Reports.
Choose Service Tag New Application.
Enter criteria to view the application: the license number/tag number from the tag_no field, the owner's person ID (PID), and the password.
Print the application to mail, or export to PDF format to email.
Store an animal_id memo to document the date and time when the application was generated and mailed or emailed.

sections.heading: Applications Received
steps: Scan the application to the license record of the animal in the tag window.
The licensing supervisor submits the application and attached documents to the Chief of Veterinary Services for review.

steps.step: If the application is denied, the licensing supervisor enters notes in Chameleon and mails or emails a denial letter:
sub: Press F3 to open the reports drop down menu.
Scroll down to Licensing Reports.
Choose Service Tag Denial Letter.
Enter criteria to view the letter: enter the password and the applicable title.
Print to mail, or export to PDF format to email.

steps.step: If the application is approved:
sub: New: the licensing supervisor will store a LIC SERV tag type in the Chameleon tag/link window, and a permanent service dog tag is mailed to the dog handler.
Renewal: the licensing supervisor will renew the existing LIC SERV tag record.

related.name: ADA National Network service animals booklet (https://adata.org/publication/service-animals-booklet)

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2018 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log). Source screenshots of Chameleon windows not carried over.

documents.title: Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department
documents.purpose: To provide guidelines to meet State mandated retention periods.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all Department of Animal Services (DAS) staff responsible for impounding animals and determining hold times.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-08 versions effective September 1, 2009; October 7, 2009; December 9, 2009; February 8, 2010; and July 19, 2010
references: California Food and Agricultural Code section 31754 (Holding Periods for Relinquished Animals)
California Food and Agricultural Code sections 31752 and 31108 (Impound Holding Period for Stray Cats and Dogs)

definitions.def: Any animal taken into the custody of the public pound or animal control services.
As of January 1, 2012, defined as Monday through Saturday, whenever the shelter is open.
Any animal meeting one of the ownership identification criteria listed in the Owner Known Animal section of this policy.
Any animal impounded into the shelter without any of the criteria noted under Owner Known animals.
Any pet turned in by the purported owner or custodian for either the purpose of euthanasia or adoption.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Comply with retention requirements in accordance with the guidelines below and the Standard Operating Procedure applicable to your Division. This applies to all Department staff involved in the impoundment of animals.

steps.step: Treat an animal as Owner Known if it meets any of the following:
sub: Wearing a Riverside County license which provides verifiable owner information (an in-service telephone number or valid address).
DAS implanted microchip, regardless of current owner information or registration.
Current owner information that can be validated and provides a verifiable address and/or telephone number (tags, microchips, tattoos, etc.).
Confiscated from private property (abandonment, owner arrest, etc.).
Owner physically identifies the animal and provides proof of ownership either in the field or when visiting the shelter.
At the field officer's discretion, any potential owner information provided by a third party may be considered to describe ownership.

steps.step: Do not hold the following as Owner Known animals for 10 days:
sub: Any animal without proper identification, such as a tag with only the animal's name and no owner information.

Any animal with a dead-end microchip, tag, or tattoo for which staff are not able to locate any current or valid owner information. Additionally, the microchip must not be a DAS microchip; if the chip was implanted by DAS, hold 10 days as stated above.
Any animal with attached information that is speculative in nature and not able to be verified by any means.
Any animal with speculative information provided by a third party (such as "I think the dog lives there").

sections.heading: Owner Known Animal: Holding and Documentation
steps: Hold Owner Known Animals for 10 calendar days from the time notification has been sent by first class mail. These animals should have a Chameleon status of "Known Wait."

steps.step: For verified ownership of impounded animals:
sub: Enter all owner information into Chameleon through the Tag/Link Window.
Change the status to "Known Wait" and change the due out date to 10 calendar days from the impound date.

Replace the "Owner Info" Chameleon memo type with "O Notified." Give owners a specific date by which to redeem the animal and document the date in Chameleon. An Operations Chief may make special arrangements to extend the holding period and due out date by placing a note in the "Owner Info" memo attached to the animal ID.

steps: If all avenues to contact the owner using identification found on the animal are exhausted and no further information can be found, change the status from "Known Wait" to "Stray Wait" in Chameleon after supervisory approval.

sections.heading: Stray Wait Animal
steps: Hold a Stray Wait animal for four (4) business days, not including holidays, weekends, or the day of impound, as reflected in current state law.

steps.step: Hold an OwnSur Wait animal for four (4) business days, not including legal holidays, weekends, or the day of impound. The details are as follows:
sub: The animal is available for owner redemption or adoption on the day of impound as well as the four (4) additional business days. A two (2) day board fee and owner surrender fee shall be charged to the owner relinquishing the pet.
On the fifth day, the animal may be released to a non-profit rescue, redeemed by the owner, or held for a longer adoption period.
After the fifth day, the animal may be released for euthanasia or held for a longer adoption period.

related.name: Memo types: Owner Info, O Notified; statuses: Known Wait, Stray Wait

documents.title: Donation Intake Policy and Procedure
documents.purpose: To establish a comprehensive policy for the acceptance and processing of donations received by the Department of Animal Services.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. The Shelter Director and their designee have the right to accept or refuse any gift. Only the Board of Supervisors can sign, or give permission for the Director to sign, a grant contract.
documents.scope: Applicable to all personnel in the Department of Animal Services who receive donations.
documents.supersedes: None identified in source
references: Riverside County Board of Supervisors Policy A-30, Coordination of Grants Protocol
Riverside County Board of Supervisors Policy A-52, Sponsorships
DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information and Procedures
IRS substantiation requirements for charitable gift acknowledgment

definitions.def: Cash or cash equivalent gifts made through currency, check, electronic funds transfer, wire transfer, credit card, or automatic deductions from bank accounts. May be made as a stand-alone donation, on a monthly basis, or attached to a service payment such as license renewal.

Gifts from individuals, corporations, and other businesses including equipment, services, or media purchased for RCDAS staff members and volunteers to use in their work, donations of pet food, or other pet products. These items are sometimes lightly used.
Gifts donors commit to in the present but that RCDAS does not receive or have available for use until the future, typically when donors are deceased.

A specific gift to RCDAS in a revocable testamentary document such as a will or revocable living trust, including gifts in which RCDAS is named as a beneficiary of a financial account, 401K plan, IRA, brokerage account, or life insurance policy.

steps.step: Gift acceptance policies and procedures are designed to give prospective donors the greatest flexibility within legal parameters in making gifts to support the RCDAS mission. Donations can be monetary or in-kind, and one-time or monthly. This policy seeks to do the following:
sub: Protect the interests of donors of charitable gifts to RCDAS.
Protect the interests of RCDAS.
Delineate the administrative responsibilities of RCDAS with respect to charitable gifts received.

sections.heading: Gift Acceptance Rules
steps: Use of Gifts: Make every effort to ensure that the charitable intentions of each donor are fulfilled. No program, agreement, trust, contract, or commitment shall be urged upon any prospective donor that would benefit RCDAS at the expense of the donor's interest and welfare. All gifts must serve the charitable purposes of RCDAS and are subject to the ultimate approval of the Shelter Director and their designee, who have the right to accept or refuse any gift. Gifts that are too restrictive, too difficult to administer, or for purposes outside the mission or in conflict with the values of RCDAS will not be accepted.
Use of Legal Counsel: Seek the advice of legal counsel in matters relating to acceptance of gifts when appropriate.

Conflict of Interest: Encourage all prospective donors to seek the assistance of personal legal and financial advisors in matters relating to their gifts and the resulting tax and estate planning consequences.

Gift Types: RCDAS will accept, among other gift types, cash and cash equivalents; deferred gifts, including bequests and beneficiary designations; and company products and services (gifts-in-kind). Certain gifts, including any outright gift not listed above or deferred gifts not previously discussed, are subject to review by the Shelter Director and their designee prior to acceptance.

Executorship: RCDAS will not serve as the executor of any estate, nor recommend specific individuals as executors. Encourage every potential donor to select their own executor from among trusted family, friends, and/or legal or financial advisors.

Confidential Information: Keep all information concerning donors and prospective donors (names, addresses, phone numbers, names of beneficiaries, nature and worth of estates, etc.) strictly confidential unless the donor grants permission to use selective information for recognition, referral, testimonial, or example.

Investment of Gifts: Riverside County reserves the right to make any and all investment decisions regarding gifts received. Upon making a gift, donors give up all rights, interest, and title to the asset contributed, including the right to choose investments, investment managers, or brokers.
Acknowledgment and Reporting: Prepare and send each donor a letter of acknowledgment meeting IRS substantiation requirements for gifts.

Periodic Review: The Shelter Director and Principal Accountant shall periodically review gift acceptance policies and procedures to determine if amendments are necessary to reflect changes in the law and address new situations.

Acceptance or Refusal: The Department may at its discretion refuse to take donations, gifts, or grants on a case by case basis if the acceptance obligates the Department or originates from a source contrary to the mission and vision of RCDAS.
Sponsorships: Sponsorships must comply with Riverside County Board of Supervisors Policy A-52, Sponsorships.

sections.heading: Donation Types and Recognition
steps: Request a donation during each business transaction, when appropriate. An acceptable script at the end of each transaction is: "Would you like to donate to help a homeless pet today?" Use judgment and discretion in situations where soliciting would be inappropriate (for example, during an animal cruelty investigation or with a highly agitated customer), and place a note in the Chameleon Person ID window to indicate why a donation was not requested.
Collect the donor's information and enter it in the Chameleon Donor window at the time of donation. Record all donations in the Chameleon database, regardless of whether the donor remains anonymous.

Monetary donations (stand-alone and attached to service payments): One-time donations are mailed, delivered, submitted via the website, or received over the phone; constituents may also donate monthly. The Director or their designee runs a daily report to identify donations requiring recognition, and the Donation Associate generates a prompt thank-you response (post card, letter, or phone call) based on the amount donated and at the Director's discretion.

In-kind donations: Accept in-kind donations at the front counter at any shelter location, or arrange pickup of larger donations in the field. For large donations needing storage, coordinate with the Donations Associate and the Animal Services Chief overseeing warehouse operations. Examples include blankets, towels, newspapers, animal food, and animal supplies or enrichment toys. Recognition follows the same daily report and thank-you process as monetary donations.

In Memoriam and In Honorarium donations: Donors may donate in honor of or in memory of a loved one or a beloved pet and receive a personalized thank-you letter. Donations may be made anonymously if requested. Send a letter to the honoree when appropriate; letters notifying an honoree shall not disclose the monetary amount or the donor's name unless permission is given. Coordinate bequests, trusts, and wills with the Director of Animal Services and seek legal guidance from County Counsel as necessary.

Wish List items: Managers may request at any time that an item be added to the Department's Amazon Wishlist so members of the public can contribute to day-to-day shelter operations. Wishlist items shall be approved by the Director and should enhance the care of the animals and further the Department's mission.

Grants: Seek grants throughout the year within the time limits or granting periods set by each grantor. All grants go through the grant administrator; managers who find an opportunity must coordinate the application through the grant administrator. All awarded grants must be approved through a Form 11 sent to the Board of Supervisors. All grants are subject to Board Policy A-30. The grant administrator ensures all grant reports and follow-up requirements are submitted in a timely manner.

Fundraising events: Whenever possible, work with outside agencies interested in partnering for fundraising purposes. Approval of these events and partnerships is at the discretion of the Director and shall not conflict with the mission or values of RCDAS. Complete a request form detailing the purpose of the event and forward it to the Director for approval.

sections.heading: Chameleon Procedure: Donation Categories
steps: Care Fund: Contributions help improve care for homeless and abandoned pets and promote their adoption by allowing funds to be used in areas of greatest need.

Disaster Relief Fund: Contributions support the rescue, sheltering, and treatment of animals in the event of a disaster, help train volunteers in life-saving tasks during disaster response and recovery, and educate the community about disaster preparedness.

Animal Health Fund: Contributions support treatment of injured or ill animals, medical care by shelter veterinarians, emergency treatment or hospitalization in outside institutions, behavior programs, and special care for animals suffering from cruelty or neglect and those in the Foster Care Program.

Pet Zone Fund: Contributions support programs that help owners retain their pets and become responsible pet owners, including spay or neuter, education and outreach, community cats, diversion and safety net programs, and other programs supporting the human-animal bond and pet owners in crisis.

sections.heading: Chameleon Procedure: Data Quality Requirements
steps: Mandatory email: The user must enter a valid email address to store or update a person record (contact types EMAIL, BOTH, MAIL, REFUSED, TEXT per VT_PERSON_CONTACT_STAT). Per DAS 000-01, accurate email addresses are necessary for specific transactions such as licensing and adoption. A monthly report on bad, missing, and good emails is emailed to managers and supervisors, and supervisors receive a user report for follow-up on records with no email or refused status.
Mandatory donation yes/no box: The person window donation field requires a Y or N for tracking purposes.
Daily transaction reporting: A transaction report is emailed to the supervisor daily showing the number of donation transactions per user at the front counter.
Forced donation line items: The Chameleon administrator forces donation line items to show on receipts for any adoption, owner surrender, RTO, euthanasia request, and licensing transaction.

Daily audit: The supervisor runs a daily receipt transaction report to review missing item codes. The absence of donation item codes, or a $0.00 entry, is a flag for the supervisor to follow up with staff on why donation item codes are missing or zeroed out.

sections.heading: Chameleon Procedure: Website Donations
steps: The donation portal is located on the top right corner of www.rcdas.org. The donor clicks "SAVE A LIFE!" to link to the HLP donation portal form.

The donor completes the online form with name, address, phone, and email, makes a donation by choosing one or more fund categories, and may add comments or instructions such as in memory of, in honor of, or acknowledgment requests.

sections.heading: Chameleon Procedure: Monetary Donation Data Entry

steps.step: Include the donation item codes in the debit script:
sub: Donation Care Fund
Donation Disaster Relief
Donation Animal Health Fund
Donation Pet Zone Fund

steps: Audit: The supervisor performs a daily audit of transactions to track missing or zeroed out donation item codes.

sections.heading: Chameleon Procedure: In-Kind Donation Entry (Front Counter)

steps.step: If a constituent is unable to wait for a receipt to be generated in Chameleon, or prefers to remain anonymous, use the designated donation receipt books available at all front counter locations:
sub: Complete the receipt form, including the donor's name, address, and email. If the donor wishes to remain anonymous, write "anonymous."
Itemize the donation, specifying the amount and value if applicable.
Give the yellow copy to the donor. Keep the white copy and enter the information in the Chameleon Donation window.

steps: Access the Donation window from Windows > Person > Donation.

steps.step: Record who donated:
sub: Person ID: individual donor; required.
Group ID: required if the donor is an organization. Click Group ID, pop up [F4] to bring up the group window, and search first to avoid duplicates; if no existing record exists, store [F9].
Co-Donor1: second individual donor, if applicable.
Co-Donor2: third individual donor, if applicable.

steps.step: Record what was donated:
sub: Amount: if in-kind, enter $0.
Donation Type (then click OK): Equipment, Landscape, Multiple, Other, Pledge, or Time.
Use: indicate how a donor prefers or earmarks their donation to be used.
Campaign: identify a specific fundraising activity (sustaining, endowment, capital project, breed support, an animal by name, etc.).
Account No: enter the account code for tracking and report use.
Location (Extra1): required; identifies who collected the donation (see Appendix A location codes).
Description: enter the item description.

steps: Store the donation window, then go to the receipt field and press F4 to bring up the receipt window. Itemize each donated item using the donation item code and store the receipt.

steps.step: Acknowledgements, Honorarium, and In Memoriam: if the donor specifies for whom the donation is in memory of or in honor of:
sub: Honor ID 1, 2, or 3 (Person): use for acknowledgment of, in honor of, or in memory of a person. In the field across from each Person ID, enter A for in acknowledgment, H for in honor of, or M for in memory of.
Honor ID 4 (Group): use for acknowledgment of, in honor of, or in memory of an organization, with the same A/H/M codes.

Honor ID 5 (Animal) or List of Pets: enter an existing Animal ID or store a new Animal ID; if there is more than one pet, enter the names of the pets in the List of Pets field, with the same A/H/M codes.
A/H/M Notes: enter text specified by the donor.

sections.heading: Automated Notifications
steps: E-receipts are automatically emailed to the customer upon completion of a transaction, including donation receipts.

E-thank you: a thank-you email is automatically sent to a customer whose transaction includes a donation line item in the Chameleon receipt greater than or equal to $10. The template is signed by the Director of Animal Services and includes the Department tax ID (95-6000930).
The e-receipt automated notification is documented in the Chameleon Contact window (contact type EMAIL, report E-RECEIPT).

documents.searchText: to establish a comprehensive policy for the acceptance and processing of donations received by the department of animal services. applicable to all personnel in the department of animal services who receive donations. director of animal services. the shelter director and their designee have the right to accept or refuse any gift. only the board of supervisors can sign, or give permission for the director to sign, a grant contract. monetary donations cash or cash equivalent gifts made through currency, check, electronic funds transfer, wire transfer, credit card, or automatic deductions from bank accounts. may be made as a stand-alone donation, on a monthly basis, or attached to a service payment such as license renewal. gifts-in-kind gifts from individuals, corporations, and other businesses including equipment, services, or media purchased for rcdas staff members and volunteers to use in their work, donations of pet food, or other pet products. these items are sometimes lightly used. deferred gifts gifts donors commit to in the present but that rcdas does not receive or have available for use until the future, typically when donors are deceased. bequests a specific gift to rcdas in a revocable testamentary document such as a will or revocable living trust, including gifts in which rcdas is named as a beneficiary of a financial account, 401k plan, ira, brokerage account, or life insurance policy. policy statement {"step": "gift acceptance policies and procedures are designed to give prospective donors the greatest flexibility within legal parameters in making gifts to support the rcdas mission. donations can be monetary or in-kind, and one-time or monthly. this policy seeks to do the following:", "sub": ["protect the interests of donors of charitable gifts to rcdas.", "protect the interests of rcdas.", "delineate the administrative responsibilities of rcdas with respect to charitable gifts received."]} gift acceptance rules use of gifts: make every effort to ensure that the charitable intentions of each donor are fulfilled. no program, agreement, trust, contract, or commitment shall be urged upon any prospective donor that would benefit rcdas at the expense of the donor's interest and welfare. all gifts must serve the charitable purposes of rcdas and are subject to the ultimate approval of the shelter director and their designee, who have the right to accept or refuse any gift. gifts that are too restrictive, too difficult to administer, or for purposes outside the mission or in conflict with the values of rcdas will not be accepted. use of legal counsel: seek the advice of legal counsel in matters relating to acceptance of gifts when appropriate. conflict of interest: encourage all prospective donors to seek the assistance of personal legal and financial advisors in matters relating to their gifts and the resulting tax and estate planning consequences. gift types: rcdas will accept, among other gift types, cash and cash equivalents; deferred gifts, including bequests and beneficiary designations; and company products and services (gifts-in-kind). certain gifts, including any outright gift not listed above or deferred gifts not previously discussed, are subject to review by the shelter director and their designee prior to acceptance. executorship: rcdas will not serve as the executor of any estate, nor recommend specific individuals as executors. encourage every potential donor to select their own executor from among trusted family, friends, and/or legal or financial advisors. confidential information: keep all information concerning donors and prospective donors (names, addresses, phone numbers, names of beneficiaries, nature and worth of estates, etc.) strictly confidential unless the donor grants permission to use selective information for recognition, referral, testimonial, or example. investment of gifts: riverside county reserves the right to make any and all investment decisions regarding gifts received. upon making a gift, donors give up all rights, interest, and title to the asset contributed, including the right to choose investments, investment managers, or brokers. acknowledgment and reporting: prepare and send each donor a letter of acknowledgment meeting irs substantiation requirements for gifts. periodic review: the shelter director and principal accountant shall periodically review gift acceptance policies and procedures to determine if amendments are necessary to reflect changes in the law and address new situations. acceptance or refusal: the department may at its discretion refuse to take donations, gifts, or grants on a case by case basis if the acceptance obligates the department or originates from a source contrary to the mission and vision of rcdas. sponsorships: sponsorships must comply with riverside county board of supervisors policy a-52, sponsorships. donation types and recognition request a donation during each business transaction, when appropriate. an acceptable script at the end of each transaction is: "would you like to donate to help a homeless pet today?" use judgment and discretion in situations where soliciting would be inappropriate (for example, during an animal cruelty investigation or with a highly agitated customer), and place a note in the chameleon person id window to indicate why a donation was not requested. collect the donor's information and enter it in the chameleon donor window at the time of donation. record all donations in the chameleon database, regardless of whether the donor remains anonymous. monetary donations (stand-alone and attached to service payments): one-time donations are mailed, delivered, submitted via the website, or received over the phone; constituents may also donate monthly. the director or their designee runs a daily report to identify donations requiring recognition, and the donation associate generates a prompt thank-you response (post card, letter, or phone call) based on the amount donated and at the director's discretion. in-kind donations: accept in-kind donations at the front counter at any shelter location, or arrange pickup of larger donations in the field. for large donations needing storage, coordinate with the donations associate and the animal services chief overseeing warehouse operations. examples include blankets, towels, newspapers, animal food, and animal supplies or enrichment toys. recognition follows the same daily report and thank-you process as monetary donations. in memoriam and in honorarium donations: donors may donate in honor of or in memory of a loved one or a beloved pet and receive a personalized thank-you letter. donations may be made anonymously if requested. send a letter to the honoree when appropriate; letters notifying an honoree shall not disclose the monetary amount or the donor's name unless permission is given. coordinate bequests, trusts, and wills with the director of animal services and seek legal guidance from county counsel as necessary. wish list items: managers may request at any time that an item be added to the department's amazon wishlist so members of the public can contribute to day-to-day shelter operations. wishlist items shall be approved by the director and should enhance the care of the animals and further the department's mission. grants: seek grants throughout the year within the time limits or granting periods set by each grantor. all grants go through the grant administrator; managers who find an opportunity must coordinate the application through the grant administrator. all awarded grants must be approved through a form 11 sent to the board of supervisors. all grants are subject to board policy a-30. the grant administrator ensures all grant reports and follow-up requirements are submitted in a timely manner. fundraising events: whenever possible, work with outside agencies interested in partnering for fundraising purposes. approval of these events and partnerships is at the discretion of the director and shall not conflict with the mission or values of rcdas. complete a request form detailing the purpose of the event and forward it to the director for approval. chameleon procedure: donation categories care fund: contributions help improve care for homeless and abandoned pets and promote their adoption by allowing funds to be used in areas of greatest need. disaster relief fund: contributions support the rescue, sheltering, and treatment of animals in the event of a disaster, help train volunteers in life-saving tasks during disaster response and recovery, and educate the community about disaster preparedness. animal health fund: contributions support treatment of injured or ill animals, medical care by shelter veterinarians, emergency treatment or hospitalization in outside institutions, behavior programs, and special care for animals suffering from cruelty or neglect and those in the foster care program. pet zone fund: contributions support programs that help owners retain their pets and become responsible pet owners, including spay or neuter, education and outreach, community cats, diversion and safety net programs, and other programs supporting the human-animal bond and pet owners in crisis. chameleon procedure: data quality requirements mandatory email: the user must enter a valid email address to store or update a person record (contact types email, both, mail, refused, text per vt_person_contact_stat). per das 000-01, accurate email addresses are necessary for specific transactions such as licensing and adoption. a monthly report on bad, missing, and good emails is emailed to managers and supervisors, and supervisors receive a user report for follow-up on records with no email or refused status. mandatory donation yes/no box: the person window donation field requires a y or n for tracking purposes. daily transaction reporting: a transaction report is emailed to the supervisor daily showing the number of donation transactions per user at the front counter. forced donation line items: the chameleon administrator forces donation line items to show on receipts for any adoption, owner surrender, rto, euthanasia request, and licensing transaction. daily audit: the supervisor runs a daily receipt transaction report to review missing item codes. the absence of donation item codes, or a $0.00 entry, is a flag for the supervisor to follow up with staff on why donation item codes are missing or zeroed out. chameleon procedure: website donations the donation portal is located on the top right corner of www.rcdas.org. the donor clicks "save a life!" to link to the hlp donation portal form. the donor completes the online form with name, address, phone, and email, makes a donation by choosing one or more fund categories, and may add comments or instructions such as in memory of, in honor of, or acknowledgment requests. chameleon procedure: monetary donation data entry {"step": "include the donation item codes in the debit script:", "sub": ["donation care fund", "donation disaster relief", "donation animal health fund", "donation pet zone fund"]} audit: the supervisor performs a daily audit of transactions to track missing or zeroed out donation item codes. chameleon procedure: in-kind donation entry (front counter) {"step": "if a constituent is unable to wait for a receipt to be generated in chameleon, or prefers to remain anonymous, use the designated donation receipt books available at all front counter locations:", "sub": ["complete the receipt form, including the donor's name, address, and email. if the donor wishes to remain anonymous, write \"anonymous.\"", "itemize the donation, specifying the amount and value if applicable.", "give the yellow copy to the donor. keep the white copy and enter the information in the chameleon donation window."]} access the donation window from windows > person > donation. {"step": "record who donated:", "sub": ["person id: individual donor; required.", "group id: required if the donor is an organization. click group id, pop up [f4] to bring up the group window, and search first to avoid duplicates; if no existing record exists, store [f9].", "co-donor1: second individual donor, if applicable.", "co-donor2: third individual donor, if applicable."]} {"step": "record what was donated:", "sub": ["amount: if in-kind, enter $0.", "donation type (then click ok): equipment, landscape, multiple, other, pledge, or time.", "use: indicate how a donor prefers or earmarks their donation to be used.", "campaign: identify a specific fundraising activity (sustaining, endowment, capital project, breed support, an animal by name, etc.).", "account no: enter the account code for tracking and report use.", "location (extra1): required; identifies who collected the donation (see appendix a location codes).", "description: enter the item description."]} store the donation window, then go to the receipt field and press f4 to bring up the receipt window. itemize each donated item using the donation item code and store the receipt. {"step": "acknowledgements, honorarium, and in memoriam: if the donor specifies for whom the donation is in memory of or in honor of:", "sub": ["honor id 1, 2, or 3 (person): use for acknowledgment of, in honor of, or in memory of a person. in the field across from each person id, enter a for in acknowledgment, h for in honor of, or m for in memory of.", "honor id 4 (group): use for acknowledgment of, in honor of, or in memory of an organization, with the same a/h/m codes.", "honor id 5 (animal) or list of pets: enter an existing animal id or store a new animal id; if there is more than one pet, enter the names of the pets in the list of pets field, with the same a/h/m codes.", "a/h/m notes: enter text specified by the donor."]} automated notifications e-receipts are automatically emailed to the customer upon completion of a transaction, including donation receipts. e-thank you: a thank-you email is automatically sent to a customer whose transaction includes a donation line item in the chameleon receipt greater than or equal to $10. the template is signed by the director of animal services and includes the department tax id (95-6000930). the e-receipt automated notification is documented in the chameleon contact window (contact type email, report e-receipt). riverside county board of supervisors policy a-30, coordination of grants protocol riverside county board of supervisors policy a-52, sponsorships das policy 000-01, general chameleon information and procedures irs substantiation requirements for charitable gift acknowledgment

documents.title: Animal Reclaimed by Owner (Animal Redemptions)
documents.purpose: To establish a procedure to record when an animal is reclaimed by its owner that contributes to accurate inventory tracking of animals impounded at the Riverside County Department of Animal Services shelter sites and to provide the customer with an itemized receipt.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: All personnel responsible for processing the owner reclaim of an impounded animal using Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: SOP Animal Redemptions, effective April 20, 2023; revised from January 13, 2022; March 29, 2021; August 23, 2018; September 12, 2016; February 4, 2016; January 20, 2011; December 30, 2009; December 10, 2009; May 27, 2009
references: DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window
DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information
Food and Agriculture Code 32003
Food and Agriculture Codes 31108.5 and 31752.2 (positive identification requirements)
SOP Owner Notification Procedures

definitions.def: Return to owner; the outcome type recorded when an impounded animal is reclaimed by its owner or custodian.

A transaction where the owner identifies their pet via PetHarbor, provides all necessary identification proofs, pays all fees via Converge phone payment, and agrees to pick up the animal via Acuity appointment. The ASR creates a waitlist.me appointment to notify the ACT of the incoming appointment.

A transaction where the owner made an Acuity appointment to visit with the ACT and view the animal to positively identify whether the impounded animal is their pet. Once the animal is positively identified, the ACT with diversion creates the links in Chameleon then sends a Microsoft Teams confirmation to kennelcardrequest@rivco.org, notifying the ASR that the patron is ready for payment.

Similar to a virtual consult except the customer is at the shelter and wants to positively confirm whether the animal on PetHarbor is their pet. This requires the customer to make a same-day appointment, depending on availability.
Outcome subtype used when the animal remains at the shelter for spay or neuter surgery before release.
Owner turn-in; used with RTO/OTI transactions where the pet is outcomed as RTO then re-intaken for owner-requested adoption or euthanasia.

sections.heading: 1. General Provisions
steps: Process all animals impounded at DAS that are reclaimed by the owner or custodian through the Chameleon software system. This function records, by individual Animal ID number, the outcome type, calculates fees, and prints a receipt that includes license information, microchip records, medical treatments, and rabies vaccination certificate, if applicable.

Effective January 1, 2022, no healthy dog or cat impounded at DAS shall be reclaimed to an owner without being microchipped unless veterinary staff certifies in writing that the dog or cat is medically unfit for the microchipping procedure.

Flag animals impounded at the shelter with verifiable identification (refer to SOP Owner Notification Procedures) as kennel status KNOWN WAIT. This ensures the picture is no longer posted on PetHarbor via www.rcdas.org on the lost and found website.

Enter an OWNER INFO memo type for the Animal ID along with the status change to document the verifiable identification found on the impounded animal. This is replaced with NOTIFIED once the owner or custodian identifies and confirms ownership of an impounded animal.

sections.heading: 2. Identity Verification
steps: Request a government issued identification from the owner, pursuant to Food and Agriculture Codes 31108.5, 31752.2, and 32003, which require positive identification for relinquishment of an animal and for record keeping.
Match the information against the owner information on the kennel window screen or possible owner information on the memo window screen.
Scan a copy of the approved government issued identification through ChamCam and attach it to the Person ID of the owner or possible owner.

sections.heading: 3. Confirm the Animal Is Cleared for Redemption

steps.step: If the animal was impounded under quarantine restriction:
sub: Verify that the quarantine has been completed as noted in the Animal Memo with the veterinarian's initials.
If approved for completion of the quarantine at another location, verify the Animal Memo contains approval from the veterinarian or rabies control.

steps: If the animal was impounded under Confiscate or investigation hold status, verify that it has been removed from these restrictions. Review notes in the activity memo and animal memo thoroughly. If notes are not in Chameleon, notify the owner that you are not able to proceed with the transaction and that a supervisor will contact them. Email applicable staff and cc your immediate supervisor, then proceed with the next customer.

If the animal was impounded under a vicious declaration, verify that the owner has met the requirements and Field Services has given approval for the release. Review notes in the activity memo and animal memo thoroughly. If notes are not in Chameleon, notify the owner that you are not able to proceed with the transaction and that a supervisor will contact them. Email applicable staff and cc your immediate supervisor, then proceed with the next customer.

sections.heading: 4. Check for Prior Impound History on the Animal
steps: Place the mouse cursor on the Animal ID or Person ID of the owner.
Press Ctrl+F4 to bring up the animal or person view window.
Review the impound grid and note any outcome type of RTO.

steps.step: If prior impound history is located:
sub: Verify that it is the same animal.
Verify that the previous intake date is within the twelve month period from today's date.
Press the ESC key to close the window.

sections.heading: 5. Owner Reclaim Processes
steps: Use one of the three processes for owner reclaims: pre-paid pick-up RTO, virtual consult, or on-site appointment (see Definitions).

sections.heading: 6. RTO City Requirements
steps: Apply the jurisdiction requirements below. There are no exemptions for spay or neuter on impounded animals that do not meet the age limit. Medical reasons approved by DAS veterinary staff are the only allowance for pets to go on a contract.

steps.step: Riverside City: microchip, rabies, and license required. Spay or neuter required for all dogs and cats 7 months of age or older.
sub: Male dogs and cats over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.
Female dogs and cats over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter.

steps.step: Riverside County, Jurupa Valley, and Eastvale: microchip, rabies, and license required. Spay or neuter required for all dogs and cats 4 months of age or older.
sub: Male dogs and cats over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.
Female dogs and cats over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter.

steps.step: Fontana: all dogs and cats require microchip and rabies. Spay or neuter required for all pit bulls only, 4 months of age or older.
sub: Male dogs over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.
Female dogs over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter.

steps.step: Colton: microchip and rabies required. Spay or neuter required for all dogs and cats 4 months of age or older.
sub: Male dogs and cats over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.
Female dogs and cats over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter.

steps: Rialto: microchip, rabies, and licensing required. Only when the animal is picked up by Rialto PD/ACO does the Rialto apprehension fee apply. No spay or neuter requirements.

RTO/OTI for adopt or euthanasia: licensing applies when applicable; no rabies, microchip, or spay or neuter cost. Charge regular RTO fees plus the OTI fee for adopt or euthanasia (not both). Outcome the pet as RTO, then re-intake the pet for OTI adopt or euthanasia.

steps.step: Pit bull ordinance exemptions (Riverside City and County only):
sub: Currently licensed and a registered breeder.
Working dog for law enforcement.
Service assistance dog.
In training at a licensed kennel and the dog has a license at an out-of-jurisdiction location (OOJ).

sections.heading: 7. Process Owner Reclaim at the Shelter
steps: Update the computer with all current information, both for the animal and the owner.
Record the driver's license of the owner or custodian in the Person Window identifier1 field of Chameleon.

steps.step: Issue a dog license if the dog is not licensed and resides in our licensing jurisdiction. Assess a late fee if either of the following criteria apply:
sub: The dog is over four months of age and the owner has not made a reasonable effort to license the dog after 30 days of ownership.
The current license has not been renewed within 30 days of the renewal due date.

steps: If the animal is staying at the shelter for spay or neuter, DO NOT change the animal sex to S or N and DO NOT enter S or N in the tag/link window S/N field.
Set the tag status for tag type LIC to TEMPORARY. The tag problem code is A.
Proceed with the redemption. Make sure that the Tag Animal ID matches the Kennel Animal ID to ensure that all debts owed are automatically generated by the Chameleon receipt.

steps.step: If the Tag Animal ID does not match the Kennel Animal ID:
sub: Email your immediate supervisor or lead the correct Kennel Animal ID and the old Tag Animal ID as the duplicate.
Delete the old Tag Animal ID from the tag/link window.
Type the Kennel Animal ID in the tag/link window.
Update the tag/link window to save the changes.
Press enter to go back to the kennel window.

steps.step: Complete the Outcome section of the kennel window:
sub: Outcome type: RTO.
Outcome subtype: CHNGED MND, CONTRACT, FAILED SFT, or SURGWAIT, as applicable.
Outcome by: staff's three letter initials.
Outcome date: press F5 to automatically generate the current date.
Outcome time: press F5 to automatically generate the current time.

steps: Update or press F8 to save changes to the kennel window.

Click on the receipt field of the kennel window and press F4 to bring up the Receipt window. Chameleon automatically brings up the applicable charges (see Appendix A for the shelter RTO fee schedules).

steps.step: Make sure the following information is contained in each of the items charged:
sub: Animal ID.
Reference No.
Fee Type.
Dept.
Item Price.

steps: Review all charges.
Ask the customer the mode of payment for the transaction: cash, check, or credit card.
Store the receipt upon payment and print. If the customer does not have an email address, print one copy of the receipt.

steps.step: If the redeemed pet is not spayed or neutered:
sub: Print one copy of the receipt.
Have the owner or custodian sign the Intent to Comply.
Scan a legible copy to the Person ID.

steps: Press enter to bring the receipt number back to the outcome receipt number field, then press F8 to update the kennel window.

sections.heading: 8. Process Redemption by Field Officer
steps: Apply the following fees when returning an owned dog or cat in the field.
Unaltered, not licensed, first impound: RTO F $40.00 plus STATE FINE 1 $35.00; total $75.00.
Unaltered, not licensed, second impound: RTO F $40.00 plus STATE FINE 2 $50.00; total $90.00.
Unaltered, not licensed, third and subsequent impound: RTO F $40.00 plus STATE FINE 3 $100.00; total $140.00.

sections.heading: 9. City of Rialto RTOs
steps: Charge the owner or custodian per the City of Rialto fee schedules in Appendix B.

When selling a license, the license number generated by Chameleon begins with a U number. The apprehension fees are in addition to the impound fees normally charged. Pay attention to the dog's apprehension fee: on the third impound an additional home check fee must be collected.
The e-receipts and paper receipts reflect the Rialto licensing statement (Appendix C) only if a line item contains LIC RT.

steps.step: Veterinary staff will perform surgery based on the surgery list only. At no time shall veterinary staff handle any paperwork related to RTOs. The surgery list only displays animals that meet the following criteria:
sub: The kennel record has a receipt number in the kennel receipt field.
Outcome subtype is SURGWAIT.
Outcome date is blank.
Animal gender is F or M.

steps.step: Spay or Neuter Clinic (when open for business):
sub: Pick up RTO paperwork from the admissions outbox to wait for completed surgeries.

A postmaster job generates an email to notify the S/N front counter of completed surgeries so adopters can be contacted via telephone or email when their pet is ready for pickup. The postmaster job is based on the following criteria: visit type is SURGERY EXAM - R (a treatment record must be stored depending on the surgery location), animal gender is S or N, and treatment date is today.
Contact the adopter for a pick-up time.
Pick up microchip pamphlets from the spay or neuter clinic.
Prepare the aftercare packet.
Override tag number U with the implanted microchip record.
Issue the license number, override tag number U in tag type record LIC, change the tag status to CURRENT, and remove the problem code.
Enter the outcome date and outcome time when the adopter or custodian picks up their pet after spay or neuter surgery.
Change the outcome subtype from SURGWAIT to NEW.

steps.step: Adoption/Admissions (when the S/N clinic is closed):
sub: Enter the outcome date and outcome time when the adopter or custodian picks up their pet after spay or neuter surgery.
Change the outcome subtype from SURGWAIT to NEW.
Override tag number U with the implanted microchip record.
Issue the license number, override tag number U in tag type record LIC, change the tag status to CURRENT, and remove the problem code.
Provide the adopter the after-care packet and microchip pamphlet that the S/N front counter prepared.

related.name: Kennel Window, Receipt Window, Tag/Link Window, Person Window, Memo Window, ChamCam
RTO Fee Breakdown Sheet (Dog and Cat versions)
PetHarbor lost and found listings via www.rcdas.org
Acuity appointment scheduling; waitlist.me; Converge phone payment; Microsoft Teams (kennelcardrequest@rivco.org)

appendices: Appendix A: Shelter RTO fee schedules (Chameleon item codes)
Appendix B: City of Rialto RTO fee schedules, license codes, and apprehension fees
Appendix C: City of Rialto receipt licensing statement
Appendix D: RTO Fee Breakdown Sheets (Dog and Cat)

transactions where the pet is outcomed as rto then re-intaken for owner-requested adoption or euthanasia. 1. general provisions process all animals impounded at das that are reclaimed by the owner or custodian through the chameleon software system. this function records, by individual animal id number, the outcome type, calculates fees, and prints a receipt that includes license information, microchip records, medical treatments, and rabies vaccination certificate, if applicable. effective january 1, 2022, no healthy dog or cat impounded at das shall be reclaimed to an owner without being microchipped unless veterinary staff certifies in writing that the dog or cat is medically unfit for the microchipping procedure. flag animals impounded at the shelter with verifiable identification (refer to sop owner notification procedures) as kennel status known wait. this ensures the picture is no longer posted on petharbor via www.rcdas.org on the lost and found website. enter an owner info memo type for the animal id along with the status change to document the verifiable identification found on the impounded animal. this is replaced with notified once the owner or custodian identifies and confirms ownership of an impounded animal. 2. identity verification request a government issued identification from the owner, pursuant to food and agriculture codes 31108.5, 31752.2, and 32003, which require positive identification for relinquishment of an animal and for record keeping. match the information against the owner information on the kennel window screen or possible owner information on the memo window screen. scan a copy of the approved government issued identification through chamcam and attach it to the person id of the owner or possible owner. 3. confirm the animal is cleared for redemption {"step": "if the animal was impounded under quarantine restriction:", "sub": ["verify that the quarantine has been completed as noted in the animal memo with the veterinarian's initials.", "if approved for completion of the quarantine at another location, verify the animal memo contains approval from the veterinarian or rabies control."]} if the animal was impounded under confiscate or investigation hold status, verify that it has been removed from these restrictions. review notes in the activity memo and animal memo thoroughly. if notes are not in chameleon, notify the owner that you are not able to proceed with the transaction and that a supervisor will contact them. email applicable staff and cc your immediate supervisor, then proceed with the next customer. if the animal was impounded under a vicious declaration, verify that the owner has met the requirements and field services has given approval for the release. review notes in the activity memo and animal memo thoroughly. if notes are not in chameleon, notify the owner that you are not able to proceed with the transaction and that a supervisor will contact them. email applicable staff and cc your immediate supervisor, then proceed with the next customer. 4. check for prior impound history on the animal place the mouse cursor on the animal id or person id of the owner. press ctrl+f4 to bring up the animal or person view window. review the impound grid and note any outcome type of rto. {"step": "if prior impound history is located:", "sub": ["verify that it is the same animal.", "verify that the previous intake date is within the twelve month period from today's date.", "press the esc key to close the window."]} 5. owner reclaim processes use one of the three processes for owner reclaims: pre-paid pick-up rto, virtual consult, or on-site appointment (see definitions). 6. rto city requirements apply the jurisdiction requirements below. there are no exemptions for spay or neuter on impounded animals that do not meet the age limit. medical reasons approved by das veterinary staff are the only allowance for pets to go on a contract. {"step": "riverside city: microchip, rabies, and license required. spay or neuter required for all dogs and cats 7 months of age or older.", "sub": ["male dogs and cats over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.", "female dogs and cats over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter."]} {"step": "riverside county, jurupa valley, and eastvale: microchip, rabies, and license required. spay or neuter required for all dogs and cats 4 months of age or older.", "sub": ["male dogs and cats over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.", "female dogs and cats over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter."]} {"step": "fontana: all dogs and cats require microchip and rabies. spay or neuter required for all pit bulls only, 4 months of age or older.", "sub": ["male dogs over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.", "female dogs over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter."]} {"step": "colton: microchip and rabies required. spay or neuter required for all dogs and cats 4 months of age or older.", "sub": ["male dogs and cats over 12 years of age do not require spay or neuter.", "female dogs and cats over 10 years of age do not require spay or neuter."]} rialto: microchip, rabies, and licensing required. only when the animal is picked up by rialto pd/aco does the rialto apprehension fee apply. no spay or neuter requirements. rto/oti for adopt or euthanasia: licensing applies when applicable; no rabies, microchip, or spay or neuter cost. charge regular rto fees plus the oti fee for adopt or euthanasia (not both). outcome the pet as rto, then re-intake the pet for oti adopt or euthanasia. {"step": "pit bull ordinance exemptions (riverside city and county only):", "sub": ["currently licensed and a registered breeder.", "working dog for law enforcement.", "service assistance dog.", "in training at a licensed kennel and the dog has a license at an out-of-jurisdiction location (ooj)."]} 7. process owner reclaim at the shelter update the computer with all current information, both for the animal and the owner. record the driver's license of the owner or custodian in the person window identifier1 field of chameleon. {"step": "issue a dog license if the dog is not licensed and resides in our licensing jurisdiction. assess a late fee if either of the following criteria apply:", "sub": ["the dog is over four months of age and the owner has not made a reasonable effort to license the dog after 30 days of ownership.", "the current license has not been renewed within 30 days of the renewal due date."]} if the animal is staying at the shelter for spay or neuter, do not change the animal sex to s or n and do not enter s or n in the tag/link window s/n field. set the tag status for tag type lic to temporary. the tag problem code is a. proceed with the redemption. make sure that the tag animal id matches the kennel animal id to ensure that all debts owed are automatically generated by the chameleon receipt. {"step": "if the tag animal id does not match the kennel animal id:", "sub": ["email your immediate supervisor or lead the correct kennel animal id and the old tag animal id as the duplicate.", "delete the old tag animal id from the tag/link window.", "type the kennel animal id in the tag/link window.", "update the tag/link window to save the changes.", "press enter to go back to the kennel window."]} {"step": "complete the outcome section of the kennel window:", "sub": ["outcome type: rto.", "outcome subtype: chnged mnd, contract, failed sft, or surgwait, as applicable.", "outcome by: staff's three letter initials.", "outcome date: press f5 to automatically generate the current date.", "outcome time: press f5 to automatically generate the current time."]} update or press f8 to save changes to the kennel window. click on the receipt field of the kennel window and press f4 to bring up the receipt window. chameleon automatically brings up the applicable charges (see appendix a for the shelter rto fee schedules). {"step": "make sure the following information is contained in each of the items charged:", "sub": ["animal id.", "reference no.", "fee type.", "dept.", "item price."]} review all charges. ask the customer the mode of payment for the transaction: cash, check, or credit card. store the receipt upon payment and print. if the customer does not have an email address, print one copy of the receipt. {"step": "if the redeemed pet is not spayed or neutered:", "sub": ["print one copy of the receipt.", "have the owner or custodian sign the intent to comply.", "scan a legible copy to the person id."]} press enter to bring the receipt number back to the outcome receipt number field, then press f8 to update the kennel window. 8. process redemption by field officer apply the following fees when returning an owned dog or cat in the field. unaltered, not licensed, first impound: rto f $40.00 plus state fine 1 $35.00; total $75.00. unaltered, not licensed, second impound: rto f $40.00 plus state fine 2 $50.00; total $90.00. unaltered, not licensed, third and subsequent impound: rto f $40.00 plus state fine 3 $100.00; total $140.00. 9. city of rialto rtos charge the owner or custodian per the city of rialto fee schedules in appendix b. when selling a license, the license number generated by chameleon begins with a u number. the apprehension fees are in addition to the impound fees normally charged. pay attention to the dog's apprehension fee: on the third impound an additional home check fee must be collected. the e-receipts and paper receipts reflect the rialto licensing statement (appendix c) only if a line item contains lic rt. 10. rto surgwait flow {"step": "veterinary staff will perform surgery based on the surgery list only. at no time shall veterinary staff handle any paperwork related to rtos. the surgery list only displays animals that meet the following criteria:", "sub": ["the kennel record has a receipt number in the kennel receipt field.", "outcome subtype is surgwait.", "outcome date is blank.", "animal gender is f or m."]} {"step": "spay or neuter clinic (when open for business):", "sub": ["pick up rto paperwork from the admissions outbox to wait for completed surgeries.", "a postmaster job generates an email to notify the s/n front counter of completed surgeries so adopters can be contacted via telephone or email when their pet is ready for pickup. the postmaster job is based on the following criteria: visit type is surgery exam - r (a treatment record must be stored depending on the surgery location), animal gender is s or n, and treatment date is today.", "contact the adopter for a pick-up time.", "pick up microchip pamphlets from the spay or neuter clinic.", "prepare the aftercare packet.", "override tag number u with the implanted microchip record.", "issue the license number, override tag number u in tag type record lic, change the tag status to current, and remove the problem code.", "enter the outcome date and outcome time when the adopter or custodian picks up their pet after spay or neuter surgery.", "change the outcome subtype from surgwait to new."]} {"step": "adoption/admissions (when the s/n clinic is closed):", "sub": ["enter the outcome date and outcome time when the adopter or custodian picks up their pet after spay or neuter surgery.", "change the outcome subtype from surgwait to new.", "override tag number u with the implanted microchip record.", "issue the license number, override tag number u in tag type record lic, change the tag status to current, and remove the problem code.", "provide the adopter the after-care packet and microchip pamphlet that the s/n front counter prepared."]} das policy 000-03, chameleon kennel window das policy 000-01, general chameleon information food and agriculture code 32003 food and agriculture codes 31108.5 and 31752.2 (positive identification requirements) sop owner notification procedures

documents.title: Guidelines in Impounding Special Attention Animals in Chameleon
documents.purpose: To establish consistent procedures when initiating an impound record in Chameleon for special attention animals.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Any employee of DAS who has the responsibility in the acceptance, custodial care, and disposition of special attention animals.
documents.supersedes: SOP Guidelines in Impounding Special Consideration Animals in Chameleon, effective October 9, 2015
references: DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window
DAS Memo September 12, 2008, Disposition of Animals Impounded as Confiscate
DAS Memo September 25, 2014, Special Consideration Animals/Operational Holds
DAS Policy 300-04, Protocol for Animals Held in Protective Custody
DAS Policy 000-38, Completion of the Chameleon Tag/Link Window
DAS Policy 000-45, Chameleon Memo Window
DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Procedure
Riverside County Ordinance 630, Title 6, Section 11
Food and Agriculture Code 32001, Lost and Found Animal Information Requirements

definitions.def: Any animal that meets any of the following criteria: (a) police confiscate or police assist, which can include but is not limited to owner arrest, owner in hospital, owner deceased, or vehicle abandonment; (b) quarantines; (c) vicious investigations; (d) 597 suspects.

sections.heading: Completing the Chameleon Kennel Window for a Special Attention Impound
steps: Verify that all information is correct and thorough when creating the Chameleon kennel screen. Include any and all information relating to the impoundment of the animal to help provide the correct amount of retention time, owner verification, owner notification, and any special handling by veterinary and shelter staff. This includes making sure the hold request is marked with the appropriate type (refer to DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window), making sure all notes are attached to the Animal ID number, and that any and all contact or custodian information is accurate.
Begin in the Chameleon kennel window.

steps.step: Kennel Status:
sub: Enter UNAVAIL to prevent the picture of the animal from being displayed on the website (www.petharbor.com).
Enter KNOWN WAIT if verifiable owner information exists but the owner does not know that their animal was impounded, to display the animal picture on the website.
Press F4 to bring up the other list of options which will display the picture of the animal impounded.

steps: Tag: if the animal has a verified identifiable owner, find (F12) the license, microchip, or link record. If a license, microchip, or link record does not exist, store (F9) a tag/link record (refer to DAS Policy 000-38, Completion of the Chameleon Tag/Link Window).
Other ID: if the animal is impounded by a contracted city, enter the city's reference record number.
Activity No and Seq: enter the associated DAS field service activity number and sequence that resulted in the impound of the animal.
Animal ID: this is automatically populated once the information is carried over from the tag/link window.

Intake Person From: this generally refers to the person who turned in the animal for impoundment or the contracted city PID. Press F4 to bring up the person window. Refer to DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Procedure, for performing a person record search and storing (F9) a person record.
Intake Type: enter CONFISCATE.
Intake Subtype: press F4 to choose from a list of applicable options. Refer to DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window.
Intake Cond: press F4 to choose from a list of applicable animal conditions at the time of impoundment.
Intake Date: press F5 to date stamp the current date, or enter the date the animal was impounded.
Intake Time: press F5 to time stamp the current time, or enter the time the animal was impounded.
Due Out: enter the date and time the animal becomes property of DAS or the date the field or kennel supervisor approves.

Impound Location: press F4 to bring up the address checker window and enter the street number and correct spelling of the street name. Refer to DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Procedure, on how to enter cross street format.
Jurisdiction: this is automatically entered if the address checker window was used from the impound location field; otherwise, press F4 to choose the applicable jurisdiction.
Intake By: enter the first name initial and full last name of the employee, or Chameleon userid, or field service assigned number.
If complete owner information is present, use the PID from the tag/link window to populate the Outcome Person To field for the purpose of notification.
Hold: enter a Y to prevent a user from entering an outcome type and outcome date.
Outcome Request: press F4 to choose from applicable options to flag users on the reason for the confiscate. This requires an Animal ID memo (refer to DAS Policy 000-45, Chameleon Memo Window).
