documents.title: How to Impound a Stray in Chameleon
documents.purpose: To establish consistent procedures when impounding animals using the correct intake types and applicable intake subtypes in Chameleon.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Any employee of DAS who has the responsibility in the acceptance, custodial care, and disposition of impounded animals.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to Impound a Stray in Chameleon (titled How to Impound An Animal in Chameleon in the document header), effective July 10, 2019
references: DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window
Food and Agriculture Code 30504, Puppies in Litters
Food and Agriculture Code 31751, Litters of Kittens
Food and Agriculture Code 32001, Lost and Found Animal Information Requirements
Riverside County Ordinance 630.18
SOP Identifying Owned Animals
DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information

definitions.def: Having been received into custody of any animal control center, animal control officer, animal control vehicle, or peace officer duly authorized by the County of Riverside to receive such animal.
Any person who intentionally provides care or sustenance for any animal, including but not limited to a dog or cat, for any period exceeding a total of thirty (30) days.
Any person who intentionally provides care or sustenance for any animal, including but not limited to a dog or cat, on behalf of another, or represents the interests of the owner.
An impounded animal that may or may not have an owner (viable identification).

sections.heading: 1. General Requirements
steps: Verify that all information is correct and thorough when creating the Chameleon kennel screen. Include any and all information relating to the impoundment of the animal to help provide the correct amount of retention time, owner verification, owner notification, and any special handling by veterinary and shelter staff. This includes making sure the hold request is marked with the appropriate type (refer to DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window), making sure all notes are attached to the Animal ID number, and that any and all contact or custodian information is accurate.
Begin in the Chameleon kennel window.

sections.heading: 2. Stray, Owner Known (viable identification at time of impound: license tag, microchip, tattoo, or the person turning in provides owner information)
steps: Enter the kennel number: Receiving, and depending on shelter location, add the location at the beginning. For example, RReceiving.
Tab to the Status field and enter KW for Known Wait, or press F4 and select Known Wait.

Acquire a valid government ID from the person turning in the animal. This ensures positive identification of the person and confirms that the person is in a jurisdiction that DAS contracts with or provides service to.
Tab to the Person From field and pop up (F4) to bring up the person window.

Verify with the person turning in the animal that the address on the identification provided is the most current address. If yes, perform a SUPERSEARCH by address (refer to Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information, on how to perform a Supersearch).

If a record is located, verify that the phone number and email address are current. If a record is not located, create a new person record. Once the person record is stored, press enter to bring the Person ID back to the kennel window Person From field.
Shift+Tab to the Tag field and pop up (F4) to bring up the Tag/Link window. DO NOT SKIP THIS STEP. This prevents creating a duplicate ID from the beginning.

steps.step: In the Tag/Link window:
sub: If the person turning in the animal has the address of the owner, pop up (F4) the Person ID field and perform a SUPERSEARCH by address (refer to Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information).
If the animal is scanned and has a microchip, perform a tag number search in the Tag/Link window.
If an owner is located, check whether there are animals associated with that person by looking at the grid at the bottom of the person window.
If any of the animals match the animal being impounded, double mouse-click to bring up the animal window. Make note of the Animal ID. Close the animal window.
Press enter to bring the information of the displayed person record back to the Tag/Link window.
Press F12 to find animals associated with the Person ID.
Double mouse-click on the CURRENT status associated with the Animal ID that matches the impounded animal. This displays the owner and animal in the Tag/Link window.
Press enter to bring the owner and Animal ID back to the kennel window.

If an owner cannot be located through the Tag/Link window, create an Animal ID. Click on the Animal ID field and pop up (F4) to bring up the animal window. Once the Animal ID is stored, press enter to bring the Animal ID to the kennel window.

steps.step: Once all external windows are done, finish the kennel window:
sub: Enter S for stray, or press F4 to pop up and select Stray.
If impounding the animal at the shelter site, enter OTC or press F4 and select OTC.
Tab to the Cond field and enter BAR as the default; otherwise, press F4 and select the applicable condition for the animal at time of impound.
Tab to the intake date field and press F5 to enter the system date. Chameleon automatically enters the current date.

Chameleon automatically enters the current time when the kennel window was opened, so it is important to record the time when the animal was impounded. Tab to the intake time field and press F5 to enter the system time.
Enter the date manually when the animal becomes county property (retention period) and the time when it is available to the public.
Enter the specific cross streets where the animal was impounded from (for example, Clay St/Van Buren Bl, Jurupa Valley CA 92509, or the address of where it was impounded from).
Enter the jurisdiction based on where the animal was found (impound location).
Tab to the intake by field and enter your userID.
Store (F9) the record and print the kennel card.

sections.heading: 3. Stray, No Viable Identification at Time of Impound (no license tag, microchip, or tattoo, and the person turning in does not know who owns the animal)
steps: Enter the kennel number: Receiving, and depending on shelter location, add the location at the beginning. For example, RReceiving.
Tab to the Status field and enter S for Stray Wait, or press F4 and select Stray Wait.

Acquire a valid government ID from the person turning in the animal. This ensures positive identification of the person and confirms that the person is in a jurisdiction that DAS contracts with or provides service to.
Tab to the Person From field and pop up (F4) to bring up the person window.

Verify with the person turning in the animal that the address on the identification provided is the most current address. If yes, perform a SUPERSEARCH by address (refer to Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information, on how to perform a Supersearch).

If a record is located, verify that the phone number and email address are current. If a record is not located, create a new person record. Once the person record is stored, press enter to bring the Person ID back to the kennel window Person From field.

Shift+Tab or place the cursor on the Animal ID field, then press the function key F4 to pop up the animal window. Create an animal record (refer to Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information, on how to store an Animal ID).
Press the enter key to bring the animal information back to the kennel window.

steps.step: Once all external windows are done, finish the kennel window:
sub: Enter S for stray, or press F4 to pop up and select Stray.
If impounding the animal at the shelter site, enter OTC or press F4 and select OTC. For other applicable intake subtypes, refer to Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window.
Tab to the Cond field and enter BAR as the default; otherwise, press F4 and select the applicable condition for the animal at time of impound.
Tab to the intake date field and press F5 to enter the system date. Chameleon automatically enters the current date.

Chameleon automatically enters the current time when the kennel window was opened, so it is important to record the time when the animal was impounded. Tab to the intake time field and press F5 to enter the system time.
Enter the date manually when the animal becomes county property (retention period) and the time when it is available to the public.
Enter the specific cross streets where the animal was impounded from (for example, Clay St/Van Buren Bl, Jurupa Valley CA 92509, or the address of where it was impounded from).
Enter the jurisdiction based on where the animal was found (impound location).
Tab to the intake by field and enter your userID.
Store (F9) the record and print the kennel card.

documents.title: How to Impound an Animal Using Chameleon ChamTransfer
documents.purpose: To provide a consistent guide on the use of ChamTransfer when impounding an animal in Chameleon.
documents.authority: The ChamTransfer application is managed, maintained, and handled by the designated Chameleon administrator of the Department of Animal Services.
documents.scope: The designated Chameleon administrator, front counter ASRs, designated TReceiving staff, and authorized staff who use ChamTransfer.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to Impound Animals using Chameleon ChamTransfer, effective April 27, 2022
references: DAS Policy 000-03, Completion of the Chameleon Kennel Window
SOP Impounding an Animal in Chameleon

definitions.def: A Chameleon application that enables the quick and easy transfer of animal records between Chameleon shelters. It transfers animal information, animal profile, memos, medical history, microchip data, and the latest animal image. Receiving an animal record can be accomplished with as little as three mouse clicks; sending an animal can be done with as little as two mouse clicks. This applies only to outside agencies who use Chameleon and also use ChamTransfer.

sections.heading: Impounding via ChamTransfer
steps: Launch ChamTransfer by clicking on the icon.
Log in. The user ID is the Chameleon user ID and the password is the Chameleon password.
The main screen will display.
Click on Refresh to ensure that data in the Transfer IN tab has not been processed. This minimizes duplicate kennel records or extra impound records.
If, after clicking the Refresh button, there is still data in the Transfer IN (receive) tab, click on the first data row in Transfer IN. ChamTransfer will highlight the selected record.
Click the IN button.
Do not close ChamTransfer until reconciliation with the Chameleon kennel window has been performed.
Locate the contract city email notification for impounded animals through kennelcardrequest.

steps.step: Complete the Chameleon kennel window by:
sub: Find (F12) the first record Animal ID in the kennel window from the action history in ChamTransfer.
Based on the email from the contract city, match the described animal/Other ID with the animal description to obtain the impound location.

Enter the impound location. If an address, press F4 on the impound location field to enter the street number and street name, then press OK. If cross streets, press Shift+F1 on the impound location field for the correct format for entering cross streets.
Press F8 to update the kennel record.

steps: Print the kennel record for the city officer.
Repeat the two preceding steps until all action history animals have a complete kennel record.
Click on the Transfer History tab of ChamTransfer.
Take a screenshot of the imported Animal IDs. Pay attention to the date.
Reply to the contract city email and attach the screenshot of the Animal IDs transfer history for the specific date only.
Click on Quit to close ChamTransfer.

SOP Impounding an Animal in Chameleon
Contract city email notifications via kennelcardrequest

documents.title: How to Transfer Impounded Animals Between DAS Shelters Using the Chameleon Kennel Window
documents.purpose: To provide a procedure that is consistent and accurate for DAS staff to follow while maintaining the integrity of the animal's impound record, tracking, and inventory.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Staff who transport impounded animals between DAS shelters.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to Transfer Impounded Animals Between DAS Shelters Using the Chameleon Kennel Window, effective September 28, 2014
references: DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window

sections.heading: 1. The First Transfer (removing the animal from the original shelter)
steps: Any DAS staff member who is removing an animal from the original shelter is responsible for completing the Chameleon kennel record with an outcome type TRANSFER, outcome subtype DAS, outcome date, outcome time, and outcome by.
Enter the Person ID of the DAS shelter that the animal is going to in the Outcome Person To field.
Enter TRANSFER in the outcome type field.
Pop up (F4) from the outcome subtype field to bring up a list and choose DAS.
Press the function key F5 in the outcome date field so Chameleon enters the current date.
Press the function key F5 in the outcome time field so Chameleon enters the current time.
Enter the staff member's userid in the outcome by field.
Save or update (F8) the kennel record.

sections.heading: 2. When the Animal Arrives at the DAS Shelter Destination
steps: The DAS staff member who receives the animal is responsible for creating an impound record ensuring that the animal was received at its destination.
Perform a search (F12) on the Animal ID with an outcome type of TRANSFER and an outcome subtype of DAS.
Place the cursor in the impound number of the original kennel window and erase the kennel number (KYY).
Change the kennel number to the specific kennel number for that DAS shelter.
Erase the PID in the Intake Person From field and replace it with the PID of the DAS shelter the animal came from.
Erase the original intake type and replace it with TRANSFER.
In the intake subtype field, pop up (F4) to bring up a list and choose DAS.
Press F5 in the intake date, intake time, and due out date fields.
Do not change the impound location or jurisdiction.
Enter the staff member's userid in the intake by field.
Delete all outcome information except for the weight and medical grade.
Store (F9) the new impound record.

sections.heading: 3. When the Animal Is Coming Back to the Original Shelter
steps: The DAS staff member who is removing the animal from the shelter is responsible for entering the outcome information.
Perform a search (F12) on the Animal ID that is currently housed at the shelter.
Enter the Person ID of the DAS shelter that the animal is going to in the Outcome Person To field.
Enter TRANSFER in the outcome type field.
Pop up (F4) from the outcome subtype field to bring up a list and choose DAS.
Press the function key F5 in the outcome date and time fields so Chameleon enters the current date and time.
Enter the staff member's userid in the outcome by field.
Save or update (F8) the kennel record.

sections.heading: 4. The Animal Has Reached Its Destination (back at the original shelter)
steps: The DAS staff member who receives the animal is responsible for creating a new impound record on the returning animal.
Perform a search (F12) on the Animal ID with an outcome type of TRANSFER and an outcome subtype of DAS.
Place the cursor in the impound number of the original kennel window and erase the kennel number (KYY).
Change the kennel number to the specific kennel number for that DAS shelter.
Erase the PID in the Intake Person From field and replace it with the PID of the DAS shelter the animal came from.
Press F5 in the intake date, intake time, and due out date fields.
Do not change the impound location or jurisdiction.
Enter the staff member's userid in the intake by field.
Delete all outcome information except for the weight and medical grade.
Store (F9) the new impound record.

sections.heading: 5. To Review Whether the Procedure Was Successful
steps: Clear (F11) the Chameleon kennel window.
Enter the Animal ID or press F6 to choose the most recent Animal ID record.
Find, or press F12, to find the records.
Review the kennel table grid, which displays available records and the sequence of each impound record.

documents.title: Microchip Transactions
documents.purpose: To establish uniform and consistent guidelines when storing, updating, or removing microchip transactions using the Chameleon tag window. Any animal that is implanted with a microchip by DAS shall have the microchip number recorded and stored in the Chameleon tag/link window.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: DAS staff who record, update, or remove microchip records in the Chameleon tag/link window, including shelter, S/N clinic, and veterinary staff.
documents.supersedes: SOP Microchip Transactions, effective October 4, 2019; revised from May 29, 2019; December 19, 2016; July 11, 2012; April 1, 2009
references: DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information

definitions.def: Tag type for a microchip implanted at a shot clinic.
Tag type for a microchip implanted during adoption transactions.
Tag type for a microchip implanted at offsite events for DAS.
Tag type for a microchip implanted under the Community Cat Program (SNR/TNR).
Tag type for a microchip not implanted by DAS where proof was provided.
A placeholder tag number generated by Chameleon when a microchip has not yet been assigned or implanted.

sections.heading: A. Pre-empted Microchipped Adoption Animals, Implanted by DAS (RCAC/SJAC/CVAC)
steps: From the kennel window, click on the caduceus icon to bring up the treatment window.
If the treatment record that relates to the Animal ID is not displayed automatically, press the F12 function key to find the treatment record.
Otherwise, double click on the applicable visit type to display the microchip number implanted in the animal being microchipped.
Click on the TAG icon located on the top menu bar to bring up the Tag/Link window.

steps.step: In the Person ID field, store or locate owner information:
sub: If being adopted, enter the PID of the adopter.

If pre-empted for rescue, events, or offsite locations, enter the following placeholder PID until the PID of the adopter or rescue replaces it: P0287142 for Western Riverside Shelter only; P0377476 for Coachella Valley Animal Campus; P0649511 for San Jacinto Animal Campus; P0770819 for Blythe Shelter. Note: these placeholder PIDs are not to be updated over with new owner information.

steps: Tag Animal ID must be the same as the Kennel Animal ID.
Enter the microchip number in the Tag No field in this format: 0813331111. No extra characters.
In the Tag Type field, choose MCHIP (implanted at shot clinic), MCHIP ADPT (implanted during adoption transactions), or MCHIP OFFSITE (implanted at offsite events for DAS).
In the Subtype field, choose the applicable type of microchip.
In the Tag Term field, enter 99.
Leave the tag_exp field blank. Chameleon will enter the date based on the tag date and tag term.
In the Batch No field, enter the lot number.
Press F9 to store the record.

sections.heading: B. Community Cat Program, SNRs and TNRs, Implanted by DAS
steps: Enter Best Friends' PID P0984710 in the Person ID field.
Store the Animal ID.
If using the Chameleon ScanIt program, launch MicrochipC to activate the script for scanning the microchip for CCP. This is recommended to save time.
If entering the microchip manually, enter the microchip number in the Tag No field. Be careful: errors can lead to not being able to locate the owner.
Enter MCHIP CCP in the tag type field.
Key in AVID in the subtype field.
Tag status must be CURRENT.
Tag term is 99. Chameleon will automatically complete the tag exp date field.
Enter the microchip lot number in the Batch No field.
Enter the Person ID from where the animal was picked up from. This ensures that staff do not get confused on ownership of the animal.

sections.heading: C. Previously Microchipped (Return Adoptions), Implanted by DAS
steps: From the kennel window, press F4 on the Tag field.
Verify whether a U number was assigned to the microchip record in the receipt window.

steps.step: Old owner:
sub: If yes, write down that U number, then replace the existing microchip number in the Tag No field with this U number and update.
If no, delete the microchip number from the Tag No field, then press F9 to store and generate a U number.
Change the status of the record to OWNER SUR.

steps.step: New owner:
sub: If the microchip has not yet been assigned, enter the microchip number in the Tag No field, enter the new owner's PID, make sure the Animal ID is the same as the Kennel Animal ID, then press F9 to store the record.
If a U number was generated, enter the microchip number in the Tag No field, then press the F12 key to find the record.
Place the cursor on the Person ID field (previous adopter) and delete the PID.
Press F4 to pop up the person window and create the new adopter record.
The Tag Animal ID must be the same Animal ID as in the kennel window.
Press F9 to store the record.

sections.heading: D. New Adoptions (no microchip assigned)
steps: From the kennel window, press F4 on the Tag field.
Leave the tag number blank.
In the tag type and tag subtype fields, enter the applicable microchip validations.
Enter 99 in the tag term.
Press F9 to store the record. This generates a U number.
Continue with the adoption process.
In the receipt window, make sure that under the microchip line item, the reference number is the U number stored from the tag window.
S/N clinic staff or vet staff will replace the U number with the microchip number in the tag window once it is implanted.

sections.heading: E. Microchip Sold at the S/N Front Counter (no microchip assigned), Implanted at Any DAS Location
steps: You must be in the Tag/Link window.
Prior to creating the Person ID, follow procedures on searching for existing records on the owner and animal.
If the owner has an existing record, verify whether the animal being microchipped matches the existing Animal ID description. Use the same number. Do not store a new Animal ID.
If there is no existing person or animal record, store a new PID and AID.
Place the cursor in the tag number field.
Using the barcode scanner, scan the microchip. This automatically stores a microchip record.
When veterinary staff provides the batch number of the microchip, go to the batch number field and enter the AVID batch number.
Press F8 to update the record.
If the barcode scanner is not working, follow the manual entry steps in Section A (microchip number format, tag type, subtype, tag term, and batch number).

sections.heading: F. Public Microchips (not implanted by DAS)
steps: If the animal was impounded, start from the kennel window, then pop up the Tag field.
If the transaction is due to a license compliance, start from the Tag/Link window.
If the activity was because of a field service call, start from the activity window, then pop up the Tag No field.
In the Person ID field of the tag window, perform a search to avoid duplicates prior to creating the microchip owner.
Press enter to bring the PID back to the tag window.
If the person or owner does not have an existing Animal ID for the microchipped animal, create an Animal ID. Refer to Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information.
Enter the microchip number in the Tag No field in this format: 0813331111. No extra characters.
In the Tag Type field, choose MCHIP-PUB (not implanted by DAS but proof was provided).

steps.step: In the Subtype field, choose the applicable type of microchip:
sub: 24PETWATCH: used with MCHIP-PUB.
AVID: implanted by DAS; used with MCHIP ADPT, MCHIP OFFSITE, and MCHIP tag types.
PUB-AVID: used with MCHIP-PUB.
PUB-HOMEA: used with MCHIP-PUB.
PUB-ISO: used with MCHIP-PUB.
PUB-RESQ: used with MCHIP-PUB.

steps: In the Tag Term field, enter 99.
Leave the tag_exp field blank. Chameleon will enter the date based on the tag date and tag term.
Leave the Batch No field blank.
A secondary contact using the second Person ID can be added, provided a search is performed to avoid duplicates prior to creating a new record.
Press F9 to store the record.

Placeholder PID: P0377476.
Placeholder PID: P0649511.
Placeholder PID: P0770819.

documents.title: Notice of Impoundment (NOI) Letter Template
documents.purpose: To notify an owner identified through a license, ID tag, microchip, or other identification that their pet may be at a Riverside County animal shelter and must be redeemed on or before the stray hold deadline.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services (letter issued on Department letterhead).
documents.scope: RCDAS staff who generate and send owner notification letters for impounded animals with owner identification.
documents.supersedes: None identified in source
references: Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department (10 day owner known hold)

sections.heading: Template Content
steps: Send the notice on Department of Animal Services letterhead showing the Director's title, mailing address (6851 Van Buren Blvd, Jurupa Valley CA 92509), telephone (951) 358-7387, fax (951) 358-7300, website (www.rcdas.org), and email (shelterinfo@rivco.org).
Address the letter to the owner's name and mailing address on record, with the date of issue and the heading NOTICE OF IMPOUNDMENT.

State that the owner's pet may be at the Riverside County Animal Shelter at the applicable shelter location address, and that identification on the pet (a license, ID tag, microchip, etc.) prompted the notice.

Instruct the owner to contact Animal Services by coming directly to the shelter or calling (951) 358-7387 on or before the 10 day stray hold deadline by 11 a.m., to bring a form of identification and proof of ownership if available, and to contact shelterinfo@rivco.org if they need assistance keeping their pet.
List shelter hours: Tuesday through Friday 11:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., and Saturday and Sunday 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.

Include the animal description (name, age, sex, breed, color, animal type) and remind the owner to bring a copy of all current vaccination records, photos and/or videos for proof of ownership, and the spay/neuter certificate if the pet is altered.
Include the impound information block: impound number, animal reference number, found date, and found time.

related.name: Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department

revisions.desc: Source letter template with example merge data

revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Letter content unchanged; example recipient and animal merge data removed; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Owner Surrender and Rehome Support Request Form - Dogs
documents.purpose: To establish a consistent process for RCDAS staff to administer, review, and act on information gathered through the Owner Surrender/Rehome Support Request Form for dogs. The form captures the behavioral, medical, and household history of a dog whose owner is seeking to surrender or rehome the animal. Consistent use supports accurate animal records, informs appropriate placement decisions, reduces unnecessary shelter intake through diversion where appropriate, and improves adoption matching by preserving owner knowledge about the animal.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. RCDAS staff are authorized to collect owner-provided intake information under California Food and Agriculture Code sections 31752 and 31754 and Riverside County Ordinance No. 630.
documents.scope: All RCDAS staff who interact with owners seeking to surrender or rehome a dog, including Animal Services Representatives (ASRs), Animal Care Technicians (ACTs), intake counselors, and other designated staff. Applies at all shelter locations and to any intake interaction initiated in person or remotely.
documents.supersedes: None identified in source
references: California Food and Agriculture Code sections 31752 and 31754
California Health and Safety Code section 121685 (10-day bite quarantine)
Riverside County Ordinance No. 630
SOP 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department
RCDAS Intake Diversion Program Guidelines

definitions.def: The voluntary relinquishment of an animal by its owner to an RCDAS shelter facility.
A diversion pathway in which RCDAS staff assist an owner in finding a new placement for their animal without formal shelter intake, when the animal meets diversion criteria.
An RCDAS staff member trained to conduct intake interviews, review surrender forms, and identify diversion opportunities.
The RCDAS team responsible for identifying and coordinating alternatives to shelter intake, including owner-assisted rehoming, foster placement, and community referrals.
The unique animal identification number assigned in Chameleon at or prior to intake.

A mandatory 10-day quarantine period required by California Health and Safety Code section 121685 for any animal that has bitten a person, during which the animal must be observed for signs of rabies.
The RCDAS shelter management software system used to document all animal records and intake transactions.

sections.heading: Scheduling and Pre-Intake Form Distribution
steps: When an owner contacts RCDAS to request a surrender appointment, direct the owner to complete the Owner Surrender/Rehome Support Request Form: Dogs prior to or at the time of the appointment.
For appointments scheduled by phone, send or provide the form link and instruct the owner to bring a completed copy to their appointment or submit it in advance.

sections.heading: Form Review at Intake Appointment
steps: At the time of intake, the intake counselor or designated staff member reviews the completed form in full before beginning the intake interview.
Verify that all required fields are complete. If any fields are blank or unclear, ask the owner to provide the missing information before proceeding.
Enter all form data into Chameleon at or before the time of intake, including the animal's name, age, weight, breed or breed mix, and all behavioral and medical responses.

sections.heading: Bite History Review
steps: Address the bite history question before any other intake steps proceed; it is a required safety screen.
If the owner indicates the dog has bitten someone in the past 10 days, immediately notify Animal Control.
Document the incident description and date provided by the owner in Chameleon and flag the record for a mandatory bite hold per California Health and Safety Code section 121685.
Do not proceed with standard intake processing for a bite-hold animal until a supervisor has reviewed the record and confirmed the appropriate quarantine protocol.
If the owner indicates no bite history in the past 10 days, proceed to the next section of the form.

sections.heading: Surrender Reason and Diversion Assessment
steps: Document the owner's stated reason for surrender in Chameleon using the categories on the form: lack of time, behavior issue, medical issue, moving/housing, or other.
For each surrender reason, consult the RCDAS Intake Diversion Program Guidelines to assess whether diversion resources are available and appropriate before proceeding to formal intake.

sections.heading: Medical History Review
steps: Review the owner's responses regarding current medical problems and medications.
If the owner indicates the dog has active medical issues or takes medications, flag the record in Chameleon for veterinary review and attach or note any records provided.
Remind the owner to email any available veterinary records to shelterinfo@rivco.org with the A# in the subject line if records were not brought to the appointment.
Document all medications listed by the owner in the Chameleon animal record.

sections.heading: Behavioral and Household History
steps: Treat each behavioral history response as documentation, not conversation, and enter all responses accurately into Chameleon. This section provides placement-critical information that directly affects where and with whom the animal can be safely placed.

sections.heading: Media and Records Collection
steps: At the conclusion of the intake appointment, confirm with the owner whether any photos, videos, or medical records will be submitted by email.
Provide the owner with the submission email address and confirm the A# to reference in the subject line.
Once submitted media and records are received, attach them to the animal's Chameleon record.

sections.heading: Record Completion and Handoff
steps: Before the owner departs, confirm that the Chameleon record is complete, including all form fields, narrative responses, medical flags, and bite history notation if applicable.

Notify the appropriate shelter team (veterinary staff, IDT, or kennel staff) of any flags identified during the intake review, including bite holds, active medical conditions, or diversion referrals in progress.
A supervisor or senior staff member reviews intake records for completeness at the end of each shift.

related.name: Chameleon Shelter Management Software (animal records and intake transactions)
Owner Surrender/Rehome Support Request Form: Dogs (Attachment A)
SOP 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded by the Department

appendices: Appendix A: RCDAS Owner Surrender/Rehome Support Request Form: Dogs (attached separately)

documents.title: RTO Fee Breakdown Sheets (Dog and Cat)
documents.purpose: To provide staff a standard worksheet for itemizing and quoting the fees and fines an owner must pay to redeem an impounded dog or cat (return to owner).
documents.authority: Riverside County Department of Animal Services fee schedule as reflected on the October 2020 sheets.
documents.scope: Staff who provide return-to-owner fee quotes to owners of impounded dogs and cats.
documents.supersedes: October 2020 RTO Fee Breakdown Sheet (Cat) V.4 (merged into this document as Appendix B)

sections.heading: Using the Fee Breakdown Sheet
steps: Enter the date and the Animal ID number on the sheet.
Circle each applicable fine or fee and its cost, using the dog sheet (Appendix A) or cat sheet (Appendix B) as appropriate.
Fill in variable amounts where indicated: city/county license fee and penalty (dog sheet), other medications, and board fee calculated at $31.60 per day.
Record the total estimated charges.
Record the initials of the individual giving the quote and the name of the individual the quote was given to.
Advise the owner that the quote is valid through the end of the business day indicated on the sheet.

appendices: Appendix A: RTO Fee Breakdown Sheet (Dog)
Appendix B: RTO Fee Breakdown Sheet (Cat)

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Dog and cat sheets combined into one document; amounts unchanged from October 2020 versions; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Ramona Humane Society Impound Billing
documents.purpose: To establish a consistent procedure for billing Ramona Humane Society (RHS) on impounded animals at the shelter.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Any Department of Animal Services staff who flags an impound record for billing and generates a bill on animals that should have been impounded at the Ramona Humane Society instead of DAS.
documents.supersedes: SOP Ramona Humane Society Impound Billing, effective July 11, 2017

definitions.def: Ramona Humane Society.

sections.heading: Monthly RHS Billing Procedure
steps: Any DAS supervisor who wants to bill RHS for impound fees shall enter RAMONA in the Extra1 field of the current Animal ID memo.
At the beginning of each month, the Chameleon administrator runs the Ramona billing report for the previous month based on the kennel outcome date.
The Chameleon administrator emails the report to the supervisor for review of report accuracy.
The supervisor removes RAMONA from the Extra1 field for any animal that should not be charged to RHS.
Once the supervisor approves the report, the Chameleon administrator emails Fiscal the report to initiate billing.

steps.step: Fiscal bills RHS for the following:
sub: Impound fee.
Board fee.

related.name: Memo Window (Animal ID memo, Extra1 field); Ramona billing report

documents.title: Statement of Intent to Comply (Spay-Neuter Release Form)
documents.purpose: To document the agreement of an owner or custodian redeeming an impounded unaltered dog or cat that the animal will be spayed or neutered as a condition of release, pursuant to Riverside County Ordinance 630, Section 12(f).
documents.authority: Riverside County Department of Animal Services; form executed by the owner or custodian under penalty of perjury and processed by the releasing clerk.
documents.scope: Owners or custodians redeeming an unaltered dog or cat impounded pursuant to state and/or local law, and the RCDAS staff who process the release.
documents.supersedes: None identified in source
references: Riverside County Ordinance 630, Section 12(f)
Riverside County, California Code (administrative citation and civil penalty provisions)

steps.step: When an unaltered dog or cat is impounded pursuant to state and/or local law, in addition to satisfying applicable requirements for the release of the animal (including but not limited to payment of impound fees pursuant to the Ordinance), the owner or custodian shall also do one of the following:
sub: Provide written proof of the dog or cat's prior sterilization, if the conditions cannot or do not make this assessment obvious to Department personnel.
Have the dog or cat spayed or neutered by a Department veterinarian at the expense of the owner or custodian. Such expense may include additional fees due to extraordinary care required.

Arrange for another licensed veterinarian to spay or neuter the animal. The veterinarian or owner shall return to the Department, within ten (10) business days, a statement confirming that the dog or cat has been spayed or neutered.

sections.heading: Owner Declaration and Consequences of Non-Compliance
steps: The owner or custodian declares under penalty of perjury that they are the owner or custodian of the animal described on the form, which was impounded by the Department of Animal Services.

By signing, the owner or custodian agrees that the animal is released on the condition that it will be spayed or neutered at their cost, and that a certificate of sterility signed by a licensed veterinarian will be provided to the Department within ten (10) business days from the release date.

Failure to provide the certificate of sterility within the required time period will result in the revoking of the unaltered license without refund of license and/or late fees, and will result in the issuance of an administrative citation, which shall include a monetary civil penalty in accordance with the Riverside County, California Code.

The owner or custodian declares under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct and that they will comply with the conditions of the animal's release.

The clerk processing the release records the animal information block (Animal ID, name, type, age, breed, color, date of birth, sex, weight, pickup date, microchip number, out by), the owner's or custodian's name, driver's license number, signature, date, and the clerk's identification on the form.

related.name: Statement of Intent to Comply form

revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Form language unchanged; customer personal information in the source example removed; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Intra-Department Transfer of Mail with Money
documents.purpose: To establish a procedure for mail with cash or check or other monetary instruments that has been opened by an employee other than the one for whom it is intended, and how to properly distribute it to the appropriate person for processing.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to all personnel in the Department of Animal Services who handle mail.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-23, revised from March 7, 2017; September 29, 2009; April 19, 2007
references: Auditor Controller's Office requirements
DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling

definitions.def: The form (Attachment A) used to log cash, checks, or other monetary instruments received by mail for accountability of transfer.

sections.heading: Distribution of Opened Mail
steps: Log in all mail that is opened and determined to have cash or check or other monetary instruments inside for payment of services with the Department of Animal Services.
Distribute the logged mail to the processing group or person that helps ensure compliance with the Auditor Controller's Office requirements.
This process helps ensure proper processing of payments and assists the employee in transfer verification.

sections.heading: Daily Mail Batch Slip
steps: Log all cash, checks, or other monetary instruments received into the Daily Mail Batch Slip (Attachment A) for accountability of transfer before submitting to the appropriate person.
The employee who opens the mail must sign the slip before any transfer takes place.
Upon completion of the Daily Mail Batch Slip, a second person must verify the information and sign the slip.

sections.heading: Acceptance of Mail Transfer
steps: After the Daily Mail Batch Slip is verified, the designee transfers the mail along with the form to the appropriate division person.
The designated person receiving the transfer verifies and signs the Daily Mail Batch Slip confirming that all cash, checks, or other monetary instruments have been transferred to them.

sections.heading: Reconciliation
steps: At the end of shift, reconcile the cash, checks, or other monetary instruments along with the Daily Mail Batch Slip and drop them in the safe for deposit per cash handling Policy DAS 000-22.
Attach the Daily Mail Batch Slip to the Daily Deposit for deposit tracking and accountability.

appendices: Appendix A: Daily Mail Batch Slip (form, attached separately)

revisions.author: RCDAS

documents.searchText: to establish a procedure for mail with cash or check or other monetary instruments that has been opened by an employee other than the one for whom it is intended, and how to properly distribute it to the appropriate person for processing. this procedure is applicable to all personnel in the department of animal services who handle mail. director of animal services daily mail batch slip the form (attachment a) used to log cash, checks, or other monetary instruments received by mail for accountability of transfer. distribution of opened mail log in all mail that is opened and determined to have cash or check or other monetary instruments inside for payment of services with the department of animal services. distribute the logged mail to the processing group or person that helps ensure compliance with the auditor controller's office requirements. this process helps ensure proper processing of payments and assists the employee in transfer verification. daily mail batch slip log all cash, checks, or other monetary instruments received into the daily mail batch slip (attachment a) for accountability of transfer before submitting to the appropriate person. the employee who opens the mail must sign the slip before any transfer takes place. upon completion of the daily mail batch slip, a second person must verify the information and sign the slip. acceptance of mail transfer after the daily mail batch slip is verified, the designee transfers the mail along with the form to the appropriate division person. the designated person receiving the transfer verifies and signs the daily mail batch slip confirming that all cash, checks, or other monetary instruments have been transferred to them. reconciliation at the end of shift, reconcile the cash, checks, or other monetary instruments along with the daily mail batch slip and drop them in the safe for deposit per cash handling policy das 000-22. attach the daily mail batch slip to the daily deposit for deposit tracking and accountability. auditor controller's office requirements das policy 000-22, cash handling

documents.title: Animal Foster Care Program
documents.purpose: This Standard Operating Procedure establishes the Animal Foster Care Program for the Riverside County Department of Animal Services (RCDAS). The program creates a structured pathway for volunteers and employees to temporarily care for animals outside of shelter facilities, reducing shelter population, improving animal welfare outcomes, and increasing the save rate for animals who require more time or a home environment to become adoption-ready.
documents.authority: The program operates under California Food and Agriculture Code Section 17005, which authorizes shelters to facilitate the temporary placement of animals in foster homes. The Shelter Operations Chief or designee, in coordination with a shelter veterinarian or designee, holds authority to approve animals for foster placement and to determine eligibility. The Foster Care Coordinator or designee is responsible for approving and managing all foster caregiver applications and agreements.
documents.scope: This procedure applies to all RCDAS staff, volunteers, and approved foster caregivers involved in the temporary placement of shelter animals. It covers all animal species impounded at a Riverside County shelter facility. Animals involved in active field services cases or investigations require individual evaluation before being considered for foster placement and may require management approval.
documents.supersedes: SOP 000-31, effective October 7, 2009; revised August 27, 2020
references: California Food and Agriculture Code Section 17005
DAS Policy 000-36, Euthanasia Policy
SOP 000-03, Outcoming Animals in Chameleon
WeeCare Foster Policy

definitions.def: Temporary placement of a shelter animal in an approved private home for the purpose of improving health, behavior, socialization, or adoption readiness.
A kitten or puppy less than eight weeks of age that is not legally available for adoption under California law.
The RCDAS staff member responsible for administering the foster program, approving applications, and matching animals to foster caregivers.
RCDAS emergency foster protocol applicable to time-sensitive or urgent foster placements.
The RCDAS shelter management software system used to document all animal records and foster transactions.

sections.heading: Animal Eligibility for Foster

steps.step: Consider any animal impounded at a Riverside County shelter facility for foster placement, including:
sub: Underage animals (kittens and puppies under eight weeks of age) that are not yet legally available for adoption
Animals with medical or behavioral conditions that will benefit from individualized home care
Healthy adult animals that would benefit from a home environment to improve adoption prospects

steps: Complete the Protocol for Identification of "Needs Foster" Animals to flag eligible animals in the shelter system.
Evaluate animals involved in active investigations on a case-by-case basis before foster placement is considered.

sections.heading: Veterinary and Operations Review
steps: Before any animal leaves the shelter for foster placement, the Shelter Operations Chief or designee and a shelter veterinarian or designee must evaluate the animal and confirm its suitability for fostering.
Document this review in Chameleon.

sections.heading: Volunteer Foster Caregiver Enrollment
steps: All volunteer foster caregivers must be approved by the Foster Care Coordinator or another approved staff member.
Complete the online orientation at www.rcdas.org.
Submit the Animal Foster Application through the online application portal.
Await review and approval by the Foster Care Coordinator, who will research the applicant in Chameleon.
Receive notification of approval status; approved applicants begin receiving notifications of animals available for foster.

sections.heading: Employee Foster Enrollment
steps: Any approved RCDAS employee may participate in the foster program.
Submit the Animal Foster Application (Attachment A) and receive approval through the direct supervisor and shelter manager.
Contact the Foster Care Coordinator to complete the Animal Foster Caretaker Agreement (Attachment B).
Ensure the Agreement is signed and all notes are entered into Chameleon before the animal leaves the facility.
Employee foster caregivers receive first priority to adopt animals in their care at the conclusion of the foster period. All standard adoption fees and legal limits apply.
Emergency foster care placements must follow the WeeCare Foster Policy.

sections.heading: Foster Placement Documentation

steps.step: Complete all foster program paperwork before an animal is removed from RCDAS premises. Required documentation includes:
sub: Completed Animal Foster Caretaker Agreement, filled out by the Foster Care Coordinator
Chameleon Kennel Window completion per Attachment C and SOP 000-03
Veterinary clearance documented in Chameleon

steps: Direct any questions or exceptional circumstances that arise during the placement process to the Foster Care Coordinator or designee and escalate to shelter management as needed.

sections.heading: Adoption of Foster Animals
steps: Give priority consideration to foster caregivers who choose to adopt the animal in their care.

steps.step: Apply the following terms to all foster-to-adopt situations:
sub: Foster caregivers receive a 50% discount on the standard adoption fee
All applicable fees and legal limits on the number of animals remain in effect
Foster caregivers are encouraged to identify potential adopters in their network for animals they are unable to adopt themselves
Foster caregivers may extend the employee or foster discount to adopters they recruit

related.name: www.rcdas.org (online foster orientation and application portal)

appendices: Appendix A: Animal Foster Application (attached separately)
Appendix B: Animal Foster Caretaker Agreement (attached separately)
Appendix C: Chameleon Kennel Window Completion Guide (attached separately)
Appendix: 000-31 Animal Foster Care Program Attachment D (attached separately)
Appendix: 000-31 Animal Foster Care Program Attachment E (attached separately)
Appendix: 000-31 Animal Foster Care Program Attachment F (attached separately)
Appendix: 000-31 Animal Foster Care Program Attachment Neonate (attached separately)

documents.title: Adoption Process
documents.purpose: This SOP establishes the adoption program procedures for all RCDAS campuses. The adoption program encompasses adoption counselor training, customer service flow, adoption applications and counseling interviews, animal observation and interaction, hold procedures, spay/neuter compliance, and post-adoption follow-up. The goal of the adoption program is to place shelter animals into suitable homes through a conversation-based, welcoming process that matches animals with adopters based on lifestyle, experience, and the individual animal's needs. All adoptable animals, and treatable animals when appropriate, are made available for adoption or transfer to approved rescue and adoption partners.
documents.authority: The Director of Animal Services establishes adoption program policies. Shelter supervisors oversee daily adoption operations and confirm adoption denials. Adoption Counselors carry out adoption counseling, animal matching, and hold procedures under supervisory guidance.
documents.scope: This procedure applies to all RCDAS staff and volunteers involved in the adoption process at all campuses, including Animal Services Counselors, Animal Care Technicians, Customer Service Representatives, front office personnel, veterinary staff, and volunteers trained to assist with adoptions.
documents.supersedes: Draft successor to Policy 300-06, Adoption of Shelter Animals (October 8, 2009)
references: California Food and Agricultural Code Section 17005 (Adoptable and Treatable Animals Policy)
California Food and Agricultural Code Sections 30503 and 31751.3 (Spay/Neuter Before Adoption)
California Food and Agricultural Code Sections 31108, 31752, 31753, 31754 (Holding Periods)
California Civil Code Section 1834.4 (Statewide Adoption Preference)
California Penal Code Section 599d (Statewide Adoption Policy)
California Food and Agricultural Code Section 32003 (Required Shelter Records)
ASV Guidelines for Standards of Care in Animal Shelters, 2nd Edition (2022), Sections 2 and 10
ASPCA Conversation-Based Adoption Counseling Guidelines
RCDAS Policy 402, Euthanasia Policy
Riverside County Ordinance No. 630

definitions.def: An animal that is eight weeks of age or older, healthy, and free of behavioral or temperamental characteristics that would pose a health or safety risk to the community. Adoptable animals are suitable for placement as companion animals (California Food and Agricultural Code Section 17005).

An animal that is not currently adoptable but could become adoptable with reasonable effort. Treatable animals have a manageable behavioral issue or medical condition that is typically responsive to behavior modification or medical treatment. See RCDAS Policy 402, Euthanasia Policy, for the expanded definition.
A trained RCDAS staff member who conducts adoption interviews, matches animals with adopters, provides education on pet care and transition, and facilitates the adoption process.
A form (Attachment A) completed when an owner relinquishes an animal, capturing the animal's history, behavior, health information, and household context to support future placement.
A form (Attachment B) completed by prospective adopters that captures lifestyle information, housing, household composition, and experience with animals to support the matching process.
A reservation placed on an animal that is not yet available for adoption, giving a prospective adopter an appointment to visit the animal on the day it becomes available.
A colored collar placed on an adopted animal to indicate that the animal has been adopted and to identify the adopter.

An adoption approach that uses open-ended questions and discussion to understand an adopter's lifestyle and match them with a suitable animal. The counselor focuses on education and successful placement rather than screening applicants against a checklist of requirements.

sections.heading: Education and Training of Adoption Personnel
steps: All Animal Services Counselors complete training during their probationary period covering the full adoption counseling process, including customer flow, required paperwork, and adoption fees and services.

sections.heading: Customer Flow
steps: Customer service personnel or volunteers greet all visitors as they enter. Signage directs visitors to adoptable animals and to Animal Services Counselors for assistance.

Animal Services Counselors are assigned throughout the adoption areas and are responsible for proactively engaging visitors, answering questions, facilitating introductions, and assisting with pet selection. Visitors are not assigned to a specific counselor and may seek assistance from any available Animal Services Counselor.

Visitors may view animals before completing an Adoption Application. Animal Services Counselors are available throughout the facility to provide information, discuss animal behavior and needs, and assist visitors in identifying potential matches.

sections.heading: Adoption Counseling and Matching
steps: When a visitor is ready to discuss a potential adoption, an Animal Services Counselor assists them. The adoption process takes place in a pleasant and welcoming atmosphere that respects the adopter's experience and knowledge.

Use a conversational approach with open-ended questions to understand the adopter's lifestyle, household, expectations, and experience with animals. The interview is a discussion focused on finding a successful match, and the Adoption Counseling Checklist (Attachment D) is used to guide the conversation and provide education, rather than as a rigid set of requirements the applicant must pass.

steps.step: If the visitor has identified a specific animal, check the Chameleon system to confirm the animal is available for adoption and free of holds, and review the following information to support the matching discussion:
sub: Owner Surrender Animal Profile (if applicable)
Behavioral observation notes
Health information notes

steps: If the animal is available, the Animal Services Counselor and the visitor view the animal together. If the animal is not yet available, the Animal Services Counselor explains the hold process.
If the visitor has not selected an animal, assist them in viewing animals that are identified as good matches based on the interview.
The visitor completes the Adoption Application if they have not already done so. All animal information on the application is completed before the adoption can be processed.

The Animal Services Counselor makes the final decision to approve the adoption. The counselor focuses on finding a way to make the adoption succeed and looks for solutions rather than reasons to deny.

sections.heading: Hold Process
steps: If an animal is not yet available for adoption, a hold may be placed to reserve a time for the prospective adopter to visit once the animal becomes available.
An Adoption Counselor or Animal Care Technician places the hold by entering a "possible adoption note" in the Chameleon system.
The prospective adopter receives a Hold Slip (Attachment C) indicating the date and a one-hour appointment window on the day the animal becomes available.
Mark the cage card to indicate how many holds are on file so that other staff, volunteers, and prospective adopters are informed.
Each animal may have up to three holds placed on it.

If the first-hold adopter's application is denied or the adopter does not return during their appointment, review the second hold. Repeat the process for the third hold. If no hold results in an adoption, the animal becomes available on a first-come, first-served basis.
Place holds only on animals that are not yet available for adoption. Animals that are currently available are adopted on a first-come, first-served basis.

sections.heading: Completing the Adoption: Animal Already Spayed/Neutered
steps: When the adoption is approved and the animal has already been spayed or neutered, the Animal Services Counselor or trained personnel/volunteer brings the animal to the veterinary team for an examination.
Veterinary personnel provide any necessary medications, vaccinations, and microchipping.

The Animal Services Counselor or trained personnel/volunteer brings the completed application to front office personnel. The application is signed by the assisting Animal Services Counselor or designated staff.
Complete all animal information on the Adoption Application (Attachment B) before front counter personnel process the adoption.
The adopter completes payment at the front counter.
The animal leaves with the new guardian at this time.

documents.title: Adoption on Contract Business Flow Process
documents.purpose: To provide a consistent process for the follow-up of mandatory spay or neuter requirements for dogs or cats adopted through the Department of Animal Services.
documents.authority: In compliance with the California Food and Agriculture Code, no public shelter or rescue may release a cat or dog that has not been spayed or neutered unless deemed medically unfit by veterinary staff. The adopter must provide proof of sterilization within 30 business days. This SOP applies regardless of the adopter's jurisdiction.
documents.scope: This procedure applies to all Department of Animal Services staff responsible for tracking and enforcing adoption contracts that involve pending spay/neuter procedures. It includes shelter supervisors and any staff using the shelter software system.
documents.supersedes: SOP, Adoption on Contract Business Flow Process, effective August 31, 2016
references: California Food and Agriculture Code Sections 30503, 30804.7, 31751.3(a)(1)
Riverside County Ordinance 630.16, Section 22
SOP: Initiating Administrative Citations
SOP: Initiating State Spay/Neuter Violation Administrative Citation

definitions.def: Notification sent to the adopter requesting proof of the spay/neuter procedure.

sections.heading: Spay/Neuter Contract Reminder
steps: On the 15th business day after adoption, a shelter software postmaster job sends notification emails to shelter supervisors.
The Supervisor or their designee runs the Spay/Neuter Contract Reminder letter from the shelter software reports dropdown.
Mail or email the letter to the adopter.

sections.heading: Verifying Contract Details in the Shelter Software
steps: Open the Tag/Link window.
Enter the animal ID in the tag/link field.
Press F12 to locate the relevant record with CONTRACT in the cert no field.
Check notes, animal window, and treatment screens for proof of sterilization.
Press F3 to open the reports menu.
Select and display the Spay/Neuter Contract Reminder Letter.
Ensure animal and owner information match the tag/link record.
Print or email the letter.

steps.step: Create a memo animal ID note with:
sub: Date sent
"Mailed s/n contract reminder letter with a due date of mm/dd/yy"
User ID

sections.heading: Refund Process
steps: If proof of sterilization is provided within 30 business days, issue a full refund of the spay/neuter deposit.
If proof is delayed beyond 30 business days, the deposit is forfeited.

sections.heading: Citation Process
steps: On the 31st day of the month or the first day of the following month, a second Chameleon postmaster job notifies supervisors.
Initiate an administrative citation per the relevant SOP (Initiating Administrative Citations; Initiating State Spay/Neuter Violation Administrative Citation).
Mail or email the citation to the adopter.

related.name: SOP: Initiating State Spay/Neuter Violation Administrative Citation
Tag/Link, Animal, Treatment, Memo, Reports (Spay/Neuter Contract Reminder Letter), Postmaster jobs

documents.title: Protocol for the Identification of Needs Rescue Animals
documents.purpose: To provide consistent guidelines for identifying impounded animals that are in need of rescue and may have medical and/or behavioral conditions that do not make them suitable for adoption by the general public but may find forever homes with the assistance of rescue organizations.
documents.authority: Only approved designated staff members can assign a NEEDS RESC status to an animal.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable only to the designated DAS employees charged with selecting and identifying NEEDS RESC animals.
documents.supersedes: SOP Protocol for the Identification of Needs Rescue Animals, effective March 25, 2018; revised from December 5, 2016, September 12, 2016, and August 17, 2015 versions (August 17, 2015 version absorbed)
references: Chameleon Kennel Window
Chameleon Animal Profile Window
PetHarbor

definitions.def: Animals in imminent need of rescue and in danger of being euthanized if not picked up by a rescue. Contact the rescue coordinator during business hours immediately to facilitate pickup if you are interested in a red tagged animal.

Animals that can no longer be kept in the shelter given their current medical or behavior condition. These animals need a rescue commitment immediately. Contact the rescue coordinator during business hours immediately to facilitate pickup. Those in the yellow category may become red based on their ongoing condition.

Animals that have met their legally mandated retention period but have been deemed medically or behaviorally unadoptable. Contact the rescue coordinator during business hours immediately to facilitate pickup. Those in the green category may become yellow or red based on their ongoing condition.

sections.heading: Designated Staff Authorized to Assign NEEDS RESC
steps: Western: Rescue Coordinator, Senior ACTs, Shelter Supervisor, or Shelter Chief.
San Jacinto Animal Campus: Rescue Coordinator, Senior ACT, Shelter Supervisor, or Shelter Chief.
Coachella Valley Animal Campus: Supervising ACT or Designee.

sections.heading: Identification and Notification
steps: Designated staff members shall enter a brief description of why the animal is flagged for needs rescue (medical or behavior) in the comment field of the Chameleon animal profile window.
Staff conducting daily population rounds shall email the designated staff above using rescue@rivco.org with any animal whose adoptable status changes due to medical.

Designated staff will further identify, through the Chameleon kennel window Extra2 field, which animals are in need of rescue prior to being placed on the euthanasia list, using the Red, Yellow, and Green categories defined above.
Any staff who has concerns about a particular animal can email the rescue team directly.
The list of NEEDS RESC animals will be posted on PetHarbor and refreshed every half hour (30 minutes).
A Chameleon postmaster notification to active DAS adoption partners (members of Chameleon group G002144) is emailed twice a day, at 9:00 AM and at 6:00 PM.

sections.heading: Chameleon Procedure
steps: Start in the Chameleon kennel window.
Make sure that the kennel record displayed does not have an outcome date.
Enter NEEDS RESC in the kennel status field.

steps.step: In the Sub-status field, choose:
sub: BEHAVIOR
MEDICAL

steps.step: In the Extra2 field, choose:
sub: RED
YELLOW
GREEN

steps.step: Mouse-click on the animal profile icon.
sub: Enter a brief description of the reason why the animal was flagged as needs rescue (medical or behavior; red, yellow, or green).
Store (F9) the animal profile record.

sections.heading: Reconciliation
steps: Open Chameleon.
Left-mouse click on Reports.
Scroll down to Kennel Management Reports.
Choose Observed Behavior Report.
Enter the shelter location, kennel review date or kennel due out date, and status NEEDS RESC.
Choose animal type.
Press OK.

campusVariations.variation: NEEDS RESC may be assigned by the Rescue Coordinator, Senior ACTs, Shelter Supervisor, or Shelter Chief.
NEEDS RESC may be assigned by the Rescue Coordinator, Senior ACT, Shelter Supervisor, or Shelter Chief.
NEEDS RESC may be assigned by the Supervising ACT or Designee.

documents.title: Short Term Dog Foster Outings
documents.purpose: Short term dog foster outings give a shelter dog a break from the stress of the shelter and an opportunity for new experiences, and provide valuable information about the dog's personality, behavior, and needs, helping adopters or rescuers make more informed decisions. The shelter offers different options for short term outings.
documents.authority: The foster coordinator confirms dog availability and schedules outings.
documents.scope: Members of the public and volunteers participating in short term dog foster outings, and the staff who support them.
documents.supersedes: Short Term Foster Trips Outing SOP, updated December 30, 2024
references: RCDAS website (www.rcdas.org) short term foster presentation and videos
Short Term Foster Agreement
Foster Agreement

sections.heading: Outing Types
steps: Day trip.
Weekend trip.
Longer outings.

sections.heading: Registering
steps: Visit the RCDAS website at www.rcdas.org to review the short online presentation and video.
Watch all the videos on dog behavior online.
Fill out a short term foster agreement for each dog you take out.

sections.heading: Requirements

steps.step: You must be available to pick up and return the dog during regular business hours:
sub: Monday through Friday, 10 AM to 4 PM (3:30 PM drop off).
Wednesdays, 10 AM to 7 PM (6:30 PM drop off).

steps: Keep dogs on a leash at all times.
Do not take dogs to any dog park or open spaces, and do not let them run freely.
If the dog you have taken is too much for you to handle, bring the dog back to the shelter to avoid any unnecessary situations that can be avoided.
Always have the Adopt Me vest on the dog in public places unless you intend to adopt.

sections.heading: Next Steps
steps: If you are interested in taking a specific dog for an outing, contact the foster coordinator at foster@rivco.org to verify that the dog is available.
Confirm the pickup date and time with the foster coordinator.
Once you have confirmed with staff that you will be picking up a specific dog, staff will make a note in the dog's record concerning where it is going and when it will be returning.
During your scheduled appointment, check in at the front desk and speak to the customer service team.
The customer service team can direct you to the kennel the pet is in, and a kennel staff member can assist with a quick meet and greet.

If the meet and greet goes well and you believe it is a good fit, the staff member will take the dog to ensure it is up to date on vaccines and receives a microchip while you work on foster paperwork.
The front counter will need to see a valid ID, record your driver's license number, confirm your current address and contact information, and have you sign a Foster Agreement if you have not already.

sections.heading: Supplies
steps: Supplies are checked out with the dog, including a backpack with the correct size martingale collar, leash, a seat belt fastener for driving in the car, water bowl, poop bag dispenser, and Adopt Me vest.

sections.heading: Handling Guidance and Reminders
steps: Take time to introduce yourself to the dog you will be walking. The dog may be excitable. Be calm and assertive and try not to get the dog any more excited or playful than it already is.
If you feel that you cannot control the dog, return it and select a dog that you can safely handle.
Do not wrap the leash around your hand. It would be terrible for a dog to get loose, but much worse if the dog dragged you, the handler, into a dangerous traffic situation.
Walk your dog on a short leash. A long leash gives the dog more strength and leverage.

Be aware of other dogs, bicyclists, runners, and walkers that might cross your path and give them the right of way. Keep your distance from other animals and do not introduce shelter dogs to privately owned dogs.
Be visible. Wear bright colors so you can be easily seen by oncoming traffic. Cross safely: look both ways when you cross traffic and remember that the prey drive of any dog can kick in at any time.
Be aware of your surroundings and do not let the dog walk far in front of you or far behind you.
Do not use headphones or talk on a cell phone while walking the dog. Use all your senses to ensure that you, those around you, and the dog remain safe.
Pay attention to the dog's respiration rate and overall appearance and attitude.
Since many of the dogs have been relatively non-mobile for weeks or months, they may become fatigued quite quickly. Offer water regularly and take breaks periodically so the dog can catch his breath.
If you notice the dog acting strangely or you find yourself in a dangerous situation, do not hesitate to contact foster@rivco.org.
If there is an emergency, such as a person being bitten, call the main line at 951-358-7387 or the foster desk line at 951-358-7973.

sections.heading: Additional Tips
steps: Never leave the dog in a car.

If the ground is hot, so are the dog's feet. Check the temperature of the ground using the 7 Second Rule: if you cannot hold the back of your hand on the pavement without discomfort, it is too hot for the dog.

documents.title: Transfer of Animals to Partner Organizations
documents.purpose: This SOP establishes the criteria RCDAS uses to screen and approve partner organizations for animal transfers, and the standards that govern animal care during transport. Transfers to approved partners are a critical live-outcome pathway. Ensuring that partner organizations meet consistent standards protects the animals in RCDAS care, supports RCDAS's live release rate goals, and maintains accountability for every animal transferred out of RCDAS custody.
documents.authority: The RCDAS Director or designee holds authority to approve, suspend, or revoke partner organization status. Shelter managers or designees are responsible for verifying that partner eligibility criteria are current before initiating any transfer. No animal may be transferred to an organization that has not received written approval from RCDAS management.
documents.scope: This procedure applies to all RCDAS staff involved in coordinating, approving, or executing animal transfers to rescue groups, humane societies, and municipal shelters. It covers both in-state and out-of-state transfers of any species in RCDAS custody.
documents.supersedes: New draft SOP (undated); overlaps with Rescue Procedure for the Transfer of Animals to Adoption Partner Organizations, effective June 5, 2017
references: California Food and Agriculture Code §§ 31752, 31754
California Health and Safety Code § 122290 (spay/neuter requirements)
Riverside County Ordinance No. 630
RCDAS Partner Organization Transfer Agreement

definitions.def: Any rescue organization, humane society, or municipal shelter that has completed the RCDAS approval process and holds a current signed Transfer Agreement with RCDAS, authorizing receipt of animals from RCDAS custody.
The legal transfer of ownership and care responsibility for an RCDAS animal to an approved partner organization.

A written commitment by the partner organization that an animal received from RCDAS will be released as a live outcome or that RCDAS will be notified with sufficient time to reclaim the animal before any euthanasia decision is made.

sections.heading: Partner Organization Approval

steps.step: Before any transfer may occur, a prospective partner organization must complete the RCDAS application process and receive written approval. To qualify, the organization must meet all of the following criteria, verified in writing prior to approval:
sub: The organization is a rescue group, humane society, or municipal shelter whose primary mission is the placement of animals in permanent adoptive homes or long-term rescue settings.

The organization provides a written live outcome guarantee: either the animal will be released as a live outcome, or RCDAS will be notified at least 48 hours before any scheduled euthanasia and given the opportunity to reclaim the animal at no cost.
The organization does not make animals in its care available for research purposes.
The organization is in compliance with all applicable local, state, and federal laws governing animal cruelty, public health, and animal care.
The organization submits a signed attestation confirming compliance with criteria 1 through 4 (Attachment A).
The organization provides up to three references upon RCDAS request, which may include a veterinary reference.

steps: Approved organizations must sign the RCDAS Transfer Agreement (Attachment B) before the first transfer is executed.
Approvals are reviewed annually. RCDAS may suspend or revoke partner status at any time if an organization falls out of compliance.

sections.heading: Additional Requirements for Out-of-State Transfers
steps: All animals must be spayed or neutered prior to leaving RCDAS, or the receiving organization must provide a written agreement to sterilize the animal before it is placed for adoption.

RCDAS staff contact the receiving state's veterinarian or equivalent regulatory authority to confirm current interstate transport requirements, including health certificate requirements, species restrictions, and any applicable permits. Staff document this verification in the animal's Chameleon record and on the Out-of-State Transport Checklist (Attachment C).
A current health certificate issued by a licensed veterinarian is required for all out-of-state transfers in accordance with the destination state's requirements.

steps.step: Before any animal is transferred to a partner organization, staff verify and document the following in Chameleon:
sub: The animal has completed its legally required stray hold period, if applicable.
The animal has received intake vaccinations and a health screening. Any known medical conditions are disclosed to the receiving organization in writing.
The partner organization's approval status is current and their signed Transfer Agreement is on file.
The transfer is recorded in Chameleon as a live outcome under the appropriate transfer subtype before the animal leaves the facility.

sections.heading: Transport Standards
steps: Transport each animal in a clean, appropriately sized, and secure enclosure. Animals are not doubled up in transport enclosures unless they are a confirmed bonded pair or same-litter juveniles.
Transport vehicles are temperature-controlled, and animals are not left unattended in vehicles in conditions that could cause heat stress, hypothermia, or other environmental injury.
Animals have access to water during transport. Food is provided for transports exceeding four hours.
All transport documentation, including health records and the signed Transfer Agreement, accompanies the animal and is provided to the receiving organization at the time of transfer.

sections.heading: Documentation and Record Keeping
steps: Document all transfers fully in Chameleon before the animal leaves the facility. Required documentation includes the receiving organization's name, the transfer date, the staff member who authorized and processed the transfer, and confirmation that all pre-transfer requirements were met.
Retain a copy of the signed Transfer Agreement and any health documentation provided to the receiving organization in RCDAS records.

Partner Organization Application and Attestation (Attachment A)
Rescue Procedure for the Transfer of Animals to Adoption Partner Organizations (Rescues)

Appendix B: RCDAS Transfer Agreement (attached separately)
Appendix C: Out-of-State Transport Checklist (attached separately)
