documents.title: Transferring Ownership of a Licensed Dog
documents.purpose: To establish a consistent process for transferring ownership of a licensed dog as required by the Riverside County Codified Ordinance.
documents.authority: The Health Officer or his designee receives written notification of transfers of ownership.
documents.scope: DAS staff processing license ownership transfers and replacement license tags.
documents.supersedes: SOP Transfer and Replace A License Tag in Chameleon (Transferring Ownership of a Licensed Dog), effective May 24, 2008
references: Policy 100-1, Pet Licensing
Riverside County Codified Ordinance
P&P General Chameleon Information

sections.heading: Requirements Prior to Transferring Ownership of a Licensed Dog
steps: The new owner must notify the Health Officer or his designee in writing of the transfer of ownership within thirty (30) days.
Attach the request to Chameleon through the Chamcam twain device.
The cost is six dollars ($6).
Give a new tag to the new owner.
The new license record must have the same tag type, tag expiration date, and vaccination expiration date as the old license record.
Store (F9) the new license record containing the new owner.
The old license record containing the previous owner must be valid and current (not expired or temporary).
The receipt window documents the LIC TRANS FEE and LIC DUP transactions.
The old license record must have a status of GAVE AWAY and is updated (F8).

sections.heading: Replacing a License Tag

steps.step: Go to the Chameleon Tag/Link Window (refer to P&P General Chameleon Information).
sub: Type the entire license number in the tag number field. Format LYY-######.
Press the F12 key or click on the magnifying glass icon to find the record.
Verify the following information: the correct license record is displayed, the license is not expired, and the vaccination is current.

steps: Prior to creating a new Person ID for the new owner, refer to General Chameleon Information on how to prevent duplicate records.
Use the same Animal ID.

steps.step: Issue the new tag number.
sub: Type in the new tag number, keeping the LYY the same.
Verify the tag jurisdiction.
Do not change the following information: tag type, tag price, or tag expiration date.
Press the F5 key or type in the date of the transaction.
Delete the receipt number currently displayed.
Store the record or press the F9 function key.
Click on the receipt number field.
Press the F4 key to pop up the receipt window and apply the transaction as described below.
Print the receipt.
Threefold the receipt so the name and address of the owner is displayed through a window envelope.
Staple the new tag to the folded receipt.
Enclose in an envelope and mail to the pet owner.
Press the enter key to bring the receipt information back to the Tag/Link window.

steps.step: Apply the receipt transaction in the receipt window.
sub: Double click on the defaulted license item code.
Change the item code to LIC TRANS FEE.
Verify the correct fee is charged by clicking on the $ icon.
Add the item code and fees to the line item of the receipt window by pressing the + icon or ALT+A.
Repeat the first step (double click on the defaulted license item code).
Change the item code to LIC DUP.
Override the fee displayed to zero ($0).
Add the item code and fees to the line item of the receipt window by pressing the + icon or ALT+A.
Click once on the defaulted license item code to highlight the line item.
Remove the line item from the grid of the receipt window by pressing the - icon.
Enter the amount paid based on the type of payment.
Press the F9 key to store the receipt.

steps.step: Process the old tag record.
sub: Copy the Animal ID, then clear (F11) the screen.
Paste the Animal ID into the AID field, then find (F12) the records.
On the grid to the right side of the Tag/Link window, double click on the previous owner's record to display it on the window.
Change the old tag record status from RENEWED to GAVE AWAY.
Update the record or press the F8 function key.

steps: The list box of the Animal View record should show two license numbers and Person IDs, signifying multiple ownerships past and present.

related.name: Written letter of request from the new pet owner (signed)

documents.title: Completion of the Chameleon Kennel Window
documents.flag: Numbered (=Policy per rule) but reads as a step-by-step protocol
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure for the completion of the Chameleon Kennel Window to document and track state mandated activities.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all personnel responsible for using the Chameleon Kennel Window.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-03, effective November 22, 2021; revised from May 20, 2021; August 21, 2019; May 28, 2019; April 3, 2019; January 13, 2017; April 20, 2016; April 13, 2016; April 7, 2016; July 17, 2015; April 13, 2015; January 28, 2015; September 17, 2014; April 28, 2014; December 29, 2011; August 10, 2008
references: California Food and Agriculture Code Section 32003, Record Keeping Requirements
DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Information

sections.heading: State Record Keeping Mandate

steps.step: Maintain accurate records on each animal taken up, medically treated, or impounded, as required by Food and Agriculture Code (F&A) 32003. The records shall include all of the following information and any other information required by the California Veterinary Medical Board:
sub: The date the animal was taken up, medically treated, euthanized, or impounded.
The circumstances under which the animal was taken up, medically treated, euthanized, or impounded.
The names of the personnel who took up, medically treated, euthanized, or impounded the animal.
A description of any medical treatment provided to the animal and the name of the veterinarian of record.
The final disposition of the animal, including the name of the person who euthanized the animal or the name and address of the adopting party.

steps: Retain these records for three years after the date the animal's impoundment ends. (Enacted 1998.)

Complete a kennel window for every animal impounded. This applies to all staff and volunteers employed by the department. Input shall be as accurate and detailed as possible to assist the public in locating lost animals. Careful attention is essential, as the information input on this screen may adversely affect the outcome of an animal and information published on the web through PetHarbor.

sections.heading: Kennel Window Fields (Animal and Location)
steps: Use the popup (F4) in the following fields (see Appendix A) and choose the appropriate selection.
Impound No: This is the unique impound number generated by Chameleon for each animal's individual kennel impound record.

steps.step: Litter No: Enter the mother's impound number on all litter mates of all litters.
sub: For a mother dog or cat with a litter (weaned or unweaned), create one kennel record (window) for the mother and one kennel record (window) for each individual in the litter, entering the mother's Impound No. in this field.
For a litter without its mother (weaned or unweaned), create one kennel record (window) for each individual in the litter. Leave the Impound No. field blank.
For all litters containing both deceased and live individuals, create one kennel impound (window) for all the animals and outcome the deceased animals with an outcome of DISPOSAL.

steps: Kennel No: Enter the appropriate location/kennel of the impounded animal. The kennel number is the basis for the shelter location posted on PetHarbor as well as on all statistical and billing reports. Campus-specific kennel number ranges are listed in the Campus Kennel Location Codes section below.

steps.step: Status: Enter the appropriate adoption status of the impounded animal.
sub: AVAILABLE: impounded animal has met retention period requirements (not applicable for Field). The picture and animal information shows on the shelter website under Adoptable Pets and disappears from Lost and Found Pets.
OWSUR AVAL: OTI animal has met retention period (not applicable for Field). The picture and animal information shows on the shelter website under Adoptable Pets.

KNOWN WAIT: live/dead animals with any owner information, including owner arrests (where animal is being held) and owner known quarantines. Requires an OWNER INFO memo and/or Tag/Link record. Use if the owner does not have a specific name, address, or phone number (such as for a rabies tag). Do not create Tag/Link if the owner does have a specific name, address, or phone number and cannot be located in the system. Create a Tag/Link and attach a memo type of OWNER INFO to the animal ID with all available information.
OW SUR EUTH: pets surrendered by owners requesting the service of euthanasia.
OW SUR WAIT: owner released live animals, including owner release quarantines that have not met the retention period (be sure to enter the animal's name in the animal window).
STRAY WAIT: live animals without any owner information. The picture and animal information shows on the shelter website under Lost and Found Pets.

UNAVAIL: dead animals without identification, stray quarantined live animals, live/dead animals for head test, live/dead police holds, live disaster holds. This status prevents the animal's picture and information from showing on the shelter website.
NEEDS RESC: animals flagged for rescue. The animal's picture and animal information is displayed in the Adoption Partners webpage of rcdas.org.
SURGERY: requires an intake subtype of FETUS. Specifically used to document animals that were euthanized in the uterus when the mother is spayed.

steps.step: Sub Status: Enter the applicable sub status.
sub: Enter BILLING to send a bill to constituent.
Enter MEDICAL or BEHAVIOR to specify the kennel status NEEDS RESC.
Enter CCP to flag specific cats for the community cat program.

steps: Tag: Enter the link, license number, or microchip information from the Tag/Link window, if known. For example, L07-123456 would indicate a license tag number, and 123456789 would indicate a microchip number.

steps.step: Other ID: Enter other identification of the animal if applicable.
sub: Blue collar tag sometimes placed on dogs upon arrival at shelter.
Secondary ID number shall be applied to dogs; cats get the AID on a neck band. Dogs in the clinic may not need the secondary ID number.
Confiscation number used in the field during a raid to identify each animal until a Chameleon kennel record can be stored.

steps: Activity No: Enter the activity number if the animal impounded was the result of an activity initiated impound. If more than one animal was impounded on one activity call, all animals of that activity will have the activity number on their kennel impound record.
Field next to Activity No: Used for an activity sequence other than one.
Animal ID: Create an animal ID and enter it here. To create the bite icon in the Animal Window if the animal was involved in a bite/scratch, enter a Y in the bite box.
Behavior Assessment: Used by the behavior team to document behavior scores of impounded animals.

steps.step: Extra2: Based on the kennel status NEEDS RESC, this field requires one of the following to be entered:
sub: GREEN: animals that have met their legally mandated retention period but have been deemed medically or behaviorally unadoptable. Those in the green category may become yellow or red, or may be euthanized based on their ongoing condition.

YELLOW: animals that can no longer be kept in the shelter given their current medical or behavior condition. These animals need a rescue commitment immediately. Those in the yellow category may become red or may be euthanized based on their ongoing condition.
RED: animals in imminent need of rescue and in danger of being euthanized if not picked up by a rescue.

steps.step: Blythe Shelter (B):
sub: B D 01 to B D 34: dogs.
B C 01 to B C 34: cats.
B DQ 16 to B DQ 19: quarantined dogs.
B L 01 to B L 05: livestock pens.
B O 01 to B O 10: outside cages for overflow dogs.
B FOSTER: animals in foster homes.
B FREEZER: depository for dead animals except for rabies testing and necropsies.
B MORGUE: rabies testing and necropsy storage.
B RECEIVING: receiving.

steps.step: Riverside Shelter (R):
sub: R RECEIVING, R 007, and RR 001 to RR 021: receiving.
R 008 to R 011: puppies.
R 012 to R 055: stray wait dogs in Kennel A.
R 056 to R 087: available dogs in Kennel B.
R 088 to R 105: dog quarantine.
R 106 to R 135: stray wait dogs in Kennel D.
R TREAT 1 to R TREAT 9: treatment room, for animals receiving treatments.
R 309, R 310, R 314, R 315: outside receiving kennels used for fowl.
R OUT 01 to R OUT 20: outside runs in back of shelter, dogs and swine.
R AN 01 to R AN 35: available now animals in available now room and public hall.
R 200 to R 203: dogs in maternity and nursing.
R 204 to R 229: dog isolation.
R EUTH: used only for pets in the euthanasia room.
R OT 1, R 301 to R 308: cat isolation.
R 300, R 311, R 312, R 313, R 316, R 451, R 452, R 453, RQ 01 to RQ 42, and RCR 001 to RCR 013: cat receiving and cat quarantine.
R 321 to R 360 and R 411 to R 450: cats.
R 361 to R 380: cat maternity.
R 381 to R 410: kittens.
R GROOMING: foster being held in Room 70 until processed.
R AN 01 to R AN 30: small animals that are available now (located in the available now room and public hall).
R 319 to R 320: Shoreline Kennels (see Supervising ACT).
R 600 to R 684: rescue kennel. Animals housed in this pod are waiting for rescue transports or held until a rescue is found.
R LOT: Do Not Use.
R CLINIC: self-explanatory.
R EVENT: pets going to offsite adoption events and returning to the shelter if not adopted on the same day. This requires an Animal ID memo of the event location.
R PETSMART: animal transferred to PetSmart and retained at PetSmart for adoption. The animal is not returned to the shelter.
R FOSTER: only used for approved fostered animals by the Foster Coordinator.
R FIELD: used by Field Officer impounds. Upon delivery to the shelter, this Kennel No is then changed to the actual cage where the animal is impounded.
R FREEZER: depository for dead animals except for rabies testing and necropsies.
R LAB: specimen at the lab.
R MORGUE: rabies testing and necropsy storage.
R VET: injured impounded animals impounded at an off-site veterinary office.
R HOME: pets impounded by Animal Control Officers (ACO) and returned to their owners in the field without getting to the shelter.
R CONF: used for confiscated animals.
R BEHAVIOR: animals moved to behavior room for SAFER and behavior testing.

steps.step: CVAC Thousand Palms Shelter (T):
sub: T 100 to T 128: impounded puppies.
T 300 to T 386 and T 412 to T 415: impounded cats.
T 211 to T 220: dogs in Dog Court Yard.
T 129 to T 134: cats in condos located in the Adoption Galley.
T 001 to T 030: dogs in Kennel A.
T 050 to T 079: dogs in Kennel B.
T 400 to T 411: cat isolation.
T N001 to T N020: cats maternity ward/kitten nursery (CVAC).
TCQ 01 to TCQ 28: cat quarantine.
TE 1 to TE 4: treatment/exam room.
TDQ 01 to TDQ 11: dog quarantine.
T 200 to T 210: dog isolation.
TR 01 to TR 24: receiving for all animals.
TR 25 to TR 50: receiving hallway for all animals.
T EVENT: pets going to offsite adoption events and returning to the shelter if not adopted on the same day. This requires an Animal ID memo of the event location.
T GROOMING: used when impounded animals are moved to the grooming room. This is a temporary kennel until the animal goes back to its regular kennel.
T PETSMART: pets going to PetSmart that are not returning to the shelter. This requires an outcome and a memo of the PetSmart location.
T FIELD: used only for impounds by Field Officers. Upon delivery to the shelter, this Kennel No is then changed to the actual cage where the animal is impounded.
T FOSTER: only used for approved fostered animals by the Foster Coordinator.
T FREEZER: depository for dead animals except for rabies testing and necropsies.
T HOME: pets impounded by Animal Control Officers (ACO) and returned to their owners in the field without getting to the shelter.
T LAB: specimen at the lab.
T MORGUE: rabies testing and necropsy storage for dead animals.
T VET or T OVET: injured impounded animals impounded at an off-site veterinary office.
T EVAC or T CONF: used for confiscated or evacuated animals.
T CLINIC: self-explanatory.

steps.step: San Jacinto (SJ):
sub: SJ CLINIC: used by San Jacinto vet staff for public spay/neuters performed at the San Jacinto clinic.
SJ001 to SJ015; SJTR1 to SJTR3; SJRCVG, SJFIELD: receiving.
SJ016 to SJ026: PC cats.
SJ027 to SJ030 and SJ633 to SJ635: cats in quarantine.
SJ100 to SJ111: used for PC dogs.
SJ200 to SJ215: dogs in quarantine.
SJ300 to SJ313: dogs in isolation.
SJ400 to SJ425: stray dogs, building C.
SJ500 to SJ528: stray dogs, building D.
SJ600 to SJ629: stray cats, building H.
SJ630 to SJ632 and SJ654 to SJ659: cats in isolation.
SJ636 to SJ653 and SJ660 to SJ663: used for stray cats.
SJ700 to SJ740: available cats.
SJOut1 to SJOut10: barn.
SJ800 to SJ828: needs rescue building.
SJ900 to SJ926: available dogs.
SJLife001 to SJLife006: life rooms.
LEAF: animals impounded for Animal Friends of the Valleys jurisdiction.

sections.heading: Intake Information (Fields 13-28)

steps.step: Person From (Person ID): Enter the person ID of the person surrendering the animal or the individual requesting the animal to be impounded.
sub: If this is a caller requesting the animal be impounded, this will be the caller ID number from the activity window.
The source ID on owner releases will always be the owner's person ID number.
For field initiated impounds, the source ID will be the officer's person ID number (example: P0999385).
For any others not mentioned above, create a person ID number (per DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Information) and enter it.

steps.step: Type: Enter the intake type selection that best indicates the reason for the animal's impoundment.
sub: CONFISCATE: all quarantines, police holds (if the animal is being held for investigation), declared vicious impounds, owner deceased, disaster reliefs.
DISPO. REQ: owner released dead animals.
EUTH. REQ: for live animals surrendered by the owner requesting the service of euthanasia.
OWNER SUR: for owner release live animals (not including owner release quarantines).
FOSTER: an animal that was returned from foster. (Do not duplicate animal ID number or person ID number.)
RETURN: an animal that was recently (e.g., within thirty days) adopted from the shelter and is being returned. (Do not duplicate animal ID number or person ID number.)
RTO: only used with intake subtype of ABANDONMNT and requires an existing original impound record that has an outcome type of RTO and an outcome subtype of ABANDONMNT.
STRAY: anything not mentioned above.

TRANSFER: animals received from another shelter including those moved from one DAS shelter site to another (e.g., animals relocated for surgery within DAS shelters). This intake type requires a subtype.

steps.step: Subtype: Defines the intake type. Enter the selection that best explains the intake type or section responsible for the animal's impound.
sub: AN CONTROL: use with intake type of transfer from city or county animal control agency or shelter other than DAS.
ABANDONMNT: use with intake type of confiscate or RTO.
DAS: use with intake type of transfer and within DAS relocation only.
BITE: used when an animal is impounded because of a bite or scratch and requires an intake type. Overrides all other subtypes.
CRUELTY: use with intake type of confiscate or owner sur.
EVACUATION: use with intake type of confiscate and to track disaster response impounds.
EVICTION: use with intake type of confiscate.
FIELD: animals impounded by field for any intake type.
HOSPITAL: use with intake type of confiscate.
NIGHT: for owner release and stray animals impounded by the stand-by ACO.
OTC (Over The Counter): impounded at front counter except for RETURNs or BITEs.
IN 30 DAYS: use with intake type of RETURN for animals adopted from DAS that are brought back within 30 days from the date of adoption.
> 30 DAYS: use with intake type of OWNER SUR for animals adopted from DAS that are brought back after 30 days from adoption date.
OWNER DIED: use with intake type of CONFISCATE.
POLICE: use with intake type of CONFISCATE.
PUB SAFETY: use with intake type of CONFISCATE.
SPCA: use with intake type of TRANSFER. For animals transferred from public pounds, public or private shelters, societies for the prevention of cruelty to animals, or humane societies.
SPEC CIRC: use with intake type of CONFISCATE for disaster documentation. Activates postmaster job for disaster impounds.
FETUS: used with intake type SURGERY for animals euthanized in the uterus when the mother is spayed.

steps: Cond: Enter the selection that best describes the condition in which the animal was impounded (e.g., for dead enter DOA; for unweaned litters enter NURSING). Exception: for quarantines or bites, enter QUARANTINE.
Date: Enter the date of impound, live or dead, or press F5 to automatically enter the current date.

Time: Enter the time of impound, live or dead. This should be consistent with the ending time of the activity associated with the animal impound, if applicable, or press F5 to automatically enter the current time.

steps.step: Due Out: Date animal is available for adoption. This date is not to be changed.
sub: For dead animals without owner information: same as date of impound.
For dead animals tagged or with any owner information: add three days, not including date of impound or holidays.
Tag-Morgue animals (not including owner known/tagged dead animals): same as date of impound.
Live stray animals (including wildlife) with no verifiable identification: add three days, not including date of impound or holidays.
Live stray animals (including tagged wildlife) with verifiable identification: add ten days, not including date of impound or holidays.
Owner release live animals: same as date of impound.
Quarantines: same as quarantine release date: 10 full days from date of bite. Stray quarantine: 3 full days as stray impounds, then FRA test. Owned quarantine: 10 full days as owner known.
Agency impounds (e.g., 597s, owner deceased, fires, etc.): add one month.

steps: Review: Date animal is up for review. Enter the date the animal is scheduled to be dispositioned or re-evaluated by an ACT or veterinarian. Can be changed as needed for more than one review.
OS Source: Where the owner originally got the animal. Use when intake type is OWNER SURRENDER. Press F4 to display list of where animal was adopted from (e.g., use shelter for adoption returns).
OS Reason: Reason the owner is turning in the animal. For owner release live animals, enter the selection that best explains the reason for the release.

steps.step: Impound Location: Follow this format to accurately collect mapping data.
sub: Cross street: Enter only the cross streets from which the animal was originally impounded, in the format shown on the example (e.g., Jurupa/Van Buren), to retain confidentiality on the exact address of the impound location, even if out of the county's jurisdiction. This information is shown on the website.

Address: Press the F4 key to bring up the enter address screen and enter the street number and street name only. Press OK or Enter to bring the information into the impound location field. The address checker will automatically enter the city, state, and zip code as well as the jurisdiction.

steps: Jurisdiction: Popup (F4) and enter the appropriate jurisdiction from which the animal was impounded.
Invoice: For use only by the Fiscal Division. Do not use.
City Control: Control number of an animal impounded by a contracted city officer (e.g., Blythe, La Quinta, Desert Hot Springs).
By: Enter the badge number of the impounding officer, or first and middle name initials and last full name of non-field personnel. For example, JTGuzman.
Receipt No: Enter the receipt number for the animal impound (e.g., OTI).

sections.heading: Outcome Information (Fields 29-41)
steps: Person To (Person ID): Enter the person ID number of the adopter for adoptions, foster parent for fosters, agency for transfers, or owner for RTO. This field should always be blank on outcomes of EUTH and DISPOSAL.

steps.step: Type (dispositions finalized by the impounding officer): Enter the selection that best indicates the reason for the animal's outcome.
sub: DIED: if a live impounded animal dies while on the unit.
DISPOSAL: BIFs and dead animals not requiring a head test.
EUTH: rattlesnakes and animals euthanized by a field officer.
RTO: any animal returned to its owner or custodian in the field. Use with outcome subtype FIELD.
RELOCATE: use when relocating impounded wildlife. Used with outcome subtype WILDLIFE.
TRANSFER: use with subtype LEAF when transferring impound to a contracted shelter.

steps.step: Type (dispositions finalized by other than the impounding officer):
sub: ADOPTION: any animal sold to or purchased by a new owner as defined by state law. Requires person verification by producing current and valid government identification. Requires applicable outcome subtype.
DIED: if a live impounded animal dies while in the care of DAS. Requires applicable outcome subtype.
DISPOSAL: all dead on arrival animals. Use with applicable outcome subtype: RABIES TEST (dead animal submitted for rabies testing) or RETURN (dead animal previously adopted from DAS within 30 days).

FOSTER: any animal fostered out with the approval of the foster coordinator or veterinary staff. These animals are still considered part of the kennel inventory and property of DAS. Requires applicable outcome subtype: AMBASSADOR (see SOP on foster ambassador), EMPLOYEE (fostered to DAS employee), VOLUNTEER (fostered to DAS volunteer), RESCUE GRP (fostered to an approved DAS rescue partner), FIELD TRIP (separates from regular fosters), or LOST CONTCT (used when the foster coordinator has lost contact information with the foster parent).

MISSING: any animal that is found during inventory to not be physically at the shelter after all research has been exhausted by designated personnel. Requires applicable outcome subtype: AT VET (animal was taken to contract emergency clinic and went missing), ENROUTE (animal went missing while transporting to destination), ESCAPED (animal escaped and witnessed by staff), IN FOSTER (if fostered animal is not returned by foster parent by its due date and foster parent notifies DAS of animal disappearance), IN KENNEL (animal went missing and not witnessed how; based on city contracts, will require DR number and a police report after 24 hours; an animal ID memo must be created by staff reporting the animal missing, date, DR number, investigating police officer, employees involved, and date of memo), or STOLEN (based on city contracts, will require DR number and a police report after 24 hours, with the same animal ID memo requirements).

RTO: any animal returned to its owner or custodian at the shelter or out in the field. Requires person verification by producing current valid government identification. If the animal is returned to its owner or custodian by front counter shelter staff, use the applicable outcome subtype: COUNTER (not staying for spay or neuter), ABANDONMNT (if not reclaimed by owner or custodian), CHNGED MND (used for intake type of owner sur or euth req), or SURGWAIT (scheduled for DAS spay or neuter).
TRANSFER: animals transferred to rescue, other DAS shelters, SPCA, another jurisdiction animal shelter, humane society, or rehab groups. Requires applicable outcome subtype.

RELEASED: used specifically for working shelter cats and community cat program, with the following subtypes: CCP CAT (final outcome of community cat), EAR TIPPED (used for working shelter cats), OFFSITE (community cat being moved to off-site spay/neuter clinic), or SURGWAIT (scheduled for DAS spay or neuter).

steps.step: Subtype: The outcome subtype is dependent on the outcome and may not always be used. If TRANSFER, EUTH, or DIED is used, enter the proper selection from the following lists.
sub: TRANSFER, use only: AN CONTROL (public pounds, public or private shelter, city or county animal control agency or shelter, society for the prevention of cruelty to animals, or humane society); DAS (use with outcome type of transfer and within DAS site only); BRAND INSP; CONTRACT; LEAF (animals impounded for Animal Friends of the Valleys); AT VET; PETSMART; RAMONA; ADOPTABLE (in case of wrongful euthanasia); RESCUE GRP; SPCA.
EUTH, use only: ABATEMENT; OWNER REQ; RABIES TST; TOO YOUNG; TRT-BEHV; TRT-MEDI; UNTRT-BEHV; UNTRT-MEDI; SHOT (animals humanely euthanized out in the field with firearm); WILDLIFE.
DIED, use only: AT VET; ENROUTE; IN FOSTER; IN KENNEL; IN SURGERY; RABIES TST; ANML SAMAR.

ADOPTION, use only: PETSMART (used only for adoption transactions at PetSmart); NEW (used only for all new adopters); CONTRACT (used only when the animal is sent home without being spayed or neutered due to a medical issue; must have approval of veterinary staff and is documented in the Treatment Window; a Tag/Link window is created and must have contract in the cert no field); OFFSITE (adoptions that occur at a different venue based on an event); SURGWAIT (used only when the animal being adopted is waiting for spay or neuter surgery at DAS; after storing a receipt, remove the outcome date and time; S/N front counter staff will enter the outcome date and time once animal is picked up by adopter after spay or neuter surgery). This is not to be used for rescue groups.

steps: Cond: Enter the condition of the animal upon its outcome.

Date: Outcome date. Enter the date the final disposition of the animal is being recorded, live or dead, or press F5 to automatically enter the current date. Note: if the Outcome Type field is completed, then the Outcome Date field is to be completed as well, unless the animal is still housed at the shelter waiting for spay or neuter surgery.
Time: Enter the time that the outcome is being completed or press F5 to automatically enter the current time.
Weight: Weight of the animal. Must be entered on all ADOPTION, EUTH, RTO, and TRANSFER outcomes.
Dose: Enter the number of ccs of drug used for euthanasia, if applicable.
Dose 2: Enter the number of ccs for the sedative used along with the euthanasia solution.
Bottle: Bottle number of the euthanasia solution. If data is entered in the Dose and Dose 2 fields, then entry in this field is required.
Medical Grade (Extra5): Used only by veterinary staff to enter the medical grade of an animal.

steps.step: By: Enter the initials of the person doing the outcome of the animal or the ACO's badge number.
sub: Enter the badge number of the officer performing the euthanasia. For other than an officer, enter the initials of both personnel performing euthanasia while logged into location R EUTH.
For adoptions, RTOs, and transfers to rescue groups, enter the initials of the adoption counselor and the front counter clerk completing the receipt (e.g., FLG/LAH).
For all others, enter the initials of the person completing the outcome in the kennel window.

steps: Receipt No: Create a Chameleon receipt number per policy and enter it here, if applicable.

sections.heading: Specific Information (Fields 42-49)
steps: Hold: Enter Y to indicate a hold on the animal. Entering any of the Request values below will create the hold icon upon storing/updating the kennel window. N: do not use. Y: to be used anytime the Request field is completed.

steps.step: Request: Indicates what kind of hold is on the animal. Enter the following when an animal impound is not routine:
sub: HOLDNOTIFY: possible owner information needing follow-up notification. A memo shall be initiated with a type of OWNER INFO or O NOTIFY and used when the status is KNOWN WAIT. The memo shall include the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the reservation and will be attached to the animal ID number. For confiscate intakes, used for short term/owner known holds.

CONFISCATE: use when holding animals for police or if the animal is evidence. A memo shall be initiated with a type of INVESTIGATE. The memo shall include the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the reservation and will be attached to the animal ID number. For confiscate intakes, used for long term or indefinite holds.

POSS ADOPT: for any reservations initiated. A memo shall be initiated with a type of POSS ADOPT. The memo shall include the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the reservation and will be attached to the animal ID number.
QUARANTINE: any quarantine, live or dead.

DO NOT PTS: any operations hold other than police holds (e.g., possible vicious investigations), safety net holds, or special consideration, indicating the animal is not to be euthanized. A memo shall be initiated with a type of INVESTIGATE, including the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the reservation, attached to the animal ID number. A memo shall be initiated with a type of SAFETY NET, including the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the hold, attached to the animal ID number.

DO NOT RTO: any operations hold other than police holds (e.g., possible vicious investigations). A memo shall be initiated with a type of INVESTIGATE, including the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the reservation, attached to the animal ID number.

HEARING: animal impounded with a hearing pending. The hearing clerk or investigating officer is responsible for: initiating a memo with a type of HEARING; entering a Y in the HOLD field; including in the memo the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the hold, attached to the animal ID number; and entering a brief statement in the COMMENT field such as Hold for Hearing or Hearing set for 1/1/2010.

NO ADOPT: impounded animal not recommended for adoption. A memo shall be initiated with a type of DANGER, including the information (or person ID number) of the individual requesting the reservation, attached to the animal ID number.
POSS RESC: impounded animal is being held for a breed rescue group approved by the rescue/foster coordinator.

steps: Comments: Enter any other information here. This information does not show on the website and comments in this field are not accessible to any user who does not display the actual impound record. Enter comments in a professional manner, as all records are available for public records.

steps.step: TO DO: Used by the vet staff doing the kennel walk-through. Vet staff can generate schedules for medical treatments to be given to the animals.
sub: Complete: use to complete a treatment.
Cancel: use to cancel a treatment.
New: use to enter a new treatment.

steps.step: Special tabs:
sub: Kennel tab: look for animals currently in the shelter (matching the descriptions entered to the left in the boxes).
Lost: performs searches on kennel_no LOST and based on information entered in the special searches fields.
Foster: performs a search and displays all animals currently in foster.
PetHarbor.com: goes to the PetHarbor website where the animal pictures are found.
Other Shelters: looks for animals at other shelters via PetHarbor.

steps.step: Toolbar icons:
sub: Square box at far left: Grey (ready); Yellow (you have modified fields; update to complete update); Red (unable to update/store; something is wrong with the change); Green (successful, you may continue).
Magnifying glass: Find (F12).
Yellow flag: find next record.
Layered documents: update record (F8).
Green plus sign: store record.
Red minus sign: delete; System Administrator rights only.
Square box: clear window.
Question mark: pop-up help, if accessible.
Animal: pop-up animal profile.
Caduceus: pop-up the Treatment Window.
Person with small dog on leash: pop-up adopter profile.
Person with large dog on leash: pop-up adopter interview.

steps: Search Back: Chameleon will ask if you want to automatically search back 13 days for an animal if it has an outcome. If you want to search back further than 13 days, use the Search Back box. Enter the number of days to search back and press F12 to find.
Grid: shows the results, if any, for any searches.

sections.heading: Non-Routine Impounds
steps: Complete any non-routine impound not covered by this policy at the direction of an ACT or Field supervisor.

sections.heading: Arrests/Special Information for ACT Staff
steps: For animals impounded by or for the police, create a memo that contains the following information: the arresting officer's name and badge number, the agency's case (DR) number, and whether or not the animal is being held for investigation.
If the animal is not being held, indicate to whom it may be released.
End the memo with the date and name of the person (first initial and full last name) creating the memo.

campusVariations.variation: Uses B-prefix kennel location codes; see Campus Kennel Location Codes section.
Uses R-prefix kennel location codes; see Campus Kennel Location Codes section.
Uses T-prefix kennel location codes; see Campus Kennel Location Codes section.
Uses SJ-prefix kennel location codes, plus LEAF for Animal Friends of the Valleys jurisdiction impounds; see Campus Kennel Location Codes section.
