revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from November 2021 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Removed references to the Animal Placement Evaluation Team (APET), which has been disbanded; responsibilities reassigned per current practice pending pass 2 review.

documents.searchText: to establish a uniform procedure for the completion of the chameleon kennel window to document and track state mandated activities. applicable to all personnel responsible for using the chameleon kennel window. director of animal services state record keeping mandate {"step": "maintain accurate records on each animal taken up, medically treated, or impounded, as required by food and agriculture code (f&a) 32003. the records shall include all of the following information and any other information required by the california veterinary medical board:", "sub": ["the date the animal was taken up, medically treated, euthanized, or impounded.", "the circumstances under which the animal was taken up, medically treated, euthanized, or impounded.", "the names of the personnel who took up, medically treated, euthanized, or impounded the animal.", "a description of any medical treatment provided to the animal and the name of the veterinarian of record.", "the final disposition of the animal, including the name of the person who euthanized the animal or the name and address of the adopting party."]} retain these records for three years after the date the animal's impoundment ends. (enacted 1998.) complete a kennel window for every animal impounded. this applies to all staff and volunteers employed by the department. input shall be as accurate and detailed as possible to assist the public in locating lost animals. careful attention is essential, as the information input on this screen may adversely affect the outcome of an animal and information published on the web through petharbor. kennel window fields (animal and location) use the popup (f4) in the following fields (see appendix a) and choose the appropriate selection. impound no: this is the unique impound number generated by chameleon for each animal's individual kennel impound record. {"step": "litter no: enter the mother's impound number on all litter mates of all litters.", "sub": ["for a mother dog or cat with a litter (weaned or unweaned), create one kennel record (window) for the mother and one kennel record (window) for each individual in the litter, entering the mother's impound no. in this field.", "for a litter without its mother (weaned or unweaned), create one kennel record (window) for each individual in the litter. leave the impound no. field blank.", "for all litters containing both deceased and live individuals, create one kennel impound (window) for all the animals and outcome the deceased animals with an outcome of disposal."]} kennel no: enter the appropriate location/kennel of the impounded animal. the kennel number is the basis for the shelter location posted on petharbor as well as on all statistical and billing reports. campus-specific kennel number ranges are listed in the campus kennel location codes section below. {"step": "status: enter the appropriate adoption status of the impounded animal.", "sub": ["available: impounded animal has met retention period requirements (not applicable for field). the picture and animal information shows on the shelter website under adoptable pets and disappears from lost and found pets.", "owsur aval: oti animal has met retention period (not applicable for field). the picture and animal information shows on the shelter website under adoptable pets.", "known wait: live/dead animals with any owner information, including owner arrests (where animal is being held) and owner known quarantines. requires an owner info memo and/or tag/link record. use if the owner does not have a specific name, address, or phone number (such as for a rabies tag). do not create tag/link if the owner does have a specific name, address, or phone number and cannot be located in the system. create a tag/link and attach a memo type of owner info to the animal id with all available information.", "ow sur euth: pets surrendered by owners requesting the service of euthanasia.", "ow sur wait: owner released live animals, including owner release quarantines that have not met the retention period (be sure to enter the animal's name in the animal window).", "stray wait: live animals without any owner information. the picture and animal information shows on the shelter website under lost and found pets.", "unavail: dead animals without identification, stray quarantined live animals, live/dead animals for head test, live/dead police holds, live disaster holds. this status prevents the animal's picture and information from showing on the shelter website.", "needs resc: animals flagged for rescue. the animal's picture and animal information is displayed in the adoption partners webpage of rcdas.org.", "surgery: requires an intake subtype of fetus. specifically used to document animals that were euthanized in the uterus when the mother is spayed."]} {"step": "sub status: enter the applicable sub status.", "sub": ["enter billing to send a bill to constituent.", "enter medical or behavior to specify the kennel status needs resc.", "enter ccp to flag specific cats for the community cat program."]} tag: enter the link, license number, or microchip information from the tag/link window, if known. for example, l07-123456 would indicate a license tag number, and 123456789 would indicate a microchip number. {"step": "other id: enter other identification of the animal if applicable.", "sub": ["blue collar tag sometimes placed on dogs upon arrival at shelter.", "secondary id number shall be applied to dogs; cats get the aid on a neck band. dogs in the clinic may not need the secondary id number.", "confiscation number used in the field during a raid to identify each animal until a chameleon kennel record can be stored."]} activity no: enter the activity number if the animal impounded was the result of an activity initiated impound. if more than one animal was impounded on one activity call, all animals of that activity will have the activity number on their kennel impound record. field next to activity no: used for an activity sequence other than one. animal id: create an animal id and enter it here. to create the bite icon in the animal window if the animal was involved in a bite/scratch, enter a y in the bite box. behavior assessment: used by the behavior team to document behavior scores of impounded animals. {"step": "extra2: based on the kennel status needs resc, this field requires one of the following to be entered:", "sub": ["green: animals that have met their legally mandated retention period but have been deemed medically or behaviorally unadoptable. those in the green category may become yellow or red, or may be euthanized based on their ongoing condition.", "yellow: animals that can no longer be kept in the shelter given their current medical or behavior condition. these animals need a rescue commitment immediately. those in the yellow category may become red or may be euthanized based on their ongoing condition.", "red: animals in imminent need of rescue and in danger of being euthanized if not picked up by a rescue."]} campus kennel location codes {"step": "blythe shelter (b):", "sub": ["b d 01 to b d 34: dogs.", "b c 01 to b c 34: cats.", "b dq 16 to b dq 19: quarantined dogs.", "b l 01 to b l 05: livestock pens.", "b o 01 to b o 10: outside cages for overflow dogs.", "b foster: animals in foster homes.", "b freezer: depository for dead animals except for rabies testing and necropsies.", "b morgue: rabies testing and necropsy storage.", "b receiving: receiving."]} {"step": "riverside shelter (r):", "sub": ["r receiving, r 007, and rr 001 to rr 021: receiving.", "r 008 to r 011: puppies.", "r 012 to r 055: stray wait dogs in kennel a.", "r 056 to r 087: available dogs in kennel b.", "r 088 to r 105: dog quarantine.", "r 106 to r 135: stray wait dogs in kennel d.", "r treat 1 to r treat 9: treatment room, for animals receiving treatments.", "r 309, r 310, r 314, r 315: outside receiving kennels used for fowl.", "r out 01 to r out 20: outside runs in back of shelter, dogs and swine.", "r an 01 to r an 35: available now animals in available now room and public hall.", "r 200 to r 203: dogs in maternity and nursing.", "r 204 to r 229: dog isolation.", "r euth: used only for pets in the euthanasia room.", "r ot 1, r 301 to r 308: cat isolation.", "r 300, r 311, r 312, r 313, r 316, r 451, r 452, r 453, rq 01 to rq 42, and rcr 001 to rcr 013: cat receiving and cat quarantine.", "r 321 to r 360 and r 411 to r 450: cats.", "r 361 to r 380: cat maternity.", "r 381 to r 410: kittens.", "r grooming: foster being held in room 70 until processed.", "r an 01 to r an 30: small animals that are available now (located in the available now room and public hall).", "r 319 to r 320: shoreline kennels (see supervising act).", "r 600 to r 684: rescue kennel. animals housed in this pod are waiting for rescue transports or held until a rescue is found.", "r lot: do not use.", "r clinic: self-explanatory.", "r event: pets going to offsite adoption events and returning to the shelter if not adopted on the same day. this requires an animal id memo of the event location.", "r petsmart: animal transferred to petsmart and retained at petsmart for adoption. the animal is not returned to the shelter.", "r foster: only used for approved fostered animals by the foster coordinator.", "r field: used by field officer impounds. upon delivery to the shelter, this kennel no is then changed to the actual cage where the animal is impounded.", "r freezer: depository for dead animals except for rabies testing and necropsies.", "r lab: specimen at the lab.", "r morgue: rabies testing and necropsy storage.", "r vet: injured impounded animals impounded at an off-site veterinary office.", "r home: pets impounded by animal control officers (aco) and returned to their owners in the field without getting to the shelter.", "r conf: used for confiscated animals.", "r behavior: animals moved to behavior room for safer and behavior testing."]} {"step": "cvac thousand palms shelter (t):", "sub": ["t 100 to t 128: impounded puppies.", "t 300 to t 386 and t 412 to t 415: impounded cats.", "t 211 to t 220: dogs in dog court yard.", "t 129 to t 134: cats in condos located in the adoption galley.", "t 001 to t 030: dogs in kennel a.", "t 050 to t 079: dogs in kennel b.", "t 400 to t 411: cat isolation.", "t n001 to t n020: cats maternity ward/kitten nursery (cvac).", "tcq 01 to tcq 28: cat quarantine.", "te 1 to te 4: treatment/exam room.", "tdq 01 to tdq 11: dog quarantine.", "t 200 to t 210: dog isolation.", "tr 01 to tr 24: receiving for all animals.", "tr 25 to tr 50: receiving hallway for all animals.", "t event: pets going to offsite adoption events and returning to the shelter if not adopted on the same day. this requires an animal id memo of the event location.", "t grooming: used when impounded animals are moved to the grooming room. this is a temporary kennel until the animal goes back to its regular kennel.", "t petsmart: pets going to petsmart that are not returning to the shelter. this requires an outcome and a memo of the petsmart location.", "t field: used only for impounds by field officers. upon delivery to the shelter, this kennel no is then changed to the actual cage where the animal is impounded.", "t foster: only used for approved fostered animals by the foster coordinator.", "t freezer: depository for dead animals except for rabies testing and necropsies.", "t home: pets impounded by animal control officers (aco) and returned to their owners in the field without getting to the shelter.", "t lab: specimen at the lab.", "t morgue: rabies testing and necropsy storage for dead animals.", "t vet or t ovet: injured impounded animals impounded at an off-site veterinary office.", "t evac or t conf: used for confiscated or evacuated animals.", "t clinic: self-explanatory."]} {"step": "san jacinto (sj):", "sub": ["sj clinic: used by san jacinto vet staff for public spay/neuters performed at the san jacinto clinic.", "sj001 to sj015; sjtr1 to sjtr3; sjrcvg, sjfield: receiving.", "sj016 to sj026: pc cats.", "sj027 to sj030 and sj633 to sj635: cats in quarantine.", "sj100 to sj111: used for pc dogs.", "sj200 to sj215: dogs in quarantine.", "sj300 to sj313: dogs in isolation.", "sj400 to sj425: stray dogs, building c.", "sj500 to sj528: stray dogs, building d.", "sj600 to sj629: stray cats, building h.", "sj630 to sj632 and sj654 to sj659: cats in isolation.", "sj636 to sj653 and sj660 to sj663: used for stray cats.", "sj700 to sj740: available cats.", "sjout1 to sjout10: barn.", "sj800 to sj828: needs rescue building.", "sj900 to sj926: available dogs.", "sjlife001 to sjlife006: life rooms.", "leaf: animals impounded for animal friends of the valleys jurisdiction."]} intake information (fields 13-28) {"step": "person from (person id): enter the person id of the person surrendering the animal or the individual requesting the animal to be impounded.", "sub": ["if this is a caller requesting the animal be impounded, this will be the caller id number from the activity window.", "the source id on owner releases will always be the owner's person id number.", "for field initiated impounds, the source id will be the officer's person id number (example: p0999385).", "for any others not mentioned above, create a person id number (per das policy 000-01, chameleon general information) and enter it."]} {"step": "type: enter the intake type selection that best indicates the reason for the animal's impoundment.", "sub": ["confiscate: all quarantines, police holds (if the animal is being held for investigation), declared vicious impounds, owner deceased, disaster reliefs.", "dispo. req: owner released dead animals.", "euth. req: for live animals surrendered by the owner requesting the service of euthanasia.", "owner sur: for owner release live animals (not including owner release quarantines).", "foster: an animal that was returned from foster. (do not duplicate animal id number or person id number.)", "return: an animal that was recently (e.g., within thirty days) adopted from the shelter and is being returned. (do not duplicate animal id number or person id number.)", "rto: only used with intake subtype of abandonmnt and requires an existing original impound record that has an outcome type of rto and an outcome subtype of abandonmnt.", "stray: anything not mentioned above.", "transfer: animals received from another shelter including those moved from one das shelter site to another (e.g., animals relocated for surgery within das shelters). this intake type requires a subtype."]} {"step": "subtype: defines the intake type. enter the selection that best explains the intake type or section responsible for the animal's impound.", "sub": ["an control: use with intake type of transfer from city or county animal control agency or shelter other than das.", "abandonmnt: use with intake type of confiscate or rto.", "das: use with intake type of transfer and within das relocation only.", "bite: used when an animal is impounded because of a bite or scratch and requires an intake type. overrides all other subtypes.", "cruelty: use with intake type of confiscate or owner sur.", "evacuation: use with intake type of confiscate and to track disaster response impounds.", "eviction: use with intake type of confiscate.", "field: animals impounded by field for any intake type.", "hospital: use with intake type of confiscate.", "night: for owner release and stray animals impounded by the stand-by aco.", "otc (over the counter): impounded at front counter except for returns or bites.", "in 30 days: use with intake type of return for animals adopted from das that are brought back within 30 days from the date of adoption.", "> 30 days: use with intake type of owner sur for animals adopted from das that are brought back after 30 days from adoption date.", "owner died: use with intake type of confiscate.", "police: use with intake type of confiscate.", "pub safety: use with intake type of confiscate.", "spca: use with intake type of transfer. for animals transferred from public pounds, public or private shelters, societies for the prevention of cruelty to animals, or humane societies.", "spec circ: use with intake type of confiscate for disaster documentation. activates postmaster job for disaster impounds.", "fetus: used with intake type surgery for animals euthanized in the uterus when the mother is spayed."]} cond: enter the selection that best describes the condition in which the animal was impounded (e.g., for dead enter doa; for unweaned litters enter nursing). exception: for quarantines or bites, enter quarantine. date: enter the date of impound, live or dead, or press f5 to automatically enter the current date. time: enter the time of impound, live or dead. this should be consistent with the ending time of the activity associated with the animal impound, if applicable, or press f5 to automatically enter the current time. {"step": "due out: date animal is available for adoption. this date is not to be changed.", "sub": ["for dead animals without owner information: same as date of impound.", "for dead animals tagged or with any owner information: add three days, not including date of impound or holidays.", "tag-morgue animals (not including owner known/tagged dead animals): same as date of impound.", "live stray animals (including wildlife) with no verifiable identification: add three days, not including date of impound or holidays.", "live stray animals (including tagged wildlife) with verifiable identification: add ten days, not including date of impound or holidays.", "owner release live animals: same as date of impound.", "quarantines: same as quarantine release date: 10 full days from date of bite. stray quarantine: 3 full days as stray impounds, then fra test. owned quarantine: 10 full days as owner known.", "agency impounds (e.g., 597s, owner deceased, fires, etc.): add one month."]} review: date animal is up for review. enter the date the animal is scheduled to be dispositioned or re-evaluated by an act or veterinarian. can be changed as needed for more than one review. os source: where the owner originally got the animal. use when intake type is owner surrender. press f4 to display list of where animal was adopted from (e.g., use shelter for adoption returns). os reason: reason the owner is turning in the animal. for owner release live animals, enter the selection that best explains the reason for the release. {"step": "impound location: follow this format to accurately collect mapping data.", "sub": ["cross street: enter only the cross streets from which the animal was originally impounded, in the format shown on the example (e.g., jurupa/van buren), to retain confidentiality on the exact address of the impound location, even if out of the county's jurisdiction. this information is shown on the website.", "address: press the f4 key to bring up the enter address screen and enter the street number and street name only. press ok or enter to bring the information into the impound location field. the address checker will automatically enter the city, state, and zip code as well as the jurisdiction."]} jurisdiction: popup (f4) and enter the appropriate jurisdiction from which the animal was impounded. invoice: for use only by the fiscal division. do not use. city control: control number of an animal impounded by a contracted city officer (e.g., blythe, la quinta, desert hot springs). by: enter the badge number of the impounding officer, or first and middle name initials and last full name of non-field personnel. for example, jtguzman. receipt no: enter the receipt number for the animal impound (e.g., oti). outcome information (fields 29-41) person to (person id): enter the person id number of the adopter for adoptions, foster parent for fosters, agency for transfers, or owner for rto. this field should always be blank on outcomes of euth and disposal. {"step": "type (dispositions finalized by the impounding officer): enter the selection that best indicates the reason for the animal's outcome.", "sub": ["died: if a live impounded animal dies while on the unit.", "disposal: bifs and dead animals not requiring a head test.", "euth: rattlesnakes and animals euthanized by a field officer.", "rto: any animal returned to its owner or custodian in the field. use with outcome subtype field.", "relocate: use when relocating impounded wildlife. used with outcome subtype wildlife.", "transfer: use with subtype leaf when transferring impound to a contracted shelter."]} {"step": "type (dispositions finalized by other than the impounding officer):", "sub": ["adoption: any animal sold to or purchased by a new owner as defined by state law. requires person verification by producing current and valid government identification. requires applicable outcome subtype.", "died: if a live impounded animal dies while in the care of das. requires applicable outcome subtype.", "disposal: all dead on arrival animals. use with applicable outcome subtype: rabies test (dead animal submitted for rabies testing) or return (dead animal previously adopted from das within 30 days).", "foster: any animal fostered out with the approval of the foster coordinator or veterinary staff. these animals are still considered part of the kennel inventory and property of das. requires applicable outcome subtype: ambassador (see sop on foster ambassador), employee (fostered to das employee), volunteer (fostered to das volunteer), rescue grp (fostered to an approved das rescue partner), field trip (separates from regular fosters), or lost contct (used when the foster coordinator has lost contact information with the foster parent).", "missing: any animal that is found during inventory to not be physically at the shelter after all research has been exhausted by designated personnel. requires applicable outcome subtype: at vet (animal was taken to contract emergency clinic and went missing), enroute (animal went missing while transporting to destination), escaped (animal escaped and witnessed by staff), in foster (if fostered animal is not returned by foster parent by its due date and foster parent notifies das of animal disappearance), in kennel (animal went missing and not witnessed how; based on city contracts, will require dr number and a police report after 24 hours; an animal id memo must be created by staff reporting the animal missing, date, dr number, investigating police officer, employees involved, and date of memo), or stolen (based on city contracts, will require dr number and a police report after 24 hours, with the same animal id memo requirements).", "rto: any animal returned to its owner or custodian at the shelter or out in the field. requires person verification by producing current valid government identification. if the animal is returned to its owner or custodian by front counter shelter staff, use the applicable outcome subtype: counter (not staying for spay or neuter), abandonmnt (if not reclaimed by owner or custodian), chnged mnd (used for intake type of owner sur or euth req), or surgwait (scheduled for das spay or neuter).", "transfer: animals transferred to rescue, other das shelters, spca, another jurisdiction animal shelter, humane society, or rehab groups. requires applicable outcome subtype.", "released: used specifically for working shelter cats and community cat program, with the following subtypes: ccp cat (final outcome of community cat), ear tipped (used for working shelter cats), offsite (community cat being moved to off-site spay/neuter clinic), or surgwait (scheduled for das spay or neuter)."]} {"step": "subtype: the outcome subtype is dependent on the outcome and may not always be used. if transfer, euth, or died is used, enter the proper selection from the following lists.", "sub": ["transfer, use only: an control (public pounds, public or private shelter, city or county animal control agency or shelter, society for the prevention of cruelty to animals, or humane society); das (use with outcome type of transfer and within das site only); brand insp; contract; leaf (animals impounded for animal friends of the valleys); at vet; petsmart; ramona; adoptable (in case of wrongful euthanasia); rescue grp; spca.", "euth, use only: abatement; owner req; rabies tst; too young; trt-behv; trt-medi; untrt-behv; untrt-medi; shot (animals humanely euthanized out in the field with firearm); wildlife.", "died, use only: at vet; enroute; in foster; in kennel; in surgery; rabies tst; anml samar.", "adoption, use only: petsmart (used only for adoption transactions at petsmart); new (used only for all new adopters); contract (used only when the animal is sent home without being spayed or neutered due to a medical issue; must have approval of veterinary staff and is documented in the treatment window; a tag/link window is created and must have contract in the cert no field); offsite (adoptions that occur at a different venue based on an event); surgwait (used only when the animal being adopted is waiting for spay or neuter surgery at das; after storing a receipt, remove the outcome date and time; s/n front counter staff will enter the outcome date and time once animal is picked up by adopter after spay or neuter surgery). this is not to be used for rescue groups."]} cond: enter the condition of the animal upon its outcome. date: outcome date. enter the date the final disposition of the animal is being recorded, live or dead, or press f5 to automatically enter the current date. note: if the outcome type field is completed, then the outcome date field is to be completed as well, unless the animal is still housed at the shelter waiting for spay or neuter surgery. time: enter the time that the outcome is being completed or press f5 to automatically enter the current time. weight: weight of the animal. must be entered on all adoption, euth, rto, and transfer outcomes. dose: enter the number of ccs of drug used for euthanasia, if applicable. dose 2: enter the number of ccs for the sedative used along with the euthanasia solution. bottle: bottle number of the euthanasia solution. if data is entered in the dose and dose 2 fields, then entry in this field is required. medical grade (extra5): used only by veterinary staff to enter the medical grade of an animal. {"step": "by: enter the initials of the person doing the outcome of the animal or the aco's badge number.", "sub": ["enter the badge number of the officer performing the euthanasia. for other than an officer, enter the initials of both personnel performing euthanasia while logged into location r euth.", "for adoptions, rtos, and transfers to rescue groups, enter the initials of the adoption counselor and the front counter clerk completing the receipt (e.g., flg/lah).", "for all others, enter the initials of the person completing the outcome in the kennel window."]} receipt no: create a chameleon receipt number per policy and enter it here, if applicable. specific information (fields 42-49) hold: enter y to indicate a hold on the animal. entering any of the request values below will create the hold icon upon storing/updating the kennel window. n: do not use. y: to be used anytime the request field is completed. {"step": "request: indicates what kind of hold is on the animal. enter the following when an animal impound is not routine:", "sub": ["holdnotify: possible owner information needing follow-up notification. a memo shall be initiated with a type of owner info or o notify and used when the status is known wait. the memo shall include the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the reservation and will be attached to the animal id number. for confiscate intakes, used for short term/owner known holds.", "confiscate: use when holding animals for police or if the animal is evidence. a memo shall be initiated with a type of investigate. the memo shall include the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the reservation and will be attached to the animal id number. for confiscate intakes, used for long term or indefinite holds.", "poss adopt: for any reservations initiated. a memo shall be initiated with a type of poss adopt. the memo shall include the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the reservation and will be attached to the animal id number.", "quarantine: any quarantine, live or dead.", "do not pts: any operations hold other than police holds (e.g., possible vicious investigations), safety net holds, or special consideration, indicating the animal is not to be euthanized. a memo shall be initiated with a type of investigate, including the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the reservation, attached to the animal id number. a memo shall be initiated with a type of safety net, including the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the hold, attached to the animal id number.", "do not rto: any operations hold other than police holds (e.g., possible vicious investigations). a memo shall be initiated with a type of investigate, including the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the reservation, attached to the animal id number.", "hearing: animal impounded with a hearing pending. the hearing clerk or investigating officer is responsible for: initiating a memo with a type of hearing; entering a y in the hold field; including in the memo the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the hold, attached to the animal id number; and entering a brief statement in the comment field such as hold for hearing or hearing set for 1/1/2010.", "no adopt: impounded animal not recommended for adoption. a memo shall be initiated with a type of danger, including the information (or person id number) of the individual requesting the reservation, attached to the animal id number.", "poss resc: impounded animal is being held for a breed rescue group approved by the rescue/foster coordinator."]} comments: enter any other information here. this information does not show on the website and comments in this field are not accessible to any user who does not display the actual impound record. enter comments in a professional manner, as all records are available for public records. {"step": "to do: used by the vet staff doing the kennel walk-through. vet staff can generate schedules for medical treatments to be given to the animals.", "sub": ["complete: use to complete a treatment.", "cancel: use to cancel a treatment.", "new: use to enter a new treatment."]} {"step": "special tabs:", "sub": ["kennel tab: look for animals currently in the shelter (matching the descriptions entered to the left in the boxes).", "lost: performs searches on kennel_no lost and based on information entered in the special searches fields.", "foster: performs a search and displays all animals currently in foster.", "petharbor.com: goes to the petharbor website where the animal pictures are found.", "other shelters: looks for animals at other shelters via petharbor."]} {"step": "toolbar icons:", "sub": ["square box at far left: grey (ready); yellow (you have modified fields; update to complete update); red (unable to update/store; something is wrong with the change); green (successful, you may continue).", "magnifying glass: find (f12).", "yellow flag: find next record.", "layered documents: update record (f8).", "green plus sign: store record.", "red minus sign: delete; system administrator rights only.", "square box: clear window.", "question mark: pop-up help, if accessible.", "animal: pop-up animal profile.", "caduceus: pop-up the treatment window.", "person with small dog on leash: pop-up adopter profile.", "person with large dog on leash: pop-up adopter interview."]} search back: chameleon will ask if you want to automatically search back 13 days for an animal if it has an outcome. if you want to search back further than 13 days, use the search back box. enter the number of days to search back and press f12 to find. grid: shows the results, if any, for any searches. non-routine impounds complete any non-routine impound not covered by this policy at the direction of an act or field supervisor. arrests/special information for act staff for animals impounded by or for the police, create a memo that contains the following information: the arresting officer's name and badge number, the agency's case (dr) number, and whether or not the animal is being held for investigation. if the animal is not being held, indicate to whom it may be released. end the memo with the date and name of the person (first initial and full last name) creating the memo. california food and agriculture code section 32003, record keeping requirements das policy 000-01, chameleon general information

documents.title: Employee Dress Code
documents.purpose: This dress code policy is intended to provide clear guidelines for the professional appearance of Riverside County Animal Services employees. It is meant to promote professionalism, employee safety, good public and customer relations, and accomplish the objectives of the policy without infringing upon the personal rights of employees. Any exception to this policy is at the discretion of, and subject to authorization by, the Director and/or designee.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Animal Services employees.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-06 Dress Code, effective October 29, 2014 (revised from May 25, 2011, October 5, 2009, and June 5, 2000). An unapproved redlined draft revision dated April 14, 2025 exists and is noted in the edit log for Pass 2 reconciliation.
references: County of Riverside Memorandums of Understanding with LIUNA, SEIU, and unrepresented management
Board of Supervisors Policy C-5, Reimbursement for Damaged Clothing or Property

definitions.def: A small pointed or tufted beard on a man's chin.
The hair that grows on a man's cheeks and chin.

sections.heading: Background
steps: The appearance of all employees is important to the total operation and effectiveness of Animal Services. The general public's, visitors', and clients' perceptions of Animal Services are influenced in part by the appearance of staff as well as the level of courtesy, professionalism, and compassion staff provide. A well-groomed and professional appearance helps create favorable impressions, whereas appearance that distracts clients, visitors, or fellow employees does not reflect the type of impression appropriate to the Animal Services environment. These standards are based on the prevailing attitudes of professionals in the local community as they dress, conduct themselves, and conduct their business.
The Animal Services dress code policy is consistent with Memorandums of Understanding with LIUNA, SEIU, and unrepresented management.

It is not possible to cover every conceivable question of dress and grooming in a written policy. The best and most effective control has to come from each employee's good judgment of what is best for Animal Services' obligations to customer service and the animals in their care.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Comply with and adhere to the County of Riverside Memorandum of Understanding dealing with dress codes. Attire and grooming shall be neat, clean, modest, conservative, and free from offending odors, and shall meet the highest professional standard of good taste.
Wear identification badges so they are easily visible at all times while on duty.

There may be some dress code differences among the different department divisions and programs (see the division sections below). These depend on the work environment, nature of work performed, involvement in animal care activities, or uniform requirements. Said differences are in addition to this policy and not in conflict with it. Refer to departmental and local policies for additional information.
It is the responsibility of supervisors and managers to enforce this policy. Employees must be in compliance with the dress code at all times.

sections.heading: General Dress Code Guidelines (Attachment I)
steps: Acceptable professional community standards: Dress and grooming must leave a positive professional image with citizens and customers while being consistent with the highest expectations of the public served by the Department of Animal Services.
Personal appearance: Dress and grooming will be modest, professional, non-provocative, and in good taste so as not to offend co-workers, county employees, or the public.

Personal grooming: Maintain good personal grooming to promote a professional image and maintain safety in the workplace. Grooming shall be neat and clean. Hair, including facial hair (beards, mustaches, goatees, and sideburns), must be clean, neat, properly trimmed, and free from offending odors. Hands and nails should be neat and clean as well.
Makeup: Makeup should be soft and complementary, in keeping with appropriate business appearance.

steps.step: Tattoos and jewelry, except as noted or approved by the Director or designee:
sub: Excessive tattoos must be covered. All decisions regarding what is acceptable or unacceptable will be made at the manager's discretion.

All jewelry worn by employees must be appropriate so it does not detract from a professional appearance. All facial piercing jewelry, such as nose piercing, tongue piercing, eyebrow piercing, lip piercing, or any other facial piercing jewelry, is prohibited.
Jewelry not required to be worn is not covered under the County Reimbursement for Damaged Clothing or Property policy (see County Policy C-5) should it be damaged or stolen in the line of duty.

steps: Apparel: Work clothing should not restrict movement and should allow staff to perform all tasks within their job specifications. Apparel should be clean and in good repair (without holes, frays, discoloration, wrinkles, or stains). It should be in keeping with acceptable standards of dress for the particular assignment of the employee on a given day; for example, an employee working in an office should wear business attire while those working in rugged areas will have different standards. Individuals working with equipment must comply with County safety codes.

Shoes: Shoes shall be professional and complementary to the business environment. Safety shall be considered when selecting shoes for business wear. Heels, wedges, and platforms over 3 inches, flip flops, and house slippers are not acceptable in any Department of Animal Services work environment. Heels, open toed shoes, and boots may be acceptable dependent upon the safety issues involved in the work environment.
Identification badges: County issued identification badges and badges issued to Animal Control Officers must be worn at all times.

Political messages: Recognizing the non-partisan and neutral position the Department of Animal Services must maintain while working in a political environment, employees shall not wear apparel or buttons with a political message in support of political candidates, written religious statements, or miscellaneous slogans. Non-partisan union buttons are acceptable. Buttons provided by Animal Services as part of an advertising or communications program are permissible.

Miscellaneous: Non-prescription sunglasses are not to be worn indoors. Employees who come to work in street clothes and change prior to starting work are only subject to dress code requirements while on the job. Only department issued hats are permitted.
Reimbursement for damaged clothing: Refer to Board of Supervisors Policy C-5 for guidelines.

sections.heading: Administration Dress Code (Attachment II)
steps: Clothing may be any color, pattern, and fabric which is in fashion and appropriate for business wear.

Shirts should be buttoned conservatively, and shirts with shirttails should be tucked into trousers. Exceptions include blouses or shirts without shirttails designed to be worn outside. Any slits in skirts and dresses should be conservative and in good taste.
Jeans may be worn only on Fridays. Exceptions are a department or agency sponsored event during which jeans can be worn, or work on a project where durable clothing is necessary.
Acceptable hemline lengths range from 2 to 3 inches above the knee to ankle length.

Unacceptable office attire includes, but is not limited to, shorts, overalls, sweat suits, exercise or warm-up pants, bare midriffs, bare backs, and tank tops. Low necklines, obvious bralessness, and very sheer fabric are also considered unacceptable.

Blouses and shirts may not contain vendor logos, emblems, or advertisements. Blouses and shirts with imprinted messages, political statements, or humorous or novelty phrases may be offensive to others and are unacceptable.

Casual Fridays: Employees may dress down on Fridays unless the nature of the person's position, the discretion of a division manager, or contact with the public and customer departments dictates otherwise. Casual Friday dress code is the same as the provisions above with this exception: employees generally required to wear business attire may dress in jeans. Where permitted, jeans or overalls of a denim material should be neat and clean. Torn, faded, or frayed jeans are not permitted.

sections.heading: Field Services Dress Code (Attachment III)

steps.step: The Field Services ACO uniform consists of the following:
sub: Dark blue polo shirt, provided by DAS annually.
Dark blue BDU pants, provided by DAS annually.
Black leather belt, provided by DAS as needed.
Riverside County Animal Control black hat or plain black hat, provided by DAS annually or purchased by employee.
ACO black jacket, provided by DAS at initial hire or as needed.
Body armor, provided by DAS at initial hire or as needed.
7 point star badge with belt holder, provided by DAS at initial hire or as needed.
Dark blue DAS T-shirt with star logo and RCDAS on back, provided by DAS annually.
Dark blue or black crew neck undershirt, purchased by employee.
Black leather tactical/service boots, purchased by employee.
Black boot socks, purchased by employee.
Duty belt, purchased by employee (optional).

steps.step: Dark blue polo shirt (short or long sleeved):
sub: Wear the polo shirt at all times while on duty, tucked into the BDU pants.
Keep the polo shirt clean and free of any foul odors.
Do not alter or modify the polo shirt in any way unless approved by DAS.
Additional adornments may only be worn after approval has been granted by supervision.
Keep the bottom two buttons of the polo shirt buttoned.

steps.step: Dark blue BDU pants:
sub: Wear BDU pants at all times while on duty.
Keep BDU pants clean and free of any foul odors.
It is the officer's discretion whether the pants are bloused or not. If they are not bloused, the draw strings at the ankles must be removed.
BDU pants are to fit properly with pant hemlines above the floor.

steps: Black leather belt: Wear the black leather belt correctly around the waist.

steps.step: Riverside County Animal Control hat, black or plain black hat (ball cap or beanie):
sub: If the ACO wears a hat, it must be a black Riverside County DAS issued ball cap with all stickers and tags removed, or a solid black ball cap.
Wear all hats with the bill forward, clean, and free of any foul odors.
Remove hats during all court appearances and hearings.
Do not alter or adorn hats without supervisor approval.
Employees may purchase additional black ball caps.
Employees may also purchase and wear a plain, solid black beanie style hat during inclement weather.

steps.step: Black jacket:
sub: Only the DAS issued jacket may be worn during inclement weather.
Keep the jacket clean and free of any foul odors.
Do not alter or adorn jackets without supervisor approval.

steps.step: Body armor:
sub: Body armor is custom ordered for the particular wearer.
Maintain body armor according to the manufacturer's instructions.
Wear body armor at the discretion of the ACO or when mandated due to special circumstances by field supervision.
Keep body armor clean and free of foul odors.
If the armor is not being worn while on duty, keep it in the ACO's locker at their assigned station.

steps: Star badge (7 point) with badge holder: Wear the badge and holder clipped to the belt on the front left side of the body.
Dark blue DAS T-shirt: The issued DAS T-shirt can be worn during special events or circumstances with supervisor approval (see Exceptions). Keep it clean and free of any foul odors.

steps.step: Dark blue or black undershirt (purchased by employee):
sub: Wear an undershirt at all times while on duty.
Keep the undershirt clean and free of any foul odors.
Only crew neck undershirts will be accepted.
Printing on T-shirts must not be visible under the uniform polo shirt.

steps.step: Black leather tactical/service boots (purchased by employee):
sub: Wear black leather boots at all times while on duty.
Tie laces neatly.
Keep black leather boots clean and free of any foul odors.
Polish boots when attending events, meetings, or court appearances.

steps: Black boot socks (purchased by employee): Wear black boot socks at all times while on duty, clean and free of any foul odors.
Duty belt (purchased by employee, optional): Wear the duty belt correctly around the waist.

Grooming for men and women: DAS recognizes the minimum standards for grooming established in both SEIU and LIUNA MOUs as well as DAS Policy 000-06. Terminology used in all three of these documents includes words such as neat, clean, modest, conservative, and professional. The following guidelines are written within the MOU and DAS policy requirements but clarify in more detail what is considered appropriate and professional for the type of facial hair worn by ACOs.

Facial hair: Facial hair (beards, mustaches, goatees, and sideburns) must be clean, neat, properly trimmed for the style, modest, conservative, and free from offending odors. Facial hair must be kept no longer than 1/2 inch in length. This allows flexibility to the wearer but prevents distractions or other safety hazards. No facial hair is to be worn under the jaw line or on the neck. To set the utmost professional look of DAS Field Services ACOs, it is strongly suggested men be clean shaven. If mustaches are worn, they should be traditional, defined as closely trimmed and on the upper lip only. Sideburns should be kept no longer than the ear lobe.
Men's hair styles: Hair must be neatly trimmed, off the ears and off the collar.

steps.step: Women's hair styles:
sub: Short hair must be kept off the face.
Bangs are to be kept out of the eyes.
Medium to long hair must be pulled back neatly and kept out of the face, such as in a bun, pony tail, braid, or clip.
Medium to long hair must be kept in such a style that it will hang no longer than shoulder length.

steps: Nails: Nails must be kept clean and neatly manicured. If used, only modest polish color is acceptable.

Accessories: All belt or duty belt accessories are to be black or a solid dark color. Other accessories such as sunglasses, watches, or hair accessories must be a solid dark color. Lanyards should not be visible when there is the possibility of public contact.
Jewelry: Refer to the General Dress Code Guidelines of this policy.

Tattoos: All visible tattoos above the wrist shall be covered by a black or natural colored compression sleeve or product designed for similar usage. Officers may elect to wear a long sleeved uniform shirt as well.
Examples of proper wearing of the uniform: Women, see Exhibits A, B, C. Men, see Exhibits D, E, F.

steps.step: Exceptions: uniform modifications during special events or special circumstances.
sub: The uniform policy may be modified during special events such as adoption events, or special circumstances such as evacuations during fires or removing large numbers of animals due to warrants or cruelty cases. No matter the circumstances, the modification will be established by a supervisor.
Usage of body armor will be mandated when it has been determined that a threat exists or could exist on any particular site.
Modifications may also occur due to reasons beyond DAS control.

sections.heading: Shelter Services Dress Code (Attachment IV)
steps: Shelter Services employees will be provided five (5) uniforms, each consisting of a DAS T-shirt and BDU trousers. Worn out or damaged uniforms, as determined by the Director or designee, may be replaced by turning in the worn out or damaged article. All uniforms purchased by the County shall be returned by the employee upon termination. Additional uniforms may be purchased by the employee at his/her expense.
Tuck DAS T-shirts neatly into pants.
All Animal Care Technicians must wear black leather or nylon boots or shoes as part of their uniform.
BDU trousers must have the draw strings tied and hidden, or removed if not bloused. Boot laces must be hidden.

Baseball caps are permitted only if they are plain, black, dark blue, or blue in color, with the bill always facing forward. The only permissible logo on hats will be the Riverside County or department logo.

sections.heading: Veterinary Services Dress Code (Attachment V)
steps: Scrubs may be in any pattern, color, and fabric. Pajama style scrubs are not acceptable.
Scrubs should be neat, clean, and in good repair. Worn out, torn, or damaged uniform scrubs are not acceptable attire.
Any T-shirts worn under scrubs should be clean and free of visible printing.
When selecting footwear, safety is always to be considered first. Shoes should have a non-skid sole and socks must be worn at all times.
The following shoe styles are not permitted: thongs, clogs, house slippers, sandals, and open-toed shoes.

sections.heading: Riverside County Sheriff Work Release Program (Attachment VII)

steps.step: Appropriate attire while providing service to the county's Department of Animal Services:
sub: Comfortable shoes; no open toes.
Casual shirt or blouse; no tank tops or halters.
Work pants: full length jeans, denim, khaki, etc.
No baggy clothes; clothes must fit to body.
Baseball caps must be worn forward only.
No obscenities or gang references may be used.

sections.heading: Volunteer Staff Dress Code (Attachment VIII)

steps.step: Volunteer dress code:
sub: Designated volunteer shirt.
Name badge.
Jeans or other heavy-weight fabric pants.
No sweat or jogging pants may be worn.
No loose fitting or baggy shirts.
Dress and grooming will be modest, professional, non-provocative, and in good taste so as not to offend co-workers, county employees, or the public.

steps: Inappropriate attire is at the discretion of the Director or designee. Volunteers wearing inappropriate attire may be sent home to change or be released from service.

sections.heading: Enforcement
steps: Supervisors and managers are responsible for enforcing the dress code policy. Employees must be in compliance at all times.

Managers and supervisors will counsel anyone whose dress and general personal appearance do not reflect the spirit of the dress code and guidelines. Violations will not be tolerated, and the violator(s) may be requested to return home to change into more appropriate attire.
An employee must be given written notice for the first incident of wearing improper attire. Thereafter, the employee can be sent home with loss of pay as a result of a violation.

For the first occurrence, employees will be permitted an hour on company time to go home, change, and return to work. Time in excess of one (1) hour will be on the employee's own time. If an employee has no time to cover the time in excess of one (1) hour, or has been previously counseled for taking longer than the allotted time, he/she may be subject to absent time without pay.
Failure to comply with dress code provisions as outlined will result in the implementation of progressive disciplinary measures.

and professional for the type of facial hair worn by acos. facial hair: facial hair (beards, mustaches, goatees, and sideburns) must be clean, neat, properly trimmed for the style, modest, conservative, and free from offending odors. facial hair must be kept no longer than 1/2 inch in length. this allows flexibility to the wearer but prevents distractions or other safety hazards. no facial hair is to be worn under the jaw line or on the neck. to set the utmost professional look of das field services acos, it is strongly suggested men be clean shaven. if mustaches are worn, they should be traditional, defined as closely trimmed and on the upper lip only. sideburns should be kept no longer than the ear lobe. men's hair styles: hair must be neatly trimmed, off the ears and off the collar. {"step": "women's hair styles:", "sub": ["short hair must be kept off the face.", "bangs are to be kept out of the eyes.", "medium to long hair must be pulled back neatly and kept out of the face, such as in a bun, pony tail, braid, or clip.", "medium to long hair must be kept in such a style that it will hang no longer than shoulder length."]} nails: nails must be kept clean and neatly manicured. if used, only modest polish color is acceptable. accessories: all belt or duty belt accessories are to be black or a solid dark color. other accessories such as sunglasses, watches, or hair accessories must be a solid dark color. lanyards should not be visible when there is the possibility of public contact. jewelry: refer to the general dress code guidelines of this policy. tattoos: all visible tattoos above the wrist shall be covered by a black or natural colored compression sleeve or product designed for similar usage. officers may elect to wear a long sleeved uniform shirt as well. examples of proper wearing of the uniform: women, see exhibits a, b, c. men, see exhibits d, e, f. {"step": "exceptions: uniform modifications during special events or special circumstances.", "sub": ["the uniform policy may be modified during special events such as adoption events, or special circumstances such as evacuations during fires or removing large numbers of animals due to warrants or cruelty cases. no matter the circumstances, the modification will be established by a supervisor.", "usage of body armor will be mandated when it has been determined that a threat exists or could exist on any particular site.", "modifications may also occur due to reasons beyond das control."]} shelter services dress code (attachment iv) shelter services employees will be provided five (5) uniforms, each consisting of a das t-shirt and bdu trousers. worn out or damaged uniforms, as determined by the director or designee, may be replaced by turning in the worn out or damaged article. all uniforms purchased by the county shall be returned by the employee upon termination. additional uniforms may be purchased by the employee at his/her expense. tuck das t-shirts neatly into pants. all animal care technicians must wear black leather or nylon boots or shoes as part of their uniform. bdu trousers must have the draw strings tied and hidden, or removed if not bloused. boot laces must be hidden. baseball caps are permitted only if they are plain, black, dark blue, or blue in color, with the bill always facing forward. the only permissible logo on hats will be the riverside county or department logo. veterinary services dress code (attachment v) scrubs may be in any pattern, color, and fabric. pajama style scrubs are not acceptable. scrubs should be neat, clean, and in good repair. worn out, torn, or damaged uniform scrubs are not acceptable attire. any t-shirts worn under scrubs should be clean and free of visible printing. when selecting footwear, safety is always to be considered first. shoes should have a non-skid sole and socks must be worn at all times. the following shoe styles are not permitted: thongs, clogs, house slippers, sandals, and open-toed shoes. riverside county sheriff work release program (attachment vii) {"step": "appropriate attire while providing service to the county's department of animal services:", "sub": ["comfortable shoes; no open toes.", "casual shirt or blouse; no tank tops or halters.", "work pants: full length jeans, denim, khaki, etc.", "no baggy clothes; clothes must fit to body.", "baseball caps must be worn forward only.", "no obscenities or gang references may be used."]} volunteer staff dress code (attachment viii) {"step": "volunteer dress code:", "sub": ["designated volunteer shirt.", "name badge.", "jeans or other heavy-weight fabric pants.", "no sweat or jogging pants may be worn.", "no loose fitting or baggy shirts.", "dress and grooming will be modest, professional, non-provocative, and in good taste so as not to offend co-workers, county employees, or the public."]} inappropriate attire is at the discretion of the director or designee. volunteers wearing inappropriate attire may be sent home to change or be released from service. enforcement supervisors and managers are responsible for enforcing the dress code policy. employees must be in compliance at all times. managers and supervisors will counsel anyone whose dress and general personal appearance do not reflect the spirit of the dress code and guidelines. violations will not be tolerated, and the violator(s) may be requested to return home to change into more appropriate attire. an employee must be given written notice for the first incident of wearing improper attire. thereafter, the employee can be sent home with loss of pay as a result of a violation. for the first occurrence, employees will be permitted an hour on company time to go home, change, and return to work. time in excess of one (1) hour will be on the employee's own time. if an employee has no time to cover the time in excess of one (1) hour, or has been previously counseled for taking longer than the allotted time, he/she may be subject to absent time without pay. failure to comply with dress code provisions as outlined will result in the implementation of progressive disciplinary measures. documents.title: Operational Hours, Meal and Rest Periods
documents.purpose: To set the rules for Department of Animal Services staff for meal and rest periods within work schedules. Managers, supervisors, and leads are responsible to ensure their non-exempt employees are provided the meal and break periods established by this policy.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Department of Animal Services employees.
documents.supersedes: CHA Policy P-5
references: Board Policy C-6, Alternate Work Schedules

definitions.def: The days and times services are provided to the organization's customers/clients.
The days and times that staff are assigned to work.

sections.heading: Background
steps: Board Policy C-6, Alternate Work Schedules, requires all departments to provide public services 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. five days per week. It defines alternate work schedules and provides the procedure for their use and approval. Departments have the authority to establish operational hours and work schedules within the policy boundaries.

sections.heading: Meal Periods
steps: Provide all non-exempt employees who work a shift of at least five (5) hours a 30 minute minimum meal break.
Do not pay employees for meal periods; employees shall not work during this time.
Do not schedule meal periods within one hour of a shift start or end.
If there is more than one employee in an office, stagger lunch breaks to allow for service continuation during the lunch period.

sections.heading: Rest Periods
steps: Consider rest periods as time worked. Employees who fail to take a rest break shall forgo it.

Standard practice in most department work environments provides non-exempt employees a fifteen (15) minute rest period every four (4) hours of work scheduled. Some work may require a different practice due to work environment, tasks performed, and business requirements.
At a minimum, entitle non-exempt employees to one fifteen (15) minute rest period every four (4) working hours.
Ensure that breaks are staggered so that normal business is not disrupted.
Schedule rest periods in accordance with departmental requirements, but in no instance shall rest periods be scheduled within one (1) hour of a shift start, end, or meal period.
Rest periods may not be cumulative or used to report to work late or leave early.
Grant employees required to work beyond their regular work schedule a ten (10) minute rest period for each additional two (2) hours worked.
Managers, supervisors, and leads have the authority to establish rest period duration and frequency within these guidelines.

sections.heading: Example of Common Schedules
steps: Clinical staff, non-veterinarian: 5/40 work schedule; hours of operation 7:40 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday through Friday.
Clinical staff, veterinarian: 9/80 work schedule; potential animal care needs 24 hours, 7 days, in addition to clinical hours.
Phone center staff: 5/40 work schedule; hours of operation 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
Shelter admissions staff: 5/40 work schedule; hours of operation 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Monday through Saturday.

Animal care workers, various shelter based positions: 5/40 work schedule; public shelter hours 11:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday through Saturday (except Blythe: Monday through Friday 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m., but open to 7:00 p.m. on Wednesday).
Animal Control Officers: 9/80 work schedule; hours 7:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
Administrative staff: 9/80 work schedule; hours 8:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.

Each of the above recommendations is to meet the business needs of the department in keeping with the operational hours. Placing staff on 5/40 schedules provides greater flexibility and maximum coverage for customer service and animal care. Placing administrative and veterinary staff on the 9/80 schedule expands the opportunities to provide support to staff working seven days per week.

campusVariations.variation: Public shelter hours Monday through Friday 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m., open to 7:00 p.m. on Wednesday (per 2012 schedule table).

related.name: Board of Supervisors Policy C-6, Alternate Work Schedules

documents.title: Department of Animal Services Smoking Policy
documents.purpose: To establish standards for protecting the health of DAS employees and the public they serve in regard to smoking, in compliance with the California Health and Safety Code, County Board of Supervisors policy, and current local ordinances. Its intent is to ban smoking within all DAS buildings, facilities, and County vehicles, and to limit smoking to designated outdoor areas.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Department of Animal Services employees.
documents.supersedes: CHA Policy A-9
references: Board of Supervisors Policy A-23
California Health and Safety Code Section 1234 (citation as it appears in the original; verify)
County Ordinance 866
DAS Policy 000-46, Department Employees Interaction with the Work Release Program

definitions.def: Any building or structure that is owned, leased, or otherwise operated by the County of Riverside DAS, and the land/property belonging to that building or structure, including County vehicles.

The carrying or holding of a lighted pipe, cigar, or cigarette of any kind, including electronic cigarettes, or any other lighted smoking equipment. The lighting, emitting, or exhaling of smoke from a pipe, cigar, or cigarette of any kind, including electronic cigarettes.

sections.heading: Background
steps: Research has shown the harmful health effects of smoking and the potential danger of passive exposure in smoke-filled atmospheres. The fact that employees' and the public's health may be endangered at a Department of Animal Services (DAS) work site due to passive inhalation of cigarette smoke should not be ignored. DAS has the special responsibility to establish and maintain a safe and healthful environment for DAS employees and the public that we serve.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Smoking is prohibited in all DAS buildings, facilities, and County vehicles, to provide optimal standards of health and safety for all DAS employees and the public.
Smoking may be permitted in outdoor areas designated by DAS management. Such areas will be prominently marked.

sections.heading: Smoking and Non-Smoking Standards

steps.step: Restrict smoking in outdoor areas to locations away from building entrances.
sub: Signs: Post signs conspicuously near all major entrances stating that smoking is prohibited within the building or facility per County Ordinance 866. It is the responsibility of DAS management to arrange for the posting of the signs to enforce this policy.

Waste containers: Make containers for disposal of smoking materials, such as cigarette butts, available at designated outdoor areas away from entrances to DAS buildings, facilities, and County vehicles.

steps: DAS employees will not smoke in the designated smoking areas for the Work Release participants or where customer service is provided.
Do not smoke anywhere within any DAS building, facility, or County vehicle.

Do not smoke at or near entrances to DAS facilities, buildings, or County vehicles, or outside of the designated smoking areas as referenced on the attached Exhibits A (Riverside), B (San Jacinto), C (Coachella Valley), and D (Blythe) shelter sites. All employees will show courtesy to others and clean up all their smoking debris or trash.

Per DAS Policy 000-46, Department Employees Interaction with the Work Release Program, DAS will designate smoking areas only to be used by the Work Release participants as referenced on Exhibits A, B, and C.

To assist employees, the County's Health Education program offers a Smoking Cessation Program for County employees. Employees are authorized to attend the program without charge and on County time (refer to Board of Supervisors Policy A-23 for more information).
Any DAS employee violating this policy shall be subject to the implementation of progressive disciplinary measures, up to and including discharge.

campusVariations.variation: Designated employee and Work Release smoking areas shown on Exhibit A site map.
Designated employee and Work Release smoking areas shown on Exhibit B site map.
Designated employee and Work Release smoking areas shown on Exhibit C site map.
Designated employee smoking areas shown on Exhibit D site map.

related.name: DAS Policy 000-46, Department Employees Interaction with the Work Release Program

Appendix B: San Jacinto campus site map with designated smoking areas (Exhibit B of original; map image not reproduced in this migration)
Appendix C: Coachella Valley Animal Campus site map with designated smoking areas (Exhibit C of original; map image not reproduced in this migration)
Appendix D: Blythe campus site map with designated smoking areas (Exhibit D of original; map image not reproduced in this migration)

documents.title: Feeding Protocol
documents.purpose: In the pursuit of improved animal diets and proper routine feeding schedules, these guidelines have been established. All shelter animals, regardless of their term of residence, should receive a good-quality, balanced diet that is appropriate for their life stage. Food selected should be produced by a company that guarantees complete and balanced nutrition.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Animal Services Field and Shelter Services employees.
documents.supersedes: Policy 300-09, effective October 7, 2009 (revised from July 11, 2006)

sections.heading: General Guidelines
steps: Offer all animals water at all times.
Maintain a sufficient number of adequately sized stainless steel food and water bowls to accommodate all animals.

Feed puppies and kittens who are six (6) to twelve (12) weeks of age three (3) times per day. Feed puppies and kittens who are twelve (12) weeks to twelve (12) months twice a day. Feed adults at least once a day.
Store all food in air-tight plastic containers to prevent spoilage and to avoid attracting rodents and insects.
Use only animal food that is in-date and enclosed in manufacturer's sealed bags.
Make every attempt to keep the brand and type of food consistent for each species of animal.

sections.heading: Newly Impounded Animals
steps: Offer all new impounds water while being processed. The processing time for new impounds shall not exceed one hour.

sections.heading: Newborn Animals
steps: Feed all newborns without mothers every two to four hours, depending on age, with an appropriate milk replacer, in a foster care or nursery environment separate from the general population.
House all newborns with mothers separate from the general population, with an increase in the food intake for the mother to support milk production demands.

sections.heading: Sick or Injured Animals
steps: Keep all sick or injured animals' diets under the supervision and direction of veterinary services. Make changes to the diet of a clinic patient only under the direction of the veterinary staff.

sections.heading: Puppies and Dogs

steps.step: Dry food can be made palatable to the greatest number of dogs as follows:
sub: Mix one-half can of wet food with one pound of dry dog food for each adult dog. Add enough warm water to mix well; mix and feed.
Moisten dry puppy food for young pups.
Dry puppy chow and adult dog food may be left in self-feeders.

steps: If animals are fed between 1:00 p.m. and 3:00 p.m., their stools can be removed before closing and the dogs will be in clean runs overnight.

Feed puppies and dogs enough food to meet their nutritional needs based on such factors as body weight and label instructions. Check food bowls after approximately 20 minutes. If an animal has eaten all his/her food, place an additional small amount of food in the run or cage.

sections.heading: Kittens and Cats
steps: Feed cats and kittens dry or canned cat food. Do not feed dog food to cats (or vice versa).

Generic pet foods are not recommended. Often, the nutrients in generic pet foods are not readily available for digestion and absorption by an animal. Tests cited in the original policy showed that puppies and kittens fed generic pet foods had a greater incidence of illness, improper growth, and other physical abnormalities, and required one and one-third times as much food per pound of weight gained. Use only products that are made by major national pet food companies and are 100 percent nutritionally complete.

sections.heading: Small Critters and Birds
steps: Free feed all critters such as rabbits, rats, mice, guinea pigs, and others, along with birds, food appropriate to the diet of such species. Make water available at all times.

sections.heading: All Other Animals
steps: Feed other species not included in this protocol at the Chief Veterinarian's direction.

documents.title: Breaking Up Dog Fights
documents.purpose: To establish a universal and safe protocol for breaking up dog fights.
documents.authority: Not stated in the original SOP.
documents.scope: Kennel staff working in and around the kennels.
documents.supersedes: None. Original SOP effective October 20, 2009.
references: None cited in the original SOP.

definitions.def: A stocked station near the kennels holding the equipment listed in Section A for responding to dog fights.
A tool used to pry open a dog's mouth when the dog has bitten and will not release.

steps.step: Keep the following items stocked in safety stations near the kennels:
sub: Fog horns.
Direct Stop spray.
Two catch poles.
Break sticks.
Leather gloves.
Sorting panels (livestock).
Blanket.

sections.heading: B. Breaking Up a Dog Fight
steps: Stay calm and use the appropriate level of response for what is happening. Stay outside of the kennel.

steps.step: Start with loud noise to distract and separate the dogs:
sub: Raised voice.
Banging on the kennel door.
Dropping the guillotine door.
Fog horn.

steps: If fighting continues, spray the dogs in the face region with a steady stream of water from the hose.
If this does not stop the fighting, escort the public from the kennels and lock the doors. Call for help from additional staff.

steps.step: Use the following methods, entering the kennel only if you have assistance from other staff and it is necessary:
sub: Spray the aggressor in the face with water.
Spray the aggressor in the face with Direct Stop spray.
Use catch poles to remove and separate the dogs.
Use sorting panels to separate the dogs and shield yourself from being bitten.
Separate the dogs by grabbing their hind legs and lifting them off the ground while pulling them apart.
Toss a thick blanket on the dogs so they cannot see.

sections.heading: C. A Dog That Bites and Will Not Let Go

steps.step: If one of the dogs has grabbed onto and will not release the other dog, try the following methods:
sub: Spray the aggressor up the nose or in the ear canal with a steady stream of water from the hose.
Spray the aggressor directly in the face (eye and nose region) with Direct Stop spray. Do not use outside if windy; it may get in your eyes.

steps: As a last resort, put on gloves and use a break stick to pry open the dog's mouth. At least two people are needed; the other person should be ready to pull on the other dog as soon as the stick breaks the hold.

steps.step: Before using the methods above, you and your assistants should have a plan for how you will keep the dogs separated the instant the dog releases its grip on the other dog. Options include:
sub: Using a sorting panel.
Using the guillotine door.
Removing the dog being attacked from the kennel.
Tossing a heavy blanket on the aggressor so it can no longer see while you remove the other dog from the kennel.
Leashing both dogs prior to separating them; once separated, pull the dogs apart using the leashes.

sections.heading: D. After the Fight
steps: Easy to break up (verbal fight): If the fight was easily broken up with a loud sound, separate the dogs if needed with the guillotine door. Give them a chance to calm down while you write notes in Chameleon and check for previous fighting notes. If there are no other notes about fighting and you separated the dogs, you may try to reintroduce them. If the dog has been in one minor fight before, consider moving the dog to a different kennel. If the dog has been in two or more fights before, remove the dog, kennel the dog alone, and put a handwritten note on the card that says Dog Aggressive.

Fights that are harder to break up: Separate the dogs with the guillotine door, remove the aggressor, and place it in a kennel alone. Write notes in Chameleon and put a handwritten note on the card that says Dog Aggressive.

Fights that result in injuries: Separate the dogs with the guillotine door, remove the aggressor, and place it in a kennel alone. Write notes in Chameleon. Take the injured dog to the vet or place notes on the vet board. Come see or email behavior staff about the incident.

sections.heading: E. Notes in Chameleon
steps: Enter notes in Chameleon immediately after a dog fight. These notes are extremely important.

steps.step: Include the following information in the notes:
sub: Today's date.
Who started the fight and the ID number of the other dog(s) that were fighting.
How easy or hard the fight was to break up.
Injuries sustained by either of the dogs.
Your name (the first letter of your first name followed by your complete last name).
