revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2009 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.searchText: to establish a universal and safe protocol for breaking up dog fights. kennel staff working in and around the kennels. not stated in the original sop. safety station a stocked station near the kennels holding the equipment listed in section a for responding to dog fights. break stick a tool used to pry open a dog's mouth when the dog has bitten and will not release. a. safety stations {"step": "keep the following items stocked in safety stations near the kennels:", "sub": ["fog horns.", "direct stop spray.", "two catch poles.", "break sticks.", "leather gloves.", "sorting panels (livestock).", "blanket."]} b. breaking up a dog fight stay calm and use the appropriate level of response for what is happening. stay outside of the kennel. {"step": "start with loud noise to distract and separate the dogs:", "sub": ["raised voice.", "banging on the kennel door.", "dropping the guillotine door.", "fog horn."]} if fighting continues, spray the dogs in the face region with a steady stream of water from the hose. if this does not stop the fighting, escort the public from the kennels and lock the doors. call for help from additional staff. {"step": "use the following methods, entering the kennel only if you have assistance from other staff and it is necessary:", "sub": ["spray the aggressor in the face with water.", "spray the aggressor in the face with direct stop spray.", "use catch poles to remove and separate the dogs.", "use sorting panels to separate the dogs and shield yourself from being bitten.", "separate the dogs by grabbing their hind legs and lifting them off the ground while pulling them apart.", "toss a thick blanket on the dogs so they cannot see."]} c. a dog that bites and will not let go {"step": "if one of the dogs has grabbed onto and will not release the other dog, try the following methods:", "sub": ["spray the aggressor up the nose or in the ear canal with a steady stream of water from the hose.", "spray the aggressor directly in the face (eye and nose region) with direct stop spray. do not use outside if windy; it may get in your eyes."]} as a last resort, put on gloves and use a break stick to pry open the dog's mouth. at least two people are needed; the other person should be ready to pull on the other dog as soon as the stick breaks the hold. {"step": "before using the methods above, you and your assistants should have a plan for how you will keep the dogs separated the instant the dog releases its grip on the other dog. options include:", "sub": ["using a sorting panel.", "using the guillotine door.", "removing the dog being attacked from the kennel.", "tossing a heavy blanket on the aggressor so it can no longer see while you remove the other dog from the kennel.", "leashing both dogs prior to separating them; once separated, pull the dogs apart using the leashes."]} d. after the fight easy to break up (verbal fight): if the fight was easily broken up with a loud sound, separate the dogs if needed with the guillotine door. give them a chance to calm down while you write notes in chameleon and check for previous fighting notes. if there are no other notes about fighting and you separated the dogs, you may try to reintroduce them. if the dog has been in one minor fight before, consider moving the dog to a different kennel. if the dog has been in two or more fights before, remove the dog, kennel the dog alone, and put a handwritten note on the card that says dog aggressive. fights that are harder to break up: separate the dogs with the guillotine door, remove the aggressor, and place it in a kennel alone. write notes in chameleon and put a handwritten note on the card that says dog aggressive. fights that result in injuries: separate the dogs with the guillotine door, remove the aggressor, and place it in a kennel alone. write notes in chameleon. take the injured dog to the vet or place notes on the vet board. come see or email behavior staff about the incident. e. notes in chameleon enter notes in chameleon immediately after a dog fight. these notes are extremely important. {"step": "include the following information in the notes:", "sub": ["today's date.", "who started the fight and the id number of the other dog(s) that were fighting.", "how easy or hard the fight was to break up.", "injuries sustained by either of the dogs.", "your name (the first letter of your first name followed by your complete last name)."]} none cited in the original sop.

documents.title: CVAC Animal Care Technician (ACT) Duties
documents.purpose: To set out the standard operating procedures for Animal Care Technicians (ACTs) at the Coachella Valley Animal Campus (CVAC), covering daily cleaning and care by housing area, receiving, euthanasia support, dish room and food storage duties, Chameleon record keeping, adoptions, stray intake, and owner turn-ins.
documents.authority: Not stated in the original SOP.
documents.scope: Animal Care Technicians assigned to the Coachella Valley Animal Campus (CVAC).
documents.supersedes: None. Original SOP undated (content circa 2009 based on internal examples).
references: None cited in the original SOP.

definitions.def: Animal Care Technician.
Quarantine.
Isolation.
The disinfectant product used for cleaning kennels, cages, and floors. Requires 10 minutes of contact time.
Owner turn-in (owner surrender).
Return to owner.
Dead on arrival.

sections.heading: 1. Cat Quarantine (Cat QT)
steps: Read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request).
If the animal seems ill or has anything abnormal going on, contact the technician and write it on the treatment board located in receiving.
Make sure the Animal ID matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description of the animal.
Make sure the cat is in its feral cat den, then set the den outside of the cage.
Remove any soiled paper, towels, or toys.

Place dirty laundry in the basket. Shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. If the animal had panleukopenia, FeLV, or any other contagious disease, throw away the towel or bedding that was in with that animal.
Wipe out all excess debris from the cage. Wipe up any mess the cat made.
Replace the food and water bowls with fresh ones.
If the litter box is messy (diarrhea), replace it with a new one; otherwise scoop it and add more litter.
Place the cat den back in the cage and open the cat port hole.

steps.step: If the cage is empty:
sub: Spray the entire cage with Husky. It must have 10 minutes of contact time.
Wipe out the entire cage thoroughly, starting with the top of the cage, back of cage, side walls, and bottom surface. The doors need to be taken off and foamed, scrubbed, and rinsed.
Line the cage with newspaper.
Put a clean towel or blanket in the cage with a clean litter box and a clean set of food bowls.

steps: Make sure all empty cages are cleaned and ready for the next animal.
Take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. Once your area is cleaned, do the dishes from your area (see Dish Room section).
Take all dirty laundry to the laundry room.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. Make sure all trash bags are tied shut.
Clean all windows and sills.
Sweep the entire floor of excess debris. Pull all cages out from the wall and clean under them.
Mop using Husky. Empty the bucket when finished.
Put equipment back in its area. Report all broken equipment to your supervisor.
Clean the utility cart and restock all supplies.
If you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in Chameleon (see Chameleon Move-Out Procedures section).
Spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes.

sections.heading: 2. Dog Isolation (ISO) and Quarantine (QT)
steps: Read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). If any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board.
Make sure the Animal ID matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description.
Take the dog out of the kennel and hook the leash to the hook on the wall.
Remove soiled bedding, toys, and food and water dishes.

Place dirty laundry in the basket. Shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. If the animal had parvo or distemper, throw away the towel or bedding that was in with that animal.
Rinse out the kennel thoroughly, removing all excess debris.
Foam the entire kennel, including all kennel walls, doors, and water bowls, with Husky. Foam must have 10 minutes of contact time.
Scrub the entire kennel and wipe out the water bowls and the tops of the kennel walls.
Rinse out the entire kennel thoroughly.
Squeegee and place clean blankets in the kennel.
Place fresh water in the kennel. Check that the automatic water dish is working.
Return the dog to its original kennel.
Make sure all empty kennels are cleaned and ready for a new occupant.
Take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. Once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see Dish Room section).
Take dirty laundry to the laundry room.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. Make sure the trash bag is tied shut.
Rinse the entire floor of excess debris. Foam with Husky. Scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly.
Put equipment back in its assigned area. Report all broken equipment to your supervisor.
Restock all supplies.
If you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in Chameleon (see Chameleon Move-Out Procedures section).
Spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes.

sections.heading: 3. Puppy Room
steps: Read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). If any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board.
Make sure the Animal ID matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description.
Take the dog out of the cage and into a clean temporary one.
Remove soiled bedding, shredded paper, toys, and food and water dishes.
Place dirty laundry in the basket. Shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket.
Clean the cage thoroughly, removing all excess debris.
Spray the entire cage with Husky.
Wipe out the entire cage, including the top, sides, bottom, and cage door (inside and out).
Line the cage with newspaper and shredded paper.
Return the pup to its original cage.
Clean the temporary cage before the next pup goes in it.
Make sure puppies have fresh food and water at all times.
Make sure all empty kennels are cleaned and ready for a new occupant.
Take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. Once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see Dish Room section).
Take dirty laundry to the laundry room.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. Make sure all trash bags are tied shut.
Sweep the entire floor of excess debris. Mop with Husky. Scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly. Empty the mop bucket when finished.
Put equipment back in its assigned area. Report all broken equipment to your supervisor.
Restock all supplies.
If you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in Chameleon (see Chameleon Move-Out Procedures section).
Spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes.

sections.heading: 4. Available Cats and Critters
steps: Read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). If any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board.
Make sure the Animal ID matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description.

Leave the cat in the cage and clean around it (less stress on the animal). If the cage is a mess, remove the cat and put it in one of the blue crates. Clean the cage and put the cat back into its original cage.
Remove soiled bedding, shredded paper (for critters), and food and water bowls.

Place dirty laundry in the basket. Shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. If the bedding is not soiled, leave it in the cage with the cat; their scent is already on it and it will help the cat feel less stressed.

steps.step: If the cage is empty:
sub: Clean the cage thoroughly, removing all excess debris.
Spray the entire cage with Husky. It must have 10 minutes of contact time.

Wipe out the entire cage thoroughly, starting with the top of the cage, back of cage, side walls, bottom surface, and cage door (both sides). If it is an available cage, the doors need to be taken off and foamed, scrubbed, and rinsed.

steps: Return the cat or critter to its original cage with clean paper.
Clean the temporary cage before the next cat or critter goes in it.
Make sure cats and critters have fresh food and water at all times.
Make sure all empty cages are cleaned and ready for a new occupant.
Take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. Once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see Dish Room section).
Take dirty laundry to the laundry room.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. Make sure all bags are tied shut.
Sweep the entire floor of excess debris. Mop with Husky (once weekly with degreaser). Scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly. Empty the mop bucket when finished.
Put equipment back in its assigned area. Report all broken equipment to your supervisor.
Restock all supplies.
If you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in Chameleon (see Chameleon Move-Out Procedures section).
Spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes.

sections.heading: 5. Kennel A (Available Dogs) and Kennel B (Stray Dogs). The source SOP lists identical steps for both kennels.
steps: Read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). If any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board so veterinary staff can look at the animal.
Make sure the Animal ID matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description.
Lock all of the dogs outside.
Remove all bedding, toys, and food and water dishes.
Place dirty laundry in the basket. Shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket.
Rinse out the kennel thoroughly, removing all excess debris.
Lift the drain covers and make sure nothing is stuck under the lid. Disinfect with Husky.
Foam the entire kennel, including all kennel walls, doors, and water bowls, with Husky. Foam must have 10 minutes of contact time.
Scrub the entire kennel and wipe out the water bowls and the tops of the kennel walls.
Rinse out the entire kennel thoroughly and squeegee dry.
Check that the automatic water dish is working; if not, give each dog fresh water.
Lock all of the dogs inside and repeat the same steps for the outside runs.
Make sure all empty kennels are cleaned and ready for a new occupant.
Take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. Once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see Dish Room section).
Take dirty laundry to the laundry room.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back.
Rinse the entire floor of excess debris. Foam with Husky (once weekly with degreaser). Scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly.
Put equipment back in its assigned area. Report all broken equipment to your supervisor.
Restock all supplies.
Clean the counter tops and put away all items.
If you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in Chameleon (see Chameleon Move-Out Procedures section).
Spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes.

sections.heading: 6. Receiving
steps: Read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). If any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board so veterinary staff can look at the animal.
Once the technician has finished the intake of the animal, they will place their initials on the top right corner of the kennel card. The animal is then ready to be moved out.
If the animal has not already been weighed, weigh the animal and update the weight in Chameleon.
Make sure the kennel card has the boxes marked stating that the animal received its vaccinations, was scanned for a microchip, and has the correct due out date.
Make sure the animal's picture is clearly visible.
Move the animal to the appropriate place.
Hang the kennel card on the cage or kennel.
Write down the new kennel location on the kennel card.
Go to Chameleon and change the location (see Chameleon Move-Out Procedures section).
Remove soiled bedding, shredded paper, toys, and food and water bowls.

Place dirty laundry in the basket. Shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. Throw the bedding away if the animal had parvo, distemper, FeLV, panleukopenia, or any other contagious disease.
Clean the cage thoroughly, removing all excess debris.
Spray the entire cage with Husky. It must have 10 minutes of contact time.

Wipe out the entire cage thoroughly, starting with the top of the cage, back of cage, side walls, bottom surface, and cage door (both sides). At the end of the day the doors need to be taken off and foamed, scrubbed, and rinsed.

The ACT assigned to receiving will carry a radio with them at all times unless they are on a break. The person with the radio will respond to customer service calls, including but not limited to RTOs, OTIs, strays, and DOAs.
Assist the technician with taking pictures, vaccinations, or microchips when needed.
Move all animals out of receiving as soon as possible. Animals should not be left in receiving overnight without prior approval from the senior, supervisor, or veterinary staff.
Give any animal being left overnight a clean litter box (if needed) and fresh food and water.
Pull all cages out from the wall nightly; sweep and mop the floors. Empty the mop bucket when finished.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back.
Restock the supply cart before your shift ends.

sections.heading: 7. Euthanasia
steps: The veterinary technician will prepare a daily list of the animals that need to be euthanized.
On occasion you will receive owner-requested euthanasias; these take priority over the regular list.
Make sure you have the correct paperwork from the front office and that it is clearly marked for euthanasia (owner requested). Make sure there is a signature on the request.

Once you get the list from the veterinary staff, go to the areas where the animals are being held and bring them to the euthanasia room. The second person on the team is to also check the list against the animals being brought into the room.
Make sure the E-Jay bands (paper collars) are attached to the kennel card.
Never euthanize an animal in front of another animal. Pull the curtain down over the kennels of the animals that are waiting.
Make sure the animal you have is in fact the correct animal. Check the Animal ID and the description. The second person on the team is also to check the Animal ID and the description.
Pull up the correct dosage and record it in the euthanasia log book.
Outcome the animal in Chameleon (see Chameleon Outcome Procedures for Euthanasia section).
Line each barrel with a black trash bag. Place all animals gently into the lined barrel. Once the barrel is full, return it to the freezer.
Check all kennel cards against the euthanasia log book before you leave. Make sure all of the animals are logged properly with the Animal ID, dose, and your initials.
Once you are done for the day, run another pre-euthanasia list and compare it to the list you were working from. Make sure all of the animals have been taken care of before you leave.
There will always be two euthanasia-certified technicians in the euthanasia room at all times performing the euthanasias.
Make sure all barrels are in the freezer.
Restock all supplies (Husky, syringes, paper towels, etc.).
Clean and disinfect all cages.
Take all dirty laundry to the laundry room.
Remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back.
Sweep and mop the floors; empty the mop bucket when finished.
Lock up the safe. Make sure both the exterior and interior doors are locked.

sections.heading: 8. Dish Room
steps: Before you run dishes or litter pans through the dish washer, make sure all food and fecal matter has been scraped off.
For dishes that have hard food stuck on them, soak them in the sink or a barrel with Husky.
Wear gloves and use a scrub brush or sponge to wipe the inside and outside of the bowls.
Stack the dishes on the tray, gently overlapping them. Shut the door; the unit is automatic. Once the light is off, open the door and stack the clean dishes on the rack to dry.
Do not overlap the dishes while they are wet. Allow them to dry thoroughly, then stack them.
For litter pans, follow the same steps as above.
Do not soak dishes and litter pans together. Clean all of the food bowls first, then the litter pans.
Clean out the food trap daily.
Clean the top and sides of the dish washer and the prep area.
Sweep the entire area and mop with Husky daily.

sections.heading: 9. Food Storage Area
steps: Make sure all trash has been taken out to the container first thing in the morning and at the end of your shift.
Make sure all litter, newspaper, and food are neatly stacked in the appropriate place.
Once you are finished with the forklift, park it in the right space and return the key. Only certified employees are allowed to use the forklift.
Keep all supplies and storage items neatly stacked.
Sweep the floor daily.

sections.heading: 10. Chameleon Move-Out Procedures (updating a kennel location)
steps: If Chameleon has not been opened for the day, double click on the Chameleon icon.
Enter your location, user ID, and password, then click OK.
Click on Windows, scroll to Kennels, and left click.
When the kennel screen appears, place the cursor in the Animal ID field and type in the ID.
Press F12 (find). Verify the kennel record showing is the one you need, by Animal ID and description.
Move the cursor to the Kennel No field and press F4 (popup).
Highlight the location, then click OK.
The colored dot in the upper left corner should be yellow and read Modified Fields. Press F8 (update).
When the message box asking if you are sure you want to update appears, click OK. The colored dot in the upper left corner should now be green and read Store/Update OK.
Each time you leave the computer, log off to prevent other users from using your logon ID: go to File and click Sign In, which returns you to the logon screen.

sections.heading: 11. Chameleon Outcome Procedures for Euthanasia
steps: Type in the Animal ID and press F12 (find).
Make sure the Animal ID and description match the animal's kennel card (sex, color, etc.).
Read all notes in the computer for holds, Do Not PTS notations, and similar restrictions.
Go to the outcome field. EUTH should already be there; if not, press F4 and select EUTH.
Make sure there is a subtype (a reason for the euthanasia).
Enter the amount used under Dose, enter your initials under By, and enter the time of euthanasia.
Press F8 to store the changes.

sections.heading: 12. Adoption Steps
steps: Approach and greet the customers and answer any questions they may have. Inform customers of the price of an adoption and what is included in the price.

steps.step: If the customer is interested in viewing an animal, the adoption counselor must make sure the animal is available for adoption by following these steps:
sub: Log into Chameleon and open the Kennel Window.
Go to Animal ID, type in the Animal ID number, and press F12 to search for the kennel record.
Go to Due Out Date to make sure the animal is available.
Click on the Notes icon and read all notes.
If the animal is available, it can be taken out of its kennel to be visited within the Get Acquainted area.
After the visit, if the customer is interested in adoption, the adoption counselor will go over the adoption checklist with the customer.
Have the customer completely fill out the adoption application; adoptions staff will review it to determine if the customer is qualified to adopt the animal.
Upon qualification, the adopter should completely fill out the Adoption Agreement, the two-page Animal Samaritans Surgery Consent Form, the microchip form, and the customer service survey.

steps: Staff must review all paperwork to make sure all information is complete and correct.
The adoption counselor must give the adopter a DVD and an information packet corresponding to the animal adopted.
The adoption counselor must place a red tag with the Animal ID, the adopter's name, and phone number on it.
Write the word ADOPTED across the kennel card.

steps.step: The adoption counselor will walk the adopter to the front counter and explain the policies and procedures, and instruct the customer where to place the customer service survey:
sub: Explain the 7-day policy.
Refer the customer to the behaviorist for any questions regarding behavior issues.
Explain that there is a cost for any medications given within the 7-day period.

steps: Schedule the animal for the next available surgery date and make sure to let the adopter know the date and time of pick-up.
Thank the customer.

sections.heading: 13. Stray Intake (Chameleon)
steps: If Chameleon has not been opened yet, go to an available computer and double click on the Chameleon icon.
Enter your location, user ID, and password, then click OK.
Click on Windows, scroll to Kennel, and left click. When the kennel screen appears, press F11 to clear.
Place the cursor in the Kennel No field, press F4 (popup), and select the correct location. Use receiving if the kennel location is not yet known.
Tab to Status and press F4; choose an appropriate status.

steps.step: Tab to the Tag No field:
sub: If the animal has identification: press F4 and enter all information. Click the green check mark at the top of the screen to link the information back to the kennel screen.
If the animal has no identification: leave the field blank.

steps: Tab to the Animal ID field and press F4 (popup). Fill in all known fields: animal name (if known), type, sex, age, color, breed, condition, status, and bite (Y if the animal bit anyone, N if not). Once all information has been entered correctly, click the green check mark at the top of the page.

Tab to the Source ID field and press F4 (popup). Find or store a person record for the person responsible for the animal coming into the shelter. Once all information has been entered correctly, click the green check mark at the top of the page.
Tab to Intake Type, press F4 (popup), and choose STRAY.
Tab to Intake Subtype, press F4 (popup), and choose the correct one.
Tab to Intake Condition, press F4, and choose the right one.
Tab to Date and press F5 to automatically set the current date.
Tab to Time and press F5 to automatically set the current time.
Tab to Due Out and type in the date according to the hold dates.
Tab to Crossing and enter the nearest cross street where the animal was found.
Tab to Jurisdiction, press F4, and select the correct one.
Tab to By and input your initials.
Tab into Comment and enter any important information from the reporting party.

sections.heading: 14. Owner Turn-In
steps: Front counter staff will have the owner fill out the Dog History form and link the animal to the owner in the computer.

Front counter staff will call the behaviorist, if available. If the behaviorist is not available, adoption counselors or ACTs will be called to the front counter to review the Dog History form and talk with owners.

If the owner has not indicated any aggression on the Dog History form, and the dog appears to be friendly (adoptable), staff will let the owner know that they can sign the dog over for potential adoption. The dog will still have to undergo a full behavior assessment prior to being placed up for adoption. If the dog is deemed unadoptable, the dog may be euthanized.

steps.step: If the dog is very fearful, will not walk on leash, and/or is not socialized (treatable), staff will inform the owner that the shelter cannot take the dog in for potential adoption. The owner can either:
sub: Take the dog home with the resource list.
Sign the dog over for euthanasia.

steps.step: If the owner has indicated that the dog is being turned in because of aggression toward people or other animals (untreatable), the owner can either:
sub: Take the dog home with the resource list.
Sign the dog over for euthanasia.

steps: Front counter staff will place a note in the computer stating that staff has authorized the dog as OK for potential adoption. Example: [staff initials]: OK to OTI adopt per [authorizing staff member].

Staff will put a note in the computer as soon as possible after talking with the owner, describing the reason for turn-in, the dog's observed behavior, and the outcome offered. The source SOP includes three example memos dated March 20, 2009 covering an adoptable dog, a fearful (treatable) dog, and an aggressive (untreatable) dog.

steps.step: If the dog is a bully breed (pit bull, pit bull mix, American pit bull terrier, American Staffordshire terrier, bull terrier, Staffordshire bull terrier, miniature bull terrier, American bulldog, and mixes thereof), staff must conduct a dog-to-dog assessment prior to taking the dog in for potential adoption. Staff will take the dog to the SAFER room and, with the help of another staff member and an adoptable helper dog of the same sex and size, assess the dog's dog-to-dog behavior:
sub: If there is a question about the dog's behavior with the first helper, staff may get a second helper dog.

If the dog is loose, wiggly, and relaxed or playful with the helper, the dog is OK to take in for potential adoption and must be kenneled with others immediately after being impounded and cleared by veterinary staff.

If the dog shows any aggression (growling or trying to bite the helper), a stiff body, or a hard stare, staff will inform the owner that the shelter is unable to take the dog in for potential adoption due to the dog being dog aggressive or dog reactive, because adoptable dogs cannot be kenneled alone. The dog will then need to be taken home by the owner with the resource list or signed over for euthanasia.

steps: Anytime multiple dogs are turned in together, they must not be kenneled together; they must be kenneled with other dogs.
Front counter staff will inform customers that there will be fees if the owner changes their mind, and that they have only 2 days before the dog could be placed up for adoption or euthanized.

campusVariations.variation: This SOP applies in its entirety to CVAC only. Kennel designations (Kennel A, Kennel B, puppy room, receiving) and the Animal Samaritans surgery arrangement are specific to that campus.

Appendix B: Behavior Assessment of Animals (page 19 of the source document is blank; content missing from source)

minutes of contact time. oti owner turn-in (owner surrender). rto return to owner. doa dead on arrival. 1. cat quarantine (cat qt) read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). if the animal seems ill or has anything abnormal going on, contact the technician and write it on the treatment board located in receiving. make sure the animal id matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description of the animal. make sure the cat is in its feral cat den, then set the den outside of the cage. remove any soiled paper, towels, or toys. place dirty laundry in the basket. shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. if the animal had panleukopenia, felv, or any other contagious disease, throw away the towel or bedding that was in with that animal. wipe out all excess debris from the cage. wipe up any mess the cat made. replace the food and water bowls with fresh ones. if the litter box is messy (diarrhea), replace it with a new one; otherwise scoop it and add more litter. place the cat den back in the cage and open the cat port hole. {"step": "if the cage is empty:", "sub": ["spray the entire cage with husky. it must have 10 minutes of contact time.", "wipe out the entire cage thoroughly, starting with the top of the cage, back of cage, side walls, and bottom surface. the doors need to be taken off and foamed, scrubbed, and rinsed.", "line the cage with newspaper.", "put a clean towel or blanket in the cage with a clean litter box and a clean set of food bowls."]} make sure all empty cages are cleaned and ready for the next animal. take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. once your area is cleaned, do the dishes from your area (see dish room section). take all dirty laundry to the laundry room. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. make sure all trash bags are tied shut. clean all windows and sills. sweep the entire floor of excess debris. pull all cages out from the wall and clean under them. mop using husky. empty the bucket when finished. put equipment back in its area. report all broken equipment to your supervisor. clean the utility cart and restock all supplies. if you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in chameleon (see chameleon move-out procedures section). spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes. 2. dog isolation (iso) and quarantine (qt) read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). if any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board. make sure the animal id matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description. take the dog out of the kennel and hook the leash to the hook on the wall. remove soiled bedding, toys, and food and water dishes. place dirty laundry in the basket. shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. if the animal had parvo or distemper, throw away the towel or bedding that was in with that animal. rinse out the kennel thoroughly, removing all excess debris. foam the entire kennel, including all kennel walls, doors, and water bowls, with husky. foam must have 10 minutes of contact time. scrub the entire kennel and wipe out the water bowls and the tops of the kennel walls. rinse out the entire kennel thoroughly. squeegee and place clean blankets in the kennel. place fresh water in the kennel. check that the automatic water dish is working. return the dog to its original kennel. make sure all empty kennels are cleaned and ready for a new occupant. take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see dish room section). take dirty laundry to the laundry room. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. make sure the trash bag is tied shut. rinse the entire floor of excess debris. foam with husky. scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly. put equipment back in its assigned area. report all broken equipment to your supervisor. restock all supplies. if you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in chameleon (see chameleon move-out procedures section). spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes. 3. puppy room read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). if any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board. make sure the animal id matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description. take the dog out of the cage and into a clean temporary one. remove soiled bedding, shredded paper, toys, and food and water dishes. place dirty laundry in the basket. shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. clean the cage thoroughly, removing all excess debris. spray the entire cage with husky. wipe out the entire cage, including the top, sides, bottom, and cage door (inside and out). line the cage with newspaper and shredded paper. return the pup to its original cage. clean the temporary cage before the next pup goes in it. make sure puppies have fresh food and water at all times. make sure all empty kennels are cleaned and ready for a new occupant. take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see dish room section). take dirty laundry to the laundry room. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. make sure all trash bags are tied shut. sweep the entire floor of excess debris. mop with husky. scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly. empty the mop bucket when finished. put equipment back in its assigned area. report all broken equipment to your supervisor. restock all supplies. if you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in chameleon (see chameleon move-out procedures section). spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes. 4. available cats and critters read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). if any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board. make sure the animal id matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description. leave the cat in the cage and clean around it (less stress on the animal). if the cage is a mess, remove the cat and put it in one of the blue crates. clean the cage and put the cat back into its original cage. remove soiled bedding, shredded paper (for critters), and food and water bowls. place dirty laundry in the basket. shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. if the bedding is not soiled, leave it in the cage with the cat; their scent is already on it and it will help the cat feel less stressed. {"step": "if the cage is empty:", "sub": ["clean the cage thoroughly, removing all excess debris.", "spray the entire cage with husky. it must have 10 minutes of contact time.", "wipe out the entire cage thoroughly, starting with the top of the cage, back of cage, side walls, bottom surface, and cage door (both sides). if it is an available cage, the doors need to be taken off and foamed, scrubbed, and rinsed."]} return the cat or critter to its original cage with clean paper. clean the temporary cage before the next cat or critter goes in it. make sure cats and critters have fresh food and water at all times. make sure all empty cages are cleaned and ready for a new occupant. take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see dish room section). take dirty laundry to the laundry room. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. make sure all bags are tied shut. sweep the entire floor of excess debris. mop with husky (once weekly with degreaser). scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly. empty the mop bucket when finished. put equipment back in its assigned area. report all broken equipment to your supervisor. restock all supplies. if you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in chameleon (see chameleon move-out procedures section). spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes. 5. kennel a (available dogs) and kennel b (stray dogs). the source sop lists identical steps for both kennels. read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). if any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board so veterinary staff can look at the animal. make sure the animal id matches what is on the kennel card, picture, and description. lock all of the dogs outside. remove all bedding, toys, and food and water dishes. place dirty laundry in the basket. shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. rinse out the kennel thoroughly, removing all excess debris. lift the drain covers and make sure nothing is stuck under the lid. disinfect with husky. foam the entire kennel, including all kennel walls, doors, and water bowls, with husky. foam must have 10 minutes of contact time. scrub the entire kennel and wipe out the water bowls and the tops of the kennel walls. rinse out the entire kennel thoroughly and squeegee dry. check that the automatic water dish is working; if not, give each dog fresh water. lock all of the dogs inside and repeat the same steps for the outside runs. make sure all empty kennels are cleaned and ready for a new occupant. take all dirty dishes to the dish area; do not wait until the end of the day. once your area is clean, do the dishes from your area (see dish room section). take dirty laundry to the laundry room. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. rinse the entire floor of excess debris. foam with husky (once weekly with degreaser). scrub, rinse, and squeegee thoroughly. put equipment back in its assigned area. report all broken equipment to your supervisor. restock all supplies. clean the counter tops and put away all items. if you change the animal's kennel location, update the record in chameleon (see chameleon move-out procedures section). spot clean every 30 to 45 minutes. 6. receiving read all of the information on the kennel card (for example: caution, painful, special diet request). if any problems (blood, vomit, etc.) are noticed in the kennel, contact the technician and notate it on the technician's board so veterinary staff can look at the animal. once the technician has finished the intake of the animal, they will place their initials on the top right corner of the kennel card. the animal is then ready to be moved out. if the animal has not already been weighed, weigh the animal and update the weight in chameleon. make sure the kennel card has the boxes marked stating that the animal received its vaccinations, was scanned for a microchip, and has the correct due out date. make sure the animal's picture is clearly visible. move the animal to the appropriate place. hang the kennel card on the cage or kennel. write down the new kennel location on the kennel card. go to chameleon and change the location (see chameleon move-out procedures section). remove soiled bedding, shredded paper, toys, and food and water bowls. place dirty laundry in the basket. shake out all towels and blankets of excess debris before placing them in the basket. throw the bedding away if the animal had parvo, distemper, felv, panleukopenia, or any other contagious disease. clean the cage thoroughly, removing all excess debris. spray the entire cage with husky. it must have 10 minutes of contact time. wipe out the entire cage thoroughly, starting with the top of the cage, back of cage, side walls, bottom surface, and cage door (both sides). at the end of the day the doors need to be taken off and foamed, scrubbed, and rinsed. the act assigned to receiving will carry a radio with them at all times unless they are on a break. the person with the radio will respond to customer service calls, including but not limited to rtos, otis, strays, and doas. assist the technician with taking pictures, vaccinations, or microchips when needed. move all animals out of receiving as soon as possible. animals should not be left in receiving overnight without prior approval from the senior, supervisor, or veterinary staff. give any animal being left overnight a clean litter box (if needed) and fresh food and water. pull all cages out from the wall nightly; sweep and mop the floors. empty the mop bucket when finished. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. restock the supply cart before your shift ends. 7. euthanasia the veterinary technician will prepare a daily list of the animals that need to be euthanized. on occasion you will receive owner-requested euthanasias; these take priority over the regular list. make sure you have the correct paperwork from the front office and that it is clearly marked for euthanasia (owner requested). make sure there is a signature on the request. once you get the list from the veterinary staff, go to the areas where the animals are being held and bring them to the euthanasia room. the second person on the team is to also check the list against the animals being brought into the room. make sure the e-jay bands (paper collars) are attached to the kennel card. never euthanize an animal in front of another animal. pull the curtain down over the kennels of the animals that are waiting. make sure the animal you have is in fact the correct animal. check the animal id and the description. the second person on the team is also to check the animal id and the description. pull up the correct dosage and record it in the euthanasia log book. outcome the animal in chameleon (see chameleon outcome procedures for euthanasia section). line each barrel with a black trash bag. place all animals gently into the lined barrel. once the barrel is full, return it to the freezer. check all kennel cards against the euthanasia log book before you leave. make sure all of the animals are logged properly with the animal id, dose, and your initials. once you are done for the day, run another pre-euthanasia list and compare it to the list you were working from. make sure all of the animals have been taken care of before you leave. there will always be two euthanasia-certified technicians in the euthanasia room at all times performing the euthanasias. make sure all barrels are in the freezer. restock all supplies (husky, syringes, paper towels, etc.). clean and disinfect all cages. take all dirty laundry to the laundry room. remove all garbage, break down all boxes, and take them to the trash container out back. sweep and mop the floors; empty the mop bucket when finished. lock up the safe. make sure both the exterior and interior doors are locked. 8. dish room before you run dishes or litter pans through the dish washer, make sure all food and fecal matter has been scraped off. for dishes that have hard food stuck on them, soak them in the sink or a barrel with husky. wear gloves and use a scrub brush or sponge to wipe the inside and outside of the bowls. stack the dishes on the tray, gently overlapping them. shut the door; the unit is automatic. once the light is off, open the door and stack the clean dishes on the rack to dry. do not overlap the dishes while they are wet. allow them to dry thoroughly, then stack them. for litter pans, follow the same steps as above. do not soak dishes and litter pans together. clean all of the food bowls first, then the litter pans. clean out the food trap daily. clean the top and sides of the dish washer and the prep area. sweep the entire area and mop with husky daily. 9. food storage area make sure all trash has been taken out to the container first thing in the morning and at the end of your shift. make sure all litter, newspaper, and food are neatly stacked in the appropriate place. once you are finished with the forklift, park it in the right space and return the key. only certified employees are allowed to use the forklift. keep all supplies and storage items neatly stacked. sweep the floor daily. 10. chameleon move-out procedures (updating a kennel location) if chameleon has not been opened for the day, double click on the chameleon icon. enter your location, user id, and password, then click ok. click on windows, scroll to kennels, and left click. when the kennel screen appears, place the cursor in the animal id field and type in the id. press f12 (find). verify the kennel record showing is the one you need, by animal id and description. move the cursor to the kennel no field and press f4 (popup). highlight the location, then click ok. the colored dot in the upper left corner should be yellow and read modified fields. press f8 (update). when the message box asking if you are sure you want to update appears, click ok. the colored dot in the upper left corner should now be green and read store/update ok. each time you leave the computer, log off to prevent other users from using your logon id: go to file and click sign in, which returns you to the logon screen. 11. chameleon outcome procedures for euthanasia type in the animal id and press f12 (find). make sure the animal id and description match the animal's kennel card (sex, color, etc.). read all notes in the computer for holds, do not pts notations, and similar restrictions. go to the outcome field. euth should already be there; if not, press f4 and select euth. make sure there is a subtype (a reason for the euthanasia). enter the amount used under dose, enter your initials under by, and enter the time of euthanasia. press f8 to store the changes. 12. adoption steps approach and greet the customers and answer any questions they may have. inform customers of the price of an adoption and what is included in the price. {"step": "if the customer is interested in viewing an animal, the adoption counselor must make sure the animal is available for adoption by following these steps:", "sub": ["log into chameleon and open the kennel window.", "go to animal id, type in the animal id number, and press f12 to search for the kennel record.", "go to due out date to make sure the animal is available.", "click on the notes icon and read all notes.", "if the animal is available, it can be taken out of its kennel to be visited within the get acquainted area.", "after the visit, if the customer is interested in adoption, the adoption counselor will go over the adoption checklist with the customer.", "have the customer completely fill out the adoption application; adoptions staff will review it to determine if the customer is qualified to adopt the animal.", "upon qualification, the adopter should completely fill out the adoption agreement, the two-page animal samaritans surgery consent form, the microchip form, and the customer service survey."]} staff must review all paperwork to make sure all information is complete and correct. the adoption counselor must give the adopter a dvd and an information packet corresponding to the animal adopted. the adoption counselor must place a red tag with the animal id, the adopter's name, and phone number on it. write the word adopted across the kennel card. {"step": "the adoption counselor will walk the adopter to the front counter and explain the policies and procedures, and instruct the customer where to place the customer service survey:", "sub": ["explain the 7-day policy.", "refer the customer to the behaviorist for any questions regarding behavior issues.", "explain that there is a cost for any medications given within the 7-day period."]} schedule the animal for the next available surgery date and make sure to let the adopter know the date and time of pick-up. thank the customer. 13. stray intake (chameleon) if chameleon has not been opened yet, go to an available computer and double click on the chameleon icon. enter your location, user id, and password, then click ok. click on windows, scroll to kennel, and left click. when the kennel screen appears, press f11 to clear. place the cursor in the kennel no field, press f4 (popup), and select the correct location. use receiving if the kennel location is not yet known. tab to status and press f4; choose an appropriate status. {"step": "tab to the tag no field:", "sub": ["if the animal has identification: press f4 and enter all information. click the green check mark at the top of the screen to link the information back to the kennel screen.", "if the animal has no identification: leave the field blank."]} tab to the animal id field and press f4 (popup). fill in all known fields: animal name (if known), type, sex, age, color, breed, condition, status, and bite (y if the animal bit anyone, n if not). once all information has been entered correctly, click the green check mark at the top of the page. tab to the source id field and press f4 (popup). find or store a person record for the person responsible for the animal coming into the shelter. once all information has been entered correctly, click the green check mark at the top of the page. tab to intake type, press f4 (popup), and choose stray. tab to intake subtype, press f4 (popup), and choose the correct one. tab to intake condition, press f4, and choose the right one. tab to date and press f5 to automatically set the current date. tab to time and press f5 to automatically set the current time. tab to due out and type in the date according to the hold dates. tab to crossing and enter the nearest cross street where the animal was found. tab to jurisdiction, press f4, and select the correct one. tab to by and input your initials. tab into comment and enter any important information from the reporting party. 14. owner turn-in front counter staff will have the owner fill out the dog history form and link the animal to the owner in the computer. front counter staff will call the behaviorist, if available. if the behaviorist is not available, adoption counselors or acts will be called to the front counter to review the dog history form and talk with owners. if the owner has not indicated any aggression on the dog history form, and the dog appears to be friendly (adoptable), staff will let the owner know that they can sign the dog over for potential adoption. the dog will still have to undergo a full behavior assessment prior to being placed up for adoption. if the dog is deemed unadoptable, the dog may be euthanized. {"step": "if the dog is very fearful, will not walk on leash, and/or is not socialized (treatable), staff will inform the owner that the shelter cannot take the dog in for potential adoption. the owner can either:", "sub": ["take the dog home with the resource list.", "sign the dog over for euthanasia."]} {"step": "if the owner has indicated that the dog is being turned in because of aggression toward people or other animals (untreatable), the owner can either:", "sub": ["take the dog home with the resource list.", "sign the dog over for euthanasia."]} front counter staff will place a note in the computer stating that staff has authorized the dog as ok for potential adoption. example: [staff initials]: ok to oti adopt per [authorizing staff member]. staff will put a note in the computer as soon as possible after talking with the owner, describing the reason for turn-in, the dog's observed behavior, and the outcome offered. the source sop includes three example memos dated march 20, 2009 covering an adoptable dog, a fearful (treatable) dog, and an aggressive (untreatable) dog. {"step": "if the dog is a bully breed (pit bull, pit bull mix, american pit bull terrier, american staffordshire terrier, bull terrier, staffordshire bull terrier, miniature bull terrier, american bulldog, and mixes thereof), staff must conduct a dog-to-dog assessment prior to taking the dog in for potential adoption. staff will take the dog to the safer room and, with the help of another staff member and an adoptable helper dog of the same sex and size, assess the dog's dog-to-dog behavior:", "sub": ["if there is a question about the dog's behavior with the first helper, staff may get a second helper dog.", "if the dog is loose, wiggly, and relaxed or playful with the helper, the dog is ok to take in for potential adoption and must be kenneled with others immediately after being impounded and cleared by veterinary staff.", "if the dog shows any aggression (growling or trying to bite the helper), a stiff body, or a hard stare, staff will inform the owner that the shelter is unable to take the dog in for potential adoption due to the dog being dog aggressive or dog reactive, because adoptable dogs cannot be kenneled alone. the dog will then need to be taken home by the owner with the resource list or signed over for euthanasia."]} anytime multiple dogs are turned in together, they must not be kenneled together; they must be kenneled with other dogs. front counter staff will inform customers that there will be fees if the owner changes their mind, and that they have only 2 days before the dog could be placed up for adoption or euthanized. none cited in the original sop.

documents.title: Cat Socialization
documents.purpose: Not stated in the original SOP; the purpose field in the source document is blank. Based on the procedure content, this SOP governs how volunteers and staff socialize shelter cats through petting, play, and brushing while maintaining sanitation and animal accountability. A purpose statement is to be drafted during substantive revision.
documents.authority: Not stated in the original SOP.
documents.scope: Not stated in the original SOP. The procedure references volunteers, the volunteer office, and adoption counselors, indicating it applies to volunteers and staff who socialize cats.
documents.supersedes: None. Original SOP effective September 30, 2009.
references: None cited in the original SOP.

sections.heading: Cat Socialization Procedure
steps: Get the key to the kennels from the volunteer office or from behind the counter.
Get an apron from behind the greeter kiosk.

steps.step: Get a toy and brush to socialize the cat with.
sub: Make sure the toy and brush have been sanitized before use.

steps: Choose the cat you would like to socialize.
Write down the cat's ID number and kennel number on a note pad.
Pull the cat out, or socialize the cat in the kennel, by petting, playing, and brushing.
Make sure you have control of the cat at all times.

steps.step: If someone wants to know about the cat or visit with it:
sub: Place the cat back into its kennel.
Lock the kennel.
Let the prospective adopter know that you will find an adoption counselor or a volunteer who is allowed to do adoptions to help them.

steps: Use hand sanitizer between each cat, and use a clean brush and toy.
Log the cat that you socialized in the book and on the board before you start to socialize another one.

steps.step: Make sure the cat you just socialized has cat food, water, and a clean kennel, including:
sub: Cat blankets (clean or dirty).
Cat litter boxes.

steps: After you are finished, return the keys.

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2009 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: How to Process Animals
documents.purpose: To establish consistent animal processing procedures for Registered Veterinary Technicians and Veterinary Technicians.
documents.authority: Not stated in the original SOP.
documents.scope: Registered Veterinary Technicians and Veterinary Technicians.
documents.supersedes: None. Original SOP effective October 29, 2014.
references: None cited in the original SOP.

definitions.def: The intake examination, identification, vaccination, and record-keeping steps performed on each animal at impound.

sections.heading: A. Processing Dogs
steps: Perform a cursory examination to determine age, gender, and breed. Check for any obvious abnormalities, such as hair or dental loss, skin growths, matted hair coat, sores, or abnormal eye issues. Enter appropriate information and findings into the Chameleon treatment screen.
Scan the dog for a microchip.
Make the animal a paper collar, blue for males and white for females. With a marker, write the Animal ID, impound date, and due out date legibly on the collar.
Give dogs over the age of 8 weeks DA2PPV and Bordetella vaccinations. Give puppies under 6 months of age deworming agents according to protocol.
Address any potential medical issues according to protocol, or consult with the shelter veterinarian.
Take a picture that shows as much of the dog as possible and attach it to the Animal ID.
Input the information and picture into Chameleon according to protocol.

sections.heading: B. Processing Cats
steps: Perform a cursory examination to determine age, gender, and breed. Check for any obvious abnormalities, such as hair or dental loss, skin growths, matted hair coat, sores, or abnormal eye issues. Enter appropriate information and findings into the Chameleon treatment screen.
Scan the animal for a microchip.
Make the animal a paper collar, blue for males and white for females. With a marker, write the Animal ID and the due out date legibly on the collar.
Give every cat an FVRCP vaccination. Give kittens under 6 months of age deworming agents according to protocol.
Address any potential medical issues according to protocol or the veterinarian.
Take a picture that shows as much of the cat as possible and attach it to the Animal ID.
Input the information and picture into Chameleon according to protocol.

sections.heading: C. Medical Record Keeping
steps: Always place all information into the Chameleon medical screen. It is important for the animals that each person who comes across the record can open the computer and see all they need to know.

Maintain complete records for legal purposes. Chameleon is the department's single source for medical record keeping. Many animals are impounded by officers because of poor husbandry, neglect, and abuse, and medical records are vital in helping officers prosecute these cases.

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2014 version (header typo RIVESIDE corrected); substantive edits pending (see edit log).

under 6 months of age deworming agents according to protocol. address any potential medical issues according to protocol or the veterinarian. take a picture that shows as much of the cat as possible and attach it to the animal id. input the information and picture into chameleon according to protocol. c. medical record keeping always place all information into the chameleon medical screen. it is important for the animals that each person who comes across the record can open the computer and see all they need to know. maintain complete records for legal purposes. chameleon is the department's single source for medical record keeping. many animals are impounded by officers because of poor husbandry, neglect, and abuse, and medical records are vital in helping officers prosecute these cases. none cited in the original sop.

documents.title: Lobby Life Rooms
documents.purpose: To establish a procedure for Department of Animal Services (DAS) staff when showcasing animals in the lobby life rooms at our animal shelters. The lobby life rooms were designed to showcase animals in a tranquil home environment.
documents.authority: Owner turn-ins housed in life rooms must be approved by an animal behaviorist or designee.
documents.scope: DAS staff at shelters with lobby life rooms (Riverside and San Jacinto shelters named in original).
documents.supersedes: SOP Lobby Life Rooms, effective April 29, 2014

definitions.def: A lobby enclosure designed to showcase adoptable animals in a tranquil home environment.
Owner turn-in.

sections.heading: Dog Selection Criteria
steps: Check all notes on animal activity, medical, and kennel screens before selecting dogs to be housed in the lobby life rooms.

steps.step: Confirm the dog meets all of the following criteria:
sub: Dog must be adoptable, medical and behavior.
Dog must not be on medication.
No puppies under 6 months.
No females in heat.
No dog showing signs of urine marking.
No dog with previously observed destructive behavior.
No dog displaying excessive vocalizations.
Dog must walk on a leash.
[Criteria 9 through part of 12 are illegible in the source document.]

steps: House only 1 dog per lobby life room, with the exception of approved bonded pairs or owner turn-ins (OTIs). OTIs must be approved by an animal behaviorist or designee.
House dogs in life rooms only after they are post 10 days from impound.

sections.heading: Cat Selection Criteria
steps: Check all notes on animal activity, medical, and kennel screens before selecting cats to be housed in the lobby life rooms.

steps.step: Confirm the cat meets all of the following criteria:
sub: Cat must be adoptable, medical and behavior.
Cat must not be on medication.
[Criterion 3 is illegible in the source document.]
No females in heat.
No cat showing signs of fearful behavior.
No cat showing signs of caution behavior.
No cat who has displayed previous signs of stress.
No cat showing signs of hiding behavior.
No cat that is no longer eating or drinking.
No cat that is panting.
No cat that is drooling.
Cat must have toys.

steps: House cats in life rooms only after they are post 10 days from impound.
House cats for 5 consecutive business days, then kennel them in the adoption pod to allow another cat the opportunity.

campusVariations.variation: Dogs will only be housed in lobby life rooms during business hours and will return to their kennel at the end of each day. Dogs chosen to be featured in lobby life rooms may be selected for 5 consecutive business days and then moved back to adoptable pods to allow another dog the opportunity to be showcased in the lobby.

Dogs chosen to live continuously in lobby life rooms will stay for 5 consecutive business days. Dogs will be moved back to adoptable kennels after the 5th day if not adopted to allow another dog the opportunity to live and be showcased in the lobby. Kennel dividers are to remain open, unless cleaning or afterhours when it is acceptable for dogs to be closed in as needed.

documents.title: Microchip Scanners
documents.purpose: To ensure all animals impounded by field staff are scanned for a microchip.
documents.authority: Consult your supervisor or lead with any questions regarding your scanner.
documents.scope: All RCDAS staff who handle animals, including shelter, clinic, and field personnel. (Scope expanded from field services only per staff decision, July 2026.)
documents.supersedes: SOP Micro-chip Scanners, effective January 28, 2014 (also filed as duplicate 'SOP Scanner.pdf', identical content)
references: Euthanasia SOP
Owner Notification Procedure SOP
Policy 000-08: Holding Period for Animals Impounded by the Department

sections.heading: Scanning Requirement
steps: Scan all animals that are impounded, either dead or alive, excluding wildlife. Scanning is mandatory for all Animal Control Officers and DAS staff working in the field.
Make every attempt to contact an owner if a microchip is found.
Know how to use your assigned equipment properly. Every ACO will be issued a microchip scanner; your assigned scanner may differ from the model described below.
Consult your supervisor or lead if you have any questions regarding your scanner.

sections.heading: AVID Mini Tracker 3 Operating Instructions
steps: Power button: Turn the reader on by sliding the lower right hand button forward on the face of the reader. When powered up, the device will emit a double beep sound and the LCD will display AVID/FEC/TR/ISO. To turn off, slide the button back.

Read button: Press the upper right button marked 'Read' on the reader to put the reader into LOOKING mode. The LCD will display LOOKING. When a microchip is found, the reader will emit two beep tones and display the chip number on the LCD. If the read button is released before finding a microchip, the message NO ID FOUND will display on the LCD.

Reading: The reader antenna is located inside the reader at the top; the label marked 'READ HERE' identifies the antenna area. While pressing down the read button, scan in a circular pattern while moving toward the head, starting from the middle of the back. Scan the entire body of the animal, because microchips have been known to travel or move over time.

related.name: Policy 000-08: Holding Period for Animals Impounded by the Department

appendices: Appendix A: SOP acknowledgment and scanner custody forms

documents.title: Syringe Pole Use
documents.purpose: To provide an effective mechanism to perform euthanasia or sedation on domestic or wild animals.
documents.authority: Not stated in original.
documents.scope: Staff performing sedation or euthanasia of animals using a syringe pole.
documents.supersedes: SOP Syringe Pole Use, effective May 21, 2009
references: Euthanasia SOP

sections.heading: Using a Syringe Pole for Sedation or Euthanasia
steps: Check the plunger to ensure it is in good working condition. Replace the rubber piece as needed. Use a 3cc syringe barrel; replace as needed to ensure smooth working order.
Place a new needle on the end of the syringe with each use.
Draw solution into the syringe.
For sedation: Position the syringe pole, with solution, near the hip or rear leg muscle. For euthanasia: Position the pole syringe so that a clear path to the ventral abdomen is obtained.

When the animal is still and you can clearly see the target area, push the pole with needle into the tissue firmly and quickly to inject the solution. Pull the syringe pole back quickly when completed.
Rinse the syringe with clean water.
