documents.title: Volunteer Standard Operating Procedures
documents.purpose: To provide standard operating procedures for volunteers performing greeter, animal socialization, visitation, adoption support, grooming, event, laundry, clerical, and clinic duties at the shelter.
documents.authority: Volunteer duties are as needed and as directed by the Volunteer Coordinator. Adoption paperwork must be signed off by an Animal Care Technician (ACT).
documents.scope: All volunteers at the Riverside shelter.
documents.supersedes: SOP Volunteer 111813, dated November 18, 2013 (identical content); SOP Volunteer (undated)

definitions.def: Animal Care Technician.
Dead on arrival.
Owner turn-in.

sections.heading: Greeter
steps: Always be polite and courteous, even to the most disgruntled customer. If you need some help, ask an employee.
For ticketing, licensing, or bills, tell customers to go to the first hallway on the left to the windows across from the elevator.
If customers are dropping off a stray animal or are looking for a lost animal, direct them out the front door and immediately turn right and go into the admissions door (above the door it says Cats).
For a dog or cat adoption, direct customers to go out the back double doors and make a right. The first building is cats and the next two are dogs.
Next to the computer you will see bins that contain paperwork about spay or neuter, other shelters, vaccination clinics, etc.
The lost and found book is on the right of the computer on the desk.
The DOA (dead on arrival) book is to the right of the computer on the desk.
Restrooms are located in the first hallway on the left next to the elevator.
If a guest is requesting to see a horse, ask them to fill out an Equestrian Application. After they have completed the application, give it to the designated equestrian staff member.

sections.heading: Cat Socialization
steps: Do an initial check of all the cat kennels.

steps.step: If any of the cat food or water dishes are empty, refill them.
sub: To refill the water bowl, place the bowl under the sink and refill. Only fill the water bowl halfway.
When feeding cats, do not put the cat food bowl into the storage bin. Always use the food scooper located in the bucket under the storage bin.

steps: Remove and replace any dirty blankets or toys.

steps.step: Check the litter pans and clean litter if needed. If a pan is really dirty, retrieve a new pan, fill it with litter, and place it back into the proper kennel.
sub: Perform tasks one cage at a time so you do not mix litter pans belonging to different cats.

steps: When socializing with the cats, only approach the friendly ones. If you notice a cat has any health issues, write it on the boards located on both inner sides of the cat building.
Help keep the counters, floors, etc. clean and safe for yourself and everyone else.
Return tools to their proper locations before you leave.
Always socialize the kittens first before you socialize any adult cats.

sections.heading: Cat Visitation
steps: Ask the customer what cat they would like to visit with.
Place an 'Out for Visitation' sign on the outside of the kennel.

steps.step: Put the cat in one of the sanitized blue cat crates to transfer to one of the visitation rooms.
sub: There should be at least 2 blue crates on both inner sides of the buildings.
Never transfer a cat without using a blue crate.
Do not use cardboard boxes to transport cats.

steps.step: Wait until everyone in the customer's group is in the visitation room and the door is shut completely before you let the cat out of the blue crate.
sub: Never leave the customers unattended with the cat.
Never give medical or behavioral advice to the customer.
Always use your best judgment if the customers who want to see the cat have a large group with them. Only allow two to four people in with the cat at a time.

steps: At the end of each visitation, place the cat back into the blue crate before opening the door.
Place the cat back into its kennel.
Log the visitation in the 'Cat' binder.

sections.heading: Cat Adoption
steps: Greet the customers and answer any questions that they might have to the best of your ability. If you do not know the answer to any question, refer them to staff.

steps.step: If a guest would like to visit with a cat, first put up the 'Out for Visitation' sign, then go and retrieve the cat.
sub: Always make sure that the cat is in a blue crate whenever you transport it.
Always use your best judgment if the customers who want to see the cat have a large group with them. Only allow two to four people in with the cat at a time.

steps: Escort the customers into a cat visitation room.

steps.step: Wait until everyone in the customer's group is in the visitation room and the door is shut completely before you let the cat out of the blue crate.
sub: Never leave the customers unattended with the cat.

steps: Ask the guests if they are interested in adopting that cat.

steps.step: When they say yes, grab a clipboard with the adoption paperwork on it and hand it to the adopter to complete.
sub: After they complete the paperwork, ask the ACT to sign off.

steps.step: If the cat is being adopted, take the cat for a vet exam located in receiving.
sub: Always make sure that the cat is in a blue crate when transporting.
Before going to a vet exam, ask the ACT to print you a kennel card for the cat that you are taking.

steps.step: Once you are in receiving with the animal, stand behind the taped line and wait to be helped.
sub: Receiving is very busy and you may have to wait a while until you are seen.

steps.step: After you are done with the vet exam, return to the cattery.
sub: If the vet staff tells you any information about the cat, only tell the ACT. Never tell the customer what vet staff has told you.

steps: Give the cat to the ACT so they can finish the adoption process.
Log the adoption in the 'Cat' binder.

sections.heading: Dog Socialization
steps: Check out a kennel key from the designated area.

steps.step: Grab a dog leash, animal waste bags, and signs to be placed on kennels notifying staff and public of the animal's absence.
sub: A pen and notepad are optional.
Always return the supplies that you have borrowed to the volunteer office.
If the supplies are running low, see the Volunteer Coordinator.

steps.step: Before you socialize with any social adult dogs, always socialize with puppies first.
sub: Any puppy 6 months and under cannot go on a grassy area.

steps: Never pull a dog from the outside of the kennel.

steps.step: Once you pick a social and friendly dog, place an 'Out for a Walk' sign on the kennel card.
sub: If there is more than one dog, separate the other dogs from the one you want using the guillotine door. If you need help, ask a staff member or another volunteer to help you separate the dogs.

steps: Get the leash ready before you unlock the door.
Unlock the kennel door.
Leash up the dog you would like to take on a walk.
Always close and lock the kennel door.

steps.step: Only walk dogs in designated walking areas.
sub: Always pick up your dog's waste.
Never do a meet and greet with any of the shelter dogs or the public's dogs.
Keep a 5 ft. distance between all dogs.
Be aware of any sudden changes in the dog's body language or behavior.

steps.step: After you are done socializing with the dog, kennel it back into the original kennel.
sub: If the dog has other dogs in its kennel, use the guillotine door to separate them before you put the dog in. After you put the dog in, slowly move the guillotine door up to slowly reintroduce the dogs.
If there are any new dogs in the kennel, ask staff.
Double check the kennel card to make sure that the dog is going into the right kennel.

steps: Remove the 'Out for a Walk' sign.

sections.heading: Dog Visitation
steps: Ask the customer what dog they would like to visit with.
Ask the guests to step outside of the kennel area while you pull out the dog.
Put the 'Out Visiting' sign on the kennel.
Look over the kennel card and remember key facts like the dog's age, sex, spay/neuter status, and weight.

steps.step: Leash up the dog.
sub: If there is more than one dog, separate the other dogs from the one you want using the guillotine door. If you need help, ask a staff member or another volunteer to help you separate the dogs.

steps: Walk outside with the dog and find the guests who would like to visit with the dog.
Escort the dog and the guests to a good spot to visit with the dog.
While showing the dog, never give out medical or behavioral advice, no meet and greets, and no handing over the leash. If you do not know the answer to a question, refer them to staff.

steps.step: After the guests are done visiting with the dog, kennel it back into the original kennel.
sub: If the dog has other dogs in its kennel, use the guillotine door to separate them before you put the dog in. After you put the dog in, slowly move the guillotine door up to slowly reintroduce the dogs.
If there are any new dogs in the kennel, ask staff.
Double check the kennel card to make sure that the dog is going into the right kennel.

steps: Remove the 'Out Visiting' sign.

sections.heading: Dog Adoption
steps: Greet guests and answer any questions they might have to the best of your ability. If you do not know the answer, let the guests know that you will get an answer for them or find someone who can answer the question for them.
Ask the guests to step outside of the kennel area while you pull out the dog.
Put the 'Out Visiting' sign on the kennel.
Look over the kennel card and remember key facts like the dog's age, sex, spay/neuter status, and weight.

steps.step: Leash up the dog.
sub: If there is more than one dog, separate the other dogs from the one you want using the guillotine door. If you need help, ask a staff member or another volunteer to help you separate the dogs.

steps: Walk outside with the dog and find the guests who would like to visit with the dog.
Escort the dog and the guests to a good spot to visit with the dog.
While showing the dog, never give out medical or behavioral advice, no meet and greets, and no handing over the leash. If you do not know the answer to a question, refer them to staff.
The leash must always stay in the volunteer's hand at all times, no exceptions.

steps.step: Once the guests want to adopt the dog, hand them the clipboard with the adoption information on it for them to fill out.
sub: After they complete the paperwork, have an ACT sign it off.

steps.step: If the dog is being adopted, take the dog for a vet exam located in receiving.
sub: Before going to a vet exam, ask the ACT to print you a kennel card for the dog that you are taking.

steps.step: Once you are in receiving with the animal, stand behind the taped line and wait to be helped.
sub: Receiving is very busy and you may have to wait a while until you are seen.

steps.step: After you are done with the vet exam, return to the correct dog building.
sub: If the vet staff tells you any information about the dog, only tell the ACT. Never tell the customer what vet staff has told you.

steps: Give the dog to the ACT so they can finish the adoption process.

sections.heading: Bathing and Brushing

steps.step: Check the grooming room for supplies needed to bathe or groom a dog.
sub: Towels.
Shampoo.
Brushes and combs.

steps: Make sure the wash tub and kennels in the grooming room have been sanitized and wiped clean; if they have not been, please do so.
Check out a kennel key from the designated checkout area.

steps.step: Only dogs that are adoptable can be washed. No badly matted dogs unless you are a professional groomer.
sub: If you are a professional groomer, talk to the Volunteer Coordinator.

steps: Check with staff to see if there are any dogs that need to be bathed before they have surgery.
If spayed or neutered, ask staff to check the surgery date to make sure the dog is ready to bathe.

steps.step: Make sure you can handle the dog you are going to bathe or groom.
sub: Get help if you need it and always work with another volunteer or staff person.

steps: Place an 'Out for a Bath' sign on the kennel.
Write down the animal ID and kennel number before taking the dog out of the kennel.

steps.step: Always follow the procedures for pulling dogs.
sub: Unlock the kennel and carefully get the dog onto the leash.
Take the dog out of the kennel and lock it back up.

steps: Walk or carry the dog to the grooming room.
Fill out the grooming log with the correct information.
Either walk the dog up the ramp into the tub or pick it up and place it inside the tub.
Place the tub's harness around the dog's neck to keep it inside the tub.

steps.step: Use either warm water or cold water for the dog, depending on the weather.
sub: If you are going to use warm water, preheat the water and test it before using it on the dog to ensure that it is not too hot.

steps.step: Begin washing from the top of the dog's head down to the tail.
sub: Make sure not to get water or shampoo in the dog's eyes, mouth, nose, or ears.

steps: Rinse thoroughly from top to bottom.
Wrap the dog in a towel, transfer the dog to the drying table, and secure it with the harness. If the dog is too big for the table, place the dog on the floor and secure it to the table with a leash.
Turn on the blow dryer and see the dog's reaction. If it is not positive, proceed with towel drying. If the reaction is positive, proceed with blow drying.
Sign out of the grooming book.

steps.step: Return the dog to the proper kennel.
sub: Make sure that no new dog has been placed in the kennel; if there is one, notify staff.

steps: Remove the grooming sign from the kennel.
Go back to the grooming room and clean and disinfect everything that was used (tub, brushes, floor, etc.). All used towels must be placed inside a bag and taken to the laundry room.

sections.heading: Events

steps.step: Onsite events:
sub: Check in at the designated check-in area at the designated time.
Ask at check-in what your task will be. If you do not feel comfortable doing a certain task, tell staff.
Do the task that you have been given, and if you have any problems, report them to a staff member.

steps.step: Offsite events:
sub: Check in at the designated check-in area at the designated time.
Ask at check-in what your task will be. If you do not feel comfortable doing a certain task, tell staff.
Do the task that you have been given, and if you have any problems, report them to a staff member.
At offsite events, always tell a staff member where you are going before you leave.

steps: For all events, remember to be respectful because you are representing the Riverside County Department of Animal Services.

steps.step: Washer:
sub: Put on gloves.
Throw out bloody items or items with a lot of feces on them.
Never wash collars, leashes, or metal tags.
Set the washer on cycle 16. If it is set to cycle 16, just press 'Start'. If it is not set to cycle 16, push 'Clear', '16', 'Start'.
The washer on the left will sometimes beep and say 'Didn't fill within time'. Just restart.
Always push start right away, otherwise it will stop.
Load the washer until 3/4 full. Do not overload.
Do not run when the load is less than 2/3 full.
For large beds, heavy blankets, and pillows, fill only 1/2 full because the washer will stop if the load is too heavy.

steps.step: Dryer:
sub: Put washed items into the dryer.
No plastic toys in the dryer.
Set the dryer for 60 minutes, 15 minutes cool, high heat.
Push the green button.
Check the lint trap on the bottom of the dryer and clean it out.
Pillows and some thick items must be dried twice.
Fold items and put them on the shelves.
Clean items only on the shelf and in the cubbies.

sections.heading: Clerical

steps.step: General office duties include but are not limited to:
sub: Filing.
Typing.
Mailing.
Answering telephones.
Data entry.

steps: Clerical duties are as needed and as directed by the Volunteer Coordinator.

sections.heading: Clinic
steps: Be sure you have all supplies needed: gloves, disinfecting spray, paper towels, scrubbing pad, trashcan, newspaper, and shredded paper (when available).
Place a pair of gloves on each hand.
Empty the cage of any solid waste and newspapers.
If there is a towel or blanket in the cage, remove any solid waste and then place it into the dirty laundry basket.
Use newspaper and/or paper towels to soak up any urine or water in the kennel.
Spray all five sides of the cage liberally with disinfectant.
Spray the cage door liberally with disinfectant.
Allow the cage to soak for approximately 10 minutes.
If there is more than one cage to be cleaned at this time, you can set up the other cages to be cleaned by following steps 2 through 7.
Change gloves as often as needed.

steps.step: After soaking the cages, with gloved hands, begin to wipe the cages down.
sub: Use the scrubbing pad, paper towels, and more spray if needed.

steps: Wipe down the cage doors, paying particular attention to all the crevices where the metal meets. This is where mucus, urine, and feces can build up.
If needed, the cage door can be removed for cleaning. See a VT or RVT for assistance.
When the cage is dry, place a layer of newspaper, at least 5 sheets thick, on the entire bottom of the cage.
Place a big handful of shredded paper on top. In double cages you can use more newspaper and shredded paper, as bigger animals make bigger messes.

Bring all the dirty laundry to the outside laundry room, which is located at the top of the stray animal side of the shelter. Bring back to the clinic the equivalent of what you dropped off. For example, if you dropped off towels, bring back towels.

sections.heading: Horse Care
steps: [Section heading present in source document, but the content is missing from both available versions of the file. See edit log.]

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template; consolidated with identical undated file SOP Volunteer.pdf. Named staff replaced with role titles. Content otherwise unchanged from 2013 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

first hallway on the left next to the elevator. if a guest is requesting to see a horse, ask them to fill out an equestrian application. after they have completed the application, give it to the designated equestrian staff member. cat socialization do an initial check of all the cat kennels. {"step": "if any of the cat food or water dishes are empty, refill them.", "sub": ["to refill the water bowl, place the bowl under the sink and refill. only fill the water bowl halfway.", "when feeding cats, do not put the cat food bowl into the storage bin. always use the food scooper located in the bucket under the storage bin."]} remove and replace any dirty blankets or toys. {"step": "check the litter pans and clean litter if needed. if a pan is really dirty, retrieve a new pan, fill it with litter, and place it back into the proper kennel.", "sub": ["perform tasks one cage at a time so you do not mix litter pans belonging to different cats."]} when socializing with the cats, only approach the friendly ones. if you notice a cat has any health issues, write it on the boards located on both inner sides of the cat building. help keep the counters, floors, etc. clean and safe for yourself and everyone else. return tools to their proper locations before you leave. always socialize the kittens first before you socialize any adult cats. cat visitation ask the customer what cat they would like to visit with. place an 'out for visitation' sign on the outside of the kennel. {"step": "put the cat in one of the sanitized blue cat crates to transfer to one of the visitation rooms.", "sub": ["there should be at least 2 blue crates on both inner sides of the buildings.", "never transfer a cat without using a blue crate.", "do not use cardboard boxes to transport cats."]} {"step": "wait until everyone in the customer's group is in the visitation room and the door is shut completely before you let the cat out of the blue crate.", "sub": ["never leave the customers unattended with the cat.", "never give medical or behavioral advice to the customer.", "always use your best judgment if the customers who want to see the cat have a large group with them. only allow two to four people in with the cat at a time."]} at the end of each visitation, place the cat back into the blue crate before opening the door. place the cat back into its kennel. log the visitation in the 'cat' binder. cat adoption greet the customers and answer any questions that they might have to the best of your ability. if you do not know the answer to any question, refer them to staff. {"step": "if a guest would like to visit with a cat, first put up the 'out for visitation' sign, then go and retrieve the cat.", "sub": ["always make sure that the cat is in a blue crate whenever you transport it.", "always use your best judgment if the customers who want to see the cat have a large group with them. only allow two to four people in with the cat at a time."]} escort the customers into a cat visitation room. {"step": "wait until everyone in the customer's group is in the visitation room and the door is shut completely before you let the cat out of the blue crate.", "sub": ["never leave the customers unattended with the cat."]} ask the guests if they are interested in adopting that cat. {"step": "when they say yes, grab a clipboard with the adoption paperwork on it and hand it to the adopter to complete.", "sub": ["after they complete the paperwork, ask the act to sign off."]} {"step": "if the cat is being adopted, take the cat for a vet exam located in receiving.", "sub": ["always make sure that the cat is in a blue crate when transporting.", "before going to a vet exam, ask the act to print you a kennel card for the cat that you are taking."]} {"step": "once you are in receiving with the animal, stand behind the taped line and wait to be helped.", "sub": ["receiving is very busy and you may have to wait a while until you are seen."]} {"step": "after you are done with the vet exam, return to the cattery.", "sub": ["if the vet staff tells you any information about the cat, only tell the act. never tell the customer what vet staff has told you."]} give the cat to the act so they can finish the adoption process. log the adoption in the 'cat' binder. dog socialization check out a kennel key from the designated area. {"step": "grab a dog leash, animal waste bags, and signs to be placed on kennels notifying staff and public of the animal's absence.", "sub": ["a pen and notepad are optional.", "always return the supplies that you have borrowed to the volunteer office.", "if the supplies are running low, see the volunteer coordinator."]} {"step": "before you socialize with any social adult dogs, always socialize with puppies first.", "sub": ["any puppy 6 months and under cannot go on a grassy area."]} never pull a dog from the outside of the kennel. {"step": "once you pick a social and friendly dog, place an 'out for a walk' sign on the kennel card.", "sub": ["if there is more than one dog, separate the other dogs from the one you want using the guillotine door. if you need help, ask a staff member or another volunteer to help you separate the dogs."]} get the leash ready before you unlock the door. unlock the kennel door. leash up the dog you would like to take on a walk. always close and lock the kennel door. {"step": "only walk dogs in designated walking areas.", "sub": ["always pick up your dog's waste.", "never do a meet and greet with any of the shelter dogs or the public's dogs.", "keep a 5 ft. distance between all dogs.", "be aware of any sudden changes in the dog's body language or behavior."]} {"step": "after you are done socializing with the dog, kennel it back into the original kennel.", "sub": ["if the dog has other dogs in its kennel, use the guillotine door to separate them before you put the dog in. after you put the dog in, slowly move the guillotine door up to slowly reintroduce the dogs.", "if there are any new dogs in the kennel, ask staff.", "double check the kennel card to make sure that the dog is going into the right kennel."]} remove the 'out for a walk' sign. dog visitation ask the customer what dog they would like to visit with. ask the guests to step outside of the kennel area while you pull out the dog. put the 'out visiting' sign on the kennel. look over the kennel card and remember key facts like the dog's age, sex, spay/neuter status, and weight. {"step": "leash up the dog.", "sub": ["if there is more than one dog, separate the other dogs from the one you want using the guillotine door. if you need help, ask a staff member or another volunteer to help you separate the dogs."]} walk outside with the dog and find the guests who would like to visit with the dog. escort the dog and the guests to a good spot to visit with the dog. while showing the dog, never give out medical or behavioral advice, no meet and greets, and no handing over the leash. if you do not know the answer to a question, refer them to staff. {"step": "after the guests are done visiting with the dog, kennel it back into the original kennel.", "sub": ["if the dog has other dogs in its kennel, use the guillotine door to separate them before you put the dog in. after you put the dog in, slowly move the guillotine door up to slowly reintroduce the dogs.", "if there are any new dogs in the kennel, ask staff.", "double check the kennel card to make sure that the dog is going into the right kennel."]} remove the 'out visiting' sign. dog adoption greet guests and answer any questions they might have to the best of your ability. if you do not know the answer, let the guests know that you will get an answer for them or find someone who can answer the question for them. ask the guests to step outside of the kennel area while you pull out the dog. put the 'out visiting' sign on the kennel. look over the kennel card and remember key facts like the dog's age, sex, spay/neuter status, and weight. {"step": "leash up the dog.", "sub": ["if there is more than one dog, separate the other dogs from the one you want using the guillotine door. if you need help, ask a staff member or another volunteer to help you separate the dogs."]} walk outside with the dog and find the guests who would like to visit with the dog. escort the dog and the guests to a good spot to visit with the dog. while showing the dog, never give out medical or behavioral advice, no meet and greets, and no handing over the leash. if you do not know the answer to a question, refer them to staff. the leash must always stay in the volunteer's hand at all times, no exceptions. {"step": "once the guests want to adopt the dog, hand them the clipboard with the adoption information on it for them to fill out.", "sub": ["after they complete the paperwork, have an act sign it off."]} {"step": "if the dog is being adopted, take the dog for a vet exam located in receiving.", "sub": ["before going to a vet exam, ask the act to print you a kennel card for the dog that you are taking."]} {"step": "once you are in receiving with the animal, stand behind the taped line and wait to be helped.", "sub": ["receiving is very busy and you may have to wait a while until you are seen."]} {"step": "after you are done with the vet exam, return to the correct dog building.", "sub": ["if the vet staff tells you any information about the dog, only tell the act. never tell the customer what vet staff has told you."]} give the dog to the act so they can finish the adoption process. bathing and brushing {"step": "check the grooming room for supplies needed to bathe or groom a dog.", "sub": ["towels.", "shampoo.", "brushes and combs."]} make sure the wash tub and kennels in the grooming room have been sanitized and wiped clean; if they have not been, please do so. check out a kennel key from the designated checkout area. {"step": "only dogs that are adoptable can be washed. no badly matted dogs unless you are a professional groomer.", "sub": ["if you are a professional groomer, talk to the volunteer coordinator."]} check with staff to see if there are any dogs that need to be bathed before they have surgery. if spayed or neutered, ask staff to check the surgery date to make sure the dog is ready to bathe. {"step": "make sure you can handle the dog you are going to bathe or groom.", "sub": ["get help if you need it and always work with another volunteer or staff person."]} place an 'out for a bath' sign on the kennel. write down the animal id and kennel number before taking the dog out of the kennel. {"step": "always follow the procedures for pulling dogs.", "sub": ["unlock the kennel and carefully get the dog onto the leash.", "take the dog out of the kennel and lock it back up."]} walk or carry the dog to the grooming room. fill out the grooming log with the correct information. either walk the dog up the ramp into the tub or pick it up and place it inside the tub. place the tub's harness around the dog's neck to keep it inside the tub. {"step": "use either warm water or cold water for the dog, depending on the weather.", "sub": ["if you are going to use warm water, preheat the water and test it before using it on the dog to ensure that it is not too hot."]} {"step": "begin washing from the top of the dog's head down to the tail.", "sub": ["make sure not to get water or shampoo in the dog's eyes, mouth, nose, or ears."]} rinse thoroughly from top to bottom. wrap the dog in a towel, transfer the dog to the drying table, and secure it with the harness. if the dog is too big for the table, place the dog on the floor and secure it to the table with a leash. turn on the blow dryer and see the dog's reaction. if it is not positive, proceed with towel drying. if the reaction is positive, proceed with blow drying. sign out of the grooming book. {"step": "return the dog to the proper kennel.", "sub": ["make sure that no new dog has been placed in the kennel; if there is one, notify staff."]} remove the grooming sign from the kennel. go back to the grooming room and clean and disinfect everything that was used (tub, brushes, floor, etc.). all used towels must be placed inside a bag and taken to the laundry room. events {"step": "onsite events:", "sub": ["check in at the designated check-in area at the designated time.", "ask at check-in what your task will be. if you do not feel comfortable doing a certain task, tell staff.", "do the task that you have been given, and if you have any problems, report them to a staff member."]} {"step": "offsite events:", "sub": ["check in at the designated check-in area at the designated time.", "ask at check-in what your task will be. if you do not feel comfortable doing a certain task, tell staff.", "do the task that you have been given, and if you have any problems, report them to a staff member.", "at offsite events, always tell a staff member where you are going before you leave."]} for all events, remember to be respectful because you are representing the riverside county department of animal services. laundry {"step": "washer:", "sub": ["put on gloves.", "throw out bloody items or items with a lot of feces on them.", "never wash collars, leashes, or metal tags.", "set the washer on cycle 16. if it is set to cycle 16, just press 'start'. if it is not set to cycle 16, push 'clear', '16', 'start'.", "the washer on the left will sometimes beep and say 'didn't fill within time'. just restart.", "always push start right away, otherwise it will stop.", "load the washer until 3/4 full. do not overload.", "do not run when the load is less than 2/3 full.", "for large beds, heavy blankets, and pillows, fill only 1/2 full because the washer will stop if the load is too heavy."]} {"step": "dryer:", "sub": ["put washed items into the dryer.", "no plastic toys in the dryer.", "set the dryer for 60 minutes, 15 minutes cool, high heat.", "push the green button.", "check the lint trap on the bottom of the dryer and clean it out.", "pillows and some thick items must be dried twice.", "fold items and put them on the shelves.", "clean items only on the shelf and in the cubbies."]} clerical {"step": "general office duties include but are not limited to:", "sub": ["filing.", "typing.", "mailing.", "answering telephones.", "data entry."]} clerical duties are as needed and as directed by the volunteer coordinator. clinic be sure you have all supplies needed: gloves, disinfecting spray, paper towels, scrubbing pad, trashcan, newspaper, and shredded paper (when available). place a pair of gloves on each hand. empty the cage of any solid waste and newspapers. if there is a towel or blanket in the cage, remove any solid waste and then place it into the dirty laundry basket. use newspaper and/or paper towels to soak up any urine or water in the kennel. spray all five sides of the cage liberally with disinfectant. spray the cage door liberally with disinfectant. allow the cage to soak for approximately 10 minutes. if there is more than one cage to be cleaned at this time, you can set up the other cages to be cleaned by following steps 2 through 7. change gloves as often as needed. {"step": "after soaking the cages, with gloved hands, begin to wipe the cages down.", "sub": ["use the scrubbing pad, paper towels, and more spray if needed."]} wipe down the cage doors, paying particular attention to all the crevices where the metal meets. this is where mucus, urine, and feces can build up. if needed, the cage door can be removed for cleaning. see a vt or rvt for assistance. when the cage is dry, place a layer of newspaper, at least 5 sheets thick, on the entire bottom of the cage. place a big handful of shredded paper on top. in double cages you can use more newspaper and shredded paper, as bigger animals make bigger messes. bring all the dirty laundry to the outside laundry room, which is located at the top of the stray animal side of the shelter. bring back to the clinic the equivalent of what you dropped off. for example, if you dropped off towels, bring back towels. horse care [section heading present in source document, but the content is missing from both available versions of the file. see edit log.]

documents.title: WebChameleon Intake (Kennel)
documents.purpose: To provide consistent guidelines in the use of WebChameleon and impound transactions.
documents.authority: Not stated in the original SOP.
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable only to licensed users of the Department of Animal Services (DAS) who have a WebChameleon role of user.
documents.supersedes: SOP Chameleon WebChameleon Intake, October 13, 2015 version (revised August 20, 2016).
references: DAS Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Procedure
DAS Policy 000-03, The Chameleon Kennel Window
DAS Policy 000-08, Holding Periods for Animals Impounded
DAS Policy 300-01, Owner Turn-In Procedure
Food and Agriculture Code section 32003

definitions.def: The web and tablet (iPad) interface to the Chameleon shelter management system.
Animal ID.
Person ID.

sections.heading: 1. Home Screen
steps: The Home screen is the starting point after logging in.
Tap on the Menu icon located on the top left corner of the screen.
Tap on Kennel.
Tap on Kennel Search.
Tap on New Intake.

steps.step: The WebChameleon kennel screen consists of four tabs:
sub: Kennel.
Source.
Owner.
Animal.

sections.heading: 2. To Impound a Stray Animal: Kennel Tab. Refer to policies in traditional Chameleon.
steps: Tap on the kennel # (required field) for a list of kennel numbers, or type in RR for RReceiving for RVSD, T# for CVAC, etc.
Tap on the status (required field) for a list of kennel statuses, or type S for stray wait.
Tap on Intake Type (required field) for a list of intake types, or type ST for stray.
Tap on Sub Type, or enter the first letters of the applicable subtype.
Tap on the Intake Condition (required field) and enter BAR, or the beginning letters of the applicable condition.
Tap on the Intake Date (required field); a calendar will be displayed. Choose the date the animal was impounded.
Tap on the Intake Time (required field) and choose the time or enter the exact time of impound.
Tap on the Due Out Date and time (required field) and choose the date when the animal is available for adoption. Refer to policy 000-08.
Enter Y in the Hold field for quarantines and similar holds.
Based on policy 000-03, enter the complete address where the animal was impounded from, or enter the complete cross streets.
Tap on Jurisdiction (required field) and choose the appropriate city where the animal was impounded from.
Tap on Intake By ID (required field) and enter your badge number or user ID.

sections.heading: 3. To Impound a Stray Animal: Source Tab
steps: In accordance with Food and Agriculture Code section 32003, the complete information on who turned in the animal must be collected. The user cannot place a check mark on Skip This Information.
Prior to creating a new record, and in accordance with policy 000-01, perform a person search by name, address, phone number, driver's license, email address, etc. to prevent duplicate records.
If an existing record is located, click on the search result of the matching person record. WebChameleon will automatically populate the source screen.
If a record does not exist, proceed by completing the source screen in compliance with policy 000-01 and policy 000-03.

sections.heading: 4. To Impound a Stray Animal: Owner Tab
steps: Tap on Skip This Information to place a check mark.

sections.heading: 5. To Impound a Stray Animal: Animal Tab
steps: Prior to creating a new animal record, perform a search based on a microchip or license information, name of the dog, or type of animal to avoid duplicates, only when this information is available.
If the animal does not have a microchip or license information, proceed with creating an animal record. Refer to policy 000-01.

steps.step: To add a memo:
sub: Click on the memo icon.
Enter specific memo text to find a memo on the AID.
To create a new memo, tap on the date field to bring up the calendar and select the memo date.
Tap on memo type for a list of options. Refer to DAS policies and SOPs for applicable types.
If applicable, tap on memo subtype applying current SOPs and policies, or leave blank.
Tap on memo template and choose the appropriate template based on current policies and procedures, if applicable, or skip.
If a memo template is selected, the memo text will display template items; otherwise begin entering the memo using the iPad keyboard or by dictating using the iPad microphone.
Save the memo by tapping on the save icon. The stored memo is displayed in the list.
To close the memo window, tap on the x icon located at the top left of the screen.

steps.step: To view a memo:
sub: Related to an animal ID: tap on the memo icon from the animal window with an AID displayed.
Related to a person ID: tap on the memo icon from the person window with a PID displayed.

steps: Save the kennel record by tapping on the save icon located at the top left of the screen, then tap on Save Information.

steps.step: To attach an image:
sub: In the animal tab screen, tap on the image icon.
Tap on Choose File to upload an image.
Go to the directory where the picture file is kept, select the picture to upload, then tap Open.
The picture is displayed on the image upload screen. Tap on Upload.
The picture is now uploaded to the AID.
Close the animal image screen by tapping the x button located at the top left of the screen.

sections.heading: 6. To Impound a Confiscate Animal: Kennel Tab. Refer to policies in traditional Chameleon.
steps: Tap on the kennel # (required field) for a list of kennel numbers, or type in RR for RReceiving for RVSD, T# for CVAC, etc.
Tap on the status (required field) for a list of kennel statuses, or type U for unavail.
Tap on Intake Type (required field) for a list of intake types, or type C for confiscate.
Tap on Sub Type, or enter the first letters of the applicable subtype.
Tap on the Intake Condition (required field) and enter BAR, or the beginning letters of the applicable condition.
Tap on the Intake Date (required field); a calendar will be displayed. Choose the date the animal was impounded.
Tap on the Intake Time (required field) and choose the time or enter the exact time of impound.
Tap on the Due Out Date and time (required field) and choose the date when the animal is available for adoption. Refer to policy 000-08.
Enter the applicable reference number in the Other ID field.
Enter Y in the Hold field for quarantines and similar holds.
Based on policy 000-03, enter the complete address where the animal was impounded from, or enter the complete cross streets.
Tap on Jurisdiction (required field) and choose the appropriate city where the animal was impounded from.
Tap on Intake By ID (required field) and enter your badge number or user ID.
Refer to the Source Tab, Owner Tab, and Animal Tab sections above for the remaining steps.

related.name: WebChameleon Kennel screen (Kennel, Source, Owner, Animal tabs)

appendices: Appendix A: WebChameleon screen captures (screenshots appear throughout the source document; images not reproduced in this migration, see source file)

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2016 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: General Chameleon Information
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure for the use of the Chameleon Animal Management Software.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all DAS personnel who have access to Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-01, effective September 30, 2019; revised from August 16, 2018; August 21, 2017; August 1, 2016; November 6, 2015; July 17, 2015; May 20, 2015; August 29, 2014; April 28, 2014; May 30, 2012; May 19, 2011; May 28, 2007
references: Copyright restrictions of HLP, Inc. (Chameleon software vendor)
DAS Policy 000-01 acknowledgment requirement (form retained in personnel file)

definitions.def: The seven-digit identification number Chameleon issues for a person record.
The seven-digit identification number Chameleon issues for an animal record.
The Person ID window search mode used to locate existing person records before creating a new record.
A trait-based picture selection tool used to identify animal description when the user or customer is not familiar with animal breeds.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Know how to store, update, query, print, and research all records through applicable windows using the Chameleon Animal Management System. This is required of each DAS employee.

Do not share, copy, or print screen any photos in the database, or share, copy, or print screen any Chameleon windows, to any third party who is not a DAS employee without approval from HLP, Inc. and the Chameleon Administrator. This is prohibited under copyright law.

sections.heading: Chameleon Version
steps: Use the current version of Chameleon, which is 45g.

steps.step: Turn on the computer and log onto the network.
sub: User name: username assigned by RC-IT.
Password: enter chosen password.

steps.step: Open Chameleon and log on with the assigned location, User ID, and password.
sub: "S" for Riverside Shelter.
"T" for Thousand Palms.
"B" for Blythe.
"C" for Riverside Spay or Neuter Clinic.
"L" for Licensing mail.
"SJ" for San Jacinto Shelter.
"SJCL" for San Jacinto Spay or Neuter Clinic.
"REUTH" for use only in the Riverside Shelter euthanasia room.
"S/N MOBILE" for use only by staff working the mobile spay/neuter bus.
"SC" used only for staff working the shot clinic.
"LB" used only for authorized WebLicensing processors.
"A" used only for the Western Riverside County/City shelter admissions front counter.
"AD" used only for the Western Riverside County/City shelter adoptions/citations counters.
"LLB" used only for licensing Converge transactions.
"FTBLB" used only for collections Converge transactions.
User ID: user assigned by IT.
Password: enter password.
Sign in to: animal.

sections.heading: Setting Preferences
steps: Set preferences only with the approval of the Chameleon Administrator. Preferences allow local customization of printing, searching, and display settings for Chameleon windows.
From the Chameleon menu bar, choose Procedures, scroll down, and click on Preferences.

steps.step: Complete the main Preference window fields as applicable.
sub: Company Name: the name of the organization, printed at the top of the receipt report.
Receipt Address: the address of the organization, printed at the top of receipts.
License Address: used to get multiple lines for the address.
Receipt Phone (Phone 1): the main phone number of the organization, printed at the top of the receipt.
Fax Number (Phone 2): the main facsimile number of the organization, printed at the top of the receipt.
Bitmap Path: the local, network, or directory from which the splash page pulls the picture.
Ini Path: the local, network, or RAM drive directory in which the Chameleon ini files are located.
Dialog Scale: the default is 0.0250000. Because window sizes differ, staff should not change this default.
Disable StreetNo Suffix: placing a check mark disables the suffix field in the person address window.
Disable Street Quadrant: placing a check mark disables the QDT field in the person address window.
Grid Rows: the default is 1000. This allows the user to view records in the grid up to a maximum of 1000.
No Print Preview: placing a check mark sends the report directly to a printer without reviewing it first.

steps.step: Set Treatment Preferences to automatically load medication protocols or schedules in the treatment window.
sub: Place a check mark in Autoload ToDo Items on Store.
Enter "08:00" in the 1 x per Day Time field.
Enter "08:00, 16:00" in the 2 x per Day Time field.
Enter the times exactly as shown above.
Press OK to close the Treatment Preferences window.

steps.step: Set Activity Preferences.
sub: Enter "200" to set the street search range. This sets the activity window street range search to look 200 east and 200 west from the address entered in the activity address fields.
Click OK to accept the changes and close Activity Preferences.

steps.step: Set Tag Preferences for processing multiple licenses in the Licensing department.
sub: License months valid: 12.
Enter "VACCERT" in the Rabies Cert item code field.
Enter "LIC DAN" in the Dangerous dog item code field.
Activate normal license sequencing.
Under Tag Renewal Procedure: enter L and the current year (LYY) in the LicNo Prefix field; Tag Date Based on Today must be on; Tag Exp Based on Prev Tag Exp must be on.
Place a check mark on "Don't Enforce VacExp in Tag".
Do not place a check mark in "Don't Show VacExp Button".
Place a check mark in Carry Jurisdiction From Person. This displays the jurisdiction from the person window in the tag jurisdiction field.

steps.step: Set Kennel Preferences.
sub: Days to Review: the default is 0. Do not change.
Max Search Back: enter 1 to 99999 to search prior kennel records.
Leave the default check marks on Carry IDs from Tag Popup, Carry IDs from Activity Popup, and Carry Jurisdiction from Source.
Press OK to accept changes and close the Kennel Preferences window.

steps: Do not use the Visual Kennel Configuration at all.

steps.step: Set Address Verify Preferences.
sub: Place a check mark in front of: Verify Addresses; Don't Enforce (allow Store); Show List of Possible Matches; Copy Data to Screen on Store/Update.
Press OK to accept changes and close the window.

steps: Do not edit or change Reports Preferences.
Do not edit or change Extras without Chameleon Administrator approval.

steps.step: Set Dispatch Preferences, which set the maximum time for an ACO to be on an activity and the color settings of priorities.
sub: ACO Overdue: enter the maximum minutes for an ACO to respond to an activity.
Default Sort: provides the default sorting order of the dispatch view window.
High Priority (purple): 1.
Medium Priority (yellow): 2.
Low Priority (green): 3.
Default Zoom ACO: enter the default Person ID of the ACO.
Press OK to accept changes and close the window.

steps.step: Set Receipt Preferences.
sub: Place a check mark in front of: Auto Debt-Lookup; Debt-Lookup: get $0 Kennels; Debt-Lookup: get $0 Tags; Debt-Lookup: get $0 Treatments; No User Created Receipt Nos; No Positive Balances (to process payment plans or bills); No Negative Balances (to process overpayments).
Debt Scripts: RTO, Adopt, OwnSur, Transfer.
Press OK to accept.

steps: Leave Printer Preferences unused. Network printers are issued by IT.

steps.step: Set Visual Schedule Preferences.
sub: Place a check mark on No Schedule Overlap and No Event Overlap.
Press OK to accept changes and close the window.

steps: Do not change Database Preferences.
Do not change Fonts. This is the Chameleon font configuration.
Use Colors only to change the background color of the Chameleon window as a user preference.

steps.step: Set Violation Preferences for the violation window.
sub: Place a check mark on Carry IDs from Activity Popup and Carry CallerId into Complainant ID.
Press OK to accept changes and close the window.

steps: Press OK when finished setting applicable preferences.

sections.heading: The Tag/Link Window

steps.step: Understand the four main components of the Tag/Link window.
sub: The Person ID (PID) window contains the data fields in which the pet owner's information is stored.
The Animal ID (AID) window contains the data fields in which animal information is stored.
The Tag window contains the data fields in which the license information is stored.
The Receipt window contains the data fields in which monies collected are recorded and accounted for.

steps: Use the Tag/Link window as the main pet licensing or microchip information window. Its function is to link the person, animal, pet license or microchip, and receipt into one file. Store all information from each component properly.

steps.step: Note the features of the current version of Chameleon.
sub: Allows a memo stored directly based on the displayed tag number record.
Has the ability to view a tag/link history.
Has the ability to attach any type of image or document in different formats to the displayed tag/link record.

sections.heading: The Person ID Window: SuperSearch Mode
steps: Perform a search before creating any person record to ensure the person does not already exist in Chameleon. This preserves the integrity of the data. SuperSearch can be done two ways: by Street No and Last Name, or by Anything. Run queries one at a time.

steps.step: Search by street number and last name (Alt S).
sub: Type the street number, no space, and the last name of the person (for example, 6851Dozier), then click the StreetNoLastName button.
The results return records matching the street number and last name anywhere in the person window fields.
Use the wildcard (%) as needed, for example 6851Doz%.

steps.step: Search by anything (Alt A) using any of the accepted formats.
sub: First name, comma, last name (Robert,Diaz).
Telephone number with no area code or dashes (3587387).
Driver's license (A123456).
Last name, comma, street name (Dozier,Van Buren).
Date of birth (mm/dd/yy), for example 12/31/66.
PO BOX with no periods (PO space BOX space number, for example PO BOX 1234).
Middle name, comma, street name (MARIE,VAN BUREN or MARIE,VANB%).
Street number, comma, street name (72050,petland or 72050,petl%).
Street number, comma, street name, comma, apartment/space/suite number (5800,HAMNER,200 or 5800,HAM%,200).

steps: Place the cursor in the PID box and press F4 to pop up the window. The cursor will be at the SuperSearch field ready for data entry.
Press F11 to clear the screen so the search is clean.

If a person or multiple persons exist in the database with the specified criteria, they will appear in the list box below the SuperSearch field. Select the correct person by double clicking in the list box.
Review the person list grid, which displays all person records matching the SuperSearch, and the animal list grid, which displays all animals owned by the person.

sections.heading: The Person ID Window: Creating (Storing) a Record
steps: Place the cursor in the PID field and press F4 to pop up the window. The cursor will be at the Last Name field.

Create a PID only when one of these criteria is met: last name, first name, complete address, and telephone number; or last name, first name, and complete address. This prevents storing of unknown person records.

steps.step: Enter all known owner information.
sub: Name: first and last name.
Status: Active is for regular licenses; Senior is for pet owners over the age of 60 years old.
Address: broken into numeric street address, street suffix, street name, direction, type, apartment number, city, state, and zip code. Make sure all streets and cities are spelled correctly.

steps.step: If the pet owner wants to receive mail at an address other than their residence, enter the second address.
sub: For a PO Box mailing address: Street Number = 0; Street Name = PO BOX and the number (for example, 0 PO BOX 1234); then city, state, and zip code.
For any other mailing address, follow the standard address entry procedure.
Type = MAIL.

steps: Phone Number (Phone 1): enter the seven-digit number with the appropriate area code.
Alternate Number (Phone 2): enter the area code then the seven-digit number. Can be used for a cell phone number.
Alternate Number (Phone 3): enter the area code then the seven-digit number. Can be used for a fax or work number.

Identifier1: type an acceptable current government form of identification, such as a driver's license, state identification card, passport, or Social Security number (for example, A1234567; no need to indicate state, country, or type).
Identifier2: enter the government identification expiration date (for example, 05/16/15).

steps.step: Complete the address notes field.
sub: Include the map reference and both cross streets separated by an X. For example, 648-A1 Van Buren X Clay.
Include house directions, such as third house on the right from Main with a red brick sign.

steps.step: Complete the email address field only with a valid email address or text address. Leave it blank if the criteria are not met.
sub: To enter an email address, the entry must contain valid email address text with an @ sign, for example emailaddr@hotmail.com.

To send an email by text, enter the recipient's 10-digit wireless number followed by the @ sign and the carrier text platform, for example 5551234567@txt.att.net. See Appendix A for carrier address formats.
To send a picture or video message by text, enter the recipient's 10-digit wireless number followed by the carrier MMS platform, for example 5551234567@mms.att.net.
Do not include dashes or spaces between the numbers.

steps.step: Choose the applicable contact preference type.
sub: EMAIL: contact preference is by email only.
BOTH: contact preference is by email and mail.
MAIL: contact preference is by mail only.
REFUSED: the customer refuses to provide email or text correspondence.
TEXT: contact preference is by text only.

steps.step: Press F9 to store the information.
sub: The address checker will fill in the city, state, zip code, and jurisdiction.
The address checker may give choices; proceed accordingly.

If an error message appears and the address checker is activated, data from TLMA (GIS) is the standard for verifying the address. Make sure the street spelling matches GIS. Also check USPS to verify the correct address.

steps: Chameleon will issue a seven-digit PID number.

Verify that the city name matches the appropriate jurisdiction appearing in the jurisdiction box. With the cursor in the jurisdiction field, press F4 to pop up the window and select the correct jurisdiction.
Confirm the box in the upper left corner turns green, which signals the information was stored.

sections.heading: The Person ID Window: Updating a PID Record
steps: Place the cursor in the PID box and press F4 to pop up the PID window. The cursor will be at the Last Name field.
Find the person via query (see query procedures).
Enter any new or changed information in the appropriate fields.
Press F8 to update the record. If the update is successful, the message "Record Updated Successfully" will appear.

sections.heading: The Person ID Window: Duplicate Person Records
steps: Recognize a duplicate person record: the same person exists in Chameleon more than once with different person IDs. A husband or spouse residing in the same house is not a duplicate record.
If a duplicate person record is located and contains an old address, a mailing address, or a driver's license, transfer this information to the primary PID before submitting.

Avoid creating duplicate person records. If duplicates are avoided, there is no need to enter the old address in the Address2 section of the person window; the user can override the address in the primary address fields because Chameleon retains PID history.
Identify duplicate records when using SuperSearch mode.
If more than one record for the same person is located, double click the person record with the most complete and current information to display it in the person window.
Highlight the duplicate record by clicking once on it in the person list.
Click Procedures, then Submit Person Duplicate, then click OK to complete the submission.

sections.heading: The Animal ID Window

steps.step: To create an animal record, pop up the Animal ID window and enter all known animal information. The cursor will be at the Animal Name field.
sub: Animal Name (for example, Happy).
Type: Dog or Cat.
Sex: M or F. Enter S or N only if there is proof the animal has been altered.
Color Group: press F4 to pop up and make a selection. Do not use "OO" for dog and cat records.
Color Primary: press F4 to pop up and make a selection. If necessary, enter a secondary color.
Breed Group: leave blank; Chameleon will fill this in.
Breed Primary: press F4 to pop up and make a selection.
Breed Secondary: press F4 to pop up and make a selection. If the dog is not a mixed breed, delete "mix" from the secondary breed box.
Size: press F4 to pop up and make a selection.
Note: HLP goes by looks-like breed. DAS cannot add breeds without HLP approval because doing so breaks the PetHarbor website.

steps: Press F9 to store the information. Chameleon will automatically issue a seven-digit Animal ID number. Press Enter to return to the Tag/Link window.

steps.step: To determine whether an animal record is duplicated, perform a SuperSearch in the PID window.
sub: Double click on each person record displayed.
The grid below the person record displays animals linked, licensed, or microchipped to the person.
If a duplicate animal record is located under one or more person records, choose the most complete animal record by double clicking it in the person window. This brings up the animal window.
With the animal record highlighted in the person window, choose Procedures, then "Submit Animal Dup - Select correct animal ID."
Choose the second duplicate animal record from the same person record, or double click the other person record and click once on the animal record.
From Procedures, choose "Submit Animal Dup - Select duplicate animal ID."
A screen will display and verify the two animal IDs submitted. Click OK.
Chameleon will display that the submission was successful.

steps.step: To update an Animal ID record:
sub: Place the cursor in the Animal ID box and press F4 to pop up the window. The cursor will be at the animal name field.
Find the animal via license number.
Enter any new or changed information in the appropriate fields.
Press F8 to update the record.

steps.step: Use SCENT Matching to identify animal description through pictures, particularly when the customer or user is not familiar with animal breeds.
sub: Click on Examples (Alt+X).
Select traits for Size, Coat, Ears, Nose, and Tail.
Use Lookup Breed to display matching breeds and select one.

sections.heading: The Receipt Window

steps.step: If preferences have not been set to Create Receipt on Store, complete the receipt manually.
sub: Place the cursor in the Receipt No box and press F4 to pop up the receipt window.
If all license information stored successfully, the item code with the price for the license will be on the screen.
If any other fees, such as a late fee, replacement tag fee, or donation, have been collected, list each item separately.
Place the cursor in the Item Code field and press F4 to pop up the Item Code Inventory. Select the item and click OK.
Click the Lookup box. This fills in the appropriate Department field and price and ensures collected fees are recorded in the appropriate department.
Click the Add box to add the item to the receipt. Do this for each item on the receipt.

steps: Enter the amount of cash or check paid. The balance must always read 0.00.
Press F9 to store the information. A window will appear that reads "Process Complete: stored...stored...". Click Cancel unless a copy of the receipt is needed.
Press Enter. The receipt number will be automatically entered in the receipt field in the main transaction window.

sections.heading: The Memo Window
steps: Use the Memo window to store large amounts of general text (comments, descriptions, field reports, or ticklers) up to 2,500 characters. Memos may be attached to many different subjects. Windows cut and paste may be used from other applications.

steps.step: Complete the memo fields.
sub: Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: holds the ID for the subject of the memo (person, animal, etc.).
Memo ID Type: displays the applicable window the memo was accessed from: Activity_No, Animal_ID, Bite_No, Complaint, Group_ID, Person_ID, Receipt_No, Treat_No, Viol_No, or Tag_No.
Tag_No memos allow only the formats L##-###### (for example, L18-123456) and U##-###### (for example, U18-123456). No extra characters are allowed.
Date: normally contains the date the memo was created.

Author: automatically displays the original creator of the memo regardless of later updates. Depending on privileges, the author can override the initial memo, add to it, or erase it and enter new text.

steps.step: Select the memo type that designates the nature or topic of the memo.
sub: Accounting: used when generating payment plan, journal entry, and refund transactions.
Behv Grade: used by DAS behaviorists to grade impounded animals.
Citation: used by DAS staff to document citation-related topics.
Danger: used by DAS staff to document an explanation of who is in danger or who is dangerous.
Donation: used by DAS staff to document a donation received or used.
Event: used by DAS staff to document an animal's participation in events and how long the animal will be at an event.
Foster: used by DAS staff to document updates for fostered animals or foster parents.
Hearing: used by Field Services to document pending hearings and results of hearings.
Investigat: used by Field Services to document ongoing investigations held by police departments or cruelty issues.
Note: used by all staff for any other documentation.
Rescue Grp: used by all staff to document collaboration with rescue groups and impounded animals.
Interest: used by all staff to document constituents who are interested in adopting an impounded animal.
KEN CHANGE: used by all staff to document a change of an impounded animal's location. This information is critical to resolving inventory issues.
Safety Net: used to justify an outcome request of DO NOT PTD, indicating the animal is not to be euthanized.
O Notified: used by all staff to document verified ownership of an impounded animal and how the owner was notified.
OWNER Info: used by all staff to document possible ownership of an impounded animal.
WEB DATA: used primarily to document notes from Lockbox/WebLicensing transactions.

steps.step: Select the memo subtype, which further specifies the memo type.
sub: If the memo type is Behv Grade, the subtype is A, B, B Minus, C, Treatable, or Untreatable.
If the memo type is INVESTIGAT or HEARING, the subtype can be RESTRICTED.

steps.step: Include the following in every memo text.
sub: Date created.
Time created.
First name initial and full last name, or badge number.
Specific name of the person and/or animal the memo pertains to.

sections.heading: Chameleon Icons
steps: Find icon, or shortcut key F12.
Update icon, or shortcut key F8.
Store icon, or shortcut key F9.
Clear screen icon, or shortcut key F11.
Memo icon: displays the memo window.
Address Verifier icon, or shortcut key Alt+V.
Return icon: returns all information to the main window. Use the Enter key.
Close screen key: closes the screen without bringing the information back to the main window.
Person Profile icon: displays the profile window; used to gather information contained in a driver's license or state ID.
Animal Profile icon: displays the animal profile window.
Caduceus (Treatment) icon: displays the treatment window.
Adopter Profile icon: displays a potential adopter's window.
Adopter Interview icon: displays a potential adopter's interview window.
To Add icon: used with the memo template; adds text.
To Remove icon: used with the memo template; removes text.
Bad Check icon: used if the owner has written Animal Services a check that has been returned by the bank. Must be accompanied by an Accounting memo.
Bite icon: used when the person has an animal associated with them that has bitten. A bite window must already exist.
Donor icon: used when a person donates to the shelter.
No Adopt icon: used when a person is suspected of being a poor adopter candidate.
Dangerous icon: set when a person is known to have threatened, intimidated, or attacked any Animal Services personnel.
History icon: appears on most Chameleon windows. Allows the user to view the history of the displayed record, including changes with the date and time stamp of each change.
Copy Primary Address to Address2 icon: allows the user to transfer old addresses to the secondary address automatically.

Attachment icon: appears on most Chameleon windows and allows attaching pictures or documents in formats such as .ppt, .pdf, .bmp, .jpeg, .mp3, and .mpg. See the SOP for attaching images and documents to Chameleon.

sections.heading: Acknowledgment
steps: Sign the Acknowledgment of Department of Animal Services Policy for General Chameleon Information (Appendix B).
Retain the signed form in the employee's personnel file and attach it to the employee's Person ID in Chameleon.

DAS Policy 000-38, The Chameleon Tag/Link Window

Person, Animal, Tag/Link, Receipt, Memo, Treatment, Activity, Violation, Dispatch

revisions.desc: Original policy (most recent revision; prior revisions 2007 through 2018).

revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2019 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.searchText: to establish a uniform procedure for the use of the chameleon animal management software. applicable to all das personnel who have access to chameleon. director of animal services pid (person id) the seven-digit identification number chameleon issues for a person record. aid (animal id) the seven-digit identification number chameleon issues for an animal record. supersearch the person id window search mode used to locate existing person records before creating a new record. scent matching a trait-based picture selection tool used to identify animal description when the user or customer is not familiar with animal breeds. policy know how to store, update, query, print, and research all records through applicable windows using the chameleon animal management system. this is required of each das employee. do not share, copy, or print screen any photos in the database, or share, copy, or print screen any chameleon windows, to any third party who is not a das employee without approval from hlp, inc. and the chameleon administrator. this is prohibited under copyright law. chameleon version use the current version of chameleon, which is 45g.
