(72050,petland or 72050,petl%).", "street number, comma, street name, comma, apartment/space/suite number (5800,hamner,200 or 5800,ham%,200)."]} place the cursor in the pid box and press f4 to pop up the window. the cursor will be at the supersearch field ready for data entry. press f11 to clear the screen so the search is clean. if a person or multiple persons exist in the database with the specified criteria, they will appear in the list box below the supersearch field. select the correct person by double clicking in the list box. review the person list grid, which displays all person records matching the supersearch, and the animal list grid, which displays all animals owned by the person. the person id window: creating (storing) a record place the cursor in the pid field and press f4 to pop up the window. the cursor will be at the last name field. create a pid only when one of these criteria is met: last name, first name, complete address, and telephone number; or last name, first name, and complete address. this prevents storing of unknown person records. {"step": "enter all known owner information.", "sub": ["name: first and last name.", "status: active is for regular licenses; senior is for pet owners over the age of 60 years old.", "address: broken into numeric street address, street suffix, street name, direction, type, apartment number, city, state, and zip code. make sure all streets and cities are spelled correctly."]} {"step": "if the pet owner wants to receive mail at an address other than their residence, enter the second address.", "sub": ["for a po box mailing address: street number = 0; street name = po box and the number (for example, 0 po box 1234); then city, state, and zip code.", "for any other mailing address, follow the standard address entry procedure.", "type = mail."]} phone number (phone 1): enter the seven-digit number with the appropriate area code. alternate number (phone 2): enter the area code then the seven-digit number. can be used for a cell phone number. alternate number (phone 3): enter the area code then the seven-digit number. can be used for a fax or work number. identifier1: type an acceptable current government form of identification, such as a driver's license, state identification card, passport, or social security number (for example, a1234567; no need to indicate state, country, or type). identifier2: enter the government identification expiration date (for example, 05/16/15). {"step": "complete the address notes field.", "sub": ["include the map reference and both cross streets separated by an x. for example, 648-a1 van buren x clay.", "include house directions, such as third house on the right from main with a red brick sign."]} {"step": "complete the email address field only with a valid email address or text address. leave it blank if the criteria are not met.", "sub": ["to enter an email address, the entry must contain valid email address text with an @ sign, for example emailaddr@hotmail.com.", "to send an email by text, enter the recipient's 10-digit wireless number followed by the @ sign and the carrier text platform, for example 5551234567@txt.att.net. see appendix a for carrier address formats.", "to send a picture or video message by text, enter the recipient's 10-digit wireless number followed by the carrier mms platform, for example 5551234567@mms.att.net.", "do not include dashes or spaces between the numbers."]} {"step": "choose the applicable contact preference type.", "sub": ["email: contact preference is by email only.", "both: contact preference is by email and mail.", "mail: contact preference is by mail only.", "refused: the customer refuses to provide email or text correspondence.", "text: contact preference is by text only."]} {"step": "press f9 to store the information.", "sub": ["the address checker will fill in the city, state, zip code, and jurisdiction.", "the address checker may give choices; proceed accordingly.", "if an error message appears and the address checker is activated, data from tlma (gis) is the standard for verifying the address. make sure the street spelling matches gis. also check usps to verify the correct address."]} chameleon will issue a seven-digit pid number. verify that the city name matches the appropriate jurisdiction appearing in the jurisdiction box. with the cursor in the jurisdiction field, press f4 to pop up the window and select the correct jurisdiction. confirm the box in the upper left corner turns green, which signals the information was stored. the person id window: updating a pid record place the cursor in the pid box and press f4 to pop up the pid window. the cursor will be at the last name field. find the person via query (see query procedures). enter any new or changed information in the appropriate fields. press f8 to update the record. if the update is successful, the message "record updated successfully" will appear. the person id window: duplicate person records recognize a duplicate person record: the same person exists in chameleon more than once with different person ids. a husband or spouse residing in the same house is not a duplicate record. if a duplicate person record is located and contains an old address, a mailing address, or a driver's license, transfer this information to the primary pid before submitting. avoid creating duplicate person records. if duplicates are avoided, there is no need to enter the old address in the address2 section of the person window; the user can override the address in the primary address fields because chameleon retains pid history. identify duplicate records when using supersearch mode. if more than one record for the same person is located, double click the person record with the most complete and current information to display it in the person window. highlight the duplicate record by clicking once on it in the person list. click procedures, then submit person duplicate, then click ok to complete the submission. the animal id window {"step": "to create an animal record, pop up the animal id window and enter all known animal information. the cursor will be at the animal name field.", "sub": ["animal name (for example, happy).", "type: dog or cat.", "sex: m or f. enter s or n only if there is proof the animal has been altered.", "color group: press f4 to pop up and make a selection. do not use \"oo\" for dog and cat records.", "color primary: press f4 to pop up and make a selection. if necessary, enter a secondary color.", "breed group: leave blank; chameleon will fill this in.", "breed primary: press f4 to pop up and make a selection.", "breed secondary: press f4 to pop up and make a selection. if the dog is not a mixed breed, delete \"mix\" from the secondary breed box.", "size: press f4 to pop up and make a selection.", "note: hlp goes by looks-like breed. das cannot add breeds without hlp approval because doing so breaks the petharbor website."]} press f9 to store the information. chameleon will automatically issue a seven-digit animal id number. press enter to return to the tag/link window. {"step": "to determine whether an animal record is duplicated, perform a supersearch in the pid window.", "sub": ["double click on each person record displayed.", "the grid below the person record displays animals linked, licensed, or microchipped to the person.", "if a duplicate animal record is located under one or more person records, choose the most complete animal record by double clicking it in the person window. this brings up the animal window.", "with the animal record highlighted in the person window, choose procedures, then \"submit animal dup - select correct animal id.\"", "choose the second duplicate animal record from the same person record, or double click the other person record and click once on the animal record.", "from procedures, choose \"submit animal dup - select duplicate animal id.\"", "a screen will display and verify the two animal ids submitted. click ok.", "chameleon will display that the submission was successful."]} {"step": "to update an animal id record:", "sub": ["place the cursor in the animal id box and press f4 to pop up the window. the cursor will be at the animal name field.", "find the animal via license number.", "enter any new or changed information in the appropriate fields.", "press f8 to update the record."]} {"step": "use scent matching to identify animal description through pictures, particularly when the customer or user is not familiar with animal breeds.", "sub": ["click on examples (alt+x).", "select traits for size, coat, ears, nose, and tail.", "use lookup breed to display matching breeds and select one."]} the receipt window {"step": "if preferences have not been set to create receipt on store, complete the receipt manually.", "sub": ["place the cursor in the receipt no box and press f4 to pop up the receipt window.", "if all license information stored successfully, the item code with the price for the license will be on the screen.", "if any other fees, such as a late fee, replacement tag fee, or donation, have been collected, list each item separately.", "place the cursor in the item code field and press f4 to pop up the item code inventory. select the item and click ok.", "click the lookup box. this fills in the appropriate department field and price and ensures collected fees are recorded in the appropriate department.", "click the add box to add the item to the receipt. do this for each item on the receipt."]} enter the amount of cash or check paid. the balance must always read 0.00. press f9 to store the information. a window will appear that reads "process complete: stored...stored...". click cancel unless a copy of the receipt is needed. press enter. the receipt number will be automatically entered in the receipt field in the main transaction window. the memo window use the memo window to store large amounts of general text (comments, descriptions, field reports, or ticklers) up to 2,500 characters. memos may be attached to many different subjects. windows cut and paste may be used from other applications. {"step": "complete the memo fields.", "sub": ["memo no: the unique number generated by the system.", "memo id: holds the id for the subject of the memo (person, animal, etc.).", "memo id type: displays the applicable window the memo was accessed from: activity_no, animal_id, bite_no, complaint, group_id, person_id, receipt_no, treat_no, viol_no, or tag_no.", "tag_no memos allow only the formats l##-###### (for example, l18-123456) and u##-###### (for example, u18-123456). no extra characters are allowed.", "date: normally contains the date the memo was created.", "author: automatically displays the original creator of the memo regardless of later updates. depending on privileges, the author can override the initial memo, add to it, or erase it and enter new text."]} {"step": "select the memo type that designates the nature or topic of the memo.", "sub": ["accounting: used when generating payment plan, journal entry, and refund transactions.", "behv grade: used by das behaviorists to grade impounded animals.", "citation: used by das staff to document citation-related topics.", "danger: used by das staff to document an explanation of who is in danger or who is dangerous.", "donation: used by das staff to document a donation received or used.", "event: used by das staff to document an animal's participation in events and how long the animal will be at an event.", "foster: used by das staff to document updates for fostered animals or foster parents.", "hearing: used by field services to document pending hearings and results of hearings.", "investigat: used by field services to document ongoing investigations held by police departments or cruelty issues.", "note: used by all staff for any other documentation.", "rescue grp: used by all staff to document collaboration with rescue groups and impounded animals.", "interest: used by all staff to document constituents who are interested in adopting an impounded animal.", "ken change: used by all staff to document a change of an impounded animal's location. this information is critical to resolving inventory issues.", "safety net: used to justify an outcome request of do not ptd, indicating the animal is not to be euthanized.", "o notified: used by all staff to document verified ownership of an impounded animal and how the owner was notified.", "owner info: used by all staff to document possible ownership of an impounded animal.", "web data: used primarily to document notes from lockbox/weblicensing transactions."]} {"step": "select the memo subtype, which further specifies the memo type.", "sub": ["if the memo type is behv grade, the subtype is a, b, b minus, c, treatable, or untreatable.", "if the memo type is investigat or hearing, the subtype can be restricted."]} {"step": "include the following in every memo text.", "sub": ["date created.", "time created.", "first name initial and full last name, or badge number.", "specific name of the person and/or animal the memo pertains to."]} chameleon icons find icon, or shortcut key f12. update icon, or shortcut key f8. store icon, or shortcut key f9. clear screen icon, or shortcut key f11. memo icon: displays the memo window. address verifier icon, or shortcut key alt+v. return icon: returns all information to the main window. use the enter key. close screen key: closes the screen without bringing the information back to the main window. person profile icon: displays the profile window; used to gather information contained in a driver's license or state id. animal profile icon: displays the animal profile window. caduceus (treatment) icon: displays the treatment window. adopter profile icon: displays a potential adopter's window. adopter interview icon: displays a potential adopter's interview window. to add icon: used with the memo template; adds text. to remove icon: used with the memo template; removes text. bad check icon: used if the owner has written animal services a check that has been returned by the bank. must be accompanied by an accounting memo. bite icon: used when the person has an animal associated with them that has bitten. a bite window must already exist. donor icon: used when a person donates to the shelter. no adopt icon: used when a person is suspected of being a poor adopter candidate. dangerous icon: set when a person is known to have threatened, intimidated, or attacked any animal services personnel. history icon: appears on most chameleon windows. allows the user to view the history of the displayed record, including changes with the date and time stamp of each change. copy primary address to address2 icon: allows the user to transfer old addresses to the secondary address automatically. attachment icon: appears on most chameleon windows and allows attaching pictures or documents in formats such as .ppt, .pdf, .bmp, .jpeg, .mp3, and .mpg. see the sop for attaching images and documents to chameleon. acknowledgment sign the acknowledgment of department of animal services policy for general chameleon information (appendix b). retain the signed form in the employee's personnel file and attach it to the employee's person id in chameleon. documents.title: The Chameleon Activity Window
documents.flag: Numbered (=Policy per rule) but reads as a step-by-step protocol
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform and consistent procedure for completing the Chameleon System Activity Window.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services (approved by the Director)
documents.scope: Applicable to all DAS employees who have access to Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-04, effective August 21, 2020; revised from February 4, 2020; July 18, 2019; June 11, 2019; December 21, 2016; October 21, 2014; June 15, 2009; May 6, 2008
references: DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information (person window completion)
Contract city reporting requirements

definitions.def: An activity created by an officer in the field rather than dispatched from a received call.
The dispatcher who routes, refiles, and closes out field activities.
The person reporting the activity.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Initiate an Activity Window for every service activity completed by a field officer.
Complete the Activity Window as accurately and in as much detail as possible by the end of the work shift, to allow clear and easy research and to meet contract city reporting requirements.

sections.heading: Entering an Activity: Field Definitions
steps: Activity No: this field is automatically generated when storing an activity. The first two digits indicate the year the activity was initiated.
Seq: leave blank unless the activity needs an additional sequence number. If so, type in the next sequential number; otherwise this field is automatically generated when storing the window.

steps.step: Type: pop up (F4) and enter the appropriate type of call being initiated (see Appendix A, Activity Usage Matrix).
sub: ASSIST: requesting DAS to assist the public or law enforcement on a call (for example, attic, drain, hospital, jail, owner died).
CHECK TRAP: initiated by staff to check on traps that were set at a property.
DEAD: requesting a dead stray animal to be picked up.
DLVR: a delivery needs to be made (for example, trap, FRA to laboratory, paperwork).
INV: requesting an investigation of something (for example, no water, no food, no shelter, possible 597).
OWNED: owner requesting pick up of their pet (dead or alive).
PATROL: ACO to patrol an area.
PICK UP: requesting a pick up (for example, empty trap, freezer pick up at vet).
STRAY: reporting an animal that is not on its owner's property.
TRANSPORT: transporting animals already impounded (in truck or shelter) to an emergency veterinarian or a location other than the shelter.
WILD: used for all requests involving wild animals.
YARD CHK: check yard (for example, unstable fence securing an aggressive animal).

steps.step: Subtype: pop up (F4) and enter the appropriate subtype of call, which defines the activity type (see Appendix A).
sub: ACO, FIRE, OTHER, POLICE, PUBLIC, SPCA.
DMSTC (stray), WILD (raccoon, opossum, coyote, etc.).
PAPERWORK, LABORATORY, TRAP.
BARK (habitual), BARKF (follow-up), BITE, BITEF (follow-up), KENNEL, POSS 597, OTHER.
OWNSUR (surrender for adoption), EUTHREQST (euthanasia requested), DISPREQST (dead owned).
PATROL.
FREEZER, LABORATORY, TRAP, PAPERWORK.
AGGRS (aggressive), CONFINED (confined in a trap), HABIT (habitually loose), INJURED, OPEN YARD, ROAM (roaming area), SICK, TIED (tied to post, tree, fence, etc.).
TO VET.
AGGRES (aggressive skunk, raccoon, coyote, etc.), INJURED, OPENYARD, ROAM, SICK, TIED, TRAP.
ASSESS SITUATION (needs verification for an uncertain condition).
ONOTIFY: used with the ASSIST activity type when an ACO notifies the owner of impounded animals that are still waiting to be reclaimed.

steps.step: Priority: enter the activity's priority (see Appendix A).
sub: Priority 1: highest priority; person in danger or at risk (Code 2).
Priority 2: animal in danger or at risk.
Priority 3: urgent; assist law enforcement or next available officer.
Priority 4: today per supervisor; pick up confined animal in trap.
Priority 5: routine, normal priority.
Priority 6: low priority; deliveries, etc.

steps: Danger: if the call has a priority status of 1 or 2, enter Y to indicate the call is urgent.
Total: enter the total number of animals being reported.
Type (animal): pop up (F4) and enter the type of animal(s) being reported.
Tag No: enter the license number or microchip, if known.
Report Submitted (Extra1) field: the area Sergeant and/or area Lieutenant will enter SUBMITTED upon receipt of a PDR, DR, VR, or CITE.
Area code and Phone: enter the area code and telephone number of the activity address, if known.

steps.step: Caller ID: complete the person window (per Policy 000-01) for the person reporting the activity. If the caller information is incomplete, use the comment field to enter the partial information of the caller.
sub: Enter P0396507 if the caller does not provide a complete address and complete first and last name. Replace this once the reporting party's information is obtained.
Enter the PID of the officer for field initiated calls.
For police or sheriff departments, refer to the list provided by Field Services.

steps.step: Activity street address: where to send the officer.
sub: No: enter the street address numbers of the activity address.
Sfx: suffix of the street number, for example 1/2.
Dir: enter the street direction (N, S, E, W) of the activity address.
Name: enter the street name of the activity address.
Type: enter the street type (Ln, Dr, Cir, etc.) of the activity address.
Qdt: leave blank.
Apt: enter the apartment, space, or building number of the activity address, if known.
Zip: enter the zip code of the activity address, if known.
Common Place Name: enter the name of the business or landmark.
City: enter the city, in full, of the activity address.
State: should default to CA; if not, enter CA.
Cross Street Low: enter the first major cross street. This is a required field and must be completed because it affects officers using WebChameleon.

Cross Street High: enter the second major cross street. It must not run parallel to the first major cross street. This is a required field and must be completed because it affects officers using WebChameleon.
Comment: enter brief comments here. Enter any lengthy comments in a Memo window and attach them to the activity.

steps.step: Chameleon mapping:
sub: Geo: pop up (F4) and enter the patrol area of the activity address.
Jurisdiction: pop up (F4) and enter the city of the activity address. Press the verify icon (house) to verify the correct city (jurisdiction) has been entered.
Map Page: enter the Thomas Guide map reference page.
Latitude/Longitude: based on the activity address entered and the correct spelling of the street name as accepted by RCGIS, ChamMap automatically calculates the latitude and longitude.

steps.step: Second reporting party information:
sub: 2nd RP field (Extra2): enter the second RP name.
2nd RP phone (Extra3): enter the second RP phone number.

steps.step: Field euthanasia fields. These are not to be used for euthanasia tracking; use the kennel window for dispositions.
sub: Bottle No (Extra4): enter the bottle number in the format YY-######.
Dose (Extra5): enter the number of ccs of drug used.

steps.step: Animal and owner information:
sub: Animal ID: enter the animal ID number. Only one animal ID number may be attached to the activity. Store multiple animal IDs that were not impounded in a Memo window attached to the activity.
Animal Description: complete the animal's description if obtained.

Owner ID: if the animal for which the activity is being created has a known owner, enter the person ID number. Only one person ID number may be attached to the activity. Store multiple person IDs in a Memo window attached to the activity.
Owner Description: if a description of the owner is obtained, enter it in this field.

steps.step: Field actions and results:
sub: Status: pop up (F4) and choose the appropriate selection. If the call is stored with a status of New, the call is sent directly to dispatch. All Field Initiated Calls (FIC) shall be stored with a status of Completed.
Call: the date and time the call originated.
New: the date and time the call is to be handled.
Dispatch: the date and time the call is dispatched.
Working: the date and time the officer arrived on scene.
Complete: the date and time the officer completed the call.
Clerk ID: Chameleon stores the user ID that stored the activity, even if updated. This cannot be overridden.
Officer ID: if the call has been completed, the officer handling the call shall enter his or her Chameleon System ID number (P99XXXX). If the call is new or is to be placed in pending, leave blank.
Cancel By: user ID of staff who cancelled the activity.
Reason: reason for the cancellation of the activity.
ChamAlert: if the activity was received from WebActivity, this field displays the web notification.
Qty: enter the numerical value that directly corresponds with the Result field next to it.
Result: pop up (F4) and enter the appropriate result type (see Appendix C for result type meanings).
Only six Qty and Result codes can be recorded per call. If more than six results occurred for an incident, enter the six most important codes.

The status and date/time fields are generally completed by the system based on the routing of the call through the Dispatch window. It remains the responsibility of the officer handling the call to make sure the dates and times are correct and consistent with when the call was handled.

sections.heading: Specific Information

steps.step: Field Initiated Calls: for each FIC completed, the officer shall create and complete an Activity window, following the same instructions above with these exceptions:
sub: Caller ID: enter the P number of the officer who initiated the call.
No: if no specific address is appropriate, enter 0.
Complete the activity address information per the street address instructions above.

steps: Vicious/Potentially Dangerous Dog Investigations: initiate a separate sequence for each date that an officer works on the investigation. That officer is responsible for closing out the call with the appropriate result code(s) and generating the next sequence to be placed in pending. All completed vicious/potentially dangerous dog investigations shall have a result code of RPRT and a report written using Microsoft Word. The report will not be written in the Chameleon System Memo window.

Bite reports: all bite reports not initiated in the field (for example, by the rabies desk or clerical staff) will have a new activity sequence generated for each officer's attempts for that bite report activity. The officer who attempts service for his or her sequence is responsible for the disposition (result and times) and/or the refiling of the call for the following shift. The comment field must contain the date and time of the bite and the circumstances of the bite if available. This also requires use of the bite memo template.

Compliance checks: officers who are unable to successfully complete a compliance check shall have the activity refiled through dispatch with the appropriate result code. This generates a new sequence for the following officer, who is then responsible for the proper disposition of his or her activity sequence.

Refile: with the exception of vicious dog investigations and bite reports above, all calls requiring refile for the following shift or officer follow-up shall be placed in pending by the Dispatch Services Operator (DSO) utilizing the Dispatch View only. The DSO is required to input the appropriate result(s) in the pop-up pending Results window unless the officer is using the WebChameleon activity. Field officers shall not personally place calls in pending without prior authorization from the Supervisor. The officer will note the supervisor's name and the purpose of refiling in the Activity memo. It is the responsibility of the dispatcher or animal control officer to set the time of the new call.

Completed calls: all field officers shall close out each activity through the DSO, utilizing the current radio system, prior to handling the next activity. Field officers shall not call the DSO on the Dispatch Service line or use their personal cell phones to close out activities unless specifically requested to do so by the dispatcher or supervisor. Field officers are responsible for ensuring that all dispositions are accurate and that any needed corrections are made in the Chameleon System prior to the end of their shift.

Memos: enter all activity memos prior to the end of shift. An activity memo is required any time the activity result code does not sufficiently explain the outcome of the activity (for example, when the result type RSLVD, EDUC, UNFND, or YRDCK is used, all cruelty investigations, all agency assists, etc.).

appendices: Appendix A: Activity Usage Matrix (Attachment A of original)
Appendix B: Activity Geo Codes
Appendix C: Activity Result Types
Appendix D: Activity Window Reference Sheet (Attachment B of original, screen shot; attached separately)
Appendix E: Acknowledgment of Chameleon Activity Window Policy (form, attached separately)

revisions.author: RCDAS
revisions.desc: Original policy (most recent revision; prior revisions 2008 through February 2020). Approved by the Director of Animal Services.

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2020 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

field services."]} {"step": "activity street address: where to send the officer.", "sub": ["no: enter the street address numbers of the activity address.", "sfx: suffix of the street number, for example 1/2.", "dir: enter the street direction (n, s, e, w) of the activity address.", "name: enter the street name of the activity address.", "type: enter the street type (ln, dr, cir, etc.) of the activity address.", "qdt: leave blank.", "apt: enter the apartment, space, or building number of the activity address, if known.", "zip: enter the zip code of the activity address, if known.", "common place name: enter the name of the business or landmark.", "city: enter the city, in full, of the activity address.", "state: should default to ca; if not, enter ca.", "cross street low: enter the first major cross street. this is a required field and must be completed because it affects officers using webchameleon.", "cross street high: enter the second major cross street. it must not run parallel to the first major cross street. this is a required field and must be completed because it affects officers using webchameleon.", "comment: enter brief comments here. enter any lengthy comments in a memo window and attach them to the activity."]} {"step": "chameleon mapping:", "sub": ["geo: pop up (f4) and enter the patrol area of the activity address.", "jurisdiction: pop up (f4) and enter the city of the activity address. press the verify icon (house) to verify the correct city (jurisdiction) has been entered.", "map page: enter the thomas guide map reference page.", "latitude/longitude: based on the activity address entered and the correct spelling of the street name as accepted by rcgis, chammap automatically calculates the latitude and longitude."]} {"step": "second reporting party information:", "sub": ["2nd rp field (extra2): enter the second rp name.", "2nd rp phone (extra3): enter the second rp phone number."]} {"step": "field euthanasia fields. these are not to be used for euthanasia tracking; use the kennel window for dispositions.", "sub": ["bottle no (extra4): enter the bottle number in the format yy-######.", "dose (extra5): enter the number of ccs of drug used."]} {"step": "animal and owner information:", "sub": ["animal id: enter the animal id number. only one animal id number may be attached to the activity. store multiple animal ids that were not impounded in a memo window attached to the activity.", "animal description: complete the animal's description if obtained.", "owner id: if the animal for which the activity is being created has a known owner, enter the person id number. only one person id number may be attached to the activity. store multiple person ids in a memo window attached to the activity.", "owner description: if a description of the owner is obtained, enter it in this field."]} {"step": "field actions and results:", "sub": ["status: pop up (f4) and choose the appropriate selection. if the call is stored with a status of new, the call is sent directly to dispatch. all field initiated calls (fic) shall be stored with a status of completed.", "call: the date and time the call originated.", "new: the date and time the call is to be handled.", "dispatch: the date and time the call is dispatched.", "working: the date and time the officer arrived on scene.", "complete: the date and time the officer completed the call.", "clerk id: chameleon stores the user id that stored the activity, even if updated. this cannot be overridden.", "officer id: if the call has been completed, the officer handling the call shall enter his or her chameleon system id number (p99xxxx). if the call is new or is to be placed in pending, leave blank.", "cancel by: user id of staff who cancelled the activity.", "reason: reason for the cancellation of the activity.", "chamalert: if the activity was received from webactivity, this field displays the web notification.", "qty: enter the numerical value that directly corresponds with the result field next to it.", "result: pop up (f4) and enter the appropriate result type (see appendix c for result type meanings).", "only six qty and result codes can be recorded per call. if more than six results occurred for an incident, enter the six most important codes.", "the status and date/time fields are generally completed by the system based on the routing of the call through the dispatch window. it remains the responsibility of the officer handling the call to make sure the dates and times are correct and consistent with when the call was handled."]} specific information {"step": "field initiated calls: for each fic completed, the officer shall create and complete an activity window, following the same instructions above with these exceptions:", "sub": ["caller id: enter the p number of the officer who initiated the call.", "no: if no specific address is appropriate, enter 0.", "complete the activity address information per the street address instructions above."]} vicious/potentially dangerous dog investigations: initiate a separate sequence for each date that an officer works on the investigation. that officer is responsible for closing out the call with the appropriate result code(s) and generating the next sequence to be placed in pending. all completed vicious/potentially dangerous dog investigations shall have a result code of rprt and a report written using microsoft word. the report will not be written in the chameleon system memo window. bite reports: all bite reports not initiated in the field (for example, by the rabies desk or clerical staff) will have a new activity sequence generated for each officer's attempts for that bite report activity. the officer who attempts service for his or her sequence is responsible for the disposition (result and times) and/or the refiling of the call for the following shift. the comment field must contain the date and time of the bite and the circumstances of the bite if available. this also requires use of the bite memo template. compliance checks: officers who are unable to successfully complete a compliance check shall have the activity refiled through dispatch with the appropriate result code. this generates a new sequence for the following officer, who is then responsible for the proper disposition of his or her activity sequence. refile: with the exception of vicious dog investigations and bite reports above, all calls requiring refile for the following shift or officer follow-up shall be placed in pending by the dispatch services operator (dso) utilizing the dispatch view only. the dso is required to input the appropriate result(s) in the pop-up pending results window unless the officer is using the webchameleon activity. field officers shall not personally place calls in pending without prior authorization from the supervisor. the officer will note the supervisor's name and the purpose of refiling in the activity memo. it is the responsibility of the dispatcher or animal control officer to set the time of the new call. completed calls: all field officers shall close out each activity through the dso, utilizing the current radio system, prior to handling the next activity. field officers shall not call the dso on the dispatch service line or use their personal cell phones to close out activities unless specifically requested to do so by the dispatcher or supervisor. field officers are responsible for ensuring that all dispositions are accurate and that any needed corrections are made in the chameleon system prior to the end of their shift. memos: enter all activity memos prior to the end of shift. an activity memo is required any time the activity result code does not sufficiently explain the outcome of the activity (for example, when the result type rslvd, educ, unfnd, or yrdck is used, all cruelty investigations, all agency assists, etc.). das policy 000-01, general chameleon information (person window completion) contract city reporting requirements

documents.title: Public Records and Subpoenas
documents.purpose: It is the policy of the Department of Animal Services that records of all regulatory activities, except those specifically exempted by law or as set forth in this policy, shall be made available to the public upon request.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. The Director is the primary custodian of all records for the Department of Animal Services; the Director's designated custodian of records is responsible for processing public records requests and subpoenas.
documents.scope: All County of Riverside Animal Services employees.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-10, effective October 9, 2015; revised from October 6, 2009; May 4, 2001
references: California Public Records Act, Government Code sections 6250 et seq.
Government Code section 6256 (10-day response requirement, cited in original as CCR Sec. 6256)
Evidence Code 1563(b)(1) and 1563(b)(2)
Penal Code section 1329
Government Code section 68097.2
Food and Agricultural Code 31622
SOP Public Records Request

definitions.def: The Director of Animal Services (primary custodian) or the Director's designated custodian, responsible for processing public records requests and subpoenas.
A subpoena requesting records, which should be accompanied by an affidavit showing good cause for the disclosure of records.

sections.heading: Release Restrictions
steps: Do not release complaint investigation records before the investigation has been completed unless approval is granted by the Operations Chief or designee.
Do not disclose any correspondence between the Department of Animal Services and City or County Counsel. Such documents are protected by the attorney-client privilege.

sections.heading: Public Review of Records
steps: Require requests for public review to be submitted in person, using the Request for Records form (Appendix A).
Only the custodian of records may show public records and answer questions related to them.
Never allow unsupervised access to original records.
If complaint records are to be viewed, make photocopies that effectively blank out the complainant's phone number.

sections.heading: Photocopy Requests
steps: Require requests to be submitted by completing a Request for Records form. These requests may be submitted in person, emailed, mailed, or faxed.

Route requests to the custodian of records for review, approval, or denial. An approval, extension request, or denial response to the requestor is required within 10 days from the date the custodian of records receives the request (cited in original as CCR Sec. 6256).

If disapproved, indicate the reason in writing by letter citing the specific exemption (Government Code) that justifies the refusal to release the information (for example, complaint investigation in progress or record exempted by law) and mail it to the requesting party.

If there are records for release as determined by the custodian of records, mail a cover letter within a reasonable time frame notifying the requesting party that records are available for release, enclosed with an invoice.

steps.step: If a walk-in request can be accommodated at the time of the request:
sub: Make a copy of the requestor's license or identification and attach it to the Request for Records form.
Submit the form and identification to the custodian of records for approval.
Collect the appropriate fees (see Fees for Producing and Reproduction of Records).

steps.step: Once the custodian of records has approved the request:
sub: Go to the Chameleon contact window to determine the folder where the approved copies for release are located.
Print the approved copies of the records.
Give the copies of the records to the requestor.

steps: Research all other record requests and notify the requestor of the fees prior to their receiving the records. Once the requestor decides they would like the records, follow the steps above.

sections.heading: Subpoenaed Records
steps: The Administrative Unit serves as the primary contact and coordinator for all Department of Animal Services records served for civil and criminal cases.
Direct all subpoenas for employee personnel records (including Workers' Compensation) to be served to: Riverside County Personnel, Payroll Division, 4080 Lemon Street, Riverside, CA 92501.

If a subpoena that requires delivery of records is to be served on any individual or program, advise the server that the subpoena should be addressed to the primary custodian: Custodian of Records, Department of Animal Services, 6851 Van Buren Blvd, Jurupa Valley, CA 92509.

Ensure all subpoenas for records are served to the primary custodian or designee (Department Custodian of Records). A subpoena requesting records should be labeled a subpoena duces tecum and should be accompanied by an affidavit showing good cause for the disclosure of records.

steps.step: If the custodian of records accepts a subpoena, follow these guidelines:
sub: Verify that the subpoena identifies the Department of Animal Services as the entity to which the subpoena is issued.
Determine the type of proceeding for which the subpoena is issued (civil action, administrative agency hearing or investigation, or criminal action).
Determine the date called for in the subpoena for the records to be produced. This date shall not be less than 15 days from the date the subpoena is issued.

Because certain Department of Animal Services records are considered medical records, if the request is for animal bite records, a Request for Records form must be signed by the individual(s) involved and must be obtained prior to the release of these records.

steps: The primary custodian or designee is responsible for the transmittal of records to the courts or to an attorney in any matter dealing with a legal action. Send these records by certified mail with a return receipt requested to verify that the records were sent and received.
Under no circumstances will any subpoenaed records be released except as provided for by the above procedures.

sections.heading: Fees for Producing and Reproduction of Records
steps: Supply general requests for records at a fee of $0.10 per page for copying. Do not charge a research or clerical fee for any records request other than a subpoena.
If the subpoena served involves a plaintiff who represents an agency of the County of Riverside, payment of fees is not required.
Fees for criminal trial subpoenas can be requested in the same manner as general requests; however, records must be produced even if payment is refused.

If records are requested or subpoenaed in a civil proceeding, bill costs at $0.10 per page for standard reproduction, plus $16.00 per hour per person for clerical costs incurred in locating and making the records available (Evidence Code 1563(b)(1)).

Collect the costs for producing such documents simultaneously with actual delivery of the subpoenaed records. Until payment is made, there is no obligation to deliver the records (Evidence Code 1563(b)(2)).
Provide an itemized statement setting forth the reproduction and clerical costs incurred to the requesting party at the time of delivery of records.

sections.heading: Subpoena for Personal Appearance
steps: If the subpoena is for the appearance of staff, the custodian of records may accept the subpoena and deliver it to the applicable division supervisor.
In criminal cases, witness fees are awarded solely at the discretion of the court and cannot be collected or demanded at the time of service of the subpoena or thereafter (Penal Code Sec. 1329).

In a civil action in which the Department of Animal Services is not a party, the Department is entitled to full reimbursement of the employee's time and travel expenses. Do not accept the subpoena without a minimum of $150.00 for each day the employee is required to attend as a witness, pursuant to Government Code section 68097.2.

Treat the $150.00 per day as a deposit only. Pay the productive hourly rate plus mileage of the subpoenaed employee (including travel time) from this deposit. If actual costs are less, the party is entitled to a refund. If actual costs are more than the deposit, the party is required to pay the additional amount.
Upon completion of appearances, prepare an itemized statement, with invoice or indicated refund, and submit it to the party issuing the subpoena.
If the County of Riverside or the Department of Animal Services is a party in the action, there is no entitlement to witness fees, record production costs, or travel expenses (Evidence Code 1563(b)).

sections.heading: Notice of Determination: Appeal
steps: An animal owner who is appealing an Order After Hearing (Appendix B) issued by the Department of Animal Services has five (5) days from the date they receive the notice to appeal the decision (Food and Agricultural Code 31622). To appeal, the animal owner must file with the Superior Court at one of the locations listed in Appendix C.

Any Animal Services employee receiving a Notice of Appeal from the State of California Superior Court, such as a Notice of Appeal regarding a vicious dog order, will bring the notice to the attention of their supervisor immediately.
Forward the Notice of Appeal to the Operations Chief or designee.
Immediately forward a copy of the Notice of Appeal to the vicious dog Hearing Officer.

related.name: Request for Records form (with instruction sheet)
Contact window (locating approved copies for release)

appendices: Appendix A: Request for Records form and instruction sheet (attached separately)
Appendix B: Order After Hearing (attached separately)
Appendix C: Riverside County Superior Court locations

documents.title: Time Clock Usage
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure and provide an understanding of when and how to use the time clock and time cards.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all non-exempt staff at DAS shelter locations (those employees eligible for overtime per FLSA).
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-12, effective May 19, 2015; revised from October 27, 2014; April 15, 2013; October 6, 2009
references: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) overtime eligibility
DAS Policy 000-44, Attendance and Punctuality

definitions.def: Absent without pay.

sections.heading: Time Clock Procedure
steps: Use one time card (Appendix A) for the entire pay period, consisting of two weeks. Each time card may be different per shelter time clock.
Clock in at the start of your shift on the corresponding date on the IN line.
Employees working in the shelter will clock out for lunch breaks on the OUT line and clock back in at the end of their lunch break on the corresponding IN line.
Field staff are not required to clock in or out for lunch or standby overtime, as the Officer's Daily Trip Sheet (Appendix B) and the Officer's Standby Trip Sheet (Appendix C) serve this purpose.
Clock out at the end of your regular shift or extended shift.
Never clock in or out for another employee. No employee may do so at any time.

sections.heading: Missed Punches
steps: Notify your supervisor or the back-up supervisor on the day of the occurrence if you fail to clock in or out on a particular day or, at the very latest, on the morning of your next working day.
Provide the time card and an explanation to the supervisor.
The supervisor will fill in the correct time on the time card and initial the amendment.

sections.heading: Schedules, Overtime, and Time Off
steps: Clock in according to your work schedule. Clocking in early for overtime is not authorized without pre-approval from your immediate supervisor, manager, or deputy director.
Do not clock in early in order to leave earlier than your scheduled work shift without pre-approval from your immediate supervisor, manager, or deputy director.
Pre-approve any changes in work schedules through the proper chain of command per Policy 000-44, Attendance and Punctuality.

Employees taking time off from their regular scheduled shift will initial and handwrite the amount of time (for example, 9 hrs.) and the type of time used (for example, vacation, sick, holiday) on their time card for the appropriate day.

sections.heading: Tardiness
steps: Be at your designated workstation and ready to begin work at the start time specified by your supervisor, or you will be considered late.
Notify your supervisor if you will be late.
Employees may be docked absent without pay (AWOP), and repeated tardiness will result in further disciplinary action.
The department recognizes that special circumstances may cause a delay. At the supervisor's discretion there may be a 6-minute grace period, approved on a case-by-case basis and not done habitually.

appendices: Appendix A: Time Card (attached separately)
Appendix B: Officer's Daily Trip Sheet (attached separately)
Appendix C: Officer's Standby Trip Sheet (attached separately)

documents.title: Payment Plans and Collection of Unpaid Fees
documents.purpose: To establish process consistency and accountability for revenue collections and follow-up.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services; payment plans require documented supervisor approval; fee adjustments require supervisor, manager, or chief approval as specified
documents.scope: Applicable to all Department of Animal Services (DAS) staff who are responsible for initiation, approval, and follow-up of monies owed to DAS.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-37, Payment Plans and Collection of Unpaid Fees, effective March 6, 2017 (revised from August 1, 2009)
references: Food and Agriculture Code 31251, Fees for Impounding: fixing fees
Food and Agriculture Code 31253, Payment by Owner
Food and Agriculture Code 31254, Nonpayment: Abandonment
County of Riverside Ordinance 630.16, section 6.08.020
California Code of Regulations, Title 17, 2606.4(3)
California Laws Handbook, 2017
Payment Plan Procedure SOP

sections.heading: Payment Plans
steps: Use payment plans as a last resort, with documented supervisor approval, for owners who wish to claim their pets, in accordance with Food and Agriculture Code 31251, 31253, and 31254.
Allow pet owners or custodians a payment period of up to one year (12 months).
If no payment is made within 4 months (120 days), the account becomes delinquent and will be sent to the Franchise Tax Board (FTB). A fee of $28 will be added to the account if it is sent to the FTB.

The minimum payment must cover all mandated fees, such as State fines and license fees, unless otherwise approved by the Director or their designee. Under all circumstances, the State fine is to be collected when the pet is redeemed.
Refer to the SOP on Payment Plans for further clarification on how to initiate a payment plan receipt.

sections.heading: Fee Adjustments and License Fees
steps: Adjustment of impound fees, board fees, and other fees needs approval of a supervisor or manager.

Payment of the license renewal is due on the tag expiration date. Payments postmarked or received after the 30th day of the renewal month are considered delinquent, and the applicable late fee is assessed (see Ordinance 630.16 section 6.08.020 and California Code of Regulations, Title 17, 2606.4(3)).
The Director or their designee may adjust the late fee if it is determined that the pet owner has made a reasonable effort to comply with the deadline.

The pet owner will pay all accumulated license fees when an expired vaccination certificate or license exists, or when it is determined that a dog is over four months of age and has resided in our licensing jurisdiction in excess of 30 days. Forgiveness of accumulated dog license fees from year to year is not encouraged; accumulated fees must be collected before the dog can be currently licensed.

sections.heading: Waiving Fees
steps: We encounter many people with difficult situations every day. If there is a situation at a shelter that may warrant the adjustment of fees, contact a Manager or Chief for approval.

Managers and Chiefs approached to make this decision should be compassionate but conservative, and may want to seek out their own supervisor to find alternate resources, because the department's resources to offset these costs are very limited.

documents.searchText: to establish process consistency and accountability for revenue collections and follow-up. applicable to all department of animal services (das) staff who are responsible for initiation, approval, and follow-up of monies owed to das. director of animal services; payment plans require documented supervisor approval; fee adjustments require supervisor, manager, or chief approval as specified payment plans use payment plans as a last resort, with documented supervisor approval, for owners who wish to claim their pets, in accordance with food and agriculture code 31251, 31253, and 31254. allow pet owners or custodians a payment period of up to one year (12 months). if no payment is made within 4 months (120 days), the account becomes delinquent and will be sent to the franchise tax board (ftb). a fee of $28 will be added to the account if it is sent to the ftb. the minimum payment must cover all mandated fees, such as state fines and license fees, unless otherwise approved by the director or their designee. under all circumstances, the state fine is to be collected when the pet is redeemed. refer to the sop on payment plans for further clarification on how to initiate a payment plan receipt. fee adjustments and license fees adjustment of impound fees, board fees, and other fees needs approval of a supervisor or manager. payment of the license renewal is due on the tag expiration date. payments postmarked or received after the 30th day of the renewal month are considered delinquent, and the applicable late fee is assessed (see ordinance 630.16 section 6.08.020 and california code of regulations, title 17, 2606.4(3)). the director or their designee may adjust the late fee if it is determined that the pet owner has made a reasonable effort to comply with the deadline. the pet owner will pay all accumulated license fees when an expired vaccination certificate or license exists, or when it is determined that a dog is over four months of age and has resided in our licensing jurisdiction in excess of 30 days. forgiveness of accumulated dog license fees from year to year is not encouraged; accumulated fees must be collected before the dog can be currently licensed. waiving fees we encounter many people with difficult situations every day. if there is a situation at a shelter that may warrant the adjustment of fees, contact a manager or chief for approval. managers and chiefs approached to make this decision should be compassionate but conservative, and may want to seek out their own supervisor to find alternate resources, because the department's resources to offset these costs are very limited. food and agriculture code 31251, fees for impounding: fixing fees food and agriculture code 31253, payment by owner food and agriculture code 31254, nonpayment: abandonment county of riverside ordinance 630.16, section 6.08.020 california code of regulations, title 17, 2606.4(3) california laws handbook, 2017 payment plan procedure sop

documents.title: Completion of the Chameleon Tag-Link Window
documents.flag: Numbered (=Policy per rule) but reads as a step-by-step protocol
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure for the completion of the Chameleon Tag/Link Window.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all personnel responsible for creating a license, link, microchip, voucher, or known wait record using the Chameleon Tag/Link Window.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-38, effective October 12, 2021; revised from December 13, 2018; January 5, 2017; November 6, 2015; May 19, 2015; August 29, 2014; September 30, 2008
references: DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information (search procedures)
Current California Rabies Compendium (vaccine names)
State law requirements for attaching proof documents to license records

definitions.def: The Chameleon window used to link animal and owner or custodian information for licenses, microchips, tattoos, fosters, and other transactions.
Potentially Dangerous dog Registration/order.
Dangerous Restraining Order.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Create a Tag/Link Window for any Chameleon transaction, including foster, microchip, license, or link.
Attach proof of the pet owner's senior age, proof of spay/neuter certificate, rabies certificate, microchip paperwork, and license application to the Tag_No as required by state law when provided.

sections.heading: Field Functions: Identifiers
steps: Use this window to link the animal and owner or custodian information on animals (license, microchip, tattoo, etc.).
Person ID: the identification number of the owner linked to the animal. This number is created automatically when a person is first stored into the system in the Person window. Format: P######.

Animal ID: the identification number of the animal that has been or is being licensed. This number is created automatically when an animal is first stored into the system in the Animal window. Format: A######.

Tag No: the number of the license given to the dog, entered by the clerk who licenses the dog. Create the number by taking the license tag being issued and prefacing the tag number with an L and the last two digits of the year of the tag expiration (for example, if the tag's number is 000001, the license number would be L02-000001). The license number may also be the same number as the microchip number if the license window is being used to vaccinate an animal without licensing it. Format: LYY-###### or a microchip number.

sections.heading: Field Functions: Tag Type and Subtype

steps.step: Tag Type: shows the type of tag. The value entered must be in the Inventory; press F4 to bring up the list of values.
sub: LIC: used for license transactions.
AA: used to specify dogs that are aggressive, dangerous, vicious, or potentially dangerous.
KEN: used to specify animal permits, kennel, catteries, or sentry licenses.
RESCU: used to specify rescue permits issued by Field Services and approved by DAS.
VACCERT: used to document rabies vaccination certificates received from veterinarians.
MCHIP: used for microchip transactions.
LINK: used for any transaction other than the above tag types.
NUISANCE: used for animals which have been deemed noisy and/or a public nuisance by DAS through the administrative hearing process.
DANGEROUS REGISTRATION: an annual administrative licensing fee, in addition to the regular license fee, for animals declared dangerous or vicious by DAS or through the administrative hearing process.

steps.step: Subtype: qualifies the Tag Type field or defines the source of the LIC transaction. Pop up (F4) to display the list of validations. See Appendix A for the subtype-to-tag-type table.
sub: Cite Field: used primarily by license inspectors for non-compliance with dog licensing.
Cite Front: used for front counter-initiated citations for dog license non-compliance.

Collection: used when a collection bill is initiated; overrides what is currently in the subtype and initiates an automated notification to the collection department that a payment was made on a balance.
Field: used primarily for Field Services-initiated citations and licensing transactions.
HLP and WEB (Import, Mail, Scan, Web, Renew, New): signify Chameleon automated transactions.
Mail: license transactions received by mail.
PetData: signifies converted data.
Phone: license transactions received through telephone payments.
Shot Clini: signifies license transactions at DAS shot clinics.
Counter: used primarily for front counter license transactions only, but not with adoption.
Adopt: used primarily for license transactions with adoption.

sections.heading: Field Functions: Spay/Neuter, Jurisdiction, Status, and Problem Code
steps: S/N: use this field only when proof of sterility is provided after the licensing transaction or outcome transaction. This requires the Animal window spay/neuter fields (Completed, Proof, and Vet ID) to be completed.
Jurisdiction: denotes the area where the licensed animal lives. Jurisdiction is where revenue will be applied.

steps.step: Status: pop up (F4) to show the list of validations.
sub: CURRENT: a renewal, delinquent, or final delinquent notice is mailed depending on tag expiration.
DEAD: owner notifies the department that the pet passed away.
EUTH REQ: owner surrenders the animal for euthanasia services.
GAVE AWAY: owner gave the animal away and the new owner information is required.
GONE: owner notifies that the animal ran away.
MOVED: owner moves to a jurisdiction not contracted for licensing.
OWNER SUR: owner relinquishes the animal to a shelter for the purpose of adoption or rescue.
RENEWED: Chameleon automatically changes the current license record once the license is renewed; status of the old license number. A notice is not mailed.
REPLACE: non-compliance with an RTO intent to comply; requires an administrative citation.
REVOKED: used per administrative process.
TEMPORARY: activates an incomplete notice; used with a problem code.
VOID: duplicate tag records.
RTN MAIL: used for mail returned undeliverable, forwarding time expired.

steps: Problem Code: notes whether there is a problem with a license (vaccination expired, money, late, etc.). See Appendix B for the full problem code list.

sections.heading: Field Functions: Dates, Vaccination, and Fees
steps: Tag Date: shows the beginning date of the transaction or the date the license was sold.
Tag Term: shows the duration the license, permit, foster, microchip, PDR, or DRO is valid.

Tag Expiration: shows the ending date of the license, permit, foster, or microchip, PDR, or DRO. If the license is renewed, Chameleon calculates this based on the previous tag expiration date plus the tag term.
Vac Date: the date the rabies vaccination was given.
Vac Term: the length of time the vaccine is valid.
Vac Expiration: the date the vaccine expires. Chameleon automatically calculates this field based on the Vac Date plus the Vac Term.
Vaccine: the name of the product. Refer to the current California Rabies Compendium.
Certification No: by entering "Contract" the user is allowed to generate a Spay/Neuter Contract with the adopter.
Lot No: the vial number of the vaccine.
Lot Exp: the vaccine expiration.
Vet ID: shows the veterinarian administering the vaccine. Format: G######. Perform searches prior to storing (refer to DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information).
Batch No: for batch licensing and for keying in the group number of AVID microchips.
I1 MailDate (Extra1): date the incomplete (pending) notice was mailed.
RN MailDate (Extra2): date the license renewal or VACCERT notice was mailed.
DN MailDate (Extra3): date the delinquent license renewal or final notice for VACCERTs was mailed.
CF MailDate (Extra4): date the official license certificate was mailed.
Price: enter any fees charged to the owner in these fields, including penalty fees. Do not include donation fees.

Receipt No: the computer may automatically generate this number when the receipt for the license is stored. Once a receipt has been created in the Receipt window, the receipt number field is automatically updated on the license window. Format: RYY-######.
Person ID (alternate): denotes the alternate contact when the tag type is for a microchip only. Pop up (F4) to display the person window.

List boxes: the first list box associated with the Tag window shows all tag records associated with the animal on the screen. The second list box shows all the animals ever linked to this person and all the tag records for all of those animals.

sections.heading: Acknowledgment
steps: Sign the Acknowledgment of the Completion of the Chameleon Tag/Link Window Policy (Appendix C).
Retain the signed form in the employee's personnel file and attach it to the employee's Person ID in Chameleon.

related.name: Tag/Link Window screen shot (attachment to original)

appendices: Appendix A: Subtype validations by tag type
Appendix B: Problem codes
Appendix C: Acknowledgment of Completion of the Chameleon Tag/Link Window Policy (form, attached separately)
Appendix D: Tag/Link Window screen shot (attached separately)

documents.title: The Chameleon Memo Window
documents.flag: Numbered (=Policy per rule) but reads as a step-by-step protocol
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform system in documenting critical information that has an impact on the status of an impounded animal, licensing, quarantines, investigations, accounting, collections, and for the use of the Chameleon Animal Management Software in all transactions and activities of the Department of Animal Services.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all DAS personnel who have access to Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-45, effective April 13, 2015; revised from May 2, 2013; revised from September 20, 2012

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Each employee of the Department of Animal Services (DAS) is required to document all necessary or critical information before proceeding to the next transaction or before the end of the business day, with attention to punctuation, grammar, and spelling, using the Chameleon Memo Window.

sections.heading: The Activity Memo Window
steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Activity Window.
Note that a memo originating from the Activity Window can only be read in the Activity Window; the information is not automatically transferred to other memo fields in Chameleon.

Limit the information contained in these memos to the specific activity. Any owner information must also be copied to an Animal ID memo or Person ID memo to ensure that information is visible and obvious to all employees other than field staff.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the Activity Number of the record that is displayed in the Activity Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Activity No. based on the Activity Number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.

steps.step: Memo Type: this field designates the nature or topic of the memo.
sub: Citation: used by DAS staff to document citation related topics.
Danger: used by DAS staff to document explanation of who is in danger or who is dangerous.
Hearing: used by Field Services to document pending hearings and results of hearings.
Investigat: used by Field Services to document ongoing investigation held by police departments or cruelty issues.
Note: used by all staff for any other documentation.
OWNER Info: used by all staff to document a possible ownership of an impounded animal.

steps.step: Memo SubType: this field further specifies the memo type.
sub: If memo type is INVESTIGAT or HEARING, the subtype can be RESTRICTED.

steps: Author: this field automatically displays the original creator of the memo, regardless of the preceding updates. Depending on privileges set, the Author can override the initial memo, add to the memo, or erase the initial memo and enter new text.

steps.step: Memo Text: this field contains the general text of the memo. When a memo is added, it must always contain the following:
sub: Date created.
Time created.
First name initial and full last name or badge number.
Specific name of person and/or animal the memo is pertaining to.

sections.heading: The Animal Memo Window
steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Animal Window.
This memo can be read or accessed from any other window in Chameleon that contains the same Animal ID.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the Animal ID (AID) of the record that is displayed in the Animal Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Animal ID based on the AID number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.

steps.step: Memo Type: this field designates the nature or topic of the memo.
sub: Behv Grade: used by DAS behaviorists to grade impounded animals.
Citation: used by DAS staff to document citation related topics.
Danger: used by DAS staff to document explanation of who is in danger or who is dangerous.
Donation: used by DAS staff to document donation received or used.
Event: used by DAS staff to document an animal's participation in events and how long the animal will be at an event.
Foster: used by DAS staff to document any updates for fostered animals or foster parents.
Hearing: used by Field Services to document pending hearings and results of hearings.
Investigat: used by Field Services to document ongoing investigation held by police departments or cruelty issues.
Note: used by all staff for any other documentation.
O Notified: used by all staff to document a verified ownership of an impounded animal and how the owner was notified.
OWNER Info: used by all staff to document a possible ownership of an impounded animal.
Rescue Grp: used by all staff to document any collaboration with rescue groups and impounded animals.
Interest: used by all staff to document constituents who are interested in adopting an impounded animal.
KEN CHANGE: used by all staff to document change of an impounded animal's location. The information is critical to resolving inventory issues.
Safety Net: used to justify an outcome request of DO NOT PTD to indicate that the animal is not to be euthanized.

steps.step: Memo SubType: this field further specifies the memo type.
sub: If memo type is Behv Grade, then subtype is either A, B, B Minus, C, Treatable, or Untreatable.
If memo type is INVESTIGAT or HEARING, the subtype can be RESTRICTED.
Use memo subtype ADOPTION [AD] if the memo type is INTEREST.

steps.step: Memo Templates: pre-written memos that contain required information and guide the employee to only fill in the blanks.
sub: Adoption Exchange: template for processing adoption exchange vouchers.
Adoption Fee: template for quoting fees to potential adopters.
DOA Fee: template for quoting disposal fees to pet owners.
Interest: template for a potential adopter or owner for an impounded animal.
License Updates: template for updating license records.
ONotify Contact: template of information that documents what the owner is required to know when reclaiming their pet on the telephone.
ONotify Voicemail: template of information left on the voicemail of the possible owner to reclaim their pet.
Pledge Adoption: template of information for a sponsored impounded animal.
Public SN Surgery: template to document instructions and fees to customers when setting a spay or neuter appointment.
Retroactive License: template that documents the breakdown of retroactive license fees and penalties owed.
RTO Fee w/ Intent: template that documents what the pet owner owes for an impounded pet with the promise to provide proof of spay or neuter certificate in 10 days.
RTO Fee w/o Intent: template that documents what the pet owner owes for an impounded pet that includes the service of spay or neuter surgery.
RTS 1st, 2nd, 3rd: templates that document returned mail and attempts to obtain a correct mailing address.
Shot Clinic: template that documents what items and fees were quoted at the shot clinic.
Temporary License: template that documents missing requirements for licensing.
WebLic Notes: templates that document why the online license transaction is in a temporary status.

steps: Author: this field automatically displays the original creator of the memo, regardless of the preceding updates. Depending on the privileges set, the author can override the initial memo, add to the memo, or erase the initial memo and enter new text.

steps.step: Memo Text: this field contains the general text of the memo. When a memo is added, it must always contain the following:
sub: Date created.
Time created.
First name initial and full last name or badge number.
Specific name of person and/or animal the memo is pertaining to.

sections.heading: The Person Memo Window
steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Person Window.
This memo can be read or accessed from any other window in Chameleon that contains the same Person ID.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the Person ID (PID) of the record that is displayed in the Person Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Person ID based on the PID number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.

steps.step: Memo Type: this field designates the nature or topic of the memo.
sub: Danger: used by DAS staff to document explanation of who is in danger or who is dangerous.
Donation: used by DAS staff to document donation received or used.
Foster: used by DAS staff to document any updates for fostered animals or foster parents.
Note: used by all staff for any other documentation.
Rescue Grp: used by all staff to document any collaboration with rescue groups and impounded animals.

steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Bite Window.
This memo can only be read or accessed from the Bite Window with the record displayed that contains the same Bite No.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the Bite No. of the record that is displayed in the Bite Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Bite No based on the bite number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.

sections.heading: The Citation Memo Window
steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Citation/Violation Window.
This memo can only be read or accessed from the Citation/Violation Window with the record displayed that contains the same Citation/Violation No.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the Citation/Violation No. of the record that is displayed in the Citation/Violation Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Viol No based on the citation/violation number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.

steps.step: Memo Type: this field designates the nature or topic of the memo.
sub: Note: used by all staff for any other documentation.

sections.heading: The Treatment Memo Window
steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Treatment Window.
This memo can only be read or accessed from the Treatment Window with the record displayed that contains the same treatment number.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the Treatment No. of the record that is displayed in the Treatment Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Treat No based on the treatment number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.

steps.step: Memo Type: this field designates the nature or topic of the memo.
sub: Note: used by all staff for any other documentation.
Medical: used by veterinary staff to document medical or treatment notes.

steps: Memo SubType: not used.

sections.heading: The Receipt Memo Window
steps: Generate this memo by clicking on the memo icon located on the menu bar of the Receipt Window.
This memo can only be read or accessed from the Receipt Window with the record displayed that contains the same receipt number.
Memo No: the unique number generated by the system.
Memo ID: this field automatically displays the receipt number of the record that is displayed in the Receipt Window.
Memo ID Type: this field automatically displays Receipt No based on the receipt number displayed under the Memo ID field.
Date: this field normally contains the date the memo was created.
Memo Type: Accounting (ACC) shall be used when generating payment plan, journal entry, and refund transactions.
Memo SubType: not used.

documents.title: Use of Telephones for Personal Calls
documents.purpose: To establish the appropriateness of using a Riverside County desk or cell telephone or a personal cell phone for non-County related calls during working hours.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Department of Animal Services employees. The policy statement also extends to volunteers, contractors, and visitors.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-55, effective July 1, 2012. Replaces CHA Policy A-18.
references: CHA Policy A-18 (replaced by this policy)

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Keep personal telephone calls to or from DAS divisions, or on DAS-issued cell phones, to a minimum. This applies to all DAS employees, volunteers, contractors, and visitors.

sections.heading: Procedure
steps: Accept or make personal calls of a non-emergency nature during break or lunch periods only, using personal cell phones.
If you receive a personal call, arrange to call back later, using a non-DAS phone, during a break time, or after work hours.
Do not converse during paid time or tie up a DAS phone with non-County business.

If a valid personal emergency exists (personal emergencies, illnesses, or transportation problems of the employee or a family member), an individual may receive or place a call in order to cope with the situation.
As a guideline, personal calls should rarely, if ever, last over three (3) minutes.

Remit any expenses for personal phone calls made on a DAS phone to an outside line. The DAS Fiscal Office can provide a printout of calls and costs for each DAS phone line upon request from the Supervisor.
Employees who abuse the use of personal cell phones and/or County phones may be subject to disciplinary action.

sections.heading: Required Security Measures
steps: Do not transfer incoming personal calls received for someone else in the Department to that person. Take a message instead.
Do not release phone numbers, locations, or any other information about that person to the outside caller.
When a non-County employee calls to inquire whether someone works in any of the divisions of the Department, or asks where someone works, refer or transfer that caller to County Human Resources.

Do not divulge personal information pertaining to employees to inquiring callers, except as allowed by law for County Human Resources to provide. These measures protect the confidentiality, privacy, safety, and security of all DAS employees and staff.

revisions.author: RCDAS
revisions.desc: Original policy. Replaced CHA Policy A-18.
