documents.title: E-mail and Other Written Communications
documents.purpose: To require that written communications from or within the Riverside County Department of Animal Services (other than e-mail messages) provide a standardized letterhead identifying DAS and a consistent framework of presentation, and to set rules for e-mail use, social media, confidentiality, and litigation holds.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. No written action by any employee or committee within any Division of DAS is binding upon or constitutes an expression of policy, obligation, or commitment of DAS unless such authority has been specifically delegated and/or approved by the DAS Director.
documents.scope: All Department of Animal Services employees.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-56, effective July 11, 2018; revised from July 1, 2012. Replaces CHA Policy A-22.
references: Board of Supervisors Policy A-50, Electronic Media and Use Policy
CHA Policy A-22 (replaced by this policy)
DAS Policy 000-54, Use and Protection of County Assets (formerly CHA A-13)
DAS Policy 000-43, Requisition Authority for Purchasing Accountability (formerly CHA F-3)
DAS Policy 000-57, LAN and Internet Websites' Access (formerly HSA A-21)

definitions.def: Litigation Hold/Public Records Act contact point designated by the DAS Executive Team.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Use a standardized letterhead identifying DAS and a consistent framework of presentation for written communications from or within DAS that are not e-mail messages. Refer to Policy DAS 000-57, LAN and Internet Websites' Access, for additional information.
Acknowledge in writing that you have received and read Policy BOS A-50, Electronic Media and Use Policy (Attachment I).
Do not use the electronic mail system to communicate confidential or potentially litigious information.

Never post DAS business on social media sites unless it is from a DAS Public Information Chief or designee, whether on County internet or personal internet. This includes taking photos and posting on blogs, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, etc.

If you encounter a story worth sharing on a social media site in the course of a DAS business day, send it to the DAS Public Information Officer for review and posting, for the protection of the department and/or sensitive case information.

Treat e-mail messages as County property at all times. They are not protected from discoverability, and the County has the ability to access, review, copy, or disclose e-mail messages under the appropriate circumstances.
To maintain the confidentiality of customers or other sensitive information, communicate it in the form of a written document marked Confidential or by personal conversation.

This limitation of authority is not intended to restrict the provision of necessary and authorized services to customers in accordance with laws, regulations, and ordinances, or with accepted professional standards.

sections.heading: Use of Software and Abbreviations
steps: When generating a memo or letter for the signature of the DAS Director, do not use italic or non-professional font appearances. Acceptable fonts include Arial, Times New Roman, and Courier.
Within written communications, write out terms first and abbreviate thereafter. For example: Department of Animal Services (DAS).

sections.heading: Use of Formats and Forms Other Than E-mail
steps: DAS Memo: prepare written communication originating from DAS Administration or DAS shelters for dispatch within local County government on the DAS Memo format. DAS memos are located on the Q drive, ANMSHLT_Shared, in the Forms, letterhead, misc. info folder.

DAS Fax Sheet: faxed information shall state at the top or bottom "County of Riverside Department of Animal Services." The fax sheet is located on the Q drive, shared animal, in the Forms, letterhead, misc. info folder.
Other documents: any documents or forms generated by DAS shall state at the top or bottom "County of Riverside Department of Animal Services" with the applicable Division title and information.

sections.heading: Titles, Addresses, and Letters
steps: Use of personal names only on non-e-mailed communications sent outside DAS is discouraged. Accompany names with the proper titles when sending correspondence other than e-mail.
Identify the appropriate Division and originator's address on written communications within County government or DAS.

Include a complete mailing address on the document and the envelope for written communications dispatched outside County government or DAS, and include a complete return address with the originator's name and/or title.

Include as part of the letterhead "Riverside County Department of Animal Services" or "County of Riverside Department of Animal Services" on all non-e-mailed correspondence. Letterhead must contain a complete return address, telephone number, and Telephone Device for the Deaf (T.D.D.) number (951) 358-5124.

sections.heading: Format (Non-E-mail)
steps: DATE: always include the date the correspondence is written, typically before the body of the correspondence.
ADDRESSEE: place the address block to be readily visible before the beginning of the body of correspondence. When appropriate, the address placement will allow for the use of a window envelope.
SUBJECT: if deemed appropriate, place a subject line below the address block, for example, RE: Service Directory.
MULTIPLE PAGES: subsequent pages should have the addressee, date, and page number in the upper portion of the page.
PARAGRAPHS: the first line of each paragraph may be indented, or a block-letter format may be used.

SIGNATURE AUTHORITY: refer to DAS Policy 000-43, Requisition Authority for Purchasing Accountability. Blue-ink signatures are preferred (to readily identify the original copy), although in some cases black ink may be used. Pencil signatures are not acceptable on any written correspondence or forms.

Prepare correspondence that establishes Departmental policy, or that is addressed to local or national press, the County Board of Supervisors, the County Executive Office, or legislators at any governmental level, or that expresses the position and/or opinion of the Department regarding pending or existing legislation or proposed or existing policies of Federal, State, County, or professional organizations, only for the signature of the DAS Director or designee.

If any staff member desires to provide an opinion on any pending legislation or on any position taken by any State association or association of county officials, that person is entitled to do so as a private individual. In that case, the opinion shall not be written on County, DAS, or Department stationery, and shall not be produced on County time or by using County equipment or facilities.

Prepare documents or correspondence of a sensitive or controversial nature for the signature of the applicable Executive Team member (Deputy Director or Chief Veterinarian). This type of correspondence requires approval and coordination with the DAS Director prior to dispatch.

Prepare documents that reiterate or implement existing DAS or Departmental policy to other governmental departments for the signature of the DAS Director; this may be specifically delegated to a lower organizational level.

sections.heading: Routing
steps: Route correspondence prepared for the signature of the DAS Director through the appropriate Managers and Executive Team (Deputy Director and Chief Veterinarian) for approval.
A routing slip may be used for this purpose, or e-mail approval of an attached document may be obtained.

sections.heading: E-mail
steps: Use e-mail courteously and professionally at all times. Do not use it to transmit sensitive, inappropriate, or non-business related materials, such as jokes, chain letters, cartoons, or caricatures. Refer to BOS Policy A-50 (Attachment I) for additional direction.

E-mail photos may be added to the employee's account with the understanding that the picture will be limited to the employee's ID badge picture and/or limited to reflect one to two pet pictures, to maintain a professional level of business associated with the Department. This excludes personal family pictures, logos, cartoons, multiple pets, and sports teams, for example.

Do not use the County and DAS e-mail system to conduct personal business. Refer to DAS Policy 000-54, Use and Protection of County Assets, and DAS Policy 000-57, LAN and Internet Websites' Access, for more information.
Forward any inappropriate internal or external e-mail messages and/or attachments received to the Human Resources Services Manager immediately so appropriate measures can be taken against violators.

Keep e-mailed attachments at or under 10 MB. Files exceeding 10 MB may be blocked. Some file attachments are not allowed in the County email system; refer to Attachment VII for a list of denied file extensions.

sections.heading: Litigation Hold Procedure
steps: The DAS Executive Team designates a department person or persons to act as a Litigation Hold/Public Records Act (LHPRA) contact point upon notification from County Counsel or Risk Management of a pending or active litigation hold. The person(s) assigned are based on the scope and area impacted by the hold request.

The LHPRA person(s) serve as liaison with outside legal counsel, the Office of County Counsel, the Office of Risk Management, the Human Resources Department, information technology personnel, and litigation hold personnel from other County departments.

steps.step: LHPRA responsibilities include:
sub: Helping legal counsel place a litigation hold on emails and documents that are or may be relevant to pending or reasonably anticipated litigation involving the County of Riverside.

Providing sufficient information regarding the subject of the pending or reasonably anticipated litigation to allow departmental employees to conduct a reasonable search for potentially relevant email information.
Coordinating with information technology personnel to place a litigation hold on potentially relevant email information.
Coordinating with litigation hold personnel from other County departments, if any, involved in the pending or reasonably anticipated litigation.

DAS Policy 000-43, Requisition Authority for Purchasing Accountability
DAS Policy 000-54, Use and Protection of County Assets
DAS Policy 000-57, LAN and Internet Websites' Access

Appendix II: A, B, C, and D DAS Memo forms for the Riverside County shelter locations (attached separately)
Appendix III: A, B, C, and D DAS fax cover sheets for the Riverside County shelter locations (attached separately)
Appendix VII: List of denied file extensions (referenced in original; not included in the attachments list)

must contain a complete return address, telephone number, and telephone device for the deaf (t.d.d.) number (951) 358-5124. format (non-e-mail) date: always include the date the correspondence is written, typically before the body of the correspondence. addressee: place the address block to be readily visible before the beginning of the body of correspondence. when appropriate, the address placement will allow for the use of a window envelope. subject: if deemed appropriate, place a subject line below the address block, for example, re: service directory. multiple pages: subsequent pages should have the addressee, date, and page number in the upper portion of the page. paragraphs: the first line of each paragraph may be indented, or a block-letter format may be used. signature authority: refer to das policy 000-43, requisition authority for purchasing accountability. blue-ink signatures are preferred (to readily identify the original copy), although in some cases black ink may be used. pencil signatures are not acceptable on any written correspondence or forms. prepare correspondence that establishes departmental policy, or that is addressed to local or national press, the county board of supervisors, the county executive office, or legislators at any governmental level, or that expresses the position and/or opinion of the department regarding pending or existing legislation or proposed or existing policies of federal, state, county, or professional organizations, only for the signature of the das director or designee. if any staff member desires to provide an opinion on any pending legislation or on any position taken by any state association or association of county officials, that person is entitled to do so as a private individual. in that case, the opinion shall not be written on county, das, or department stationery, and shall not be produced on county time or by using county equipment or facilities. prepare documents or correspondence of a sensitive or controversial nature for the signature of the applicable executive team member (deputy director or chief veterinarian). this type of correspondence requires approval and coordination with the das director prior to dispatch. prepare documents that reiterate or implement existing das or departmental policy to other governmental departments for the signature of the das director; this may be specifically delegated to a lower organizational level. routing route correspondence prepared for the signature of the das director through the appropriate managers and executive team (deputy director and chief veterinarian) for approval. a routing slip may be used for this purpose, or e-mail approval of an attached document may be obtained. e-mail use e-mail courteously and professionally at all times. do not use it to transmit sensitive, inappropriate, or non-business related materials, such as jokes, chain letters, cartoons, or caricatures. refer to bos policy a-50 (attachment i) for additional direction. e-mail photos may be added to the employee's account with the understanding that the picture will be limited to the employee's id badge picture and/or limited to reflect one to two pet pictures, to maintain a professional level of business associated with the department. this excludes personal family pictures, logos, cartoons, multiple pets, and sports teams, for example. do not use the county and das e-mail system to conduct personal business. refer to das policy 000-54, use and protection of county assets, and das policy 000-57, lan and internet websites' access, for more information. forward any inappropriate internal or external e-mail messages and/or attachments received to the human resources services manager immediately so appropriate measures can be taken against violators. keep e-mailed attachments at or under 10 mb. files exceeding 10 mb may be blocked. some file attachments are not allowed in the county email system; refer to attachment vii for a list of denied file extensions. litigation hold procedure the das executive team designates a department person or persons to act as a litigation hold/public records act (lhpra) contact point upon notification from county counsel or risk management of a pending or active litigation hold. the person(s) assigned are based on the scope and area impacted by the hold request. the lhpra person(s) serve as liaison with outside legal counsel, the office of county counsel, the office of risk management, the human resources department, information technology personnel, and litigation hold personnel from other county departments. {"step": "lhpra responsibilities include:", "sub": ["helping legal counsel place a litigation hold on emails and documents that are or may be relevant to pending or reasonably anticipated litigation involving the county of riverside.", "providing sufficient information regarding the subject of the pending or reasonably anticipated litigation to allow departmental employees to conduct a reasonable search for potentially relevant email information.", "coordinating with information technology personnel to place a litigation hold on potentially relevant email information.", "coordinating with litigation hold personnel from other county departments, if any, involved in the pending or reasonably anticipated litigation."]} board of supervisors policy a-50, electronic media and use policy cha policy a-22 (replaced by this policy) das policy 000-54, use and protection of county assets (formerly cha a-13) das policy 000-43, requisition authority for purchasing accountability (formerly cha f-3) das policy 000-57, lan and internet websites' access (formerly hsa a-21)

documents.title: LAN and Internet Websites Access
documents.purpose: To establish a standard for Local Area Network (LAN) and Internet access by Department of Animal Services (DAS) employees and other workers, such as contractors, ensuring that only authorized staff have standard and secure access to the LAN, internet, and World Wide Web, and that websites are designed, developed, and established according to standardized practices.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services. The DAS Director, Deputy Director, Chief Veterinarian, branch chiefs, or department managers can authorize access to the Internet/World Wide Web.
documents.scope: All Animal Services employees and other workers, such as contractors.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-57, effective July 1, 2012. Replaces HSA Policy A-21.
references: Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986
Board of Supervisors Policy A-21
Board of Supervisors Policy A-50, Electronic Media and Use Policy
Board of Supervisors Policy H-11, Acquisition and Management of Information Systems and Services
DAS Policy 000-56, E-mail and Other Written Communications (formerly HSA A-22)
HSA Policy A-21 (replaced by this policy)

definitions.def: Local Area Network.
System Access Request form (Attachment I).
The County communications network through which all operational Internet use by DAS staff must be accomplished.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: In compliance with BOS Policy A-50, Electronic Media and Use Policy, DAS encourages the use of electronic information technology to improve the delivery of services to the citizens of the County. In some cases, Internet access and/or website development can serve this purpose.

Provide LAN and Internet access only to staff authorized to use the approved systems software and access methods, to facilitate standardization where possible and to ensure Information Technology's installation, support, and security of DAS networks and information (refer to BOS Policy H-11).

Use access to the World Wide Web, browsing or developing websites, and general LAN and Internet e-mail for County business purposes only. Refer to DAS Policy 000-56 for additional direction concerning e-mail use. Non-DAS related use is prohibited.

The information contained in any Internet communication is the property of DAS. Employees should not consider the use of and contents contained in Internet activity as private. DAS reserves the right to review and monitor the use of and the information contained in such activities. The Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 establishes that employers are permitted to monitor e-mail for business reasons.
Design and develop websites only as approved by the DAS Public Information Officer (PIO) or designee and Information Technology (IT).

sections.heading: Authorization and Requests for Access
steps: Authorization for access: the DAS Director, Deputy Director, Chief Veterinarian, branch chiefs, or department managers can authorize access to the Internet/World Wide Web.

Requests for access: the DAS employee's supervisor requests access to the LAN or to Internet websites and submits a written request for approval using the System Access Request (SAR) form (Appendix A). The supervisor forwards the approved request through the chain of command to be authorized by the branch chief or department manager, Chief Veterinarian, Deputy Director, or Director, as appropriate.

steps.step: Completion of the System Access Request (SAR) form: the request will include:
sub: The LAN/Internet services required.
The purpose and justification of the access request.
The anticipated frequency of Internet use.
The alternatives to Internet access.
The source of funding to offset the cost of access, computer upgrades if needed, and installation.

sections.heading: Purchase of LAN and Internet Software and Other Tools
steps: Submit the approved and authorized Access Request form to DAS Information Technology (IT) for ordering and installing the necessary software tools. The requesting Division will be charged for the cost of any workstation software tools needed.

DAS IT will coordinate with the requesting Division to estimate the cost of the equipment and software that may be needed for an individual workstation to gain Internet access. In some cases, the financial impact involved may outweigh the benefit to the Division.

sections.heading: Installation and Operational Use
steps: DAS IT will configure the LAN and Internet workstation to execute the access product from within Windows after logging onto the network. DAS IT will provide basic LAN and Internet access instructions at the time of installation.
DAS IT is responsible for assigning and maintaining the unique address required for Internet website access from all DAS locations.
Accomplish operational use of the Internet by DAS staff only through the County CORNET communications network as installed by DAS IT staff. For security reasons, no other access is permitted.
DAS IT will provide virus scanning training and procedures when the Internet access software is installed.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
steps: Authorized staff: use all e-mail, LAN, and Internet only for DAS business-related activities. Downloading information from Internet websites to storage on the personal computer hard drive (C drive) or the LAN hard disk drive (P drive) is discouraged. If necessary, download files onto a CD/DVD or flash drive. Where storage on a workstation or LAN hard disk drive is essential, scan for viruses prior to storing the downloaded files.
Management: ensure that the LAN and Internet are used appropriately. Refer to DAS Policy 000-56 for additional information about use of e-mail.
Supervisors: monitor the use of the LAN and Internet by employees and discontinue employee use if abuse is evident.

Employees and other workers: no employee may design or develop a website without prior approval from the Public Information Officer or designee. Coordinate approved developmental work with IT prior to the start of the work and during the developmental process. The final website product must be approved by IT prior to loading and implementing material as a website. IT will coordinate with County Communications before loading the approved website.

sections.heading: Purged E-mail
steps: In accordance with BOS Policy A-50, Electronic Media and Use Policy, all items in the Trash will be purged every 14 days.
All In and Out box items (whether read, opened, or unopened) will be purged after the 45-day email retention period has expired.
Email Trash, In, and Out box items will be backed up during routine server back-ups. Back-up tapes allow data recovery in the event of a systems crash but must be retained no longer than 30 days.

related.name: BOS Policy A-50, Electronic Media and Use Policy
BOS Policy H-11, Acquisition and Management of Information Systems and Services
DAS Policy 000-56, E-mail and Other Written Communications

appendices: Appendix A: System Access Request (SAR) form (Attachment I of original, attached separately)

revisions.author: RCDAS
revisions.desc: Original policy. Replaced HSA Policy A-21.

revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2012 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Telephone Etiquette and Voice Mail
documents.purpose: To promote good public and interdepartmental relations by encouraging professional, friendly, courteous, and consistent manners of telephone use by the Department of Animal Services (DAS).
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all Department of Animal Services employees.
documents.supersedes: None identified
references: Board of Supervisors Policy A-41, Use of Voice Mail by County Departments

sections.heading: Policy
steps: In the interest of professional, friendly, courteous, and consistent telephone service, all DAS employees are expected to adhere to telephone etiquette policies and refine telephone etiquette procedures.

All DAS employees authorized to have voice mail on their telephone will answer their telephone during normal business hours, if practical, without intervention of a voice mail recording (refer to Policy BOS A-41, Use of Voice Mail by County Departments).
Employees will adhere to Policy BOS A-41 at all times regarding the use of voice mail.
Listed telephones for maintenance/trouble reporting and dispatch desks will not be diverted to voice mail during normal business hours.

sections.heading: Voice Mail
steps: Follow the Telephone Etiquette Guidelines (Appendix A), which offer procedures that may be used by DAS for the development of division telephone etiquette procedures.

Use a voice mail message consistent with the following sample: "Hi, you've reached the voice mail box for (your name) at Animal Services. I am either away from my desk or on another call, please leave your name, number and a brief message. If this is an urgent matter, please dial (appropriate number or our main line 358-7387)."
Respond to messages left on voice mail within one workday whenever possible.

steps.step: Protect your voice mail password. The password is not to be shared with other staff. When prompted to change your password, do so. If your password is forgotten and you are unable to get into your voice mail, reset your password by logging into the following:
sub: Riverside Metro Area (includes Riverside, Norco, Corona, Woodcrest, parts of Moreno Valley): https://vmail1.rivcoca.org/ciscopca/home.do
Outside Riverside Metro Area (all other County locations not listed above): https://vmail2.rivcoca.org/ciscopca/home.do

Once logged in, click the Messaging Assistant link. To reset your voicemail PIN (passcode), locate the drop-down menu Passwords and click on it, then choose Change Pin. A page will display where you can input a new PIN number for your voicemail.
If help is needed, the RCIT Helpdesk micro is 59900.

related.name: Board of Supervisors Policy A-41, Use of Voice Mail by County Departments

appendices: Appendix A: Telephone Etiquette Guidelines

documents.title: Completion of the Chameleon Receipt Window
documents.flag: Numbered (=Policy per rule) but reads as a step-by-step protocol
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure for the completion of the Chameleon Receipt Window in receiving payments, documenting, applying accurate Board approved fees, and processing bad checks and journal entries (voiding a receipt).
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all personnel responsible for using the Chameleon Receipt Window.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-66, effective June 14, 2017; revised from October 18, 2016
references: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), as codified by the Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)
State of California Accounting Standards and Procedures for Counties, Chapter 2, Section 2.03, State Controller's Office, Division of Accounting and Reporting
California Government Code Sections 13400-13407
Office of Auditor-Controller Standard Practice Manual, Policy 104
DAS Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information

sections.heading: Policy
steps: In accordance with Chapter 2, Section 2.03 of the State of California Accounting Standards and Procedures for Counties, State Controller's Office, Division of Accounting and Reporting, and Sections 13400-13407 of the California Government Code, County departments and agencies shall establish, document, and maintain an effective internal control system that ensures accuracy, safeguards assets, and promotes effectiveness and efficiency of operations.
This includes following the physical control principle that equipment, inventories, cash, and other properties are secured physically, counted periodically, and compared with control records.
Record payment transactions accurately using the Chameleon Receipt Window for reconciliation to determine that transactions are properly processed.

sections.heading: Field Definitions
steps: Receipt No: this field is required (red label) and searchable (yellow field). Chameleon auto-generates a receipt number that starts with an R when the user stores (F9) a new receipt. The user can also enter an existing receipt number to find and display item codes associated with a specific receipt number.
Receipt Date: this field defaults to the current date based on the set date of the local computer. This field records the date when monies were received.
Reference No: enter the contact number or case number for an empty lineitem reference number. Enter the activity number for court billing.

Person ID: contains the identification number associated with the owner of the animal for the receipt. If a person has recently been stored or queried from the tag or kennel window, the computer will automatically fill in PERSON_ID with the appropriate ID number (format P#######).

Received From: enter the full name of the person who actually paid the amount on the receipt if different from the Person ID name. Up to 25 characters may be entered. Leave blank if creating a collections receipt.

Jurisdiction: Chameleon will automatically bring the person jurisdiction over to this field. This must be changed if the person jurisdiction is changed to ensure accurate tracking of revenue to contract cities.
Extra1: this field is specifically used for payment plan transactions. If the owner is requesting a payment plan and has a co-signer for the payment plan, enter the co-signer's full name.
Extra2: this field is left blank unless generating a court restitution receipt. Enter HOLD in this field to prevent the collections department from submitting the balance to the Franchise Tax Board.
Comment: this field can be left blank, or enter a brief summary of what the transaction is about.

steps.step: List Box (ALT+2): the list box at the bottom shows all the items on one receipt. If the kennel, tag, citation, bite, or treatment windows are stored or updated correctly, Chameleon performs an auto-debt lookup based on the Person ID, automatically populating all debts owed by the PID. Only items appearing in the list box are stored and printed on the receipt. List box fields:
sub: Qty: defaults to one (1) but can be changed. This field refers to the total count for each lineitem.
Item Code: this field is based on the type of transaction.
Animal ID: this field automatically displays the animal ID from the kennel window, tag/link, citation, or treatment window.

Reference No: if the debt is pulled from the kennel window, the impound number is displayed as the reference for the item code. If the debt is pulled from the tag/link window, then the microchip, license, and/or link number is displayed. Each medication and treatment type debt will display the treatment number from the treatment window.
Fee Type: based on the item code used, this field defines the group the item code belongs to. This is pulled from the lineitem inventory table.
Dept: filled in during the Inventory Lookup with the responsible department in the shelter.
Price: the charge incurred for a single unit of this line item. The price is set at the inventory level and is only based on Board of Supervisors approved fees.
Subtotal: the subtotal based on QTY x PRICE for each lineitem.
Item Name: displays the item code description defined in the inventory.

Extra3: when creating collection receipts that include license fees, it is required to enter the licensing term in this field. When creating post seizure receipts for personnel charges, it is required to enter the full name of the employee in the field.
Extra4: when creating post seizure receipts for personnel charges, it is required to enter the abbreviated title of the employee for each lineitem and/or the activity performed for overtime charges.

Source: displays the source of the debt. DSCRIPT means item codes are automatically populated based on specific outcome types, outcome subtypes, intake types, and/or intake subtypes. USER means the item code was manually added to the lineitem by the user. DebtLookup means the receipt window automatically performed a Debt Lookup when the user entered the receipt screen (if this item is checked in the receipt preference window).

steps: Total Due: the total amount due on the receipt. The total is automatically calculated by Chameleon and cannot be entered or changed.
Cash: enter the total amount of cash tendered, not what is due.
Check: enter the amount written on the check ONLY if the credit card machine rejected the check or it is a money order.
Credit: enter the total amount charged to the credit card, or the total amount written on the check ONLY if approved by the credit card machine.
Tendered: the total amount tendered based on the amounts entered in the cash, check, and credit card fields. The amount is automatically calculated and cannot be entered manually.
Card No: enter the last four digits of the credit card number.
Exp: the credit card expiration date (mm/yyyy).
Auth: enter the authorization code issued by the credit card machine for check scanned and credit card transactions.

Trans: specifically used for lockbox/weblicense transactions. Automatically entered for downloaded lockbox processes containing the unique transaction ID from the credit card vendor. Enter the check number for check transactions.

Change: the amount to be returned to the customer. This amount is automatically calculated by the computer and cannot be entered or changed, except for payment plan or collection transactions and credit transactions.

Balance: the difference between TENDERED and TOTAL. The balance is automatically calculated by the computer and cannot be entered or changed. This should always be $0.00 unless handling payment plan, collections, or credit transactions.
Listbox (top right): contains all receipts for the specific Person ID.

sections.heading: Special Features
steps: Lookup Price (ALT+L): click on this button to look up the price based on the item code entered.

Add Item to List (ALT+A): click on this button to add a line item to the list box. You must add the item to the list box to have it stored and printed on the receipt. If you forget to press Add Item, then the item is not stored or printed. It will look up the price and department if that field is blank.
Remove Item from List (ALT+E): click on this button to remove a line item from the list box.

steps.step: Debt Lookup: selecting Procedure, Debt Lookup (ALT+P-B) causes Chameleon to search for debts owed to SHELTER. Debt Lookup automatically occurs when entering the Receipt Window. That is why the message Record Not Found appears when entering the receipt window, or why the list box already has items in it after entering the receipt window. To use this feature after having entered the Receipt Window:
sub: Clear (F11) the receipt window.
Enter the Person ID.

Select Debt Lookup (ALT+P-B). This causes the computer to find a License No associated with that PERSON_ID. The computer then finds all Tag/Link, Treatment, Kennel, Violation, and Receipt transaction (balance) records that have that ANIMAL_ID.
If any of these records are missing a Receipt No, then a line item for that record is created and placed in the list box on the Receipt Window.
If there are no windows associated with that ANIMAL_ID that are missing a Receipt No, then the message Record Not Found will appear. Press Enter to remove the message.

steps.step: Journal Entry: if an error is made on a receipt, use Journal Entry (ALT+P-J) to back out the receipt with an audit trail. Receipts cannot be modified or deleted. To use this feature:
sub: The receipt to be backed out must be displayed in the Receipt Window.
Select Journal Entry (ALT+P-J).
The message displays: "The Record matching the conditions you have specified will be Backed Out of the database...."
To back out the receipt, press Enter or click on OK with the mouse.
The message "Process Completed" will appear. Press Enter to remove the message.
To cancel this process, press TAB and then press Enter, or click on CANCEL with the mouse.

steps: Bad Check: changes the receipt amounts to a negative value and marks the receipt and person record with a BAD_Check icon. Use this procedure when a check is returned by the bank.

sections.heading: Disabled Features
steps: Tag Refund: this would tag a line item for refund. This feature is disabled.
Finish Refund: this would mark the original record as refunded. This feature is disabled.

sections.heading: Types of Chameleon Receipts
steps: Balance payment receipt: based on debts owed by the Person ID. Users cannot perform a journal entry on this type, and the user should not manually create or override a balance lineitem or the system will not recognize the transaction as a balance during a debt lookup.
Payment plan receipt with OCR receipt: generally created when Field Services collects payment offsite using an official county receipt (OCR) and the customer still owes money for services rendered.
Court restitution billing for employees: generally created to provide the courts an itemized billing of personnel involved in a case.
Court restitution billing for RTO: an itemized billing for impound fees owed by the defendant in a court case.
Payment plan for RTO: in cases when the owner of an impounded animal is not able to pay in full for reclaiming their pet, a balance receipt is created with a payment plan agreement.
Payment plan from field with OCR number: if the customer is not able to pay the total amount owed, the officer documents what the customer initially pays using an OCR receipt.

Billing for unredeemed RTO: if the owner of an impounded animal does not reclaim their pet and the animal has a final disposition, the owner is billed for impound fees and services rendered while the animal was in the care of DAS. A balance receipt is created to bill the owner.

Citation collection billing: a balance receipt based on debts owed from the citation violation window and tag/link windows. The bill generated itemizes each offense for each animal and a cumulative charge for retroactive licenses owed.

Adoption receipt with OCR number: stored for adoption fees collected during offsite events using an OCR receipt. It is required to include the OCR number when storing the Chameleon receipt for audit purposes.

RTO receipt: requires storing a tag/link window record, with the kennel animal ID matching the tag animal ID, the tag person ID matching the kennel person ID, the gender of the animal, and the outcome type RTO with the specified subtype of COUNTER unless the animal is staying for spay or neuter surgery. Based on these requirements, the debt script program will populate the receipt window with required charges by calculating the number of boarding days to charge from the intake date, and debt lookup will automatically display debts from the treatment, tag, and violation windows to charge the customer.

Adoption receipt: requires storing a tag/link window record, with the kennel animal ID matching the tag animal ID, the tag person ID matching the kennel person ID, the age of the animal for cats, intake date to outcome date for dogs, and the outcome type ADOPTION with the specified subtype of NEW unless the animal is staying for spay or neuter surgery. Based on these requirements, the debt script program will populate the receipt window with required charges by calculating the length of stay for dogs and the age of the animal for cats, and debt lookup will automatically display debts from the treatment, tag, and violation windows to charge the customer.

Adoption event receipt: depending on the type of event, requires storing a tag/link window record, with the kennel animal ID matching the tag animal ID, the tag person ID matching the kennel person ID, and the outcome type ADOPTION or ADOPT VET with the specified subtype of NEW unless the animal is staying for spay or neuter surgery.

Adoption event receipt with donor paying for all adoption fees: same storage requirements as an adoption event receipt. This requires the Chameleon administrator to obtain approval from the Fiscal department to apply an existing account code or a new account code to a new lineitem code for the donor.

Adoption event receipt with donor paying for partial adoption fees: same storage requirements as an adoption event receipt. This requires the Chameleon administrator to obtain approval from the Fiscal department to apply an existing account code or a new account code to a new lineitem code for the donor.

documents.searchText: to establish a uniform procedure for the completion of the chameleon receipt window in receiving payments, documenting, applying accurate board approved fees, and processing bad checks and journal entries (voiding a receipt). applicable to all personnel responsible for using the chameleon receipt window. director of animal services policy in accordance with chapter 2, section 2.03 of the state of california accounting standards and procedures for counties, state controller's office, division of accounting and reporting, and sections 13400-13407 of the california government code, county departments and agencies shall establish, document, and maintain an effective internal control system that ensures accuracy, safeguards assets, and promotes effectiveness and efficiency of operations. this includes following the physical control principle that equipment, inventories, cash, and other properties are secured physically, counted periodically, and compared with control records. record payment transactions accurately using the chameleon receipt window for reconciliation to determine that transactions are properly processed. field definitions receipt no: this field is required (red label) and searchable (yellow field). chameleon auto-generates a receipt number that starts with an r when the user stores (f9) a new receipt. the user can also enter an existing receipt number to find and display item codes associated with a specific receipt number. receipt date: this field defaults to the current date based on the set date of the local computer. this field records the date when monies were received. reference no: enter the contact number or case number for an empty lineitem reference number. enter the activity number for court billing. person id: contains the identification number associated with the owner of the animal for the receipt. if a person has recently been stored or queried from the tag or kennel window, the computer will automatically fill in person_id with the appropriate id number (format p#######). received from: enter the full name of the person who actually paid the amount on the receipt if different from the person id name. up to 25 characters may be entered. leave blank if creating a collections receipt. jurisdiction: chameleon will automatically bring the person jurisdiction over to this field. this must be changed if the person jurisdiction is changed to ensure accurate tracking of revenue to contract cities. extra1: this field is specifically used for payment plan transactions. if the owner is requesting a payment plan and has a co-signer for the payment plan, enter the co-signer's full name. extra2: this field is left blank unless generating a court restitution receipt. enter hold in this field to prevent the collections department from submitting the balance to the franchise tax board. comment: this field can be left blank, or enter a brief summary of what the transaction is about. {"step": "list box (alt+2): the list box at the bottom shows all the items on one receipt. if the kennel, tag, citation, bite, or treatment windows are stored or updated correctly, chameleon performs an auto-debt lookup based on the person id, automatically populating all debts owed by the pid. only items appearing in the list box are stored and printed on the receipt. list box fields:", "sub": ["qty: defaults to one (1) but can be changed. this field refers to the total count for each lineitem.", "item code: this field is based on the type of transaction.", "animal id: this field automatically displays the animal id from the kennel window, tag/link, citation, or treatment window.", "reference no: if the debt is pulled from the kennel window, the impound number is displayed as the reference for the item code. if the debt is pulled from the tag/link window, then the microchip, license, and/or link number is displayed. each medication and treatment type debt will display the treatment number from the treatment window.", "fee type: based on the item code used, this field defines the group the item code belongs to. this is pulled from the lineitem inventory table.", "dept: filled in during the inventory lookup with the responsible department in the shelter.", "price: the charge incurred for a single unit of this line item. the price is set at the inventory level and is only based on board of supervisors approved fees.", "subtotal: the subtotal based on qty x price for each lineitem.", "item name: displays the item code description defined in the inventory.", "extra3: when creating collection receipts that include license fees, it is required to enter the licensing term in this field. when creating post seizure receipts for personnel charges, it is required to enter the full name of the employee in the field.", "extra4: when creating post seizure receipts for personnel charges, it is required to enter the abbreviated title of the employee for each lineitem and/or the activity performed for overtime charges.", "source: displays the source of the debt. dscript means item codes are automatically populated based on specific outcome types, outcome subtypes, intake types, and/or intake subtypes. user means the item code was manually added to the lineitem by the user. debtlookup means the receipt window automatically performed a debt lookup when the user entered the receipt screen (if this item is checked in the receipt preference window)."]} total due: the total amount due on the receipt. the total is automatically calculated by chameleon and cannot be entered or changed. cash: enter the total amount of cash tendered, not what is due. check: enter the amount written on the check only if the credit card machine rejected the check or it is a money order. credit: enter the total amount charged to the credit card, or the total amount written on the check only if approved by the credit card machine. tendered: the total amount tendered based on the amounts entered in the cash, check, and credit card fields. the amount is automatically calculated and cannot be entered manually. card no: enter the last four digits of the credit card number. exp: the credit card expiration date (mm/yyyy). auth: enter the authorization code issued by the credit card machine for check scanned and credit card transactions. trans: specifically used for lockbox/weblicense transactions. automatically entered for downloaded lockbox processes containing the unique transaction id from the credit card vendor. enter the check number for check transactions. change: the amount to be returned to the customer. this amount is automatically calculated by the computer and cannot be entered or changed, except for payment plan or collection transactions and credit transactions. balance: the difference between tendered and total. the balance is automatically calculated by the computer and cannot be entered or changed. this should always be $0.00 unless handling payment plan, collections, or credit transactions. listbox (top right): contains all receipts for the specific person id. special features lookup price (alt+l): click on this button to look up the price based on the item code entered. add item to list (alt+a): click on this button to add a line item to the list box. you must add the item to the list box to have it stored and printed on the receipt. if you forget to press add item, then the item is not stored or printed. it will look up the price and department if that field is blank. remove item from list (alt+e): click on this button to remove a line item from the list box. {"step": "debt lookup: selecting procedure, debt lookup (alt+p-b) causes chameleon to search for debts owed to shelter. debt lookup automatically occurs when entering the receipt window. that is why the message record not found appears when entering the receipt window, or why the list box already has items in it after entering the receipt window. to use this feature after having entered the receipt window:", "sub": ["clear (f11) the receipt window.", "enter the person id.", "select debt lookup (alt+p-b). this causes the computer to find a license no associated with that person_id. the computer then finds all tag/link, treatment, kennel, violation, and receipt transaction (balance) records that have that animal_id.", "if any of these records are missing a receipt no, then a line item for that record is created and placed in the list box on the receipt window.", "if there are no windows associated with that animal_id that are missing a receipt no, then the message record not found will appear. press enter to remove the message."]} {"step": "journal entry: if an error is made on a receipt, use journal entry (alt+p-j) to back out the receipt with an audit trail. receipts cannot be modified or deleted. to use this feature:", "sub": ["the receipt to be backed out must be displayed in the receipt window.", "select journal entry (alt+p-j).", "the message displays: \"the record matching the conditions you have specified will be backed out of the database....\"", "to back out the receipt, press enter or click on ok with the mouse.", "the message \"process completed\" will appear. press enter to remove the message.", "to cancel this process, press tab and then press enter, or click on cancel with the mouse."]} bad check: changes the receipt amounts to a negative value and marks the receipt and person record with a bad_check icon. use this procedure when a check is returned by the bank. disabled features tag refund: this would tag a line item for refund. this feature is disabled. finish refund: this would mark the original record as refunded. this feature is disabled. types of chameleon receipts balance payment receipt: based on debts owed by the person id. users cannot perform a journal entry on this type, and the user should not manually create or override a balance lineitem or the system will not recognize the transaction as a balance during a debt lookup. payment plan receipt with ocr receipt: generally created when field services collects payment offsite using an official county receipt (ocr) and the customer still owes money for services rendered. court restitution billing for employees: generally created to provide the courts an itemized billing of personnel involved in a case. court restitution billing for rto: an itemized billing for impound fees owed by the defendant in a court case. payment plan for rto: in cases when the owner of an impounded animal is not able to pay in full for reclaiming their pet, a balance receipt is created with a payment plan agreement. payment plan from field with ocr number: if the customer is not able to pay the total amount owed, the officer documents what the customer initially pays using an ocr receipt. billing for unredeemed rto: if the owner of an impounded animal does not reclaim their pet and the animal has a final disposition, the owner is billed for impound fees and services rendered while the animal was in the care of das. a balance receipt is created to bill the owner. citation collection billing: a balance receipt based on debts owed from the citation violation window and tag/link windows. the bill generated itemizes each offense for each animal and a cumulative charge for retroactive licenses owed. adoption receipt with ocr number: stored for adoption fees collected during offsite events using an ocr receipt. it is required to include the ocr number when storing the chameleon receipt for audit purposes. rto receipt: requires storing a tag/link window record, with the kennel animal id matching the tag animal id, the tag person id matching the kennel person id, the gender of the animal, and the outcome type rto with the specified subtype of counter unless the animal is staying for spay or neuter surgery. based on these requirements, the debt script program will populate the receipt window with required charges by calculating the number of boarding days to charge from the intake date, and debt lookup will automatically display debts from the treatment, tag, and violation windows to charge the customer. adoption receipt: requires storing a tag/link window record, with the kennel animal id matching the tag animal id, the tag person id matching the kennel person id, the age of the animal for cats, intake date to outcome date for dogs, and the outcome type adoption with the specified subtype of new unless the animal is staying for spay or neuter surgery. based on these requirements, the debt script program will populate the receipt window with required charges by calculating the length of stay for dogs and the age of the animal for cats, and debt lookup will automatically display debts from the treatment, tag, and violation windows to charge the customer. adoption event receipt: depending on the type of event, requires storing a tag/link window record, with the kennel animal id matching the tag animal id, the tag person id matching the kennel person id, and the outcome type adoption or adopt vet with the specified subtype of new unless the animal is staying for spay or neuter surgery. adoption event receipt with donor paying for all adoption fees: same storage requirements as an adoption event receipt. this requires the chameleon administrator to obtain approval from the fiscal department to apply an existing account code or a new account code to a new lineitem code for the donor. adoption event receipt with donor paying for partial adoption fees: same storage requirements as an adoption event receipt. this requires the chameleon administrator to obtain approval from the fiscal department to apply an existing account code or a new account code to a new lineitem code for the donor. generally accepted accounting principles (gaap), as codified by the governmental accounting standards board (gasb) state of california accounting standards documents.title: Plan for Use of Spontaneous Volunteers During Emergencies
documents.purpose: To establish department wide uniformity regarding the procedures to effectively review and utilize spontaneous volunteers during emergency situations at on-site and off-site locations, and to outline how the Volunteer Department will manage volunteers who show up during a disaster to ensure they are utilized in the manner best fitting the needs of DAS.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to the Volunteer Department, DAS staff, and preapproved volunteers operating Volunteer Reception Centers during emergency and disaster events.
documents.supersedes: Policy 000-71, effective November 1, 2018; SOP Utilization Plan for Use of Spontaneous Volunteers During Emergencies, effective December 12, 2018 (absorbed)

sections.heading: Policy and Philosophy
steps: Anticipate that during a disaster, volunteers either already affiliated with DAS or not affiliated with DAS will show up eager to support the department.

The Department of Animal Services recognizes the assistance that volunteers bring during emergency and disaster events. Volunteering is a valuable activity that all walks of life can participate in; however, it is department policy that volunteers will not be placed into dangerous situations or work areas that present a significant risk to their health and welfare.
Volunteers are never to be allowed at an incident considered a crime scene, such as a terrorist attack.

Ideally, volunteers would affiliate with organizations prior to a disaster and be able to fill needed roles as necessary. Affiliation brings many advantages to volunteers, including training and the ability to fill vital roles quickly and effectively when needed. However, not all volunteers affiliate prior to a disaster.

Volunteers can provide a tangible economic benefit to a community through the work they perform, which may count as the local community's match for FEMA benefits if accurate records are maintained. Volunteers can also provide logistical support by taking on administrative tasks, freeing first responders and traditional disaster response organizations to focus on their primary mission.

A Volunteer Reception Center will be set up at affected locations, operated by DAS staff or volunteers, to manage volunteer applicants. These locations will be announced in department press releases, sent through volunteer recruitment communications, and posted on the department website as necessary.

The Department of Animal Services will determine if volunteer applicants are a good match or able to handle the current emergency based on pre-existing emergent volunteer job descriptions and interview questions.

steps.step: Include in messages to the public information on whether or not volunteers are needed and what it will entail. The communication plan will include press releases and communication to the public, email messages, and social media posts covering:
sub: Whether volunteers are needed or not.
When and where they should report.
What type of work the volunteers are most needed for.
What volunteers need to bring with them.
What volunteer conditions are like.

sections.heading: Volunteer Reception Center
steps: Operate the Volunteer Reception Center with DAS staff or preapproved volunteers. All spontaneous volunteers will be required to fill out a hardcopy Disaster Volunteer Application (Attachment A) upon reporting to an on-site or off-site DAS emergency location.
Require each volunteer applicant to fill in as much information as possible.
DAS staff will review and qualify the volunteer based on the information and the emergency situation. Some volunteers may not be utilized based on volunteer application information.
Compare volunteer applications to pre-developed volunteer job descriptions to determine if the volunteer can be utilized during the current emergency.
Never allow volunteers at an incident considered a crime scene, such as a terrorist attack.

steps.step: Require volunteers to go through an orientation and training process, in which they will receive:
sub: Expectations as a volunteer.
Basic housekeeping instructions.
A briefing on the specific emergency.

sections.heading: Reporting to Volunteer Duty
steps: Require volunteers to sign in before the beginning of their shift and out at the end of their shift on the Emergency Volunteer Sign In/Out Record (Attachment B).

sections.heading: Supervising Volunteers
steps: Ensure volunteers are supervised by a DAS supervisor or assigned DAS lead at all times, except that in some cases the receiving organization will provide volunteer supervision; in other cases, teams of volunteers with a supervisor may be placed with organizations.

sections.heading: Safety
steps: Require each volunteer to follow all safety instructions given for their specific job site.

steps.step: Require volunteers to dress appropriately for weather conditions, which may require:
sub: Proper footwear, such as boots or non-skid shoes.
Clothing that covers arms and legs.
Gloves, hats, and sunscreen.
Tools: volunteers may be required to bring their own tools and will be solely responsible for their own items.

steps: Require each volunteer to bring their own water, snacks, and medications to keep themselves hydrated and safe.

steps.step: Emotional stress and crisis:
sub: Any volunteer under duress of stress or emotional discomfort should report it to their lead or supervisor immediately.
Any DAS employee or volunteer who identifies a volunteer in need of help should report it to their lead or supervisor immediately.

steps.step: Volunteer injuries: if a volunteer is injured, the volunteer will be treated as any DAS employee.
sub: The volunteer may be covered under the County's insurance plan or by a local volunteer liability coverage plan and will be required to seek reimbursement from the insurance company.
See Risk Management Volunteer Insurance Program (Attachment C) and Incident/Accident Report, Non-Automobile (Attachment D).

steps.step: Assemble materials for the Volunteer Reception Center, including:
sub: Forms.
Volunteer Applications (Attachment A).
Release of Liability forms.
Sign In/Out Sheets (Attachment B).
Training Attendance Record.
Signs.
Volunteer job descriptions (need to be developed).
Interview questions (Attachment F).
Office supplies (Attachment E).

sections.heading: Interview Questions
steps: Why have you decided to volunteer?
What skills do you have that you feel will be useful to us as a volunteer at this time?
Are you prepared to work in a possibly stressful, unfamiliar situation?
Do you speak any language other than English?
What type of volunteer work would you like to do? What are you willing to do? (Include a list of jobs needed.)
Have you ever volunteered in a disaster situation before?
Do you have your own transportation?
Do you have any questions about volunteering?
Are you under 18?
Have you ever been convicted of a crime?
Do you have any limitations, physical or other, that limit the type of volunteer work that you can safely perform?
May I see identification?
When was your last tetanus vaccination?
Have you been rabies vaccinated?

related.name: Emergency Volunteer Sign In/Out Record (Attachment B)
Risk Management Volunteer Insurance Program (Attachment C)

appendices: Appendix A: Disaster Volunteer Application (attached separately)
Appendix B: Emergency Volunteer Sign In/Out Record (attached separately)
Appendix C: Risk Management Volunteer Insurance Program (attached separately)
Appendix D: Incident/Accident Report, Non-Automobile (attached separately)
Appendix E: Office Supplies list (attached separately)
Appendix F: Interview Questions (inlined in Section: Interview Questions)

documents.title: Cat Issues Telephone Protocol
documents.purpose: To guide phone operators and front desk staff in assessing and responding to citizen calls about cat issues, and to educate citizens about the humane alternatives that exist for addressing the problems and concerns of the public regarding free-roaming cats.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to phone operators and front desk staff who receive calls from citizens about cat issues.
documents.supersedes: None identified

definitions.def: A humane method of feral cat population control in which cats are spayed or neutered, vaccinated, ear-tipped, and returned to the community.
A program that spays or neuters, vaccinates, and ear-tips a stray cat, then returns the cat to the community in an effort to decrease cat overpopulation and save the cat's life.
A large or small group of wild cats living together. A colony may contain both tame and feral cats.

sections.heading: Background
steps: Riverside County Animal Services is a statutory agency and needs legislative or contract authority to provide services to the citizens of Riverside County. While law addresses the issue of dogs at large, there is no corresponding law with regard to free-roaming cats, with the exception of cat bites and animal cruelty.

Free-roaming cats are a concern within the community for many reasons. Some citizens simply want them gone; others are concerned for their health and safety; most want to stop the breeding process through various methods, some humane, some not.
Riverside County is committed to educating citizens about the humane alternatives that exist for addressing the problems and concerns of the public in this much-misunderstood area.
When a call comes in from a citizen wanting assistance with a cat issue in their area, the phone operator or front desk staff should first assess the full extent of the citizen's need.

sections.heading: Cat Bites
steps: If a cat has bitten a human being and the citizen has it confined, Riverside County will impound the cat for 10 days from the date of bite for rabies observation.
At the discretion of Riverside County, impoundment may also be provided by an authorized veterinary hospital, at home, or at the shelter.
Citizens can bring the cat into the shelter, or an officer can be dispatched to the scene.

sections.heading: Owned Cats: Medical Services
steps: Healthy cats: Riverside County always encourages all cat owners to spay or neuter their cats and keep them indoors, properly vaccinate cats against rabies and other communicable diseases, and microchip and affix a rabies and identification tag to their cat's collar. There is no law requiring cats to be inside.

Riverside County offers its residents low-cost, or in some cases free, spay or neuter services for their cat. There are also other spay/neuter programs available. Refer the customer to the department website where they can learn more about the services and make an appointment.

Ill or injured cats: Riverside County provides euthanasia service to owners requesting to have their cat euthanized due to illness, injury, or age-related problems. The department does not provide field pick up service for this. The cat must be brought to the shelter after an appointment is made online and a telemedicine appointment occurs. There is a $25 fee for euthanasia, along with a $25 fee for disposal.

sections.heading: Owned Cats: Unwanted by Owners
steps: Encourage owners to consider relinquishment to the shelter as a last resort.

Moving: suggest an initial inquiry regarding whether the new housing accepts pets. There are many properties that allow pets; the owner can find them with patience. Also, if a pet owner is able to get an emotional support designation for their pet, most housing management companies will allow it.

Unwanted litter of kittens: Riverside County provides spay/neuter services to owners relinquishing litters of kittens (or puppies). Encourage these owners to keep the litters until they are weaned from the mother to enhance the chances of the offspring being adopted. Riverside County can assist with resources (bottles, etc.) if needed.

Riverside County does not provide field service pick up for an unwanted owned cat unless the animal has been part of a bite case, is aggressive, or is part of a cruelty situation. To turn an animal in to the shelter, an owner surrender appointment must be made on the department website.

Inappropriate elimination, including spraying: if the cat is spraying, the first question should be whether it is neutered or spayed; if not, advise the caller of Riverside County sterilization services. Advise the citizen that the single most common reason for inappropriate elimination in sterilized cats is a medical condition, and encourage them to take the cat to a veterinarian as a first step.
Behavioral issues: refer the caller to the department website for more behavioral support.

Pregnancy, childbirth, or allergy: these are big reasons why cats are given up, often on the advice of ill-informed doctors. Urge the citizen to contact their doctor for allergy medication and to contact their veterinarian for information on products and procedures available. At a minimum, encourage them to educate themselves before relinquishing a cat.
Abandoned in a home: make an appointment to bring the animal into the shelter or send a field officer to pick up.

sections.heading: Homeless, Lost, or Stray Tame Cats
steps: Statistically speaking, most tame, outdoor cats have an owner or are being cared for by multiple people. Encourage the public to assist in finding the owner of a stray cat prior to bringing it into the shelter.

If the finder chooses to take the cat into their home, they should file a found report on the department website and follow the other guidelines listed on the site. They can also be referred to the shelter for spay/neuter services.

If the finder wants to turn the cat in to the shelter, ask the person to make an appointment on the department website to bring the animal in. If a person shows up at the shelter without an appointment, they will be given an immediate appointment. The department will hold the animal for the legal hold period.
Cats will be picked up in the field if they are involved in a bite or animal cruelty case, pose a real threat to a person or to animals, or on a case by case basis approved by a supervisor.

When a stray cat is turned in, let the person know that the cat may be part of the community cat program if not returned to owner or selected for the placement program. This program will spay or neuter the cat, vaccinate the cat, ear-tip the cat, and then return the cat to the community in an effort to decrease the cat overpopulation problem and save the cat's life. Discuss the benefits of TNR and the problems with trapping.

Encourage citizens who have complaints about the behavior of owned cats to talk with the cat owner and work with them to resolve the issue. Encourage them to share information about the spay/neuter program and to speak with the community cat coordinator.

Neighborhood complaints about neighborhood cats may really be about feral cats that another neighbor is feeding. Tell the caller that the person feeding the cats should be encouraged to call the community cat coordinator, who will talk to them about the community cat program and other TNR programs in the community.

All generic cat complaints must go through the community cat coordinator prior to dispatching an officer to pick up a cat, unless the cat is injured, part of a cruelty situation, or involved in a bite. Field officer trapping should be used as a last resort.
If a field officer has a situation where they would like to proactively help, they should connect with the responding shelter's community cat coordinator to coordinate a plan.

sections.heading: Feral, Wild, or Skittish Cats
steps: There are many wild cats living on our streets. Wild cats are primarily cats born on the street to either free-roaming tame cats or to other wild cats. They have not been socialized to people and therefore are afraid of them. In some rarer cases, they may be previously owned cats that have been on the street so long that they have become afraid of people and are no longer socialized.
Wild cats may be alone, or they may be found in large or small groups called colonies. A colony of cats may contain both tame and feral cats.

Riverside County advocates Trap, Neuter, Return (TNR) as a proven, humane method of feral cat population control. The community cat coordinator is to be trained to explain why catch and kill methodologies do not work. Riverside County opposes eradication of free-roaming, feral, or wild cats.
Refer citizens who are upset that more is not being done to solve problems associated with free-roaming or feral cats in their neighborhoods to the community cat coordinator for discussion.

Neighborhood complaints about neighborhood cats may really be about feral cats that another neighbor is feeding. Tell the caller that the person feeding the cats should be encouraged to reach out to the community cat coordinator, who will talk to them about the community cat program, spay/neuter, and other TNR programs in the community.

All cat complaints must go through the community cat coordinator prior to dispatching an officer to pick up a cat, unless the cat is injured, part of a cruelty situation, or involved in a bite. Field officer trapping should be used as a last resort. Only a supervisor can authorize a cat to be picked up aside from the situations listed above.

If the caller wants to turn the cat in to the shelter, ask the person to make an appointment on the department website. If a person shows up at the shelter without an appointment, they will be given an immediate appointment. The department will hold the animal for the legal hold period.
If a field officer has a situation where they would like to proactively help, they should connect with the responding shelter's community cat coordinator to coordinate a plan.

When a stray cat is turned in, let the person know that the cat may be part of the community cat program if not returned to owner or selected for the placement program. This program will spay or neuter the cat, vaccinate the cat, ear-tip the cat, and then return the cat to the community. Discuss the benefits of TNR and the problems with trapping.

related.name: RCDAS website (spay/neuter appointments, owner surrender appointments, found reports, behavioral support)
