documents.searchText: to guide phone operators and front desk staff in assessing and responding to citizen calls about cat issues, and to educate citizens about the humane alternatives that exist for addressing the problems and concerns of the public regarding free-roaming cats. applicable to phone operators and front desk staff who receive calls from citizens about cat issues. director of animal services tnr (trap, neuter, return) a humane method of feral cat population control in which cats are spayed or neutered, vaccinated, ear-tipped, and returned to the community. community cat program a program that spays or neuters, vaccinates, and ear-tips a stray cat, then returns the cat to the community in an effort to decrease cat overpopulation and save the cat's life. colony a large or small group of wild cats living together. a colony may contain both tame and feral cats. background riverside county animal services is a statutory agency and needs legislative or contract authority to provide services to the citizens of riverside county. while law addresses the issue of dogs at large, there is no corresponding law with regard to free-roaming cats, with the exception of cat bites and animal cruelty. free-roaming cats are a concern within the community for many reasons. some citizens simply want them gone; others are concerned for their health and safety; most want to stop the breeding process through various methods, some humane, some not. riverside county is committed to educating citizens about the humane alternatives that exist for addressing the problems and concerns of the public in this much-misunderstood area. when a call comes in from a citizen wanting assistance with a cat issue in their area, the phone operator or front desk staff should first assess the full extent of the citizen's need. cat bites if a cat has bitten a human being and the citizen has it confined, riverside county will impound the cat for 10 days from the date of bite for rabies observation. at the discretion of riverside county, impoundment may also be provided by an authorized veterinary hospital, at home, or at the shelter. citizens can bring the cat into the shelter, or an officer can be dispatched to the scene. owned cats: medical services healthy cats: riverside county always encourages all cat owners to spay or neuter their cats and keep them indoors, properly vaccinate cats against rabies and other communicable diseases, and microchip and affix a rabies and identification tag to their cat's collar. there is no law requiring cats to be inside. riverside county offers its residents low-cost, or in some cases free, spay or neuter services for their cat. there are also other spay/neuter programs available. refer the customer to the department website where they can learn more about the services and make an appointment. ill or injured cats: riverside county provides euthanasia service to owners requesting to have their cat euthanized due to illness, injury, or age-related problems. the department does not provide field pick up service for this. the cat must be brought to the shelter after an appointment is made online and a telemedicine appointment occurs. there is a $25 fee for euthanasia, along with a $25 fee for disposal. owned cats: unwanted by owners encourage owners to consider relinquishment to the shelter as a last resort. moving: suggest an initial inquiry regarding whether the new housing accepts pets. there are many properties that allow pets; the owner can find them with patience. also, if a pet owner is able to get an emotional support designation for their pet, most housing management companies will allow it. unwanted litter of kittens: riverside county provides spay/neuter services to owners relinquishing litters of kittens (or puppies). encourage these owners to keep the litters until they are weaned from the mother to enhance the chances of the offspring being adopted. riverside county can assist with resources (bottles, etc.) if needed. riverside county does not provide field service pick up for an unwanted owned cat unless the animal has been part of a bite case, is aggressive, or is part of a cruelty situation. to turn an animal in to the shelter, an owner surrender appointment must be made on the department website. inappropriate elimination, including spraying: if the cat is spraying, the first question should be whether it is neutered or spayed; if not, advise the caller of riverside county sterilization services. advise the citizen that the single most common reason for inappropriate elimination in sterilized cats is a medical condition, and encourage them to take the cat to a veterinarian as a first step. behavioral issues: refer the caller to the department website for more behavioral support. pregnancy, childbirth, or allergy: these are big reasons why cats are given up, often on the advice of ill-informed doctors. urge the citizen to contact their doctor for allergy medication and to contact their veterinarian for information on products and procedures available. at a minimum, encourage them to educate themselves before relinquishing a cat. abandoned in a home: make an appointment to bring the animal into the shelter or send a field officer to pick up. homeless, lost, or stray tame cats statistically speaking, most tame, outdoor cats have an owner or are being cared for by multiple people. encourage the public to assist in finding the owner of a stray cat prior to bringing it into the shelter. if the finder chooses to take the cat into their home, they should file a found report on the department website and follow the other guidelines listed on the site. they can also be referred to the shelter for spay/neuter services. if the finder wants to turn the cat in to the shelter, ask the person to make an appointment on the department website to bring the animal in. if a person shows up at the shelter without an appointment, they will be given an immediate appointment. the department will hold the animal for the legal hold period. cats will be picked up in the field if they are involved in a bite or animal cruelty case, pose a real threat to a person or to animals, or on a case by case basis approved by a supervisor. when a stray cat is turned in, let the person know that the cat may be part of the community cat program if not returned to owner or selected for the placement program. this program will spay or neuter the cat, vaccinate the cat, ear-tip the cat, and then return the cat to the community in an effort to decrease the cat overpopulation problem and save the cat's life. discuss the benefits of tnr and the problems with trapping. encourage citizens who have complaints about the behavior of owned cats to talk with the cat owner and work with them to resolve the issue. encourage them to share information about the spay/neuter program and to speak with the community cat coordinator. neighborhood complaints about neighborhood cats may really be about feral cats that another neighbor is feeding. tell the caller that the person feeding the cats should be encouraged to call the community cat coordinator, who will talk to them about the community cat program and other tnr programs in the community. all generic cat complaints must go through the community cat coordinator prior to dispatching an officer to pick up a cat, unless the cat is injured, part of a cruelty situation, or involved in a bite. field officer trapping should be used as a last resort. if a field officer has a situation where they would like to proactively help, they should connect with the responding shelter's community cat coordinator to coordinate a plan. feral, wild, or skittish cats there are many wild cats living on our streets. wild cats are primarily cats born on the street to either free-roaming tame cats or to other wild cats. they have not been socialized to people and therefore are afraid of them. in some rarer cases, they may be previously owned cats that have been on the street so long that they have become afraid of people and are no longer socialized. wild cats may be alone, or they may be found in large or small groups called colonies. a colony of cats may contain both tame and feral cats. riverside county advocates trap, neuter, return (tnr) as a proven, humane method of feral cat population control. the community cat coordinator is to be trained to explain why catch and kill methodologies do not work. riverside county opposes eradication of free-roaming, feral, or wild cats. refer citizens who are upset that more is not being done to solve problems associated with free-roaming or feral cats in their neighborhoods to the community cat coordinator for discussion. neighborhood complaints about neighborhood cats may really be about feral cats that another neighbor is feeding. tell the caller that the person feeding the cats should be encouraged to reach out to the community cat coordinator, who will talk to them about the community cat program, spay/neuter, and other tnr programs in the community. all cat complaints must go through the community cat coordinator prior to dispatching an officer to pick up a cat, unless the cat is injured, part of a cruelty situation, or involved in a bite. field officer trapping should be used as a last resort. only a supervisor can authorize a cat to be picked up aside from the situations listed above. if the caller wants to turn the cat in to the shelter, ask the person to make an appointment on the department website. if a person shows up at the shelter without an appointment, they will be given an immediate appointment. the department will hold the animal for the legal hold period. if a field officer has a situation where they would like to proactively help, they should connect with the responding shelter's community cat coordinator to coordinate a plan. when a stray cat is turned in, let the person know that the cat may be part of the community cat program if not returned to owner or selected for the placement program. this program will spay or neuter the cat, vaccinate the cat, ear-tip the cat, and then return the cat to the community. discuss the benefits of tnr and the problems with trapping.

documents.title: Chameleon Lockbox WebLicensing Process Review and Reconciliation
documents.purpose: To provide a consistent workflow procedure for payments received via the Internet.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable only to the designated lockbox reviewers and reconcilers of the Department of Animal Services (DAS) with experience and knowledge in the different licensing transactions using the Chameleon tag/link, citation, and receipt windows, state licensing guidelines, licensing ordinances, and municipal codes. The designated staff must perform address verification by updating the Person ID along with the GIS mapping system, correct misspellings of street names, and know and understand the flow and mechanics of the license notification program.
documents.supersedes: SOP effective August 1, 2016; revised from May 19, 2015; revised from December 29, 2014
references: HLP's Chameleon Lockbox Training Manual

definitions.def: HLP's Chameleon Lockbox software imports confirmed credit card payments from the county's approved vendor into DAS' Chameleon database. Each time a shelter patron pays for a license renewal or purchases a new license via the www.rcdas.org website, or donates through the web, the credit card vendor provides HLP a data file. This data file is downloaded through the lockbox application, which enters the information into the Chameleon database, automatically scrubs the data for duplicate person records, renews or stores license records and receipts, and assigns a designated range of WebLicensing tag numbers.

sections.heading: Duties and Responsibilities of the Lockbox Reviewer
steps: Review lockbox transactions flagged as rejected on a daily basis through the lockbox reject details report.
Review the lockbox comments by logging into https://chameleonbeach.com/ and respond to the customer if applicable.
Ensure the renewal process is correct.
Verify person jurisdiction and tag jurisdiction using the Chameleon address checker (F8 or house icon) for each transaction.
Review problem codes and application of payment to license history.
Verify picture attachments.
Store microchip records when applicable.
Perform a Chameleon debt lookup for collection balances.
Change the tag subtype from HLP% or WEB% to COLLECTION if the WebLicensing transaction is in collection.
Print and mail a Chameleon generated Temporary License Notice report for each weblicense transaction.
Ensure that web memos are accurate, particularly applying state law on when the license and requirements are due regardless of the date paid.
Issue WebLicensing automated issued tags by running and printing the License Certificate by Number report.
Review if an overpayment can be applied to a credit or to a late fee from a previous transaction.
Notify the Chameleon administrator of any issues or problems relating to Lockbox in a timely and prompt manner.

sections.heading: Credit Card Receipts
steps: Confirmation of every credit card transaction through WebLicensing is automatically emailed to rabiescontrol@rivcocha.org from ELAVON.

steps.step: Print each confirmation email based on:
sub: Transaction Time, not sent time. For example, if reviewing WebLicensing payment transactions for 10/09/2014, then the only emails printed are those with transaction times that show 10/9/2014 11:55 PM. Sent date and time is California time (PST). Transaction Time is Eastern Standard Time (EST).
The sender is internetproductsupport@convergepay.com.
Only approved purchases.

sections.heading: Open the Lockbox Application
steps: Press Step 1 to display downloaded data to be processed.
Press Step 2 to process lockbox records.
Once the application finishes, it will display a message at the very bottom of the application.

sections.heading: Review WebLicense Comments (chameleonbeach.com)
steps: Log in to https://chameleonbeach.com/ using the user ID and password provided by the Chameleon administrator.
Right mouse click on License Services.
Enter the beginning transaction date and ending transaction date for WebLicense Comments.
Right mouse click on WebLicense Comments.

steps.step: Review each record for comments from customers. Pay attention to inquiries relating to:
sub: Change of address.
Microchip information.
Name change of owner.
Dog description changes.
Licensing disputes or inquiries.

steps: Copy and paste each comment into the Person ID memo with documentation of the action taken or resolution.
When responding via email, use rabiescontrol@rivcocha.org to email the response to the customer, then copy and paste the text to the Chameleon memo.

sections.heading: Chameleon Review and Reconciliation
steps: Log in to Chameleon under LB for Lockbox location.
Open the Tag/Link window.

steps.step: Review lockbox rejects:
sub: Press function key F3 on the keyboard to bring up the reports menu.
Scroll down to LICENSING REPORTS and choose Lockbox Reject Processing (weblicensing).
Go to the last page of the report to determine if any lockbox transaction was not entered into Chameleon.
Review the message explaining why lockbox rejected the record.
Bring up the license record in the tag/link window to find out why lockbox could not store the payment.
If the license record is already renewed, review the Elavon receipt to determine if the payment is a duplicate. Set aside to process for refund.
If the record was rejected because the tag number is a duplicate, review the tag window. In this case, the dog owner was cited and lockbox attempted to renew the U number to an L.
Close the report.

steps.step: After midnight transactions (posted date versus date paid): due to the time difference between Elavon and DAS, payments posted after 12 midnight need to be fixed:
sub: Press F3 to bring up the reports menu.
Scroll down to LICENSING REPORTS and choose Lockbox Daily Deposit (weblicensing only).
Enter the transaction date (yesterday's date).
Enter the password.
Go to the last page of the report.
Bring up the license record for each receipt of posted payments past 12 midnight.
Make the necessary corrections in the Person ID and tag window.
Store new receipts prior to performing a journal entry for transactions that were downloaded after 12 midnight. Make sure that the receipt date reflects the correct date.
Close the report.

steps.step: Approved transactions:
sub: Press F3 to bring up the reports menu.
Scroll down to LICENSING REPORTS and choose Lockbox Daily Deposit (weblicensing only).
Enter the transaction date (yesterday's date).
Enter the password.
Once the report is displayed, scroll or review WebLicensing transactions handled by the previous day's WebLicensing staff and those under WebLic.
Bring up the tag window.
Enter the tag/link number in the Tag No field and find (F12) the record.
Go to the Person ID field, press the F4 key, then press F8 to perform an address/jurisdiction verification.
Verify that the tag jurisdiction and person jurisdiction match.
Make sure that the tag type matches the tag jurisdiction.
Verify the tag_exp date is consistent with the tag type and the tag price.
If the tag_status is temporary, verify that the problem codes are accurate and a webdata memo is stored based on the problem codes. This is required.
With the temporary record displayed in the tag window, press F3 to bring up the reports menu. Scroll down to License Reports and choose Temporary License Notice. Print and mail.

If the original tag number begins with a U because of a problem code but an attached image meets license requirements (for example, microchip), make sure to issue a License Tag. Transfer images from the U number to the new L number through Chamcam.
Place the cursor on the receipt number field, or tab to the receipt number field, then press F4 to bring up the receipt.
Print the receipt for deposit.
Repeat these steps until all license numbers have been reviewed based on the WebLicensing daily deposit report.

sections.heading: Balancing WebLicensing
steps: Rerun the Lockbox Daily Deposit (weblicensing only) report.
Open and log in to https://www.internetsecure.com/merchants/Login.asp.
Click on TXN Query.
Choose the correct Start Date and End Date that match the weblicensing transaction date.
Place a check mark on Show approvals only.
Click on Submit Query.
Compare the amount in the Approved Transactions to the Lockbox Daily Deposit report grand total.
Fold the credit card receipts and attach them to the corresponding Chameleon receipt.
Run a tape for the credit card receipts.
Deduct other users' transactions from the lockbox daily deposit grand total. The difference must match the total of the credit card receipts.
Staple the adding machine tape to the lockbox daily deposit report.
Complete the report: Submitted by: Web Licensing; Signature: user's signature; Date: current date.
Attach the report to the Chameleon receipts and submit to the Fiscal department.

related.name: SOP Processing Telephone Payments using Converge
Tag/Link, Receipt, and Person Windows; Lockbox application

appendices: Appendix A: Acknowledgment of Chameleon Lockbox (WebLicensing) Process, Review, and Reconciliation (employee signature form)

documents.title: Creating Groups and Attaching Members in Chameleon
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform guideline for storing and assigning members to groups in Chameleon.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to staff who have access to the Group and Member windows in Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: None identified

sections.heading: Functions
steps: Member Window: this window is used to link Person IDs to a unique Group ID.

Staff who have access to the Group and Member windows must first run the Group Codes report from the Chameleon reports drop down menu to avoid duplication. The same group number can be used for multiple employees regardless of the division they belong to.
Group Window: this window links an employee or employees (PID) to the group number that they are a member of.

sections.heading: Getting to the Group Window
steps: Click on Window.
From the drop down menu, choose Person.
From the Person menu, choose Member.
Tab to the Group ID and pop up (F4).

sections.heading: Storing a Group (F9)

steps.step: Group Type: pop up (F4) to view a list of validations.
sub: DIVISION (DIV)
LOCATION (LOC)
REPORTS TO (RET)
UNIT (U)

steps: Service: pop up (F4) for a list of validations.
Group Name: the name of the particular group, organization, or business.
Telephone Number 1 to 3: enter the telephone number or fax number.

Address 1: the address fields are broken down into street number, direction, street name, street type, apt, city, state, zip code, and zip suffix. They are broken down in this manner to enable effective searches on the address fields and for a clean translation into the street mapping software. The street number must be numeric. The direction, street type, city, and state fields are validated.
Address 2: enter the complete secondary or mailing address if applicable.
Listbox: shows the members of the group.

sections.heading: Assigning Members

steps.step: Person ID (PID): enter the Person ID of the employee if known.
sub: If not known, pop up (F4) to bring up the Person window and search for employee information.
Use the working Person ID.
If the record is not found, create a Person ID.
Press the Enter key to bring the Person ID back to the window.

steps: Group ID: use the G number assigned for the group that the employee is a member of. Note: a search of the group name must be initiated first to minimize duplication or erroneous membership.

steps.step: Member Type: the status of the person for a particular group.
sub: Active
Inactive
Retired
Terminated

steps.step: Member Subtype: further qualifies the association between the person and the group.
sub: Staff
Employee
Management
Other

steps: Start Date: the begin date the person joined the group.
End Date: indicates the date when the person no longer belongs to the group.
Member Listbox: displays the groups the person belongs to.
Group Listbox: displays the members of the group.
Store the record (F9).

steps.step: If multiple employees will be assigned to the same group:
sub: Place the cursor on the Person ID field.
Delete the currently displayed PID.
Enter or look up the next employee's Person ID as described above.
Store the record (F9).

documents.title: Creating and Tracking Employee Training Attendance in Chameleon
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform guideline for storing and linking training numbers and employee attendance.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services; access to the Attendance and Evaluation windows is given by the Chameleon administrator
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable for supervisors and managers to document in Chameleon employee attendance at trainings, workshops, and conferences. It is expected that supervisors and managers scan a copy of the proof of attendance to the employee's work PID.
documents.supersedes: SOP effective May 6, 2017; revised from March 8, 2009

sections.heading: Functions
steps: Training Window: this window is used to set up courses, classes, conferences, seminars, or trainings.

Supervisors and managers must run the Training Codes report from the Chameleon reports drop down menu, based on the training date range and description of training, to avoid duplication. The same training number can be used for multiple employees regardless of the division they belong to.

steps.step: Supervisors and managers must contact the Chameleon administrator and request creation of the course information with the following required information:
sub: What is the course? A training course is a series of lessons or lectures teaching the skills that the user needs for a specific job or activity. Pop up (F4) to view a list of course validations: Cash-Handling, Chameleon, Webchameleon, Mandated Training, Orientation, Euthanasia, Conference, Workshop, P&P, Rabies Control.

What is the type of training? For example: Annual, New Employee, Refresher, Collections Review, Basics, Licensing Operation, Front Counter Operations, Receipt, Violation/Citation, Kennel Operations, Treatment, Field Operations, Clinic, Employee, Supervisor, Volunteer, Instruction, Practical, NACA, Writing, RMAP, Public Records, Annual Review.
Location of training? For example, CAC or RCIT.
Type of media used for training? PowerPoint, video, web, or book.
Dates of training.
Schedule of training. For example, 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM.
Presenter: full name of trainer or presenter.

Topic. For example: cash handling front counter, cash handling field services, Vaccert processing, license renewal credits, temporary license processing, retroactive license processing, Supersearch, navigating Chameleon, Chameleon windows, Webchameleon kennel, kennel inventory, To-Dos (daily task), field activity search, tag search, ASP, ballistic, PC 832.
Certificate given? Yes or no.
Sponsor: name of division sponsoring the training. For example, Veterinary Services.
Course description.

steps: Attendance Window: this window links an employee or employees (work PID) to the training number taken from the Training Window. In addition, this window shows the hours of attendance.

steps.step: Access the Attendance Window:
sub: From the Windows menu tab, click on Person.
From the Person menu, click on Training.
From the Training menu, click on Attendance.

steps.step: Link employees to the course:
sub: Person ID (PID): enter the Person ID of the employee if known. If not known, pop up (F4) to bring up the Person window and search for employee information. Use the working Person ID. If the record is not found, create a Person ID. Press the Enter key to bring the Person ID back to the Attendance window.
Training No: use the E number assigned for the session that the employee attended. Note: the same training number must be used for other attendees regardless of division.
Hours: enter the hours of attendance.
Store the record (F9).

steps.step: If multiple employees will be assigned to the same training course:
sub: Place the cursor on the Person ID field.
Delete the currently displayed PID.
Enter or look up the next employee's Person ID as described above.
Store the record (F9).

sections.heading: Scanning Proof of Attendance to Chameleon
steps: Place the cursor on the employee's PID.
Mouse click on Extras.
Choose CHAMCAM.
Once the CHAMCAM screen is displayed, place a check mark on DOCUMENTS. This will ensure that the picture retains its aspect ratio.
If a scanner is attached directly to the computer, choose TWAIN DEVICE. Choose the scanner, then mouse click on SELECT.
If the document or picture of the certificate is saved in a specific folder, choose BROWSE FILES.
Press the down arrow button to view choices of drives where the folder is located.
Choose the folder where the file is located. Chamcam only accepts file type jpg.
Mouse click OK.
Once the picture is displayed on the Chamcam screen, mouse click on STORE to attach the picture to the Person ID.
Mouse click OK once the image is inserted to return to the Attendance window.

appendices: Appendix A: Training Codes report, Employee Training Codes (sample report listing training numbers, types, dates, topics, and presenters; attached separately)

documents.title: How to Input Official County Receipts (OCR) into Chameleon
documents.purpose: To establish procedures for processing cash, credit cards, and checks turned in by Department of Animal Services employees collected in the field, at front counters, or at special events.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: All personnel who are authorized to handle transactions of a financial nature.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to Input Official County Receipts (OCR) into Chameleon, effective December 12, 2017 (revised from July 1, 2017 and March 7, 2017)
references: DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling
DAS Policy 000-27, Official County Receipt Tracking Policy
DAS Policy 000-45, The Chameleon Memo Window
DAS Policy 000-66, Completion of the Chameleon Receipt Window

definitions.def: A manual receipt book used in case of emergency, when the Chameleon system or WEB Chameleon is not operational. Refer to DAS Policy 000-27, Official County Receipt Tracking, for more information on OCR books.

sections.heading: A. Front Counter and Clinic Staff
steps: Transfer all OCR information into the Chameleon receipt window as soon as Chameleon comes back online. This is an expectation of each employee.
Verify your money using the Daily Deposit Batch Slip (Attachment C) at the end of your shift if Chameleon is not available.
In the Chameleon receipt window, key in the appropriate name, address, phone number, and service provided for each OCR issued.

Reflect each item code on the OCR in Chameleon's item code field for the service provided. Refer to DAS Policy 000-66, Completion of the Chameleon Receipt Window, for more information on selecting an item code.

steps.step: After entering all the fees from the OCR into the Chameleon receipt, add item code MISC FEE:
sub: Place the cursor on the item code field and type in the word MISC FEE.
Select the green $ sign.
In the reference section, type in the word OCR and the corresponding OCR number.
Make sure to leave a space between OCR and the OCR number.
Select the green + icon.

steps: Select the payment method.
Store (F9) the receipt.

sections.heading: B. Off-Site Events
steps: Use an OCR book at off-site events if the employee does not have access to WEB Chameleon.

If WEB Chameleon is not available, upon return to the shelter, immediately transfer all yellow copies of the OCR into the Chameleon receipt window. Refer to DAS Policy 000-66, Completion of the Chameleon Receipt Window.
Refer to Section A, steps 1 through 7.

sections.heading: C. Field Services
steps: At the end of each shift, gather all the yellow copies of the OCR, cash, and checks along with the Daily Deposit Batch Slip (Attachment C). Verify your work and sign the Daily Deposit Batch Slip. Refer to DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling.
Have the yellow copies of the OCR, all cash, and checks, along with the Daily Deposit Batch Slip, verified prior to end of shift per DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling.

After the employee verifies their work and has it verified by a supervisor or a lead, drop it in the safe with a witness or hand deliver it to the fiscal designee. Refer to DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling.
The fiscal deposit designee will transfer the OCR from the safe and distribute the OCR receipts to another fiscal designee to process.
The fiscal designee will enter the OCRs into Chameleon by the next business day. Refer to Section A, steps 1 through 7.

sections.heading: D. Collections
steps: Turn in all OCR billing receipts to the fiscal or collections designee at each shelter.

steps.step: The designee will then follow Section A, steps 1 through 7:
sub: All the fees on the OCR will say collection.
Do not forget to add the payment plan fee collection.

sections.heading: E. Voiding an OCR Receipt
steps: The OCR designee is responsible for entering voided receipts in Chameleon. Refer to Section A, steps 1 through 7.
Enter all voided receipts in Chameleon using the MISC FEE account code.
Enter the OCR number and the word void in the reference number and 0 in the dollar amount.
Store (F9) the receipt.
Create a memo after storing the receipt to indicate the reason why the receipt was voided. Refer to DAS Policy 000-45, The Chameleon Memo Window, for more information on creating memos.

sections.heading: F. Reconciling the OCR with the Chameleon Generated Daily Deposit Batch Control Slip
steps: At the end of the shift, after all the OCRs have been entered into Chameleon, the clerk will run the Chameleon Generated Daily Deposit Batch Control Slip (Attachment D).
Verify your receipts per DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling.
Get your work verified by a supervisor or lead per DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling.
Drop the deposit in the safe with a witness per DAS Policy 000-22, Cash Handling.
Distribute OCR copies as follows: the white copy goes to the customer, the yellow copy stays with the funds, and the pink copy stays in the book.

related.name: DAS Policy 000-27, Official County Receipt Tracking Policy
DAS Policy 000-45, The Chameleon Memo Window
DAS Policy 000-66, Completion of the Chameleon Receipt Window

appendices: Appendix A: OCR sample (Attachment A, attached separately)
Appendix B: Chameleon receipt screenshot (Attachment B, attached separately)
Appendix C: Daily Deposit Batch Slip (Attachment C, attached separately)
Appendix D: Chameleon Generated Daily Deposit Batch Control Slip (Attachment D, attached separately)

documents.title: How to Process Overpayments in Chameleon
documents.purpose: To provide consistent processes for the handling and documenting of overpayments in Chameleon and to minimize refund costs for the department.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Scope is not stated in the source document. The procedure applies to staff who process payments and overpayments in Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to Process Overpayments in Chameleon, effective November 23, 2016 (revised from September 14, 2015)
references: DAS Administrative Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Procedures
DAS SOP Animal Adoptions
DAS SOP Renewing Licenses
DAS SOP Animal Redemptions

sections.heading: A. Requirements
steps: The employee who processes a credit or overpayment cannot process a payment plan transaction.
Chameleon receipt preferences must show that NO NEGATIVE BALANCES is not checked.

steps.step: Because the receipt window attaches a negative balance (credit) to the Person ID only, Chameleon applies a credit of the entire amount to all the animals in that receipt. Therefore, the user can apply the overpayment (credit) with the following options:
sub: Apply the overpayment to one Animal ID with a second regular receipt for the other Animal IDs, or
Apply the overpayment to each Animal ID.

sections.heading: B. Overpayment in Licensing (Tag/Link Window)
steps: Check all past and present license records for all pets (ACTIVE) to apply late fees.
If the owner does not owe any late fee, verify that compliance with all licensing requirements is provided.
If a licensing requirement is missing, a CREDIT will not apply. Instead, the overpayment may be refunded or retained as a partial payment for a late fee. This requires a stored memo.
If all license requirements meet compliance, add the overpayment to the tag price.
The tag price must display the license fee, plus late fee (if applicable), plus overpayment total.
Tag status must show CURRENT.
Tag problem code must display CREDIT.
When the next renewal notice is sent, the customer will be billed for the total amount minus the credit amount due.
The credit amount displayed on the notice is based on the negative (-) balance in the receipt window.

sections.heading: C. Receipt
steps: Once the Tag/Link window is stored, pop up (F4) the receipt window.
The receipt window will show the line item from the Tag/Link window debt.
Double-click on the license line item code with the overpayment.
Press the $ icon to enter the correct price for the license line item code.
Enter the total amount received or paid from the customer in the CHECK amount field.
Enter 0.00 in the CHANGE field.
Store (F9) the receipt.
Using a LIC CREDIT template memo, complete the blanks to store a memo.
Print the receipt for deposit.

sections.heading: D. Applying a Credit
steps: An overpayment can be applied to any transaction in Chameleon, such as adoption, RTO, or licensing a pet.
Perform a debt lookup prior to generating a receipt. This will verify if a credit has been applied to a different transaction.

steps.step: Applying a credit to a license:
sub: Bring up the record of the license the credit is for. Refer to the license notice with the credit amount.
Renew the license according to SOP Renewing Pet Licenses.
In the tag price, enter the license fee and late fee if applicable.
Store the record.
Pop up the receipt window.
A balance for the credit will show beginning with a negative amount.
A positive line item for the license will offset the balance from the credit.
The total due is 0.00.

steps.step: Applying a credit to an adoption:
sub: Follow procedures in SOP Adoptions.
Once the kennel window is updated with the outcome type ADOPTION, pop up the receipt window.
Perform a debt lookup.
A balance with a negative amount is displayed.
A positive line item of an adoption should also be displayed.
The remaining amount is what the adopter owes.

steps.step: Applying a credit to an RTO:
sub: Follow procedures in SOP Animal Redemptions.
Once the kennel window is updated with the outcome type RTO, pop up the receipt window.
Perform a debt lookup.
A balance with a negative amount is displayed.
A positive line item of an adoption should also be displayed.
The remaining amount is what the pet owner owes.

documents.title: Payment Plan Procedure
documents.purpose: To define a consistent method of initiating and tracking payment plans.
documents.authority: Payment plans require approval from the immediate supervisor or designee. Deviations from the minimum down payment and storing license fees or late fees on a payment plan require supervisor approval or above.
documents.scope: Scope is not stated in the source document. The procedure involves front counter staff, supervisors and leads, deposit clerks, and billing and collections staff.
documents.supersedes: SOP Payment Plan Procedure, effective May 10, 2016 (revised from February 4, 2016; February 19, 2015; May 26, 2011; and August 1, 2009)
references: DAS Policy 000-10, Payment Plans and Collection of Fees
DAS Policy 000-38
SOP Uploading Pictures to Chameleon
SOP Administrative Citation Transactions
RCDAS Policy 000-37 Payment Plans and Collection of Unpaid Fees (governing policy)

sections.heading: Policy Restrictions (from Policy 000-37)
steps: Do not initiate payment plans in the months of April, May, or June. (Restriction carried from Policy 000-37; the reason is not stated in the policy and should be confirmed and documented during review.)

sections.heading: A. Initiating a Payment Plan (When All Efforts of Collection Have Been Exhausted): Front Counter Staff
steps: Obtain approval from the immediate supervisor or designee. Once the payment plan is approved, no fees will be adjusted.

Provide the approver a list of each item code and amount owed by the pet owner. Advise the customer of the payment plan fee of $19.00 per year. This fee shall be charged annually until the balance with RCDAS is paid off.

steps.step: Inform the pet owner of the following payment plan requirements:
sub: Verifiable physical address (visit GIS or www.usps.com).
Valid identification, currently entered in the PID Identifier1 field.

If the verified owner does not have a valid identification or resides outside California, the owner needs a co-signer with one of the following: current California ID or driver's license, current (not expired) passport, Social Security number, or military ID.
The co-signer's identification information must be entered in the receipt Extra1 field.
The co-signer's full name must be entered in the receipt Received From field.
The co-signer must have a verifiable physical address within Riverside County through GIS or Thomas Guide.
Both owner and co-signer must sign the receipt.

steps: Collect a minimum payment on all payment plans. Seek supervisor approval or above to deviate from the minimum down payment. You must also get supervisor approval to store license fees or late fees on a payment plan.
The minimum payment is required to meet mandated fees such as: state fines, license fees, rabies vaccination, payment plan fee (must be collected), and Mchip (depending on applicable jurisdiction).

steps.step: Store a payment plan receipt:
sub: From the kennel window, press the F4 key with the cursor on the receipt number field.

For outcome type RTO, unspayed/non-neutered/unlicensed (county or city contract), receipt items include: IMP 1, 2, or 3; Unalt fee - collection; State Fine 1, 2, or 3; License 1, 2, or 3; board fees - collection; payment plan fee - collection; all veterinary care fees including vaccinations - collection; Mchip (if applicable to the jurisdiction) - collection; S/N fee (if applicable to the jurisdiction) or intent to comply - collection.

For outcome type RTO, spayed/neutered/unlicensed (county or city contract), receipt items include: IMP 1, 2, or 3 - collection; License 1, 2, or 3; payment plan fee - collection; board fees - collection; all veterinary care fees including vaccinations - collection.

Depending on the mode of payment received from the customer, enter actual monies paid in the applicable fields of the receipt window (cash, check, or credit card). Include a 3% processing fee for credit cards.

If the pet owner is paying by check over the counter, the check received should have the payer's name, address, and telephone number imprinted. The person accepting the check should write payer identification such as driver's license number on the check. All of this information provides documentation that may be needed for subsequent collection efforts.
The total due shows the amount owed by the customer.
Amount paid shows the actual monies received from the customer.
Total balance will show the remaining balance owed by the customer.
If there is a license payment owed, use the line item license collections. Refer to SOP Administrative Citation Transactions on how to initiate a receipt with licensing in collections.
Refer to DAS Policy 000-38 to put the license in collection status.
City of Riverside license fees will not go on a payment plan.
Store (F9) the receipt. Chameleon will display a message that there is a non-zero balance. Press OK.

Print two receipts. The customer must sign the copy that goes with the deposit. Distribute one copy to the customer and one copy to accounting/deposit. Scan signed copies to the PID through Chamcam as a document (refer to SOP Uploading Pictures to Chameleon).
Review and discuss the terms and conditions of the payment plan with the customer.
The customer is provided 30 days to pay a portion or all of the debts owed.

steps.step: Create a Chameleon memo:
sub: Memo ID: the receipt number.
Memo type: Accounting.
Choose PAYMENT PLAN ORIG in the template field.

Memo text must include: the memo date, the name of the approver, the item codes and how the initial payment will be applied for each item code, a note that monies received will be distributed to meet mandated fees first regardless of city or county transaction, and the author of the memo.

sections.heading: B. Initiating a Payment Plan: Supervisor or Lead
steps: Review the signed receipt for discrepancy of charges owed.
Scan the signed copy to Chameleon.
Verify the memo matches the receipt.

sections.heading: C. Initiating a Payment Plan: Deposit Clerk
steps: Review the Chameleon accounting memo for distribution of the initial payment to Chameleon item codes.
Distribute monies received to meet mandated fees first, regardless of city or county transaction.
Apply the payment to the deposit report.

sections.heading: D. Initiating a Payment Plan: Billing and Collections (Monthly)
steps: Run the Payment Billing Invoice report from Chameleon for the previous month's payment plans and save it as a PDF on the Q drive.
Print the invoices and mail the customer a copy of the pre-intercept notice.

Run the >120 Days Balance Report. This report will show payment plans that are delinquent for more than 120 days. If there is a bill created that is not to be sent to the FTB, it will appear on this list highlighted in yellow.
Review the report for holds and multiple balances.

sections.heading: E. Acceptance of Payments for Balances Owed: Front Counter Staff
steps: You must be in the receipt window.
You must use the same Person ID used when the payment plan was initiated.
From the menu bar of the receipt window, choose PROCEDURES.
From the drop-down list, choose DEBT LOOKUP.
Chameleon will automatically display the item code BALANCE.

steps.step: If there is more than one BALANCE, select the correct one by referring to the reference number on the balance line item:
sub: Do not combine or add to the balance line item.
Do not JE a balance payment.

steps: Enter the amount paid by the customer in the applicable field based on type of payment.
If the customer is not paying all debts, the balance amount will show. If there are license fees or late fees on a payment plan, they need to be paid first.
Store the receipt to generate the second 30-day contract.
Print and have the customer sign all receipts.
The payment due date is 30 days from the new receipt date.
Create a memo to define how much was paid, the original receipt number of the payment plan, and how the amount is distributed for the remaining item codes owed. Memos must be entered by end of shift.

sections.heading: F. Acceptance of Payments for Balances Owed: Deposit Clerk
steps: Review the Chameleon accounting memo for distribution of the payment to Chameleon item codes.
Apply the payment to the deposit report.

sections.heading: G. Acceptance of Payments for Balances Owed: Supervisor or Lead
steps: Review the signed receipt for discrepancy of charges owed.
Ensure all memos are entered in Chameleon accurately and by the end of shift.
Run and review the Daily Balance Audit report from Chameleon.
Billing and collections repeat the monthly reporting process described in Section D.

related.name: DAS Policy 000-10, Payment Plans and Collection of Fees

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Integrated missing provision from Policy 000-37 (April-June payment plan restriction) per staff merge-review decision. Policy and SOP remain separate documents.

documents.title: Public Records Request Procedure
documents.purpose: To provide consistent processing of records requested by the public, which will ensure timely response or notification from the Department of Animal Services (DAS) to the public.
documents.authority: The department's Custodian of Records processes and responds to public records requests. Extensions require notice from the agency head or their designee. Invoiced record folders are submitted to the department's Custodian of Records for review and approval.
documents.scope: All DAS officers and employees who receive public records requests; the Custodian of Records and designated backup staff who process them.
documents.supersedes: SOP Public Records Request Procedure, effective February 10, 2015 (revised from July 9, 2014; December 11, 2013; March 29, 2010; and June 1, 2009)
references: Government Code section 6253 (10-day response and extension; cited as Stats. 2001, ch. 982)
Government Code section 6254 (exemptions)
Government Code section 6255 (denials; cited as Stats. 2000, ch. 982)
Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 5-2 (restricted personal data)
Health and Safety Code section 121690(h) (confidentiality of dog owner and veterinarian information)
Government Code sections 26205, 26205.1, 12168.7 and 2 CCR 22620.1-22620.8 (records retention, per related SOP)
DAS Policy, General Chameleon Information

definitions.def: The department employee designated to receive, process, and respond to public records requests. Day one of the response period is the day following the custodian's receipt of the request.

sections.heading: A. Forms of Requests Received
steps: Accept requests received on the official form.
Accept requests received other than on the official form, such as verbal, email, fax, or front counter requests.

sections.heading: B. When a Non-Custodian Officer or Employee Receives a Request
steps: Forward the request to the department's Custodian of Records via email, fax, or in person, but you MUST inform the requestor that day one (1) is the day following the custodian's receipt of the request, not the date the non-custodian officer or employee receives the request; or

Direct the requestor to the department's Custodian of Records. In other words, the employee may refuse to accept the request and have the customer personally file the request with the Custodian of Records.

sections.heading: C. Information That Cannot Be Released to the Public

steps.step: Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 5-2 restricts the release (either e-filing or manual filing) of certain personal data pertaining to:
sub: Last 4 digits of a Social Security or taxpayer ID number.
Year of an individual's birth (not month or day).
Minor's initials (minors involved in a bite or scratch).
Last 4 digits of a financial account number (credit card information).
Home addresses. In criminal cases, if a home address must be included, use the city and state only.

steps.step: Redact other types of information as appropriate, including:
sub: Medical information.
Trade secrets.
Informant names (who called the ACO or who turned in the animal).
Sensitive security information.
Other forms of unique identifiers, such as driver's license or passport ID.

steps: Ensure that the documents released are correctly redacted using effective and state, federal, and Board approved redaction practices, and are completely free of any hidden data. For the department's protection, do not release detailed information without going through the records request procedures.

Note that although the statute itself does not state that addresses, license data, or breeds are exempt, pursuant to Health and Safety Code 121690(h), the information obtained from a dog owner or a veterinarian is deemed confidential to the dog owner and proprietary to the veterinarian. This information cannot be used, distributed, or released for any purpose, except to ensure compliance with existing federal, state, county, or city laws or regulations.

Taking a print screen of any Chameleon window and releasing the printed copy to the public is a direct violation of the contract with the Chameleon owner, HLP, Inc., and is also a violation of the proprietary agreement with HLP, Inc.

sections.heading: D. Review of Request Information (Custodian of Records)
steps: Look over the request to determine what is being requested. Requests of reporting parties are never disclosed unless subpoenaed by the court.
Pull the requested record number (Activity No., Bite No., etc.) or address in Chameleon.
Confirm that a record exists.
Review the record for any results of impounds, citations, quarantines (bites), PDR (restraint orders), barking dog complaints, or memos regarding hearings.

Create a new folder in the Records Request folder on the shared drive (Q:\Vet_Services\RECORDS REQUEST). Examples of file names are: last name of requestor, underscore, first name (smith_john), or name of business, underscore, client full name (lawofficename_johnsmith).

sections.heading: E. Ten (10) Day Response (Chameleon Contact Window)
steps: Within 10 days from receipt of a public records request, provide verbal or written notice to the person making the request of the disclosure determination and the reasons for the determination (Gov. Code section 6253(c)).
Log into Chameleon. In the Menu tab, choose Person. From the Person menu, choose Contact.
In the Person ID field, press F4 to bring up the person window. Refer to the General Chameleon Information policy for searching records prior to storing the record.

If the requestor is a law office, enter the entire name of the law office in the last name field when creating a new person record, then enter the contact name in the first name field. Make sure to obtain an email address.
If the person record exists, press the Enter key to bring the information to the contact window.
In the contact type, choose MAIL, FACSIMILE, PHONE, FRONT COUNTER, or OTHER.
In the contact subtype, choose RECORDS RQ.
In the Contact Date field, enter the date of the request. If the time of the request is known, enter the time in the Contact Time field.
In the Status field, choose 10-DAY RESP.
In the SubStatus field, enter the Activity No., Bite No., Citation No., Person ID, Animal ID, Impound No., or court case No.
In the comment field, enter: the date the custodian received the request, what records are being requested, and the action taken, with user initials.
Store (F9) the contact window.
From the drop-down reports menu, choose PRA 10 Day Response.
Once the letter is displayed, print two (2) copies, then export the letter to PDF format.
Save the PDF letter as 10-day to the folder of the requestor.
File the department copy with the request.

sections.heading: F. Extension Response
steps: If the 10 day time limit to notify the person making a records request is extended due to unusual circumstances, the agency head or their designee shall provide written notice to the person making the request, setting forth the reasons for the extension and the date on which a determination is expected to be dispatched (Gov. Code section 6253(c)).
The extension is not based on pulling the data; it is based on writing the letter that additional time is needed to gather the data.
An existing 10-DAY RESP contact window is required.
In the contact window, perform a search on the requestor's PID.
Verify that the contact window displayed is the correct request based on the SubStatus field.
Delete the number from the Contact No field.
Tab to the date field and press F5 to enter the current date. Tab to the time field and press F5 to enter the current time.
In the Status field, press F4 to bring up a list and choose EXT RESP.
In the comment field, enter the action taken.
Store (F9) the record.
From the reports drop-down menu, choose PRA Extension Response and answer the report questions.
Once the letter is displayed on the screen, click on the export button located at the top middle of the report to activate Adobe Pro.

In Adobe Pro, click on Tools, then Forms, then the Check Box tool. Draw the checkbox next to the appropriate items listed, then click on the Select tool and click on the checkbox to place a check mark.
Save the PDF letter as extension letter to the folder of the requestor.
Print two (2) copies of the letter, one for the requestor and one for department files.
File the letter with the request.

sections.heading: G. Denials
steps: If a request is denied, in whole or in part, respond in writing to a written request for inspection or copies of public records with a determination that the request is denied (Gov. Code section 6255(b)).
The denial is not based on the time to make the determination as to whether the data can be released. It is only based on notifying the requestor in writing stating why the request is being denied.
Create a contact window following the same person search and law office steps as the 10-day response (Section E).
In the contact type, choose MAIL, FACSIMILE, PHONE, FRONT COUNTER, or OTHER.
In the contact subtype, choose RECORDS RQ.
In the Contact Date field, enter the date of the request, and the time if known.
In the Status field, choose DENIAL.
In the SubStatus field, enter the Activity No., Bite No., Citation No., Person ID, Animal ID, Impound No., or court case No.
In the comment field, enter the date the custodian received the request, what records are being requested, and the action taken.
Store (F9) the contact window.
From the drop-down reports menu, choose PRA Denial Response and answer the report questions.
Once the letter is displayed on the screen, click on the export button located at the top middle of the report to activate Adobe Pro.
Save the PDF letter as denial letter to the folder of the requestor.
Print two (2) copies of the letter, one for the requestor and one for department files.
File the letter with the request.

sections.heading: H. Completed Requests
steps: All disclosable records need to be in Chameleon.
A contact window must already exist for the request.

Create a new contact with the only difference of COMPLETED in the contact status and a different contact comment, for example: mailed cover letter and invoice, placed copies in the shared drive Public Records\Pending Payments folder under the requestor's name, with user initials.

If the results in the activity window contain RPRT, DR, PDR, AAI, or CITE, state in the comment that the responsible desk (rabies desk, citation clerk, or hearing clerk) was emailed to scan the bite report, PDR, DR, or citation and attach the report to Chameleon by the due date, and initiate a letter of extension if needed.
Enter your first initial and full last name.
Store (F9) the record.

steps.step: Notify by email the people responsible for maintaining those records to scan and attach them to Chameleon:
sub: Riverside and San Jacinto: citations - citation clerk at Western; hearings and PDRs - hearing clerk at Western; noisy animals - hearing clerk at Western; bite reports - rabies desk clerk at Western.
CVAC and Blythe: citations - citation clerk at CVAC; all other records - designated records clerk.

sections.heading: J. Compiling Reports for Records Requests (Custodian of Records or Backup Only)

steps.step: Based on the reference number (Activity No., Bite No., etc.), run the following types of reports to determine if documents were attached to Chameleon:
sub: PRA Activity Record: requires the activity street name and activity street number.
PRA Quarantine Record: requires the Bite number.
PRA Citations Disposition: requires the defendant's PID (person cited).
PRA Impound Record: requires the Animal ID.
PRA License Record: requires the owner's PID.

steps: If attachments are in Chameleon, export the report into a PDF file and name the file with the requestor's full name, for example doe_jane.
Create a folder based on the requestor's name in the ANMLSHLT_ shared drive\Vet Svcs\Records Request folder. For example, ANMLSHLT_ shared drive\Vet Svcs\Records Request\doe_jane.
Save the compiled records to the folder.
Print the records and invoice (refer to the invoicing section).
Combine the cover letter, invoice, and records.
Perform a redaction activity using Adobe Pro. Save the file as doe_jane_final.
Save the final copy to the ANMLSHLT_ shared drive\Public Records\Pending Payments\doe_jane folder. This includes creating a folder under the Pending Payments folder.

sections.heading: K. Attaching Documents or Pictures to Chameleon (Custodian of Records or Designated Staff Only)
steps: If records are not attached in Chameleon, check email to verify if staff responded by attaching files to the email.
If in the email, the file format sent should be a JPEG file.
Save the file according to the activity number, etc., in the shared directory.

steps.step: Attach the file to Chameleon:
sub: If the attached file is a citation, go to the Citation window. If the attached file pertains to PDRs, hearings, or investigation pictures, go to the Activity window. If the attached file pertains to bite reports, go to the Bite window.
Bring up the record.
Place the cursor on the violation, bite, or activity number.
From the menu bar, choose Extras. From the drop-down menu, choose Chamcam.
The applicable number should match the Image ID in Chamcam.
Place a check mark in the box labeled Document.
Click on the Browse Files icon.
Click on the down arrow to point to the right folder where the picture was saved.
Choose the applicable picture. One only. Press OK.
Click on the STORE button located near the Image ID.
Once Chameleon stores the record, click OK. Repeat these steps if attaching multiple pictures.
NEVER store a document to an Animal ID.
Press the EXIT button once done.

sections.heading: L. Invoicing Records Requests
steps: Generate an invoice using the Request for Records Invoice form. Enter the full name of the requestor, the full street address, the city, state, and zip code, and the number of pages. Under description, refer to the Activity No., Bite No., etc.
Print two (2) copies.
Bring up the contact window record of the requestor with a status of COMPLETED.
In the comment field, enter the date, time, action performed (for example, mailed invoice to the requestor), and your name.
Update (F8) the window.
Chameleon will ask if you wish to create another follow-up record. Answer NO.

Create a manila folder file for the requestor labeled in the format Last Name, First Name. If the requestor is a business, label the folder with the full business name, then in parentheses the client represented (last name, first name).
The folder should include the official request form, invoices, and printed reports.
Submit the folder to the department's Custodian of Records for review and approval.
Once approval is received, mail one copy of the invoice to the customer and retain one in the file.

sections.heading: M. Receipt of Payment for Records Requests
steps: Payments can be received by front counter staff, who will notify the custodian via facsimile, email, or interoffice mail of payment received. Payments can also be received directly.
To comply with segregation of duties, under no circumstances shall the reviewer of records be the cashier. These payments must be processed by staff other than the reviewer.

sections.heading: N. Following Up Pending Requests
steps: On a daily basis, check Chameleon for follow-up requests.
Log into Chameleon. In the Menu tab, choose Person. From the Person menu, choose Contact.
In the Contact Date field, enter today's date.
Find (F12) any contact that has a contact subtype of RECORDS RQ and any contact status except COMPLETED, or run the Contact Tracking Report from the Chameleon reports drop-down menu.

related.name: Crystal Reports: PRA 10 Day Response, PRA Extension Response, PRA Denial Response, PRA Disclosing Response

appendices: Appendix A: Sample PRA response letters from the source document (10-day response, extension response, denial letter, disclosing letter; attached separately)

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2015 version except staff names replaced with role titles; substantive edits pending (see edit log).
