b. when a non-custodian officer or employee receives a request forward the request to the department's custodian of records via email, fax, or in person, but you must inform the requestor that day one (1) is the day following the custodian's receipt of the request, not the date the non-custodian officer or employee receives the request; or direct the requestor to the department's custodian of records. in other words, the employee may refuse to accept the request and have the customer personally file the request with the custodian of records. c. information that cannot be released to the public {"step": "federal rule of civil procedure 5-2 restricts the release (either e-filing or manual filing) of certain personal data pertaining to:", "sub": ["last 4 digits of a social security or taxpayer id number.", "year of an individual's birth (not month or day).", "minor's initials (minors involved in a bite or scratch).", "last 4 digits of a financial account number (credit card information).", "home addresses. in criminal cases, if a home address must be included, use the city and state only."]} {"step": "redact other types of information as appropriate, including:", "sub": ["medical information.", "trade secrets.", "informant names (who called the aco or who turned in the animal).", "sensitive security information.", "other forms of unique identifiers, such as driver's license or passport id."]} ensure that the documents released are correctly redacted using effective and state, federal, and board approved redaction practices, and are completely free of any hidden data. for the department's protection, do not release detailed information without going through the records request procedures. note that although the statute itself does not state that addresses, license data, or breeds are exempt, pursuant to health and safety code 121690(h), the information obtained from a dog owner or a veterinarian is deemed confidential to the dog owner and proprietary to the veterinarian. this information cannot be used, distributed, or released for any purpose, except to ensure compliance with existing federal, state, county, or city laws or regulations. taking a print screen of any chameleon window and releasing the printed copy to the public is a direct violation of the contract with the chameleon owner, hlp, inc., and is also a violation of the proprietary agreement with hlp, inc. d. review of request information (custodian of records) look over the request to determine what is being requested. requests of reporting parties are never disclosed unless subpoenaed by the court. pull the requested record number (activity no., bite no., etc.) or address in chameleon. confirm that a record exists. review the record for any results of impounds, citations, quarantines (bites), pdr (restraint orders), barking dog complaints, or memos regarding hearings. create a new folder in the records request folder on the shared drive (q:\vet_services\records request). examples of file names are: last name of requestor, underscore, first name (smith_john), or name of business, underscore, client full name (lawofficename_johnsmith). e. ten (10) day response (chameleon contact window) within 10 days from receipt of a public records request, provide verbal or written notice to the person making the request of the disclosure determination and the reasons for the determination (gov. code section 6253(c)). log into chameleon. in the menu tab, choose person. from the person menu, choose contact. in the person id field, press f4 to bring up the person window. refer to the general chameleon information policy for searching records prior to storing the record. if the requestor is a law office, enter the entire name of the law office in the last name field when creating a new person record, then enter the contact name in the first name field. make sure to obtain an email address. if the person record exists, press the enter key to bring the information to the contact window. in the contact type, choose mail, facsimile, phone, front counter, or other. in the contact subtype, choose records rq. in the contact date field, enter the date of the request. if the time of the request is known, enter the time in the contact time field. in the status field, choose 10-day resp. in the substatus field, enter the activity no., bite no., citation no., person id, animal id, impound no., or court case no. in the comment field, enter: the date the custodian received the request, what records are being requested, and the action taken, with user initials. store (f9) the contact window. from the drop-down reports menu, choose pra 10 day response. once the letter is displayed, print two (2) copies, then export the letter to pdf format. save the pdf letter as 10-day to the folder of the requestor. file the department copy with the request. f. extension response if the 10 day time limit to notify the person making a records request is extended due to unusual circumstances, the agency head or their designee shall provide written notice to the person making the request, setting forth the reasons for the extension and the date on which a determination is expected to be dispatched (gov. code section 6253(c)). the extension is not based on pulling the data; it is based on writing the letter that additional time is needed to gather the data. an existing 10-day resp contact window is required. in the contact window, perform a search on the requestor's pid. verify that the contact window displayed is the correct request based on the substatus field. delete the number from the contact no field. tab to the date field and press f5 to enter the current date. tab to the time field and press f5 to enter the current time. in the status field, press f4 to bring up a list and choose ext resp. in the comment field, enter the action taken. store (f9) the record. from the reports drop-down menu, choose pra extension response and answer the report questions. once the letter is displayed on the screen, click on the export button located at the top middle of the report to activate adobe pro. in adobe pro, click on tools, then forms, then the check box tool. draw the checkbox next to the appropriate items listed, then click on the select tool and click on the checkbox to place a check mark. save the pdf letter as extension letter to the folder of the requestor. print two (2) copies of the letter, one for the requestor and one for department files. file the letter with the request. g. denials if a request is denied, in whole or in part, respond in writing to a written request for inspection or copies of public records with a determination that the request is denied (gov. code section 6255(b)). the denial is not based on the time to make the determination as to whether the data can be released. it is only based on notifying the requestor in writing stating why the request is being denied. create a contact window following the same person search and law office steps as the 10-day response (section e). in the contact type, choose mail, facsimile, phone, front counter, or other. in the contact subtype, choose records rq. in the contact date field, enter the date of the request, and the time if known. in the status field, choose denial. in the substatus field, enter the activity no., bite no., citation no., person id, animal id, impound no., or court case no. in the comment field, enter the date the custodian received the request, what records are being requested, and the action taken. store (f9) the contact window. from the drop-down reports menu, choose pra denial response and answer the report questions. once the letter is displayed on the screen, click on the export button located at the top middle of the report to activate adobe pro. save the pdf letter as denial letter to the folder of the requestor. print two (2) copies of the letter, one for the requestor and one for department files. file the letter with the request. h. completed requests all disclosable records need to be in chameleon. a contact window must already exist for the request. create a new contact with the only difference of completed in the contact status and a different contact comment, for example: mailed cover letter and invoice, placed copies in the shared drive public records\pending payments folder under the requestor's name, with user initials. if the results in the activity window contain rprt, dr, pdr, aai, or cite, state in the comment that the responsible desk (rabies desk, citation clerk, or hearing clerk) was emailed to scan the bite report, pdr, dr, or citation and attach the report to chameleon by the due date, and initiate a letter of extension if needed. enter your first initial and full last name. store (f9) the record. i. notifying records maintainers {"step": "notify by email the people responsible for maintaining those records to scan and attach them to chameleon:", "sub": ["riverside and san jacinto: citations - citation clerk at western; hearings and pdrs - hearing clerk at western; noisy animals - hearing clerk at western; bite reports - rabies desk clerk at western.", "cvac and blythe: citations - citation clerk at cvac; all other records - designated records clerk."]} j. compiling reports for records requests (custodian of records or backup only) {"step": "based on the reference number (activity no., bite no., etc.), run the following types of reports to determine if documents were attached to chameleon:", "sub": ["pra activity record: requires the activity street name and activity street number.", "pra quarantine record: requires the bite number.", "pra citations disposition: requires the defendant's pid (person cited).", "pra impound record: requires the animal id.", "pra license record: requires the owner's pid."]} if attachments are in chameleon, export the report into a pdf file and name the file with the requestor's full name, for example doe_jane. create a folder based on the requestor's name in the anmlshlt_ shared drive\vet svcs\records request folder. for example, anmlshlt_ shared drive\vet svcs\records request\doe_jane. save the compiled records to the folder. print the records and invoice (refer to the invoicing section). combine the cover letter, invoice, and records. perform a redaction activity using adobe pro. save the file as doe_jane_final. save the final copy to the anmlshlt_ shared drive\public records\pending payments\doe_jane folder. this includes creating a folder under the pending payments folder. k. attaching documents or pictures to chameleon (custodian of records or designated staff only) if records are not attached in chameleon, check email to verify if staff responded by attaching files to the email. if in the email, the file format sent should be a jpeg file. save the file according to the activity number, etc., in the shared directory. {"step": "attach the file to chameleon:", "sub": ["if the attached file is a citation, go to the citation window. if the attached file pertains to pdrs, hearings, or investigation pictures, go to the activity window. if the attached file pertains to bite reports, go to the bite window.", "bring up the record.", "place the cursor on the violation, bite, or activity number.", "from the menu bar, choose extras. from the drop-down menu, choose chamcam.", "the applicable number should match the image id in chamcam.", "place a check mark in the box labeled document.", "click on the browse files icon.", "click on the down arrow to point to the right folder where the picture was saved.", "choose the applicable picture. one only. press ok.", "click on the store button located near the image id.", "once chameleon stores the record, click ok. repeat these steps if attaching multiple pictures.", "never store a document to an animal id.", "press the exit button once done."]} l. invoicing records requests generate an invoice using the request for records invoice form. enter the full name of the requestor, the full street address, the city, state, and zip code, and the number of pages. under description, refer to the activity no., bite no., etc. print two (2) copies. bring up the contact window record of the requestor with a status of completed. in the comment field, enter the date, time, action performed (for example, mailed invoice to the requestor), and your name. update (f8) the window. chameleon will ask if you wish to create another follow-up record. answer no. create a manila folder file for the requestor labeled in the format last name, first name. if the requestor is a business, label the folder with the full business name, then in parentheses the client represented (last name, first name). the folder should include the official request form, invoices, and printed reports. submit the folder to the department's custodian of records for review and approval. once approval is received, mail one copy of the invoice to the customer and retain one in the file. m. receipt of payment for records requests payments can be received by front counter staff, who will notify the custodian via facsimile, email, or interoffice mail of payment received. payments can also be received directly. to comply with segregation of duties, under no circumstances shall the reviewer of records be the cashier. these payments must be processed by staff other than the reviewer. n. following up pending requests on a daily basis, check chameleon for follow-up requests. log into chameleon. in the menu tab, choose person. from the person menu, choose contact. in the contact date field, enter today's date. find (f12) any contact that has a contact subtype of records rq and any contact status except completed, or run the contact tracking report from the chameleon reports drop-down menu. government code section 6253 (10-day response and extension; cited as stats. 2001, ch. 982) government code section 6254 (exemptions) government code section 6255 (denials; cited as stats. 2000, ch. 982) federal rule of civil procedure 5-2 (restricted personal data) health and safety code section 121690(h) (confidentiality of dog owner and veterinarian information) government code sections 26205, 26205.1, 12168.7 and 2 ccr 22620.1-22620.8 (records retention, per related sop) das policy, general chameleon information

documents.title: Uploading Pictures or Documents to Chameleon
documents.purpose: Purpose statement was on a page of the source PDF that could not be extracted. The extracted portion addresses maintaining electronically originated documents as the official records of the County for the records' applicable retention period, and standards for attaching pictures and documents in Chameleon through Chamcam.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the extracted portion of the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Scope statement was on a page of the source PDF that could not be extracted. The procedure applies to staff who attach pictures or documents to records in Chameleon, including authorized ChamMailbox users.
documents.supersedes: SOP Uploading Pictures or Documents to Chameleon (source header: SOP Uploading pictures to chameleon_rev20210602.docx), revised June 2, 2021
references: Government Code sections 26205 and 26205.1 (Board of Supervisors authorization to destroy official records electronically recorded on a trusted system)
Government Code section 12168.7 and 2 CCR 22620.1-22620.8 (trusted system standards)

definitions.def: The Chameleon image module used to attach pictures and documents to Chameleon records. Source cites Chamcam version 7.0.2 (previous version 6.8.5) in Chameleon version 46h.

sections.heading: A. Records Retention Basis
steps: Maintain electronically originated documents as the official records of the County for the records' applicable retention period.

Government Code sections 26205 and 26205.1 allow the Board of Supervisors, at the request of a County officer, to authorize the destruction of any official record that is not expressly required to be filed and preserved if the official record is electronically recorded on a trusted system that does not permit additions, deletions, or changes to the original record images, is produced in compliance with Government Code section 12168.7 and 2 CCR 22620.1-22620.8, accurately reproduces the original record, and is conveniently accessible.

sections.heading: B. Attaching a Picture to an Animal ID
steps: Attach only the animal picture to the Animal ID. No exceptions.
To ensure that Chameleon automated jobs are not affected, keep image size to less than 1 MB, in other words, at 640 x 480.

steps.step: Load a picture of the animal from one of three sources:
sub: Load Image from File (camera, file folder).
Webcam.
ChamMailbox (used by authorized users).

steps: DO NOT attach any document to the Animal ID.
Pictures attached to the Animal ID are posted to the lost and found website in 30 minutes.

steps.step: To attach the picture:
sub: Start in the Chameleon kennel window.
Press function key F11 to clear the screen.
Place the cursor in the Animal ID field.
Press function key F12 to find the current impound record of the animal.
Place the cursor on the Animal ID field, press function key F4 to bring up the animal record window, then click on the paperclip icon.

sections.heading: C. Remaining Sections (Not Captured in Migration)
steps: [PLACEHOLDER] Pages 3 through 39 of the June 2021 source PDF could not be extracted during migration. The remaining procedures, including completing the picture attachment steps, attaching documents to other Chameleon windows (Person ID, citation, bite, and activity records), webcam and ChamMailbox loading steps, and all screenshots, remain in the source PDF and must be migrated from the original file in the substantive edit pass.

related.name: SOP Public Records Request Procedure (attaching documents for records requests)

revisions.desc: Partial migration into standard RCDAS SOP template. Text extraction captured only pages 1-2 of the 39-page source PDF; content unchanged from 2021 version for the captured portion. Full re-migration from the source file required (see edit log).

documents.title: Voiding a Chameleon Receipt
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure for voiding a transaction using Chameleon's journal entry feature.
documents.authority: Approval authority is not stated in the source document. RCDAS standard operating procedures are approved by the Director of Animal Services.
documents.scope: Responsible staff members who process voided transactions in Chameleon. The employee who stored the original receipt must be logged into Chameleon in order to journal entry the receipt.
documents.supersedes: SOP Voiding a Chameleon Receipt, effective May 27, 2009

definitions.def: The process in Chameleon for voiding line items stored in a receipt.

sections.heading: A. General Requirements
steps: Process all voided transactions in Chameleon using the Journal Entry function. The responsible staff member performs the journal entry.
Initiate a memo attached to the receipt number for every journal entry receipt.
The employee who stored the original receipt must be logged into Chameleon in order to journal entry the receipt.

sections.heading: B. Journal Entry a Receipt
steps: Go to the receipt window, enter the receipt number (for example, R00-123789) that is to be journaled out, and press F12 to pull up the receipt.
Verify that the receipt submitted for a journal entry has not been voided previously from the log of receipts located on the top right corner of the receipt window.
A previously journaled amount will appear with a negative (-) in front of it, for example -65.00, -25.00, or -6.00.
If the receipt has not been previously journaled out, go to Procedures and select Journal Entry. Chameleon automatically journal entries the receipt.
Chameleon will ask if the user is positively sure the record is to be voided out. Select OK.
Chameleon will then confirm the receipt has been journal entried.
CLEAR or exit the receipt window if a new receipt is to be created.
If the receipt window is not cleared after a journal entry, the new receipt will be stored and there will be no corresponding journal entry to the original receipt being voided out.

sections.heading: C. Creating the Memo
steps: From the receipt window, click on the memo icon located in the menu bar to bring up the memo window.
The Memo ID must be the same as the receipt number in the receipt window.
The type of memo must be Accounting.
In the template field, pop up (F4) to display types of memo and choose JOURNAL ENTRY. A template format in text will be displayed.

steps.step: The memo text must contain the following information:
sub: Date the memo was initiated.
Receipt number.
Reason for the journal entry.
If multiple animals are owned by the person, the Animal ID to which the voided transaction applies.
The employee's initials.

steps: STORE or press the F9 key to save the memo.

related.name: SOP How to Input Official County Receipts (OCR) into Chameleon (voiding an OCR receipt)

documents.title: Euthanasia Policy
documents.purpose: To ensure the accuracy, competency, and compassion of the euthanasia process for the Department of Animal Services (DAS). All euthanasia is accomplished in accordance with State and Federal laws and guidelines and in accordance with recommendations of the American Veterinary Medical Association (AVMA) and other national organizations. Staff must make every effort to identify animal owners before considering or performing euthanasia and must seek every possible alternative for a live outcome before committing to euthanasia.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: All Staff
documents.supersedes: Daily Euthanasia Log Policy (December 1, 2005); Euthanasia Policy revisions of December 13, 2007 and September 16, 2009; prior Euthanasia Policy effective March 30, 2016
references: California Food and Agricultural Code Sections 17005, 17006
California Civil Code Section 1834.4
California Penal Code Section 599d
California Business and Professions Code Section 4827
California Code of Regulations, Title 16, Section 2039 (Sodium Pentobarbital/Euthanasia Training)
California Food and Agricultural Code Section 31105 (impoundment and humane disposition of dogs)
DAS Laboratory Specimen Policy 315 (Preparation of Laboratory Specimens)
DAS Field Euthanasia Procedure Policy 340
DAS Temporary Spay/Neuter Waiver/Acknowledgement Form Standard Operating Procedure
DAS Euthanasia Standard Operating Procedure
DAS Policy 300-01, Owner Turn In Policy
DAS memorandum dated August 22, 2014 (owner-requested euthanasia categories)

definitions.def: An animal who has a poor or grave prognosis for being able to live without severe, unremitting physical pain even with prompt, necessary, and comprehensive veterinary care. (No Kill Advocacy Center)

Documented attempts by the department throughout an animal's stay in treating or placing an adoptable or treatable animal. These efforts include documentation of interest by Adoption Partners, attempts to modify behavior or treat medical conditions, and efforts to transfer animals into foster care or another agency. If all efforts fail, the animal may be euthanized as an adoptable or treatable animal.

Animals prohibited or disqualified for adoption by law, regulation, or policy. Dogs that have been declared Dangerous by the department, or that are subject to Dangerous Dog proceedings, are not made available for adoption. Animals that have been declared a public nuisance, or that are subject to public nuisance proceedings, should not be made available for adoption without a file review and concurrence of the director or designee. Wild or exotic animals may be transferred to an approved Adoption Partner or licensed rehabilitator with the authorization of the director or designee.

Per state requirements, any animal that is not deemed adoptable but could become adoptable with reasonable efforts (Civil Code sec. 1834.4, Food and Agricultural Code sec. 17005, and Penal Code sec. 599d). Treatable animals have a manageable behavioral defect and/or medical condition typically responsive to reasonable behavior modification or medical treatment that would enable them to become healthy, socialized pets. Treatable animals may be adopted without being fully rehabilitated; all treatable animals adopted from DAS must have a signed medical or behavior acknowledgment/release form on file.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Follow the policy of the State of California (Food and Agricultural Code 17005-17006) that no adoptable or treatable animal should be euthanized if it can be adopted into a suitable home. DAS is dedicated to reducing the euthanasia of adoptable and treatable animals.
Do not euthanize healthy, adoptable animals at an owner's request. Refer all such requests to a local veterinarian or veterinary clinic or hospital.

Perform all euthanasia in a humane and respectful manner with compassion toward the animals and in accordance with the departmental Euthanasia Standard Operating Procedure. Any breach of procedure or policy is subject to immediate corrective action, and acts constituting neglect, abuse, or wrongful euthanasia of any animal may lead up to and include termination.

Recognize that DAS animal shelters are mandated to be open-door facilities; denying admittance to certain species, breeds, or quantities of animals is not an option. The open-door mandate creates an environment that requires DAS to provide euthanasia when the animal shelter population exceeds the carrying capacity of the facility.

sections.heading: Qualifiers for Euthanasia of Adoptable Animals
steps: Confirm the animal is of a species or individual breed that is overrepresented within the current animal shelter population.
Select from that species or breed the animals with the lowest probability for adoption that have spent the greatest amount of time within the shelter.
Undertake such consideration only for animals impounded for 10 or more consecutive days, and only after all required holding periods have expired.

sections.heading: Alternatives and Decision Process
steps: Commit resources to alternatives to euthanasia, including adoption and adoption partner programs, veterinary care of injured or ill animals, pet identification programs, foster care, pet behavior training programs, and spay/neuter programs designed to reduce surplus pet problems within Riverside County.
Make euthanasia decisions through the shelter team, with the veterinary services team providing a secondary review.

Compile the list of euthanasia candidates (shelter team responsibility) and email it to the assigned Registered Veterinary Technician or Veterinary Assistant for final review of notes and the medical record.
Once approved, the Veterinary team member places the animal on the Chameleon euthanasia list. Euthanasia is a shared responsibility between trained shelter and veterinary staff.

sections.heading: Euthanasia Categories
steps: Select the most appropriate category for each euthanized impounded animal. Enter accurate data; no attempt should be made to skew data or manipulate statistics or outcome definitions.

steps.step: Use the following subcategories when an animal is euthanized:
sub: Adoptable (used only when euthanizing for space)
Treatable - Medical
Treatable - Behavior
Untreatable - Medical
Untreatable - Behavior
Too Young
Owner-requested euthanasia
Court Order
Rabies Test
Abatement

sections.heading: Category: Adoptable
steps: Apply this category to impounded animals that appear or present to staff as healthy and behaviorally sound (animals with no negative observed behavior notes in the system).

Euthanize animals in this category only when the shelter's animal population exceeds available housing capacity or the ability to provide adequate sanitation, shelter, or humane conditions, in combination with a lack of community resources to provide an alternative.
Use the Adpt-Sp subtype in Chameleon when documenting euthanasia under this category.

sections.heading: Category: Treatable - Medical
steps: Apply this category to animals with a manageable medical condition that is typically responsive to reasonable medical treatment that would enable them to become healthy pets.
Require an assessment by a Veterinarian, Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), or Veterinary Assistant to determine whether an animal is medically treatable.
Do not euthanize these animals except when the shelter's animal population exceeds available housing capacity or the ability to provide adequate sanitation, shelter, or humane conditions.
Document a recommendation for euthanasia in Chameleon.

sections.heading: Category: Treatable - Behavior
steps: Apply this category to animals with a manageable behavioral defect that is typically responsive to reasonable behavior intervention efforts that would enable them to become behaviorally manageable pets.
Require review by the Shelter Chief or designee to determine whether an animal is behaviorally treatable.

Do not euthanize behaviorally manageable animals except when the shelter animal population exceeds available housing capacity or the ability to provide adequate sanitation, shelter, or humane conditions.
Document a recommendation for euthanasia in Chameleon.

sections.heading: Category: Untreatable - Medical
steps: Apply this category to animals that are irremediably suffering from a serious illness or severe injuries, animals with a poor prognosis or protracted painful recovery, or animals suffering from a medical condition that is not likely to be remedied with reasonable efforts.

Animals that are irremediably suffering from a serious illness or severe injury may be euthanized to alleviate unnecessary suffering without being held for owner redemption or adoption, per state law.

Restrict the final determination to euthanize an animal as medically untreatable to the following employees: Veterinarian, Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Veterinary Assistant (VA), Animal Control Officer (ACO), Sergeants, Lieutenants, Deputy Director/Designee (i.e., Chief), or Director.

sections.heading: Category: Untreatable - Behavior
steps: Apply this category to animals observed to be dangerous or fractious to people or other animals, including animals generally described as unsocialized.

Consider as candidates for euthanasia animals that at any time prior to or during impoundment have exhibited signs of aggression toward a person or another animal, or have inflicted injury to a person or another animal through biting or severe scratching.
Document each incident promptly (shelter staff) and route it for review by the Shelter Chief or designee.
Restrict the final determination to euthanize an animal for untreatable behavior to the Shelter Chief or designee. Enter a note in Chameleon detailing the behavior and any outcomes.

Animals whose behavior is considered an isolated incident (protecting a litter, response to threatening behavior initiated by another animal, response to being moved while injured, inadvertent contact during play, puppy bites, etc.) may be made or remain available for adoption on a waiver immediately following any required holding or quarantine period. Enter a note in Chameleon detailing the behavior.

Recognize that aggressive behavior displayed by a dog or cat while in its enclosure toward another animal outside the enclosure may not be a fair indication of its behavior or suitability for adoption. Assess the animal's behavior after it is removed from its enclosure. Adoption of this animal requires a waiver.

sections.heading: Category: Too Young
steps: Recognize that state law requires all puppies and kittens to be eight (8) weeks of age to be legally adoptable. Animals under eight (8) weeks may be euthanized without being held for owner redemption or adoption.
Encourage foster care placement or rescue as resources are available. Document all reasonable efforts to this end in Chameleon; un-weaned animals may be held if ethically and medically prudent.

Newborn animals (less than eight weeks old) that need maternal care and have been impounded without their mothers (refer to Food and Agricultural Code 17006) that do not have a rescue or foster outcome immediately available, or that are so sick they meet the standard of irremediable suffering, may be candidates for euthanasia. This includes cases where stand-by Animal Control Officers are unable to provide appropriate supportive care and no rescue or foster (including the reporting party) is immediately available.

sections.heading: Category: Owner Request - At the Shelter
steps: Accept a written, signed request from an owner to have their animal humanely euthanized. There is a fee for this service, and those animals shall be euthanized immediately.

Require that all animals accepted under this service have a medical or behavioral condition, affirmed by the owner under penalty of perjury upon relinquishment of the animal, and reviewed and approved by a member of the Veterinary team.
Bring any request involving questionable circumstances, an inadequate reason, or an adoptable animal immediately to the attention of a supervisor for review.
Do not euthanize healthy, adoptable animals at an owner's request; refer the owner to a private veterinarian. Refer to DAS Policy 300-01.

steps.step: In accordance with the DAS memorandum dated August 22, 2014, animals considered for owner-requested euthanasia must fit one of the following categories:
sub: Animals determined to be Irremediably Suffering: usually determined by veterinary staff or ACOs, generally defined as situations where the animal's pain, suffering, or condition is irreversible, protracted, and not alleviated with medications, and where the prognosis is very poor or grave. Keeping such animals alive might constitute cruelty.

Animals with a documented history of aggressive behavior as evidenced by any of the following: a current or past bite report and investigation documenting an unprovoked bite on a person; any animal previously determined to be potentially dangerous, dangerous, or vicious by an ACO, hearing officer, magistrate, or other jurisdiction; a current Potentially Dangerous Animal Restraint (PDR); or a destruction order.

sections.heading: Category: Owner Requested Euthanasia - In the Field
steps: Accept a written, signed request from an owner to have their animal humanely euthanized by an Animal Control Officer. There is a fee for this service, and those animals shall be euthanized immediately if they are not able to be transported to a nearby shelter (i.e., they would not survive transport).

Do not euthanize animals in the field for behavior unless approved by a Sergeant or above, and only under extenuating circumstances. The ACO performing the euthanasia must document the approval in Chameleon.

Require that all animals accepted under this service have an untreatable medical condition or untreatable behavioral condition, as defined in this policy, affirmed by the owner under penalty of perjury upon relinquishment of the animal.
Bring any request involving questionable circumstances, an inadequate reason, or an adoptable animal immediately to the attention of a supervisor for review.
Perform the euthanasia in a non-public area whenever possible, out of sight of any public view.
Include notes regarding why the animal needed to be euthanized in the field.

sections.heading: Category: Court Order
steps: Apply this category to a destruction order of an animal, secondary to being confiscated, issued by a hearing officer or superior court judge.
Do not release or adopt these animals for any reason.

sections.heading: Category: Rabies Test
steps: Euthanasia and subsequent rabies test (FRA) is permitted for public health and safety.
Obtain approval from Veterinary Services staff for all rabies euthanasia and tests. (See Preparation of Laboratory Specimens Policy 315.)

sections.heading: Medical Emergency - Field
steps: For stray animals in the field having a medical emergency and/or the need to alleviate severe suffering that meets the definition of irremediable suffering, Animal Control Officers (ACOs) are permitted to make field assessments and euthanasia determinations in accordance with the RCDAS Irremediable Suffering Training provided by the Chief Veterinarian or designee, and as allowed under California Business and Professions Code 4827(d), which permits administering sodium pentobarbital for euthanasia of sick, injured, homeless, or unwanted domestic pets or animals without the presence of a veterinarian when the person is an employee of an animal control shelter and its agencies or humane society and has received proper training.
For animals in the field having a medical emergency that does not constitute irremediable suffering, take the injured animal to the nearest department shelter for veterinary evaluation.

After hours: if the ACO determines the animal needs immediate medical attention, go directly to the contracted emergency clinic. Fill out the authorization for treatment (Report of Animals Taken to the Veterinarian) and leave it with the clinic. Take the canary and pink copies to the shelter and return them to ACO supervision. Turn in any documentation provided by the clinic directly to the officer to the veterinary team for review and processing.

sections.heading: Medical Emergency - Animals in the Shelter
steps: During business hours, notify a department Veterinarian, Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), or Veterinary Assistant of any impounded shelter animal having a medical emergency, to determine whether the animal should be transported for off-site medical treatment or euthanized. If neither is an option, such as during a local disaster, perform euthanasia.

After hours: where veterinary personnel are unable to be contacted or are not present, the stand-by Animal Control Officer shall perform euthanasia to alleviate severe suffering. If the stand-by officer determines the animal is not irremediably suffering but is experiencing a medical emergency, immediately transport the animal to the nearest emergency animal hospital for veterinary care. If this is not an option, such as during a local disaster, perform euthanasia.

sections.heading: Euthanasia of Livestock - In the Field
steps: For injured large animals otherwise categorized as livestock, contact another ACO for assistance.
Determine whether it will be safe to euthanize the animal by injection.

If the animal cannot be euthanized safely by injection due to the animal being fractious or its location, or if the officers are unsuccessful with euthanasia by injection, notify the supervisor that the local law enforcement agency will be contacted to dispatch the animal.

sections.heading: Documentation - Field
steps: Document the need for any field euthanasia performed under this policy, including a detailed description of the animal's physical condition and any other pertinent information.

Include any owner information obtained by scanning the animal or through a collar. The ACO is responsible for notifying the owner regarding the disposition of the animal and for documenting the attempt to contact the owner.

Complete the Euthanasia Log for each administration of sodium pentobarbital in the department-provided written log. The Chief Veterinarian or designee audits the log periodically for accuracy; any inconsistencies must be corrected immediately.

sections.heading: Documentation - Shelter
steps: Veterinary staff performing euthanasia for medical reasons shall include detailed notes in the medical screen, including the reason for euthanasia, any medical diagnoses, and physical exam findings that meet the criteria for irremediable suffering.

Staff performing euthanasia for non-medical reasons shall include detailed notes in the Animal ID window, including the reason for euthanasia and any pertinent findings that meet the criteria for euthanasia in accordance with this policy.

Animal Control Officers performing euthanasia for non-medical or medical reasons shall include detailed notes in the Animal ID window, including the reason for euthanasia and any pertinent findings that meet the criteria for euthanasia in accordance with this policy.

sections.heading: Training
steps: Euthanasia Certification: all DAS staff (excluding Veterinarians and Registered Veterinary Technicians) involved in the euthanasia process are required to complete the state-mandated course defined in California Code of Regulations, Title 16, 2039 (Sodium Pentobarbital/Euthanasia Training). With this training, Business and Professions Code 4827(d) allows a certified Animal Services employee to administer sodium pentobarbital for euthanasia without the presence of a veterinarian.

Irremediable Suffering Training: all field staff involved with the euthanasia process are required to complete the RCDAS Irremediable Suffering virtual training prior to performing euthanasia in the field. The department Chief Veterinarian or designee provides this training. Field leadership is responsible for assigning the training and ensuring all staff have completed it.

Compassion Fatigue: DAS strives to create a safety net for staff involved in the euthanasia process by offering rotating schedules, scheduled out-of-rotations, required annual training for euthanasia recertification and compassion fatigue for staff exposed to the euthanasia process, and employee assistance services for issues arising from compassion fatigue. Training may be available on an as-needed basis through the Riverside County Department of Behavioral Health or through the employee assistance program.

revisions.desc: Original policy (titled Daily Euthanasia Log Policy)

revisions.author: RCDAS
revisions.desc: Previous title rescinded; retitled Euthanasia Policy

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2016 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.searchText: to ensure the accuracy, competency, and compassion of the euthanasia process for the department of animal services (das). all euthanasia is accomplished in accordance with state and federal laws and guidelines and in accordance with recommendations of the american veterinary medical association (avma) and other national organizations. staff must make every effort to identify animal owners before considering or performing euthanasia and must seek every possible alternative for a live outcome before committing to euthanasia. all staff director of animal services irremediably suffering an animal who has a poor or grave prognosis for being able to live without severe, unremitting physical pain even with prompt, necessary, and comprehensive veterinary care. (no kill advocacy center) reasonable efforts documented attempts by the department throughout an animal's stay in treating or placing an adoptable or treatable animal. these efforts include documentation of interest by adoption partners, attempts to modify behavior or treat medical conditions, and efforts to transfer animals into foster care or another agency. if all efforts fail, the animal may be euthanized as an adoptable or treatable animal. regulated animals animals prohibited or disqualified for adoption by law, regulation, or policy. dogs that have been declared dangerous by the department, or that are subject to dangerous dog proceedings, are not made available for adoption. animals that have been declared a public nuisance, or that are subject to public nuisance proceedings, should not be made available for adoption without a file review and concurrence of the director or designee. wild or exotic animals may be transferred to an approved adoption partner or licensed rehabilitator with the authorization of the director or designee. treatable (in general) per state requirements, any animal that is not deemed adoptable but could become adoptable with reasonable efforts (civil code sec. 1834.4, food and agricultural code sec. 17005, and penal code sec. 599d). treatable animals have a manageable behavioral defect and/or medical condition typically responsive to reasonable behavior modification or medical treatment that would enable them to become healthy, socialized pets. treatable animals may be adopted without being fully rehabilitated; all treatable animals adopted from das must have a signed medical or behavior acknowledgment/release form on file. policy follow the policy of the state of california (food and agricultural code 17005-17006) that no adoptable or treatable animal should be euthanized if it can be adopted into a suitable home. das is dedicated to reducing the euthanasia of adoptable and treatable animals. do not euthanize healthy, adoptable animals at an owner's request. refer all such requests to a local veterinarian or veterinary clinic or hospital. perform all euthanasia in a humane and respectful manner with compassion toward the animals and in accordance with the departmental euthanasia standard operating procedure. any breach of procedure or policy is subject to immediate corrective action, and acts constituting neglect, abuse, or wrongful euthanasia of any animal may lead up to and include termination. recognize that das animal shelters are mandated to be open-door facilities; denying admittance to certain species, breeds, or quantities of animals is not an option. the open-door mandate creates an environment that requires das to provide euthanasia when the animal shelter population exceeds the carrying capacity of the facility. qualifiers for euthanasia of adoptable animals confirm the animal is of a species or individual breed that is overrepresented within the current animal shelter population. select from that species or breed the animals with the lowest probability for adoption that have spent the greatest amount of time within the shelter. undertake such consideration only for animals impounded for 10 or more consecutive days, and only after all required holding periods have expired. alternatives and decision process commit resources to alternatives to euthanasia, including adoption and adoption partner programs, veterinary care of injured or ill animals, pet identification programs, foster care, pet behavior training programs, and spay/neuter programs designed to reduce surplus pet problems within riverside county. make euthanasia decisions through the shelter team, with the veterinary services team providing a secondary review. compile the list of euthanasia candidates (shelter team responsibility) and email it to the assigned registered veterinary technician or veterinary assistant for final review of notes and the medical record. once approved, the veterinary team member places the animal on the chameleon euthanasia list. euthanasia is a shared responsibility between trained shelter and veterinary staff. euthanasia categories select the most appropriate category for each euthanized impounded animal. enter accurate data; no attempt should be made to skew data or manipulate statistics or outcome definitions. {"step": "use the following subcategories when an animal is euthanized:", "sub": ["adoptable (used only when euthanizing for space)", "treatable - medical", "treatable - behavior", "untreatable - medical", "untreatable - behavior", "too young", "owner-requested euthanasia", "court order", "rabies test", "abatement"]} category: adoptable apply this category to impounded animals that appear or present to staff as healthy and behaviorally sound (animals with no negative observed behavior notes in the system). euthanize animals in this category only when the shelter's animal population exceeds available housing capacity or the ability to provide adequate sanitation, shelter, or humane conditions, in combination with a lack of community resources to provide an alternative. use the adpt-sp subtype in chameleon when documenting euthanasia under this category. category: treatable - medical apply this category to animals with a manageable medical condition that is typically responsive to reasonable medical treatment that would enable them to become healthy pets. require an assessment by a veterinarian, registered veterinary technician (rvt), or veterinary assistant to determine whether an animal is medically treatable. do not euthanize these animals except when the shelter's animal population exceeds available housing capacity or the ability to provide adequate sanitation, shelter, or humane conditions. document a recommendation for euthanasia in chameleon. category: treatable - behavior apply this category to animals with a manageable behavioral defect that is typically responsive to reasonable behavior intervention efforts that would enable them to become behaviorally manageable pets. require review by the shelter chief or designee to determine whether an animal is behaviorally treatable. do not euthanize behaviorally manageable animals except when the shelter animal population exceeds available housing capacity or the ability to provide adequate sanitation, shelter, or humane conditions. document a recommendation for euthanasia in chameleon. category: untreatable - medical apply this category to animals that are irremediably suffering from a serious illness or severe injuries, animals with a poor prognosis or protracted painful recovery, or animals suffering from a medical condition that is not likely to be remedied with reasonable efforts. animals that are irremediably suffering from a serious illness or severe injury may be euthanized to alleviate unnecessary suffering without being held for owner redemption or adoption, per state law. restrict the that are so sick they meet the standard of irremediable suffering, may be candidates for euthanasia. this includes cases where stand-by animal control officers are unable to provide appropriate supportive care and no rescue or foster (including the reporting party) is immediately available. category: owner request - at the shelter accept a written, signed request from an owner to have their circumstances, an inadequate reason, or an adoptable animal immediately to the attention of a supervisor for review. do not euthanize healthy, adoptable animals at an owner's request; refer the owner to a private veterinarian. refer to das policy 300-01. {"step": "in accordance with the das memorandum dated august 22, 2014, animals considered for owner-requested euthanasia must fit one of the condition is irreversible, protracted, and not alleviated with medications, and where the prognosis is very poor or grave. keeping such animals alive might constitute cruelty.", "animals with a field for behavior unless approved by a sergeant or above, and only under extenuating circumstances. the aco performing the euthanasia must document the approval in chameleon. require that all animals accepted under this service have an untreatable medical condition or untreatable behavioral condition, as defined in this policy, affirmed by the owner under penalty of perjury upon relinquishment of the animal. bring any request involving questionable circumstances, an inadequate reason, or an adoptable animal immediately to the attention of a supervisor for review. perform the euthanasia in a destruction order of an animal, secondary to being confiscated, issued by a hearing officer or superior court judge. do not release or adopt these animals for any reason. category: rabies test euthanasia and subsequent rabies test (fra) is permitted for public health and safety. obtain approval from veterinary services staff for all rabies euthanasia and tests. (see preparation of laboratory specimens policy 315.) medical emergency - field for stray animals in the field having a medical emergency and/or the need to alleviate severe suffering that meets the documents.title: The Chameleon Treatment Window
documents.flag: Numbered (=Policy per rule) but reads as a step-by-step protocol
documents.purpose: To provide consistent guidelines in recording and documenting treatment records using Chameleon.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure applies to DAS veterinary staff who are required to initiate animal treatment records in Chameleon.
documents.supersedes: 000-61 The Chameleon Treatment Window, effective May 18, 2017 and February 3, 2016

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Initiate an Animal Treatment Record on all live animals impounded by Riverside County Animal Services, as well as for public spay or neuter.
Initiate an Animal Treatment Record on dead animals in cases where the animal is a Special Attention case and a necropsy or rabies head test is requested.

sections.heading: The Treatment Window
steps: Use the pop-up (F4) in the required fields and choose the appropriate selection.
The window is divided into four areas: Visit, Treatments, Medications, and ToDos.

sections.heading: Visit Fields
steps: Visit: stores the reason the animal was brought to the clinic.
Date: automatically brings up today's date when you open the window.
Time: automatically brings up the time the window was opened.
Treatment No: generated by Chameleon upon storing the Visit. The format is T plus the last two digits of the year, a hyphen, and 6 digits (for example, T03-000001).
Animal ID: identification number of the animal being treated.

steps.step: Visit Type: these are inventory items with an item class of VISIT. Choices include:
sub: Adoption exam: used for adopted animals.
Foster exam: used for foster animals.
RTO exam: used for return to owner transaction animals.
Rescue exam: used for animals flagged for needs rescue or transfer to rescue partner transactions.
Hold exam: used for animals with adoption reservations.
Initial exam: used for newly impounded animals.
Surgery exam M (SEM): used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery on bus deployments only.
Surgery exam B (SEB): used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery at the Blythe shelter.
Surgery exam C: used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery through the Coachella Valley Animal Campus shelter.
Surgery exam R: used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery through the Western Riverside animal shelter.
Surgery exam S: used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery through the San Jacinto Animal Campus shelter.
Release exam: used for animals approved by the veterinarian for release.
Return exam: used when an animal is returned by a customer after an adoption.
Public Req.: used when a customer requests veterinary treatment on an animal impounded at DAS.
Staff Req.: used when a DAS employee requests veterinary treatment for an animal impounded at DAS.
Euth Req.: used when animals are surrendered by their owners for the service of euthanasia.
Daily Treat: used for animals impounded at DAS with ongoing treatment.
Shot Clinic Exam R: used for animals processed during a shot clinic held at the Western Riverside animal shelter.
Post exam: used for investigation of dead animals brought to the shelter.
Other Surg Exam: used for documenting all anesthesia procedures other than spay or neuter surgeries.

steps: Visit Reason: the reason for the visit.
Condition: the initial condition of the animal at the time of the exam.
Treat By: initials of the veterinary staff who performed the treatment.
Caduceus: enter a Y for impounded animals undergoing continuous treatments.
Weight: enter the weight of the animal for spay or neuter processing.
Temp: enter the temperature of the animal.
Review: the veterinarian or RVT enters a review date for a future date of treatment follow-up.
Diagnosis: not in use.
Resolved: press F5 once follow-up to the review date is completed.

Description: provides the description of the treatment. Use the template to assist in creating notes pertaining to specific visits. This is a free text field (up to 2,500 characters), or press the F5 key for stored descriptions (for example, the SOG/PUPPY NEUTER stored description covering pre-surgical exam, anesthesia, routine castration, closure, and recovery).

sections.heading: Treatment Fields
steps: Once a Treatment Number is assigned for the Visit record, itemized treatment types and medications can be added.
Treatment Type: press F4 to pop up a list of inventory items with an item class of Treatment.
Subtype: based on the Treatment type, press F4 to pop up a list of subtypes.
Price: applicable treatment price based on the treatment type.

Buttons: the Add button adds the Treatment selected from the list. The Update button updates the Treatment type; double click on the list box item to make changes, then press the button to update the treatment.

sections.heading: Medication Fields
steps: Medication: press the F4 key to pop up a list of inventory items under medication.
Dose: based on medication type; the default value is one. This cannot be changed.
Unit: default value is one. This field cannot be changed.
Qty: the number of how many of the medication given.
Times: how many times a day the medication needs to be administered.
#Day: how many days the medication needs to be administered.
Price: based on the medication, the price stored in the inventory record.

Buttons: the Add button adds the Medication selected from the list. The Update button updates the Medication type; double click on the list box item to make changes, then press the button to update the medication.

sections.heading: ToDos: Preferences
steps: From the menu tab, click on Procedures.
Choose Preferences.
Click on the Treatment button. The Treatment preferences window is now displayed.
Click on the Autoload ToDo Items on Store checkbox. A check mark allows Chameleon to auto-generate ToDo records upon adding a medication.
Set times per day in 24-hour format with a comma separating the times (for example, 2x per Day: 08:00,16:00).
Click OK to go back to the main window.
Double clicking on the ToDo listbox item opens the appropriate ToDo Window.

sections.heading: ToDo Window Fields
steps: The ToDo Window captures information on medications that need to be administered based on the medication Times and # of Days fields.
ToDo No: uniquely identifies the record; generated by Chameleon.
Animal ID: the identification number of the animal requiring the medication.
Ref No: reflects the Treatment number that the medication was stored on.
Ref Type: refers to treatment type.
Task: the name of the medication that needs to be administered to the animal.
Extra1: if the treatment is a controlled medication, enter the bottle number.
Due Date: the date that the medication needs to be administered.
Due Time: time of day the medication needs to be administered, normally based on the Treatment preferences.
Comp Date: the date that the ToDo record was completed and medication administered.
Comp Time: the time that the ToDo record was completed and medication was administered.
By: the person that administered the medication.
Description: the description of the procedure performed.

campusVariations.variation: Use Surgery exam B (SEB) for spay or neuter surgery processing.
Use Surgery exam C for spay or neuter surgery processing.
Use Surgery exam R for spay or neuter surgery processing; use Shot Clinic Exam R for shot clinics held at this shelter.
Use Surgery exam S for spay or neuter surgery processing.

appendices: Appendix A: Treatment Window, Treatment Preferences, and ToDo Window screen examples (attached separately; source screenshots)

documents.searchText: to provide consistent guidelines in recording and documenting treatment records using chameleon. this procedure applies to das veterinary staff who are required to initiate animal treatment records in chameleon. director of animal services policy initiate an animal treatment record on all live animals impounded by riverside county animal services, as well as for public spay or neuter. initiate an animal treatment record on dead animals in cases where the animal is a special attention case and a necropsy or rabies head test is requested. the treatment window use the pop-up (f4) in the required fields and choose the appropriate selection. the window is divided into four areas: visit, treatments, medications, and todos. visit fields visit: stores the reason the animal was brought to the clinic. date: automatically brings up today's date when you open the window. time: automatically brings up the time the window was opened. treatment no: generated by chameleon upon storing the visit. the format is t plus the last two digits of the year, a hyphen, and 6 digits (for example, t03-000001). animal id: identification number of the animal being treated. {"step": "visit type: these are inventory items with an item class of visit. choices include:", "sub": ["adoption exam: used for adopted animals.", "foster exam: used for foster animals.", "rto exam: used for return to owner transaction animals.", "rescue exam: used for animals flagged for needs rescue or transfer to rescue partner transactions.", "hold exam: used for animals with adoption reservations.", "initial exam: used for newly impounded animals.", "surgery exam m (sem): used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery on bus deployments only.", "surgery exam b (seb): used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery at the blythe shelter.", "surgery exam c: used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery through the coachella valley animal campus shelter.", "surgery exam r: used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery through the western riverside animal shelter.", "surgery exam s: used for animals processed for spay or neuter surgery through the san jacinto animal campus shelter.", "release exam: used for animals approved by the veterinarian for release.", "return exam: used when an animal is returned by a customer after an adoption.", "public req.: used when a customer requests veterinary treatment on an animal impounded at das.", "staff req.: used when a das employee requests veterinary treatment for an animal impounded at das.", "euth req.: used when animals are surrendered by their owners for the service of euthanasia.", "daily treat: used for animals impounded at das with ongoing treatment.", "shot clinic exam r: used for animals processed during a shot clinic held at the western riverside animal shelter.", "post exam: used for investigation of dead animals brought to the shelter.", "other surg exam: used for documenting all anesthesia procedures other than spay or neuter surgeries."]} visit reason: the reason for the visit. condition: the initial condition of the animal at the time of the exam. treat by: initials of the veterinary staff who performed the treatment. caduceus: enter a y for impounded animals undergoing continuous treatments. weight: enter the weight of the animal for spay or neuter processing. temp: enter the temperature of the animal. review: the veterinarian or rvt enters a review date for a future date of treatment follow-up. diagnosis: not in use. resolved: press f5 once follow-up to the review date is completed. description: provides the description of the treatment. use the template to assist in creating notes pertaining to specific visits. this is a free text field (up to 2,500 characters), or press the f5 key for stored descriptions (for example, the sog/puppy neuter stored description covering pre-surgical exam, anesthesia, routine castration, closure, and recovery). treatment fields once a treatment number is assigned for the visit record, itemized treatment types and medications can be added. treatment type: press f4 to pop up a list of inventory items with an item class of treatment. subtype: based on the treatment type, press f4 to pop up a list of subtypes. price: applicable treatment price based on the treatment type. buttons: the add button adds the treatment selected from the list. the update button updates the treatment type; double click on the list box item to make changes, then press the button to update the treatment. medication fields medication: press the f4 key to pop up a list of inventory items under medication. dose: based on medication type; the default value is one. this cannot be changed. unit: default value is one. this field cannot be changed. qty: the number of how many of the medication given. times: how many times a day the medication needs to be administered. #day: how many days the medication needs to be administered. price: based on the medication, the price stored in the inventory record. buttons: the add button adds the medication selected from the list. the update button updates the medication type; double click on the list box item to make changes, then press the button to update the medication. todos: preferences from the menu tab, click on procedures. choose preferences. click on the treatment button. the treatment preferences window is now displayed. click on the autoload todo items on store checkbox. a check mark allows chameleon to auto-generate todo records upon adding a medication. set times per day in 24-hour format with a comma separating the times (for example, 2x per day: 08:00,16:00). click ok to go back to the main window. double clicking on the todo listbox item opens the appropriate todo window. todo window fields the todo window captures information on medications that need to be administered based on the medication times and # of days fields. todo no: uniquely identifies the record; generated by chameleon. animal id: the identification number of the animal requiring the medication. ref no: reflects the treatment number that the medication was stored on. ref type: refers to treatment type. task: the name of the medication that needs to be administered to the animal. extra1: if the treatment is a controlled medication, enter the bottle number. due date: the date that the medication needs to be administered. due time: time of day the medication needs to be administered, normally based on the treatment preferences. comp date: the date that the todo record was completed and medication administered. comp time: the time that the todo record was completed and medication was administered. by: the person that administered the medication. description: the description of the procedure performed.

documents.title: Vaccination and Anthelmintic Protocol
documents.purpose: To establish a standard vaccination and anthelmintic protocol for all animals serviced by the Veterinary Services Division and the Field and Shelter Services Division within the Department of Animal Services.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable to all Veterinary Services Division and Field and Shelter Services Division employees.
documents.supersedes: Vaccination and Anthelmentic Protocol revisions of October 8, 2009 and April 19, 2007

sections.heading: Vaccination and Anthelmintic Protocol
steps: Administer a DHPP vaccination and an intranasal Bordetella inoculation to all dogs older than six (6) weeks of age at the time of impound or delivery to the animal shelter. Consider exceptions on a case by case basis relative to aggressive behavior and the safety of the employees.
Administer an FVRCP vaccination to all cats older than six (6) weeks of age at the time of impound or delivery to the animal shelter, with exceptions considered as above.

Deworm all puppies and kittens six months of age or younger with a weight-appropriate dose of Pyrantel Pamoate or other suitable anthelmintic, and administer a dose of Marquis paste to control coccidia infections.

Give Pyrantel Pamoate and Marquis as above, or refer to the current protocol, for any adult dog or cat that demonstrates active gastro-intestinal parasite infection and/or has noninfectious enteritis.
Administer a rabies vaccination prior to release from the shelter to all dogs three (3) months of age or older upon adoption.
Administer a rabies vaccination prior to release from the shelter to all cats three (3) months of age or older upon adoption.
Administer booster vaccination(s) as described in steps 1 and 2 to any dog or cat maintained at the shelter or in foster care for a period exceeding three (3) weeks.

related.name: Acknowledgment of Policy 100-01 (retained in employee personnel file and attached to employee Person ID in Chameleon)

appendices: Appendix A: Acknowledgment of the Vaccination and Anthelmintic Protocol Policy (employee signature form)

documents.title: Duties of a Registered Veterinary Technician
documents.purpose: To outline and define the duties and responsibilities of Clinic Registered Veterinary Technicians (RVT) in the Veterinary Services Unit.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure applies to all Registered Veterinary Technicians.
documents.supersedes: Duties of Registered Veterinary Technician, effective May 28, 2007

sections.heading: Morning Routine
steps: Divide all Animal Health Care tasks (Dog/Cat Receiving and Treatments) between scheduled RVTs.
Assign designated duties at 7:30 am for morning processing.
Evaluate the status of all hospitalized animals.
Attend to all animals brought from the emergency clinic.
Examine and vaccinate newly impounded animals on the Animal Without Treatment Report or in Dog or Cat receiving.
Observe all animals for any signs of injury or illness, noting the kennel number.
Notify the Veterinarian of all abnormalities.
Record all observations and treatments into the Chameleon Treatment Screen.
The Veterinarian determines the course of treatment; the RVT and/or Veterinarian administers treatment as directed.

sections.heading: Treatments
steps: Divide morning treatments among RVT staff (for example, Dog Receiving/Dog Treatments and Cat Receiving/Cat Treatments are done by the third RVT when three or more RVTs are present).
Complete afternoon treatments between 4:30 and 5:00.
Review and complete all active treatments on the Daily To Do List and note the animals' overall behavior, symptoms, and appetite.
Bring animals showing signs of illness within the kennels or treatment rooms to the attention of the Veterinarian if no treatment has been prescribed.
Provide Veterinarian-instructed medical care for newly impounded sick or injured animals (for example, radiographs, IV catheter/fluids, injections).
Enter all observations and treatments into the Chameleon Treatment Record.

sections.heading: Euthanasia (RVT)
steps: All RVTs must fully understand the processing of animals for euthanasia. RVTs have the right to pass animals for euthanasia.

steps.step: Morning processing consists of the programmed euthanasia of dogs, cats, exotics, and wildlife species. RVTs must know the given holding times of these animals, which are as follows:
sub: Strays: four (4) business days past the date of impound, excluding holidays or Sundays.
Licensed/Owner Known: ten (10) business days from date of notification, excluding Sundays or holidays.
Quarantines: ten (10) days from date of bite.

steps: Releases are done by the Veterinarian or Supervising RVT only.

sections.heading: Owner Request Euthanasia
steps: Perform owner-requested euthanasia when the owner relinquishes his or her pet for the purpose of euthanasia; it is performed immediately.

sections.heading: Medical/Behavioral Euthanasia
steps: Do not euthanize any animal, except those that are severely injured, sick, and/or aggressive, until the retention period has been met, as determined and recorded by staff.

sections.heading: Hold Exams
steps: Pre-exams are performed by the Veterinarian prior to adoption.
Examination by the veterinarian.
Vaccine and flea control.
Temperament evaluation.
Record on the Chameleon Treatment Screen.

sections.heading: Adoptions (Animals Placed into a New Home)
steps: Ensure the animal is examined by a veterinarian.
Provide additional vaccine and flea control if needed.
Microchip only if altered; otherwise the animal goes to surgery.
Record on the Chameleon Treatment Screen.

sections.heading: Redemptions (Animals Being Returned to Owner)
steps: Perform an exam.
Provide rabies vaccination and microchip if requested.
Record on the Chameleon Treatment Screen.
Notify the Veterinarian of sick or injured animals.

sections.heading: Releases from Surgery (Animals Released After a Medical Procedure)
steps: Check the incision and apply staples as needed.
Check the microchip if given.
Notify the Veterinarian of all abnormalities and record on the Chameleon Treatment Screen.

sections.heading: Head Test
steps: The head test is the procedure used to perform rabies testing on animals involved in bites.

steps.step: Animals impounded by Receiving Front Counter:
sub: Examine, vaccinate, and scan for a microchip.
Record findings on the Chameleon Treatment Screen.
Notify the Veterinarian of abnormalities.

steps.step: Animals unloaded from ACO vehicles:
sub: Animal Control Officers may arrive throughout the day but are allowed to unload between 3:30 and 4:30. RVTs are required to be present during unloading.
Obtain the animal ID number.
Examine, vaccinate, and scan for a microchip.
Puppies under 6 months old get Marquis Paste and Drontal.
Kittens under 6 months old get Marquis Paste and Drontal.
Provide flea and tick treatment as needed.
Record findings on the Chameleon Treatment Screen.
Notify the Veterinarian of abnormalities.

sections.heading: Other Duties
steps: Surgery.
Assist the veterinarian in emergency procedures.
Spay/Neuter Program.
Veterinary Service Inventory: maintain adequate supplies within the clinic.
Do inventory once a week or as needed, using the inventory log.
Maintain inventory within the clinic using the Want Board.
Collect and properly dispose of all outdated drugs.
Maintain logs of state-regulated drugs.

steps.step: Controlled Substances (Telazol, Fatal Plus, etc.):
sub: Clinic use.
Distribute to ACOs.

steps: Complete daily tasks and duties.
Re-stock supplies (syringes, tongue depressors, flea control, etc.).
Sweep, mop, and empty trash in the clinic as needed.
Assist Animal Care Technicians as needed.
Perform morning and afternoon rounds; check the catteries and yard for signs of illness or injury.
Attend to public and staff concerns for impounded animals.
Receive incoming calls from citizens with questions and/or concerns about animal health, post-op care on spayed and neutered animals, return policies, etc.
Draw vaccines throughout the day.
Collect samples and specimens for outside testing (for example, Vector Control, UC Davis, Fish and Game).
Work with Rescue and Volunteer Coordinators for animal placement (for example, fostering, breed-specific rescue groups, shelters).
