documents.title: Controlled Substance Bottle Log Procedure
documents.purpose: To provide an accurate record of the issuance and use of controlled substances.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services (approved the original policy October 7, 2009).
documents.scope: Applicable to all staff who check out a controlled substance.
documents.supersedes: Policy 300-07, effective July 1, 2004; revised October 7, 2009. Absorbs duplicate copy 300-07-411, Controlled Substance Bottle Log Procedure (identical content, same effective dates).

sections.heading: When to Use
steps: Complete the Controlled Substance Log (Attachment A) in full each time a controlled substance is purchased or transferred to another location.

steps.step: Fill out the log as follows:
sub: Lot Number: enter the manufacturer's lot number.
Drug: enter the type of drug the log is being used for.
Initial Amount: enter the beginning amount of ccs (this will be the same as the Amount Remaining (line 10) from the previous page).
Strength: enter the strength of the drug, if required.
Date: enter the date that the product is being transferred.
Bottle Number: enter the bottle number that is handwritten on the bottle.
Location: enter the location where the substance will be used (for example, in the field, the euthanasia room, or the Indio shelter).
Beginning Amount: enter the amount of controlled substance remaining in supply by subtracting the amount contained in the bottle from the previous Amount Remaining (line 10).
Amount Used/Received: enter the amount of product that is contained in the bottle being accounted for.

Amount Remaining: when removing a bottle, subtract the Beginning Amount (line 8) from the Amount Used/Received (line 9) and enter that total on this line. When adding a new shipment of the drug to the existing bottles, add the Beginning Amount (line 8) to the Amount Used/Received (line 9) and enter the total on this line.
Signature: the person filling out the log must sign to verify removing or adding any bottles from the supply.

steps: Account for each bottle on a separate line.

documents.title: Completion and Routing of the Annual Report of Local Rabies Control Activities
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform procedure to accurately track and compile rabies control data required by the State of California.
documents.authority: Chief Veterinarian (endorsement as Local Health Officer authorized representative); Rabies Control Office Specialist (completion)
documents.scope: Applicable to the Department's System Administrator.
documents.supersedes: SOP How to complete and route the Annual Report of Local Rabies Control Activities, effective May 28, 2007
references: P 100-4, Annual Report of Rabies Control Activities
California Department of Health Services, Veterinary Public Health Section (Annual Report of Local Rabies Control Activities forms)

definitions.def: Versus humans.
Fluorescent rabies antibody test.

steps.step: Contact the Crystal Report prime and request the following reports:
sub: Intake Summary by Jurisdiction and Intake Subtype.
Statistical Report for Citations/Complaints Issued by Jurisdictions.
Rabies Clinic Log.
Kennel Log.

steps.step: Run the following from the Chameleon Reports Menu:
sub: Outcome Summary by Jurisdiction.
Supplemental City/County Report.
Licensing in Effect by Jurisdiction.

sections.heading: Completion of the Annual Report of Local Rabies Control Activities Form (refer to lettered spaces on the form)

steps.step: Rabies Vaccination and Licensing (A-E):
sub: A: Number of "Actual Cost" rabies public vaccination clinics held. Refer to the Rabies Clinic Log total for the number of rabies clinics held.
B: Number of animal control citations issued for rabies vaccination and licensing violations. Refer to the Statistical Report for Citations total for dogs and cats, if applicable.
C: Dogs and cats vaccinated in "Actual Cost" public vaccination clinics. Refer to the Rabies Clinic Log.
D: Dogs and cats licensed in "Actual Cost" public vaccination clinics. Refer to the Rabies Clinic Log.
E: Total number of dogs and cats licensed in jurisdiction. Refer to the Licensing in Effect by Jurisdiction report.

steps.step: Canine and Feline Rabies Control (F-J):
sub: F: Dogs and cats on hand in the shelter January 1 (carried over from the prior year). Refer to last year's annual State Rabies Control Activity report.

G: Dogs and cats entering the shelter, total. Refer to the Intake Summary report. Include: dogs and cats captured by Animal Control Officers as strays (intake type Stray, intake subtype Field or Night); dogs and cats surrendered by owners, not including those surrendered for quarantine (intake types Owner Sur and Euth Req, intake subtypes Field, Night, or OTC); dogs and cats surrendered by the public (intake type Stray, intake subtype OTC); dogs and cats impounded for animal bite quarantines (any intake type, intake subtype Bite); and dogs and cats transferred from another shelter (intake type Transfer, intake subtype SPCA or An Control).

H: Disposition of dogs and cats entering shelter, total. Refer to the Outcome Summary report. Include: reclaimed by owner (outcome type RTO); adopted by new owners (outcome type ADOPTION); euthanized (outcome type EUTH); died of other causes (outcome type DIED); stolen, escaped, etc. (outcome types MISSING, ESCAPED); and transferred to another shelter (outcome type TRANSFER, outcome subtype An Control).
I: Dead dogs and cats collected. Refers to outcome type DISPOSAL.
J: Dogs and cats on hand in the shelter at year end (carried over to the current year). Refer to the kennel inventory report of December 31 of the past year.

steps.step: Animal Bite Reporting (K.1 to K.2):
sub: K: Animal bites reported, total. Refer to the County and City Quarantine Statistics.

K.1: Dog and cat bites reported, total. Based on the Chameleon Bite Window "Controlled by" criteria: Licensed (LIC), Vaccinated only (VACC), Neither licensed nor vaccinated but owned (NOVAC), and Strays (STRAY).

K.2: Other animal bites reported, total. Refer to the Supplemental City and County Report. Other domestics (excluding cats): bite types FerretVH, Cattle, Horse. Wild: bite types BATVH, OPOSSUMVH, RACCOONVH, SKUNKVH, OTHERVH.

steps.step: Animal Quarantines (L-O):
sub: L: Number of 30-day quarantines for vaccinated dogs and cats exposed to potentially rabid animals. Refer to the Supplemental County and City Report, bite types Cat30 and Dog30.
M: Number of 6-month quarantines for unvaccinated dogs and cats exposed to potentially rabid animals. Refer to the Supplemental County and City Report, bite types Cat180 and Dog180.
N: Number of 6-month quarantines for domestic livestock (horses, cattle, etc.) exposed to potentially rabid animals. Refer to the Supplemental County and City Report, bite type Horse180.
O: Number of 30-day or 6-month quarantines not completed because animals were euthanized. Refer to the number of closed-out bite reports on pet vs. wildlife.

steps.step: Agency Admin (P-Q):
sub: P: Number of animal control officers employed in jurisdiction. Obtain the number from the Watch Commander or Chief of Field Services.

Q: Name of agency or organization responsible for rabies control activities in the jurisdiction: County of Riverside Department of Animal Services, 5950 Wilderness Ave, Riverside CA 92504, (951) 358-7387.

steps.step: Completed by:
sub: Signature of the person completing the form.
Printed name of the person completing the form.
Title: Rabies Control Office Specialist.
Agency: County of Riverside Department of Animal Services.
Telephone and email of the Rabies Control Clerk.

steps.step: Endorsement by local Health Officer or authorized representative:
sub: Signature: signed by the Chief Veterinarian.
Printed name of the Chief Veterinarian.
Title: Chief Veterinarian.
Agency: County of Riverside Animal Services.
Email: Chief Veterinarian's email address.

steps: Forward the completed form by the due date to: California Department of Health Services, Veterinary Public Health Section MS 7308, PO Box 997413, Sacramento CA 95899-7413.

sections.heading: Completion of the Statement of Enforcement of Local Rabies Control Activities Form
steps: County: County of Riverside.
Effective Date: January to December of the reporting year.
Specify the area or jurisdiction for which the statement is made (for example, Coachella Valley, Thousand Palms).

steps.step: Person responsible for conducting the Rabies Control Program in the jurisdiction or area:
sub: Date: enter the current date.
Signature: signed by the Rabies Control Desk.
Printed Name: name of the Office Specialist.
Title: Rabies Control Office Specialist.
Address: 5950 Wilderness Ave, Riverside CA 92504.

steps.step: Endorsement by local Health Officer or authorized representative:
sub: Date: enter the current date.
Signature: signed by the Chief Veterinarian.
Printed Name: name of the Chief Veterinarian.
Title: Chief Veterinarian.
Address: 5950 Wilderness Ave, Riverside CA 92504.

sections.heading: Completion of the Annual Specimens Tested for Rabies Form
steps: Run the Chameleon report "Quarantine Statistics of FRA Tests."
Type in the date for each month.
The first column is the specimen tested.
Under the column "TEST," enter the total number tested for each corresponding specimen.
Under the column "POS," enter the total number tested positive for rabies for each corresponding specimen.

steps.step: Upon receipt of the Statement of Enforcement of Local Rabies Control Activities and the Annual Report of Local Rabies Control Activities from the Department of Health Services:
sub: Make copies.
Enclose instructions for the animal control agency not to complete the endorsement fields.
The Rabies Control Desk instructs the animal control agency to return completed forms by the rabies control due date.
Mail or fax copies to the non-contract animal control agencies in Riverside County.

sections.heading: Completed Forms
steps: Prepare non-contract animal control agency and County completed forms for endorsement.
The Local Health Officer or authorized representative (i.e., Clinic Veterinarian) reviews the statistics prior to endorsement.
Once the authorized representative endorses all forms, make copies and file them in the Senior Office Supervisor's office.
Mail original forms to the California Department of Health Services address listed above.

documents.title: Completion and Routing of the Rabies Control Investigation Report
documents.purpose: To establish procedures for the initiation and completion of the Rabies Control Investigation Report (Animal Bite/Scratch Report) when a human has been bitten or scratched by a wild or domestic mammal, excluding rabbits and rodents.
documents.authority: Director of Animal Services (approval); Chief Veterinarian or designee (case review and closure)
documents.scope: All Department of Animal Services (DAS) personnel who have knowledge of an animal biting or scratching a person or persons.
documents.supersedes: Policy 100-05 effective November 16, 2016; revised from March 14, 2016; December 2, 2015; December 16, 2014; October 8, 2009; May 28, 2007
references: Food and Agriculture Code Section 2606(b)(2)
P&P 700.12 (animal age determination)
Rabies Control Investigation Report (Animal Bite/Scratch Report) form
DAS Policy on Annual Report of Local Rabies Control Activities

definitions.def: Fluorescent rabies antibody laboratory test performed on a specimen submitted to the Public Health Laboratory.
Unable to quarantine; the biting animal or animal owner could not be found, or the quarantine was not completed.
Quarantine visit.
Quarantine release.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Recognize that rabies is a fatal disease communicable to both animals and humans, and take preventive measures when humans or domestic animals come in contact with a rabies suspect wild animal by bites, scratches, or other types of exposure.

steps.step: Apply preventive measures that include, but are not limited to:
sub: Rabies vaccination of all dogs four months of age or older.
Confinement of all dogs less than four months of age.
Establishment of ownership and rabies vaccination status of all dogs by licensing.
Elimination of stray animals from the environment.
Thorough investigation of all reported bites and scratches inflicted by warm-blooded mammals, and proper isolation and observation of these warm-blooded mammals.
Thorough investigation of contacts between domestic pets and wild mammals, proper isolation and observation of these domestic pets, and laboratory examination of the wild animals.
Public education regarding rabies prevention.

steps: Use the Riverside County Rabies Control Investigation Report (Animal Bite/Scratch Report) to record all applicable information needed by Public Health authorities to properly evaluate human exposures from animal bites or scratches and to determine the need for rabies prophylactic treatment. The report is also used to report exposures of pet animals to known or suspected rabid animals.

sections.heading: Submission of Report
steps: Complete the form and submit it to: Riverside County Department of Animal Services, 6851 Van Buren Blvd, Jurupa Valley CA 92509.
Agencies other than DAS submit the second copy on the day the investigation begins and the working copy when the investigation is completed.

In cases where the behavior of the animal may indicate rabies, or in which the victim received a face, head, or neck exposure by a wild, stray, or rabies suspect animal, telephone a report of the incident immediately to DAS at (951) 358-7387. Fax the completed report to DAS at (951) 358-7738, or deliver it in person.

steps.step: White (Original): the official form.
sub: The original should successively be in the possession of each person who is taking action related to the incident.

At the conclusion of an investigation, file the original at DAS. ALL signatures (report writer, investigators, owner, person placing animal in quarantine, person releasing from quarantine, veterinarian reviewing case, physician or veterinarian taking other action or closing the case, and the clerks making the necessary notification) MUST be on this copy.

steps.step: Yellow (Second Copy): the notification copy.
sub: If agencies other than DAS are retaining the white copy to investigate the case, observe, isolate, or release the animal from quarantine, mail the yellow copy to DAS on the day the bite report is initiated. This notifies the Chief Veterinarian that a possible exposure has occurred and that action is being taken.
Fill out the original as completely as possible; do not retain the yellow form while an effort is made to complete the investigation.

steps.step: Goldenrod: the FRA test copy.
sub: Submit the report with the specimen to the Lab.
The lab scans this report and returns a signed copy with the FRA test results.

steps.step: Pink: the OWNER's Notice to Quarantine form.
sub: The owner agrees to adhere to the quarantine imposed on their pet.

steps.step: White (Supplemental): reports and records actions and results during the course of the investigation.
sub: The supplemental is to remain, and be filed, with the original copy of the bite report.
Date and initial or sign (first initial, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31 and the supplemental.

sections.heading: General Instructions
steps: Treat the report as a legal document that should be easily read and understood. Brevity, completeness, accuracy, and clearness are required.
Box print using a BLACK ballpoint pen. Police/radio codes are not acceptable.
Abbreviations familiar to a layperson are acceptable. 24-hour (military) time is acceptable.
Enter all applicable information on the report. If information is not applicable, enter "N/A". If the information is unknown, leave blank.
Mark boxes with an X, not a check mark.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Case and Victim Information (Items 1a-17)
steps: 1a. IMP # (Impound Number): enter the DAS Animal ID (if applicable).
1b. CASE #: enter the bite report number assigned to the case (created by Rabies Control).
1c. ACT #: enter the DAS Activity number assigned to the case (if applicable).

2a. NAME: enter the name of the victim, last name first, followed by the first name and middle initial (if applicable). If the victim is unknown, leave the space blank and write details in section 31.
2b. VICTIM (LAST NAME): enter the victim's last name.
3. SEX (victim): mark the appropriate box.

4. AGE: enter the victim's age. If under one year, enter the number of months followed by "mos." If the victim is a minor, also complete item 7 (Name of Parent/Guardian) and item 8 (address if different).
5. TELEPHONE: enter home and work telephone numbers of the victim.
6. ADDRESS: enter residence number, direction (N, S, E, W), prefix (Via, Calle, etc.), street name, apartment or space number, city, state, and zip code.
7. PARENT/GUARDIAN (if victim is under 18 years of age): enter last name, first name, and middle initial (if applicable).
8. ADDRESS OF PARENT OR GUARDIAN: use only if different from the victim's address.
9. SOURCE OF INFORMATION (required): mark the appropriate box. If "other," indicate source and telephone number.
10. REPORT RECEIVED BY (required): enter first initial, last name, and badge number (if applicable) of the person initiating the report, and the date and time the report was received.
11. PLACE OF ATTACK: enter information regarding the exact location of the bite/scratch. Corresponding numbers from the report (for example, "in the street in front of #33") may be used.
12a. ATTACK DATE/TIME: enter the numerical date of the bite/scratch and the time of day it occurred.
12b. DATE OF BITE: enter the date of the bite/scratch.

13. CIRCUMSTANCES: enter exact information regarding the circumstances surrounding the bite/scratch. Mark the appropriate box for the question "Was the bite through material?" Report only factual knowledge, not what an animal was thinking or why an animal bit or scratched.

steps.step: 14. WOUND LOCATION:
sub: Enter the exact part of the body that was bitten/scratched, together with "right" or "left" as appropriate (for example, left calf, right hand, right thumb).
Describe the wound as a scratch, puncture, or laceration (for example, multiple punctures, 3-inch laceration).
If the bite/scratch did not cause a break in the skin, enter "no skin break." Indicate whether the wound resulted from the animal's teeth or claws if known.

steps.step: 15. SKIN BREAK:
sub: Mark the box and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable), if there is a bite/scratch that caused a break in the skin.
If no skin break, write "No" and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable).
Skin breaks reported by a physician or hospital do not need to be verified.

steps: 16. WOUND TREATMENT: enter the date the victim received treatment, including the details of the treatment. If no treatment was given, advise the victim (parent/guardian) to consult a doctor regarding wound care.
17. TREATED BY: mark the appropriate box. If a physician rendered treatment, enter the physician's name (or secondarily the hospital/facility) and the telephone number.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Veterinary, Laboratory, and Closure (Items 19-30)
steps: 19. ANTI-RABIES TREATMENT (for use by the Riverside County Chief Veterinarian or Clinic Veterinarian only): mark the appropriate box and enter "recommended."
20. VETERINARY REVIEW (for use by the Riverside County Chief Veterinarian or Clinic Veterinarian only): enter responses on applicable lines.
21-26. LABORATORY EXAM - FRA TEST (for laboratory use only): enter responses on applicable lines.

steps.step: 27. CASE CLOSED (for use by the Rabies Control Desk): the Chief Veterinarian or designee will review the report and will mark the appropriate boxes and date and sign the report in the following cases:
sub: When a stray biting animal was never located.
When laboratory exam results are positive for rabies.
When the victim or biting animal is located outside of Riverside County.
When there are unusual circumstances in the report and the Chief Veterinarian needs to review before closure.

steps: 28. NOTIFICATION (for use by Rabies Desk Clerk): mark the appropriate box, date, and sign.

29. QV VISIT DATE: conduct quarantine visits on all animals that do not have a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite. Five (5) days for dogs and cats and seven (7) days for all other animals.

30. QR DATE: enter the biting animal's quarantine release date. A telephone release may be allowed for any animal that had a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite or any animal quarantined at a veterinary hospital. All other releases will be a field release, indicated by an "FR" placed in the box by the correcting sergeant or rabies control desk.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Non-Numbered Items and Item 31
steps: Map Ref. (Map Reference): enter the Riverside County Thomas Guide Street Guide and Directory reference for both victim and owner (if applicable).

steps.step: QT Fee (Quarantine Fee): mark the appropriate box.
sub: Fee Collected: write the receipt number in the space provided.
Fee Not Collected: if not applicable, mark "NO." If the fee is to be billed, write "billed" in the receipt number section.

steps: Supplemental: mark this box when the supplemental (attached to the bite report) is initiated. The person initiating the supplemental fills out the victim and owner information at the top of the page along with boxes 2b, 12b, and 32b (Victim, Date of Bite, and Owner) along the side of the page.

steps.step: 31. MISCELLANEOUS:
sub: Use the spaces in line 31 to record pertinent information about the investigation.
Line 31 may also be used to complete any other line on the report when additional space is needed.
Do not use this space for extraneous information such as "no one home." Write that type of information on the supplemental Investigation Report.
Date and initial or sign (first name, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31.

sections.heading: Specific Instructions: Biting Animal Information (Items 32a-45)

steps.step: 32a. OWNER (Custodian):
sub: Line out the non-applicable word.
Enter the name of the owner or custodian (last, first, middle).
If the name previously entered is different from the actual owner of the animal, enter the owner's name, address, and phone number on line 31.
If the animal is a stray, enter the word "stray."
Enter both home and work (if applicable) telephone numbers.

steps: 32b. OWNER: enter the owner's last name. If the animal is a stray, leave blank; the Rabies Control Desk will enter "STRAY" before filing the report.

33. ADDRESS: enter the address where the animal is to be quarantined. If the address previously entered is different from the animal owner's actual address, enter the owner's address on line 31. Enter the numerical, direction (N, S, E, W), street name, apartment or space number, city, state, and zip code.

steps.step: 34. ANIMAL TYPE:
sub: Mark the appropriate box. If other, enter the type of animal.
Breed: enter the breed of the animal.
Name: enter the animal's name.
Sex: mark the appropriate box describing the biting animal's sex, "Male" or "Female." Write an "N" above the appropriate box if the animal is verified as having been altered.
Est. Age: enter the age of the animal, according to P&P 700.12. If unknown, enter an estimated age. If under one year, enter the number of months or weeks followed by "mos." or "wks."

steps: 35. DESCRIPTION: enter the color(s) of the biting animal. If the animal is a stray, enter all other distinguishing characteristics (long hair, short hair, large, small, pointed ears) that could be used to identify the animal.

36. LICENSE: enter the current license number and the license jurisdiction (if Riverside County, mark the box) of the animal. Enter the license expiration date. If the license is expired, or if the dog should be licensed and is not, issue a citation and enter the citation number in the space provided.

37. BEHAVIOR: a live animal must be observed before it can be quarantined. Upon quarantine, mark the appropriate box, "normal" or "abnormal." If the animal's behavior is abnormal, enter the specifics in section 31. If the animal is deceased or stray, mark the box "unknown."

steps.step: 38. TRAVELS:
sub: Out of the County: ascertain whether the animal has been outside Riverside County in the past 6 months and mark the appropriate box. If "yes," indicate when and where.

Out of the USA: ascertain whether the animal has been outside the USA in the past year and mark the appropriate box. If "yes," indicate when and where. If a detailed explanation is needed, enter "supp" and make necessary notations on the supplemental.

steps.step: 39. VACCINATION:
sub: Boxes: mark the appropriate box.
Kind: enter the type of rabies vaccine given, if available.
Date Given: enter the date the rabies vaccination was given, if available.
Expiration Date: enter the date the rabies vaccination expires.

Given By: enter the name of the veterinarian or veterinary clinic that vaccinated the animal, if available, or indicate "per comp." if the information was obtained by a computer records check through dispatch.

steps.step: 40. QUARANTINE:
sub: Boxes: mark the appropriate box describing the place of quarantine. "#33" refers to the address listed on line 33.

KENNEL #: used for animals quarantined at the Riverside County Animal Shelter. A kennel number is required for live animals. For deceased animals write "R Morgue" and place the animal in the FRA Test section.
IMPOUND TAG: the other ID assigned to the impounded animal.
MICROCHIP #: used to enter the microchip number when the impounded animal is scanned.
Other: used when the animal is quarantined at any other location. List the exact address in line 31.
If the animal is quarantined at the victim's residence, write "#6" or the actual address in the space provided.
QUARANTINE IMPOSED (required). Date: enter the date quarantine is imposed, numerically.
By: enter the first initial, last name, and badge number of the officer imposing quarantine.
Agency: Riverside County officers mark the box. City agencies mark the box labeled "City of" and write in the city name.

steps: 41. OBSERVED OK: enter the date, initials, and badge number (if applicable) when the animal is observed healthy. Use this line for quarantine visits (including daily Shelter visits by vet staff), stray bite patrols, and when an animal is observed OK during the quarantine period and at the time of quarantine release.

42. NOT OBSERVED: enter the date next to the word "Date" and initials and badge number next to the word "Initials" when the animal is not observed. Use this line for stray bite patrols and when an animal is not observed before quarantine is imposed. Note: an animal must be observed before quarantine can be imposed.

steps.step: 43. RELEASE:
sub: Boxes: mark the appropriate box (if applicable) to describe how the animal was released, "by telephone" or "upon inspection."
Date: enter the date the animal is released from quarantine.
By: enter the first initial, last name, and badge number of the person releasing the animal from quarantine.
Agency: Riverside County officers mark the box. City agencies mark the box labeled "City of" and write in the city name.

steps: 44. CAUSE OF DEATH: enter (if applicable) the date the death of the biting animal occurred and mark the appropriate box or boxes describing the cause.

45. VETERINARIAN/SYMPTOMS: mark (if applicable) the appropriate box indicating whether a veterinarian observed the animal prior to the animal's death. Mark the appropriate box relating to symptoms as obtained by the observing veterinarian prior to the animal's death.

steps.step: Agreement for Quarantine Compliance and SIGNATURE:
sub: Date: enter the date quarantine is imposed.
ID #: enter a driver's license or ID number of the animal owner or custodian in the space provided and indicate the state of issuance if other than California.
Owner: obtain the signature of the animal owner who will be responsible for the animal during the quarantine.
Custodian: obtain the signature of the custodian of the animal (if applicable) who will be responsible for the animal during the quarantine.

sections.heading: Veterinarian or Home Quarantines
steps: With the approval of the Chief of Veterinary Services, Clinic Veterinarian, or Rabies Control Desk, any severely sick, injured, or elderly animal requiring medical treatment may be quarantined at an outside private veterinarian or at the owner's home. These types of quarantines may also be approved in unusual or abnormal situations by the Chief of Veterinary Services, Clinic Veterinarian, or Rabies Control Desk.

Indicate any quarantine so approved in Section 31 or on the Supplemental of the Bite-Scratch Report, including who is approving the quarantine and the circumstances necessitating quarantine somewhere other than DAS.

Impose and release all quarantines at an outside private veterinarian through an Animal Control Officer. When imposing quarantines, the officer will direct the veterinarian to sign the bite report as the custodian. No fee will be collected for these quarantines. All quarantines at an outside private veterinarian may be released via telephone.

sections.heading: Shelter Quarantines

steps.step: In accordance with Food and Agriculture Code 2606(b)(2), impose shelter quarantine at the Department of Animal Services on the following basis:
sub: When the dog bites above the shoulders.
Severity of the bite.
When the owner releases the dog for euthanasia.
A prior bite history to any part of the body by the dog.
An unprovoked bite to a child.
The dog is unvaccinated.
Any medical index of suspicion that the dog may be rabid.

steps: The quarantine period shall be 10 days from the date of the bite.
The animal may be subjected to euthanasia and the head submitted for rabies FRA testing.

sections.heading: Chameleon Bite Window: Entering the Report
steps: Once the official bite report is completed (either the biting animal was released from quarantine or the report was closed and signed by the Chief Veterinarian), enter a human vs. animal bite report in Chameleon. Accurately entering the report in Chameleon ensures accuracy of the statistical report submitted to the State for Local Rabies Control Activities.
Click on Windows > Activity > Bite Investigation.

steps.step: Select the Bite Type:
sub: DOG10: dog vs human.
CAT10: cat vs human.
BATVH: bat vs human, possible or verified exposure.
COYOTEVH: coyote vs human.
FERRETVH: ferret vs human.
FOXVH: fox vs human.
HORSEVH: horse vs human.
OPOSSUMVH: opossum vs human.
OTHERVH: other than what is listed vs human.
RACCOONVH: raccoon vs human.
SKUNKVH: skunk vs human.

steps: Enter Bite Date and Time based on line 12a of the official bite report.
Enter Report Date based on line 10 of the official bite report.
Enter the Activity No based on line 1c: enter the activity number, pop up (F4) the activity window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Enter TagNo based on line 36 of the official bite report and search for the current license of the biting animal.

steps.step: Enter Status: pop up (F4) and select based on the current status of the report:
sub: Yellow: 2nd copy of report received by Rabies Desk.
FRA: completed report, waiting for FRA result.
Pending APR: completed UTQ report, pending approval.
RTN FIELD/VET/OTHER: incomplete, returned to appropriate area for completion.
CLOSED: completed report, case closed.

sections.heading: Chameleon Bite Window: Owner, Incident, and Victim Information
steps: Owner ID: based on line 32a, pop up (F4) the person window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Animal ID: based on line 34, pop up (F4) the animal window, then press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Prior Bites: search based on the owner's address and owner's ID to find prior bite numbers, and enter the number of prior bites for the animal.
Valid Vac: based on line 39 of the official bite report, enter "Y" for yes or "N" for no.

steps.step: Controlled By (applicable only for bite type DOG10 or CAT10):
sub: Stray = no owner information.
Lic = owned, current license and vaccination.
Vacc = owned, current rabies vaccine but no license.
Novac = owned but no rabies vaccine or license.

steps: Incident Location: refer to line 11 of the official bite report. May be a street address, cross-streets, or landmark. Pop up (F4), enter the house number and first few letters of the street name and wildcard (%), and hit OK to search.
Jurisdiction: refer to the Jurisdiction of the activity.
Geo: refer to the Geocode of the activity.
Price: for home quarantine billing purposes.
Receipt No: if the officer collected a quarantine fee, enter the receipt number.
Victim ID: person victim; refer to line 2a of the official bite report. Pop up (F4) to bring up the person window and press the enter key to bring information back to the bite window.
Age: refer to line 4 of the official bite report.

steps.step: Relation:
sub: If the owner of the dog is also the victim, enter SELF.
If the victim lives in the same area as the owner, enter NEIGHBOR.
If the victim is visiting the dog owner, enter FRIEND.
If no owner, enter STRAY.

steps: Location: enter the general location of the bite or scratch. Add specifics to the synopsis.
Severity: enter puncture, scratch, scrape, or laceration.
Circumstance: enter "See Synops" and refer to line 13 of the official bite report.
Guardian: if the victim is a minor (0 to 17 years old), refer to line 7 of the official bite report.
Witness: enter witness information whether or not the biting animal is located, or if victim or owner information does not exist. Refer to line 9 of the official bite report.

sections.heading: Chameleon Bite Window: Treatment and Quarantine Information

steps.step: Treatment by: refer to line 17 of the official bite report.
sub: If treated by hospital or doctor, enter DOCTOR.
If treated by parent, enter PARENT.
If treated by self, enter SELF.

steps: Treatment date: refer to line 16 of the official bite report.
Desc: refer to line 16 (details) of the official bite report.
Begin Date: refer to line 40 of the official bite report.
By: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. Enter the Chameleon ID or first name initial and last name of the ACO, RVT, or VET.
Location: refer to line 40 of the official bite report. Enter "SHELTER" if the quarantined animal is impounded at a DAS shelter. Enter "HOME" if quarantine was imposed by DAS at home.
Abated by: refer to line 43 of the official bite report. Enter the Chameleon ID or first name initial and last name of the officer, vet, or RVT who released the animal from quarantine.
End Date: refer to line 43 of the official bite report. Enter the date the animal was released from quarantine.
Euth By: enter the Chameleon ID or first name initial and last name of the officer, vet, or RVT who euthanized the animal before the QR date.
Euth Date: refer to line 44 of the official bite report. Enter the date the animal was euthanized before the quarantine release date.
Notified By (for Rabies Desk only): refer to line 28 of the official bite report. Enter first name initial and last name.
Notify Date: refer to line 28 of the official bite report. Enter the date the victim was notified that the biting animal did not have rabies.
Print Letter: click on Reports, Run a Report, then Quarantine Observed Notification if the animal was quarantined.

sections.heading: Chameleon Bite Window: Lab Information (FRA)
steps: ToLab Date: refer to the "DLVR" Activity. Enter the date the specimen was delivered to the lab.
Exam Date: enter the same date as the ToLab Date.
Head Date: refer to the head results fax Accession Date. Enter the accession date as shown on the results fax.
Specimen No: refer to line 22 or the accession number on the results fax. Enter the numerical code given by the lab.
Disposition: enter HEAD TEST if the biting animal is submitted for FRA.
Head Result: refer to line 23 of the official bite report. Enter NEGATIVE, POSITIVE, or UNSAT.
Print Letter: click on Reports, Run a Report, then FRA Negative Notification if the animal was tested.

sections.heading: Chameleon Bite Window: Unable to Quarantine (UTQ) Transactions
steps: Status: if the disposition is UTQ, enter PENDING APR until reviewed. Once reviewed and signed by the Chief Veterinarian, enter CLOSED.

steps.step: Disposition:
sub: Enter UTQ if the biting animal was not quarantined because the biting animal or animal owner could not be found.
Enter UTQ-EUTH if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal was euthanized (does not include FRA).
Enter UTQ-DIED if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal died.

steps: Print Letter: click on Reports, Run a Report, then UTQ Notification in Jurisdiction if the animal was not quarantined.

related.name: Rabies Control Investigation Report (Animal Bite/Scratch Report)

appendices: Appendix A: Human vs Animal bite report process flowchart (September 16, 2014) (attached separately)
Appendix B: Sample completed Rabies Control Investigation Report, Human vs Pet with Owner (attached separately)
Appendix C: Sample completed Rabies Control Investigation Report, Human vs UTQ Stray (attached separately)
Appendix D: Sample Chameleon notification letters (Quarantine Observed, FRA Negative, UTQ) (attached separately)

revisions.author: Outcomes for Pets (draft)
revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2016 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.searchText: to establish procedures for the initiation and completion of the rabies control investigation report (animal bite/scratch report) when a human has been bitten or scratched by a wild or domestic mammal, excluding rabbits and rodents. all department of animal services (das) personnel who have knowledge of an animal biting or scratching a person or persons. director of animal services (approval); chief veterinarian or designee (case review and closure) fra test fluorescent rabies antibody laboratory test performed on a specimen submitted to the public health laboratory. utq unable to quarantine; the biting animal or animal owner could not be found, or the quarantine was not completed. qv quarantine visit. qr quarantine release. policy recognize that rabies is a fatal disease communicable to both animals and humans, and take preventive measures when humans or domestic animals come in contact with a rabies suspect wild animal by bites, scratches, or other types of exposure. {"step": "apply preventive measures that include, but are not limited to:", "sub": ["rabies vaccination of all dogs four months of age or older.", "confinement of all dogs less than four months of age.", "establishment of ownership and rabies vaccination status of all dogs by licensing.", "elimination of stray animals from the environment.", "thorough investigation of all reported bites and scratches inflicted by warm-blooded mammals, and proper isolation and observation of these warm-blooded mammals.", "thorough investigation of contacts between domestic pets and wild mammals, proper isolation and observation of these domestic pets, and laboratory examination of the wild animals.", "public education regarding rabies prevention."]} use the riverside county rabies control investigation report (animal bite/scratch report) to record all applicable information needed by public health authorities to properly evaluate human exposures from animal bites or scratches and to determine the need for rabies prophylactic treatment. the report is also used to report exposures of pet animals to known or suspected rabid animals. submission of report complete the form and submit it to: riverside county department of animal services, 6851 van buren blvd, jurupa valley ca 92509. agencies other than das submit the second copy on the day the investigation begins and the working copy when the investigation is completed. in cases where the behavior of the animal may indicate rabies, or in which the victim received a face, head, or neck exposure by a wild, stray, or rabies suspect animal, telephone a report of the incident immediately to das at (951) 358-7387. fax the completed report to das at (951) 358-7738, or deliver it in person. distribution of copies {"step": "white (original): the official form.", "sub": ["the original should successively be in the possession of each person who is taking action related to the incident.", "at the conclusion of an investigation, file the original at das. all signatures (report writer, investigators, owner, person placing animal in quarantine, person releasing from quarantine, veterinarian reviewing case, physician or veterinarian taking other action or closing the case, and the clerks making the necessary notification) must be on this copy."]} {"step": "yellow (second copy): the notification copy.", "sub": ["if agencies other than das are retaining the white copy to investigate the case, observe, isolate, or release the animal from quarantine, mail the yellow copy to das on the day the bite report is initiated. this notifies the chief veterinarian that a possible exposure has occurred and that action is being taken.", "fill out the original as completely as possible; do not retain the yellow form while an effort is made to complete the investigation."]} {"step": "goldenrod: the fra test copy.", "sub": ["submit the report with the specimen to the lab.", "the lab scans this report and returns a signed copy with the fra test results."]} {"step": "pink: the owner's notice to quarantine form.", "sub": ["the owner agrees to adhere to the quarantine imposed on their pet."]} {"step": "white (supplemental): reports and records actions and results during the course of the investigation.", "sub": ["the supplemental is to remain, and be filed, with the original copy of the bite report.", "date and initial or sign (first initial, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31 and the supplemental."]} general instructions treat the report as a legal document that should be easily read and understood. brevity, completeness, accuracy, and clearness are required. box print using a black ballpoint pen. police/radio codes are not acceptable. abbreviations familiar to a layperson are acceptable. 24-hour (military) time is acceptable. enter all applicable information on the report. if information is not applicable, enter "n/a". if the information is unknown, leave blank. mark boxes with an x, not a check mark. specific instructions: case and victim information (items 1a-17) 1a. imp # (impound number): enter the das animal id (if applicable). 1b. case #: enter the bite report number assigned to the case (created by rabies control). 1c. act #: enter the das activity number assigned to the case (if applicable). 2a. name: enter the name of the victim, last name first, followed by the first name and middle initial (if applicable). if the victim is unknown, leave the space blank and write details in section 31. 2b. victim (last name): enter the victim's last name. 3. sex (victim): mark the appropriate box. 4. age: enter the victim's age. if under one year, enter the number of months followed by "mos." if the victim is a minor, also complete item 7 (name of parent/guardian) and item 8 (address if different). 5. telephone: enter home and work telephone numbers of the victim. 6. address: enter residence number, direction (n, s, e, w), prefix (via, calle, etc.), street name, apartment or space number, city, state, and zip code. 7. parent/guardian (if victim is under 18 years of age): enter last name, first name, and middle initial (if applicable). 8. address of parent or guardian: use only if different from the victim's address. 9. source of information (required): mark the appropriate box. if "other," indicate source and telephone number. 10. report received by (required): enter first initial, last name, and badge number (if applicable) of the person initiating the report, and the date and time the report was received. 11. place of attack: enter information regarding the exact location of the bite/scratch. corresponding numbers from the report (for example, "in the street in front of #33") may be used. 12a. attack date/time: enter the numerical date of the bite/scratch and the time of day it occurred. 12b. date of bite: enter the date of the bite/scratch. 13. circumstances: enter exact information regarding the circumstances surrounding the bite/scratch. mark the appropriate box for the question "was the bite through material?" report only factual knowledge, not what an animal was thinking or why an animal bit or scratched. {"step": "14. wound location:", "sub": ["enter the exact part of the body that was bitten/scratched, together with \"right\" or \"left\" as appropriate (for example, left calf, right hand, right thumb).", "describe the wound as a scratch, puncture, or laceration (for example, multiple punctures, 3-inch laceration).", "if the bite/scratch did not cause a break in the skin, enter \"no skin break.\" indicate whether the wound resulted from the animal's teeth or claws if known."]} {"step": "15. skin break:", "sub": ["mark the box and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable), if there is a bite/scratch that caused a break in the skin.", "if no skin break, write \"no\" and initial the line, including badge number (if applicable).", "skin breaks reported by a physician or hospital do not need to be verified."]} 16. wound treatment: enter the date the victim received treatment, including the details of the treatment. if no treatment was given, advise the victim (parent/guardian) to consult a doctor regarding wound care. 17. treated by: mark the appropriate box. if a physician rendered treatment, enter the physician's name (or secondarily the hospital/facility) and the telephone number. specific instructions: veterinary, laboratory, and closure (items 19-30) 19. anti-rabies treatment (for use by the riverside county chief veterinarian or clinic veterinarian only): mark the appropriate box and enter "recommended." 20. veterinary review (for use by the riverside county chief veterinarian or clinic veterinarian only): enter responses on applicable lines. 21-26. laboratory exam - fra test (for laboratory use only): enter responses on applicable lines. {"step": "27. case closed (for use by the rabies control desk): the chief veterinarian or designee will review the report and will mark the appropriate boxes and date and sign the report in the following cases:", "sub": ["when a stray biting animal was never located.", "when laboratory exam results are positive for rabies.", "when the victim or biting animal is located outside of riverside county.", "when there are unusual circumstances in the report and the chief veterinarian needs to review before closure."]} 28. notification (for use by rabies desk clerk): mark the appropriate box, date, and sign. 29. qv visit date: conduct quarantine visits on all animals that do not have a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite. five (5) days for dogs and cats and seven (7) days for all other animals. 30. qr date: enter the biting animal's quarantine release date. a telephone release may be allowed for any animal that had a current rabies vaccination at the time of the bite or any animal quarantined at a veterinary hospital. all other releases will be a field release, indicated by an "fr" placed in the box by the correcting sergeant or rabies control desk. specific instructions: non-numbered items and item 31 map ref. (map reference): enter the riverside county thomas guide street guide and directory reference for both victim and owner (if applicable). {"step": "qt fee (quarantine fee): mark the appropriate box.", "sub": ["fee collected: write the receipt number in the space provided.", "fee not collected: if not applicable, mark \"no.\" if the fee is to be billed, write \"billed\" in the receipt number section."]} supplemental: mark this box when the supplemental (attached to the bite report) is initiated. the person initiating the supplemental fills out the victim and owner information at the top of the page along with boxes 2b, 12b, and 32b (victim, date of bite, and owner) along the side of the page. {"step": "31. miscellaneous:", "sub": ["use the spaces in line 31 to record pertinent information about the investigation.", "line 31 may also be used to complete any other line on the report when additional space is needed.", "do not use this space for extraneous information such as \"no one home.\" write that type of information on the supplemental investigation report.", "date and initial or sign (first name, last name, and badge number) all entries on line 31."]} specific instructions: biting animal information (items 32a-45) {"step": "32a. owner (custodian):", "sub": ["line out the non-applicable word.", "enter the name of the owner or custodian (last, first, middle).", "if the name previously entered is different from the actual owner of the animal, enter the owner's name, address, and phone number on line 31.", "if the animal is a stray, enter the word \"stray.\"", "enter both home and work (if applicable) telephone numbers."]} 32b. owner: enter the owner's last name. if the animal is a stray, leave blank; the rabies control desk will enter "stray" before filing the report. 33. address: enter the address where the animal is to be quarantined. if the address previously entered is different from the animal owner's actual address, enter the owner's address on line 31. enter the numerical, direction (n, s, e, w), street name, apartment or space number, city, state, and zip code. {"step": "34. animal type:", "sub": ["mark the appropriate box. if other, enter the type of animal.", "breed: enter the breed of the animal.", "name: enter the animal's name.", "sex: mark the appropriate box describing the biting animal's sex, \"male\" or \"female.\" write an \"n\" above the appropriate box if the animal is verified as having been altered.", "est. age: enter the age of the animal, according to p&p 700.12. if unknown, enter an estimated age. if under one year, enter the number of months or weeks followed by \"mos.\" or \"wks.\""]} 35. description: enter the color(s) of the biting animal. if the animal is a stray, enter all other distinguishing characteristics (long hair, short hair, large, small, pointed ears) that could be used to identify the animal. 36. license: enter the current license number and the license jurisdiction (if riverside county, mark the box) of the animal. enter the license expiration date. if the license is expired, or if the dog should be licensed and is not, issue a citation and enter the citation number in the space provided. 37. behavior: a live animal must be observed before it can be quarantined. upon quarantine, mark the appropriate box, "normal" or "abnormal." if the animal's behavior is abnormal, enter the specifics in section 31. if the animal is deceased or stray, mark the box "unknown." {"step": "38. travels:", "sub": ["out of the county: ascertain whether the animal has been outside riverside county in the past 6 months and mark the appropriate box. if \"yes,\" indicate when and where.", "out of the usa: ascertain whether the animal has been outside the usa in the past year and mark the appropriate box. if \"yes,\" indicate when and where. if a detailed explanation is needed, enter \"supp\" and make necessary notations on the supplemental."]} {"step": "39. vaccination:", "sub": ["boxes: mark the appropriate box.", "kind: enter the type of rabies vaccine given, if available.", "date given: enter the date the rabies vaccination was given, if available.", "expiration date: enter the date the rabies vaccination expires.", "given by: enter the name of the veterinarian or veterinary clinic that vaccinated the animal, if available, or indicate \"per comp.\" if the information was obtained by a computer records check through dispatch."]} {"step": "40. quarantine:", "sub": ["boxes: mark the appropriate box describing the place of quarantine. \"#33\" refers to the address listed on line 33.", "kennel #: used for animals quarantined at the riverside county animal shelter. a kennel number is required for live animals. for deceased animals write \"r morgue\" and place the animal in the fra test section.", "impound tag: the other id assigned to the impounded animal.", "microchip #: used to enter the microchip number when the impounded animal is scanned.", "other: used when the animal is quarantined at any other location. list the exact address in line 31.", "if the animal is quarantined at the victim's residence, write \"#6\" or the actual address in the space provided.", "quarantine imposed (required). date: enter the date quarantine is imposed, numerically.", "by: enter the first initial, last name, and badge number of the officer imposing quarantine.", "agency: riverside county officers mark the box. city agencies mark the box labeled \"city of\" and write in the city name."]} 41. observed ok: enter the date, initials, and badge number (if applicable) when the animal is observed healthy. use this line for quarantine visits (including daily shelter visits by vet staff), stray bite patrols, and when an animal is observed ok during the quarantine period and at the time of quarantine release. 42. not observed: enter the date next to the word "date" and initials and badge number next to the word "initials" when the animal is not observed. use this line for stray bite patrols and when an animal is not observed before quarantine is imposed. note: an animal must be observed before quarantine can be imposed. {"step": "43. release:", "sub": ["boxes: mark the appropriate box (if applicable) to describe how the animal was released, \"by telephone\" or \"upon inspection.\"", "date: enter the date the animal is released from quarantine.", "by: enter the first initial, last name, and badge number of the person releasing the animal from quarantine.", "agency: riverside county officers mark the box. city agencies mark the box labeled \"city of\" and write in the city name."]} 44. cause of death: enter (if applicable) the date the death of the biting animal occurred and mark the appropriate box or boxes describing the cause. 45. veterinarian/symptoms: mark (if applicable) the appropriate box indicating whether a veterinarian observed the animal prior to the animal's death. mark the appropriate box relating to symptoms as obtained by the observing veterinarian prior to the animal's death. {"step": "agreement for quarantine compliance and signature:", "sub": ["date: enter the date quarantine is imposed.", "id #: enter a driver's license or id number of the animal owner or custodian in the space provided and indicate the state of issuance if other than california.", "owner: obtain the signature of the animal owner who will be responsible for the animal during the quarantine.", "custodian: obtain the signature of the custodian of the animal (if applicable) who will be responsible for the animal during the quarantine."]} veterinarian or home quarantines with the approval of the chief of veterinary services, clinic veterinarian, or rabies control desk, any severely sick, injured, or elderly animal requiring medical treatment may be quarantined at an outside private veterinarian or at the owner's home. these types of quarantines may also be approved in unusual or abnormal situations by the chief of veterinary services, clinic veterinarian, or rabies control desk. indicate any quarantine so approved in section 31 or on the supplemental of the bite-scratch report, including who is approving the quarantine and the circumstances necessitating quarantine somewhere other than das. impose and release all quarantines at an outside private veterinarian through an animal control officer. when imposing quarantines, the officer will direct the veterinarian to sign the bite report as the custodian. no fee will be collected for these quarantines. all quarantines at an outside private veterinarian may be released via telephone. shelter quarantines {"step": "in accordance with food and agriculture code 2606(b)(2), impose shelter quarantine at the department of animal services on the following basis:", "sub": ["when the dog bites above the shoulders.", "severity of the bite.", "when the owner releases the dog for euthanasia.", "a prior bite history to any part of the body by the dog.", "an unprovoked bite to a child.", "the dog is unvaccinated.", "any medical index of suspicion that the dog may be rabid."]} the quarantine period shall be 10 days from quarantined because the biting animal or animal owner could not be found.", "enter utq-euth if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal was euthanized (does not include fra).", "enter utq-died if the quarantine was not completed because the biting animal died."]} print letter: click on reports, run a report, then utq notification in jurisdiction if the animal was not quarantined. food and agriculture code section 2606(b)(2) p&p 700.12 (animal age determination) rabies control investigation report (animal bite/scratch report) form das policy on annual report of local rabies control activities

documents.title: Fluorescent Rabies Antibody (FRA) Test Specimen Preparation
documents.purpose: As stated in the original SOP: to provide consistent processing of records requested by the public which will ensure timely response or notification from the Department of Animal Services (DAS) to the public. Note: this purpose statement in the source document does not match the procedure content, which covers preparation of animal specimens for fluorescent rabies antibody testing following euthanasia. Flagged for correction in pass 2.
documents.authority: Authorized veterinary staff must sign off on the bite report and euthanasia before an FRA is performed.
documents.scope: Staff who perform euthanasia and prepare specimens for rabies testing.
documents.supersedes: None. Original SOP effective May 27, 2009.
references: Euthanasia SOP

definitions.def: Fluorescent rabies antibody test, performed on brain tissue to determine whether an animal was rabid.
Owner turn-in (owner surrender).

sections.heading: A. Requirements Before Performing an FRA
steps: If the animal is an OTI, obtain a signed euthanasia request form and the white copy of the bite report filled out and signed by authorized veterinary staff. The gray area, items 19 and 20 on the bite report, will be for the FRA. Note: check to make sure the owner has paid the fee for the FRA. If the bite occurred 10 days ago or more, there is no need to FRA.

If the animal is a stray with a bite report, quarantine the animal for a minimum of the required stray holding time. You may then FRA the animal after it has been signed off for euthanasia and the white copy of the bite report has been filled out and signed by authorized veterinary staff. The gray area, items 19 and 20 on the bite report, will be for the FRA. Place the remaining copies of the bite report in the Bite Report box by the spay and neuter clinic.
Proceed with the DAS protocols for identification and humane euthanasia of the animal.
Check to make sure the animal is deceased using DAS protocols.

Place the animal in the designated area or a covered cage out of public view until you are ready to remove the head from the body using one of the methods below. Cut from the mid-neckline to the base of the skull, making sure the brain cavity is intact.

sections.heading: B. Specimen Preparation Method 1: Guillotine (caution: this is very sharp)
steps: Make sure the blade is in the up position.
Place the head through the opening so that the neck is below the blade, making sure nothing else is in the way of the blade.
Wind the crank on top of the machine down until the head is completely severed from the body. Watch out for blood.
You may wrap the head in newspaper, to soak up excess blood, and place it in a black bag. Tie the bag closed.

Using the stapler, attach the white copy of the signed bite report and a copy of the kennel card to the outside of the bag above the knot to ensure the bag is not punctured. Place the bag with the head into the morgue.
Place the rest of the body in a barrel in the freezer for disposal.

sections.heading: C. Specimen Preparation Method 2: Knives, Axe, Cleaver, Scalpel, or Other Cutting Tools
steps: Cut through the skin and muscle layers to expose the neck bones.
Cut through the bones, detaching the head completely.
You may wrap the head in newspaper, to soak up excess blood, and place it in a black bag. Tie the bag closed.

Attach the white copy of the signed bite report and a copy of the kennel card to the outside of the bag above the knot, using a stapler, to ensure the bag is not punctured. Place the bag with the head into the morgue.
Place the rest of the body in a barrel in the freezer for disposal.

revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2009 version; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Owner Requested Euthanasia Criteria
documents.purpose: To establish the criteria for accepting pets for owner requested euthanasia, as changed by the executive team effective immediately upon issuance of the August 22, 2014 memorandum.
documents.authority: Executive Team; issued by the Chief Veterinarian, Veterinary Services.
documents.scope: All staff.
documents.supersedes: All-Staff Memorandum, Owner Requested Euthanasia, August 22, 2014
references: Owner surrender policy and procedure (existing at time of issuance)

definitions.def: Situations where the animal's pain, suffering, or condition is irreversible, protracted, and not alleviated with medications, and where the prognosis is very poor or grave. This determination is usually made by veterinary staff or field officers. Keeping such animals alive might constitute cruelty.

sections.heading: Criteria for Accepting Owner Requested Euthanasia
steps: Accept an animal for owner requested euthanasia only if it fits into one of the two categories below.

Category 1: Animals which have been determined to be Irremediably Suffering. This determination is usually made by veterinary staff or field officers and is generally defined as situations where the animal's pain, suffering, or condition is irreversible, protracted, and not alleviated with medications, and where the prognosis is very poor or grave. Keeping such animals alive might constitute cruelty.

steps.step: Category 2: Animals with a documented history of aggressive behavior as evidenced by any of the following:
sub: A current or past bite report and investigation which documents an unprovoked bite on a person.
Any animal which has been previously determined to be potentially dangerous, dangerous, or vicious by an Animal Control Officer, hearing officer, magistrate, or other jurisdiction.

steps: Pet owners whose animals do not meet these criteria may choose to surrender their pets to the department. Staff are reminded to engage the existing owner surrender policy and procedure in those circumstances.

revisions.desc: Migrated into standard RCDAS SOP template. Content unchanged from 2014 memorandum; substantive edits pending (see edit log).

documents.title: Protocol for Handling Expired Drugs Including Controlled Drugs
documents.purpose: To provide consistent guidelines for the monitoring, removal, and proper disposal of expired drugs or materials.
documents.authority: Chief Veterinarian or designee
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable only to the designated DAS employees charged with the inventory of pharmaceuticals.
documents.supersedes: SOP Protocol for Handling Expired Drugs, including Controlled Drugs, effective October 26, 2016 (revised from November 24, 2014). Absorbed duplicate file: SOP Protocol for Handling Expired Drugs_rev20161026 (identical content).
references: Controlled Substances Act (cited in original as 21 CFR 130)
40 CFR 261.33 (federal hazardous waste regulations)
DEA guidance on reverse distribution of controlled substances

definitions.def: Any substance or chemical designed to be administered by injection, inhalation, topical application, ingestion, electroporation, or suppository that is intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease in animals (examples: intravenous (IV) fluids, triple antibiotic ointment). This definition excludes food items normally consumed by the applicable species.

Any article intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease in humans or animals, and articles intended to affect the structure or any function of the body of humans or animals. Examples: gauze, suture, osmotic mini pumps, catheters.

The date printed on the label or package for materials with a manufacturer's expiration date. For dilutions, preparations, reconstitutions, or mixtures of drugs or fluids prepared using sterile technique and under proper storage conditions, the expiration date should be ONE MONTH from the date of preparation, unless otherwise indicated. Such materials should be labeled BY NAME and drug concentration, and include the new expiration date as soon as they are prepared. Secondary containers that hold an unadulterated solution (a drug or material from an original stock to which no other drug has been added) should be clearly labeled with the name of the drug or material and the expiration date of the original stock. An item is considered expired the day after the month or date indicated on the label; for example, an item labeled Aug. 2013 would be considered expired on Sept. 1, 2013.
Expired materials may be disposed of as municipal (regular trash) waste. Examples: unused gauze, unused suture material with no needle, unused bandage material.

definitions.term: Non-Controlled Pharmaceuticals, Both Hazardous and Non-Hazardous
definitions.def: Drugs classified as hazardous must be disposed of in compliance with federal regulations (40 CFR 261.33). Examples of hazardous drugs: cisplatin, chloramphenicol, fluorouracil, streptozocin, tamoxifen, or steroids such as testosterone, estradiol, megesterol. Whether hazardous or non-hazardous, all unused vials, expired drugs, or unwanted drugs should be disposed of properly.

Medical materials that have a component capable of puncturing or penetrating a plastic bag. Examples: needles, syringes, scalpels. Sharps that have come into contact with blood or bodily fluids should be disposed of in an infectious waste sharps container.

Expired controlled substances must be kept separate from non-expired drugs, but under the required secure storage conditions in accordance with the Controlled Substances Act (cited in the original as 21 CFR 130), labeled as "Expired," and disposed of by approved means in compliance with the Controlled Substances Act, through the reverse distribution pathway provided by the Department's current pharmaceutical vendor.

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Order all pharmaceuticals and supplies on a "Just in Time" basis to minimize inventory and assure that all drugs are utilized well before their expiration date.
Should any pharmaceutical or supply become "Short Dated," transfer and utilize these substances at one of the high volume shelters to assure compliance and avoid waste.
Return expired controlled substances separately from each shelter through reverse distribution according to DEA guidance; they are the exception to the transfer rule.

sections.heading: Procedure
steps: The Chief Veterinarian or designee is responsible for instituting adequate inventory management procedures to ensure that expired drugs and medical materials are identified and properly disposed of in a timely fashion. Expired drugs and medical materials have unknown purity, potency, stability, and efficacy. As such, their use in live animals is not an acceptable standard of veterinary practice and does not constitute appropriate veterinary care.
The Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT) is responsible for performing pharmaceutical inventory, including controlled drugs, related to the expiration date.
The RVT will remove or segregate expired drugs or pharmaceuticals and notify the distributor or manufacturer to arrange pick-up.

related.name: Employee Acknowledgment, Protocol for Handling Expired Drugs, including Controlled Drugs
Controlled Substances Act; 40 CFR 261.33; DEA reverse distribution guidance

appendices: Appendix A: Employee Acknowledgment form (attached separately)

documents.title: Rabies Lot Number Information Sharing
documents.purpose: To establish a Rabies Lot Number Information Sharing procedure.
documents.authority: Veterinary Services (opening technician assigned to receiving)
documents.scope: Opening technician assigned to receiving; front counter licensing, rabies, adoptions, and rescue teams.
documents.supersedes: SOP Rabies Lot Number Information Sharing, effective May 9, 2022

sections.heading: Procedure
steps: The opening technician assigned to receiving will seek out the rabies lot number and expiration date.
Share this information in the public services Microsoft Teams chat, distributed to the front counter licensing, rabies, adoptions, and rescue teams.
Send the chat no later than 9:00 AM daily.

documents.title: Shot Clinics
documents.purpose: To establish uniform guidelines to be utilized at the Riverside County off-site or on-site rabies clinics.
documents.authority: The Animal Services Chief or lead Office Assistant III designee ensures the smooth operation of the Rabies Clinic.
documents.scope: This procedure applies to all personnel working the rabies clinics.
documents.supersedes: SOP Shot Clinics, effective January 20, 2012
references: Health and Safety Code 121690
California Compendium of Rabies Control

sections.heading: Policy
steps: Comply with Health and Safety Code 121690 and the California Compendium of Rabies Control, which require that the County shall provide or arrange for canine rabies vaccination clinics in the community.
Hold on-site low cost rabies clinics with veterinary services on the first Wednesday of each month from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM and the third Saturday of each month from 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM.
The Animal Services Chief or lead Office Assistant III designee ensures the smooth operation of the Rabies Clinic.

sections.heading: Setting Up: Two Weeks Prior to the Clinic
steps: Email an overtime request to staff to staff the rabies clinic and arrange with the volunteer coordinator for additional assistance from volunteers to manage the line.

steps.step: Assign each staff member a shot clinic duty. The assigned duties for staff are:
sub: Data Entry clerk.
Front Counter cashier.

steps: Follow up on the email previously sent.

sections.heading: Setting Up: Day of the Clinic
steps: The assigned lead confirms with all participants the time the rabies clinic starts.
Verify that the materials are prepared: shot clinic forms, clipboards, pens, shot clinic vaccinations and fees, license tags, laptops, and starting cash.
Set up the data entry location with laptops if needed, or at admissions.
Confer with the volunteer coordinator on preparation for managing the line for the rabies clinic.
Direct staff to arrive at the clinic at 9:45 AM, prior to the scheduled time of the clinic.
Obtain and provide staff with the VET ID, the type of rabies vaccination, and the lot number.
Resolve all issues that arise out of the shot clinic.

steps.step: Brief all participating staff and volunteers on the flow of the shot clinic:
sub: Volunteers will be directed to have customers complete the shot clinic form (one form for each animal) and provide the vaccination and fees handout to customers.

Data entry clerks will be reminded to log into Chameleon with the SC location; review all records such as license fees, penalties, citations, and vaccination issues with the customer; ensure the customer understands that all issues will only be addressed at the data entry desks, as disputes will not be addressed at the cashiers; store complete and accurate license and microchip records using the Chameleon Tag/Link window; store a complete Treatment window for each animal; and write the PID and AID on the designated lines of the shot clinic form or microchip form.

Cashiers will be reminded to log into Chameleon with the SC location; update license numbers and microchip numbers in the Tag window; observe cash handling procedures; and not address disputes at the cashier stations.

steps.step: Monitor and assist staff and the rabies clinic veterinarian in the flow of duties in all assigned areas of the rabies clinic. Data entry clerks, based on the form completed by the customer, process licensing, shots, and microchips as follows:
sub: Store a new license, or renew a license, using the Chameleon Tag/Link window with a tag subtype of SHOT CLINI.
If a rabies vaccination is being requested, enter the rabies vaccination information according to procedures.
Store a microchip record with the following information: Tag type = MCHIP; Tag subtype = AVID; leave rabies vaccination information blank when selling a microchip with a license; Price = $20.

Store a Treatment window for each animal with the following information: Visit type = EXAM; Condition = BAR; Treat by = name of the RVT or VT; after storing, Treatment type = MICROCHIP with Price = 0; Medication = name of chosen vaccines with price based on the medication.
Write the PID and AID on the form.
Write the amount to be paid by the customer on the form.

steps.step: Cashiers process payments as follows:
sub: Log into location SC in Chameleon.
From the Tag/Link window, bring up the record based on the PID or AID.
If the customer is purchasing a microchip, enter the microchip number in the Tag No field, replacing the U number created by the data entry clerk, and update.
Enter the microchip lot number in the Batch Number field.
If the customer is purchasing a license, replace the U number with the city or county license number and update.
If the customer is a City of Riverside resident, explain that if payment is by credit card or check, payment will be separated according to license and microchip and/or vaccinations.
Pop up the Receipt window from the Treatment window.
Review with the customer the items they are purchasing and the total cost.
Collect payment according to procedure.
Store the receipt.
Print two receipts and the rabies vaccination certificate if purchased.
Attach one receipt, the stamped rabies certificate, and the license tag with the microchip form and syringe, and give them to the veterinary staff or assisting volunteer.
Attach the second receipt with the payment.
Balance payments received at the end of the shot clinic according to procedures.
Do not address disputes; cashiers handle collection of payments only.

steps.step: Data entry clerks:
sub: Shut down laptops if not in admissions, disconnect power plugs and data cables, and store them in the designated laptop bag.
Collect all shot clinic materials.
Deliver shot clinic materials and the laptop bag to the lead Office Assistant III.
Return tables and chairs to their original setup prior to the shot clinic.

steps.step: Cashiers:
sub: Separate the starting cash ($35) from deposits and place it in an envelope.
Run an adding machine tape for checks, cash, and credit card payments (one for city and one for county).
Run an adding machine tape for receipts (one for city and one for county).
Verify that adding machine totals and daily deposit batch slip totals match the actual deposit totals.
If not able to balance, recheck all monies and receipts, then contact the shot clinic lead.
Print the daily deposit batch slip report.
Submit to the lead for verification, review, and signature.

steps.step: Lead Office Assistant III:
sub: Review and verify all cash deposits at the end of the shot clinic. Ensure all deposits are balanced and discrepancies are resolved the same day as the shot clinic.
Run the credit card machine report.
Put the starting cash, daily deposit batch slip, credit card detail report, and deposit in an envelope.
Two or more staff members must drop the deposit in the safe.
Return and secure laptops in the designated area to be returned to the Chief the next day.
Store shot clinic materials in the designated area for the next clinic.

sections.heading: Day After the Clinic

steps.step: The shot clinic lead will fill in the Rabies Clinic Log with the following information:
sub: Date of the clinic.
Name of the lead.
Names and assigned duties of staff members.
Name of the volunteer group, if applicable.

steps: The shot clinic lead will email the log to the rabies desk and copy the manager.

steps.step: The Rabies Desk will:
sub: Run the shot clinic account code detail report to obtain the licensing revenues collected, the number of microchips sold, and the number of license transactions.
Obtain from the manager the number of dogs and cats rabies vaccinated.
Update the main Rabies Clinic Log based on the information obtained from the account code detail report, the manager, and the rabies clinic log provided by the lead Office Assistant III.
Print the month's completed rabies clinic log.
Add the printed page to the Rabies Clinic book located at the Rabies Desk.
At the end of the calendar year, submit accumulated data to the manager for the completion of the Annual Rabies Control statistics.
