documents.title: Spay or Neuter Appointment Email Request Procedure
documents.purpose: To provide consistent guidelines for maintaining and processing spay/neuter appointment emails. This procedure is also intended to decrease the need to return telephone calls until the customer pays a spay or neuter deposit ($25).
documents.authority: Department of Animal Services
documents.scope: This procedure is applicable only to staff who respond to spay or neuter appointment requests received through the RVSD SN Hotmail account.
documents.supersedes: SOP Spay or Neuter Appointment Hotmail Procedure, effective August 17, 2015
references: Policy 000-01, Chameleon General Procedure

sections.heading: Processing Incoming Appointment Request Emails

steps.step: Staff who access the email account will create a Chameleon Contact window. Go to Windows, then Person, then Contact, and complete the following:
sub: Enter the PID for the person requesting surgery. Update the PID with any new information from their email (new address, phone number, email, etc.). Make sure the email address is copied correctly and accurately; this is key.
Contact Type: EMAIL.
Subtype: SN APPT RQ.
Date and Time: the date and time their email was sent requesting an appointment.
Status: PENDING.
Sub-Status: the species with weight/sex/animal type: <50 DOG F, <50 DOG M, >50 DOG F, >50 DOG M, or CAT.
Store the Contact window.

steps: Based on the stored Contact window, a Chameleon postmaster generated email will respond to the customer and will enter the date the email was sent in the Extra1 field. The response text reads: "Thank you for contacting the County of Riverside Spay and Neuter Clinic. This email is in response to your inquiry for our services. We apologize, but there are no appointments available at this time. We perform spay and neuter surgeries Tuesday through Friday each week except for holidays. Please respond to this email with what day of the week is best for you. Once an appointment becomes available, we will contact you at the email provided. A $25 non-refundable deposit will be required to confirm your appointment. Thank you and have a great day!"

sections.heading: When the Customer Responds with a Preferred Day
steps: Access the Contact window based on the PID.
Pop up the PID of the customer.
Click on the person's head icon.
Enter 8:00 AM (start) and 17:00 (end) in military time.
Store (F9).

sections.heading: When the Customer Declines an Appointment
steps: Open the Chameleon Contact window.
Perform a search (F12) based on the PID.
Change the status to COMPLETED.
Update (F8).

sections.heading: When Veterinary Staff Announces Additional Slots on the SN Calendar
steps: Run the Contact Tracking Report. The report will be based on the oldest email date to the most current.
The report will show customers and their preferred available days.
Match customers with the spay/neuter appointment slots on the calendar.
Enter the PID on the spay/neuter appointment slot with your user ID to ensure no one takes that slot.
Contact the customer to collect the spay/neuter deposit by telephone and obtain complete animal information.
Once the deposit is collected and a Tag/Link window is stored, enter the appointment date in the animal ID with the correct Group/Vet ID.

related.name: Contact window (Contact Type EMAIL, Subtype SN APPT RQ)

documents.title: Spay-Neuter No Show Management Program
documents.purpose: The Western Riverside spay/neuter clinic and San Jacinto Animal Campus spay/neuter clinic provide 30 to 60 spay/neuter appointment slots for customers who wish to take advantage of the department's low cost spay/neuter program. The demand for these appointments often exceeds what the department offers. Customers who do not provide a 24-hour notice to reschedule or cancel their appointment create an empty slot that staff cannot fill because of pre-surgery requirements.
documents.authority: Department of Animal Services
documents.scope: Applicable to all DAS employees who schedule spay/neuter appointments.
documents.supersedes: SOP Spay/Neuter No Show Management Program, effective February 11, 2016 (revised from May 13, 2015)
references: Riverside County Board of Supervisors Ordinance 630.16 §12.A.2
Animal Services Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Policy
SOP How to Document a Spay/Neuter Appointment using the Chameleon Animal Window

sections.heading: Procedure
steps: Once the customer agrees to the spay/neuter appointment date, collect a spay/neuter deposit of $25 for each animal.
Collect the deposit either through telephone credit card payment or at the front counters.
Use the item code of S/N Fee - Dog or S/N Fee - Cat.
Apply the deposit as partial payment toward the spay/neuter fee.
The deposit is non-refundable.
The deposit is non-transferrable to another appointment unless the customer contacts the clinic with a 24-hour notice to reschedule an appointment.
The spay/neuter appointment is only confirmed upon receipt of the spay/neuter deposit.

related.name: How to Document a Spay/Neuter Appointment using the Chameleon Animal Window
Receipt window (item codes S/N Fee - Dog, S/N Fee - Cat)

documents.title: Temporary Spay-Neuter Waiver Acknowledgement Form
documents.purpose: To establish a consistent procedure for adopted dogs or cats released to a purchaser or adopter that are not spayed or neutered.
documents.authority: Department of Animal Services; veterinary staff must certify that a dog or cat is too sick or injured to be spayed or neutered before release unaltered.
documents.scope: This applies to all Department of Animal Services (DAS) employees who are responsible for adoptions.
documents.supersedes: Temporary Spay/Neuter Waiver/Acknowledgement Form Standard Operating Procedure, effective September 29, 2009
references: Food and Agriculture Code 31751.3 and 31751.7
State Mandate AB 1856 (cited in the Spay/Neuter Contract form)
Policy 000-01, General Chameleon Information
Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window
Animal Adoptions Standard Operating Procedure

sections.heading: Requirements
steps: No healthy dog or cat impounded at DAS can be sold or given away to a new adopter without being spayed or neutered unless veterinary staff certifies that the dog or cat is too sick or injured, or that it would be otherwise detrimental to the health of the dog or cat, to be spayed or neutered.
The new purchaser or adopter will pay DAS a spay/neuter deposit of $40 and sign a spay/neuter contract agreement with DAS.
A full deposit refund will be received by the purchaser or adopter if proof of sterility signed by a licensed veterinarian is provided within 30 days.
The dog or cat shall be spayed or neutered within 14 business days from the date of the agreement.

sections.heading: Chameleon Process
steps: Go to the Chameleon Kennel screen.
Follow animal adoption procedures (SOP Animal Adoption).
Tab over to the Tag screen and press F4 to pop up the screen.

steps.step: Store a Tag/Link record prior to proceeding with the adoption to generate a spay or neuter waiver:
sub: In the Cert No field, enter CONTRACT.
For any adoption, a microchip record is stored for cats and dogs.
The Kennel animal ID must match the Tag/Link animal ID.

steps: Press the F9 key to store the record.
Press the Enter key to bring the license, link, and/or microchip information back to the Kennel screen.
The new adopter from the Tag screen automatically populates the outcome Person To field in the Kennel window.
Go to Outcome Type, press F4 to pop up, and select Adoption.
Go to Outcome Sub-type, press F4 to pop up the list of validations (refer to DAS Policy 000-03, Chameleon Kennel Window), and enter CONTRACT.
Go to Date and press F5 to insert today's date.
Go to Time and press F5 to insert the current time, then go to By and type in your initials.
Press the F8 key to update the Kennel window.

Go to the Receipt No field and press F4 to bring up the Receipt window. Chameleon will perform an automatic debt lookup from the Tag/Link window, balance dues, Treatment windows, and current and prior kennel debts, and will populate the grid with the item codes of fees owed.

steps.step: The following fees will be charged at the Riverside and Banning shelters. Dog adoption, sick or injured (4 months and older):
sub: ADOPTION DOG (includes vaccines and S/N fee): $95.00.
MCHIP ADPT: $10.00.
LICENSE SN: $6.00.
SPAY/NEUTER DEPOSIT: $40.00.

steps.step: Cat adoption, sick or injured (2 months and older):
sub: ADOPTION CAT (includes vaccines and S/N fee): $65.00.
SPAY/NEUTER DEPOSIT: $40.00.
MCHIP ADPT: $10.00.

steps.step: Make sure the following information is contained in each of the items charged:
sub: Animal ID.
Reference No.
Fee Type.
Dept.
Item Price.

steps: Verify from the customer the actual city residency for applicable city codes via government ID (CDL, CID, etc.).
Review all charges.
Inquire from the customer the mode of payment for the transaction, i.e., cash, check, or credit card.
Store the receipt upon payment and print.

steps.step: All three copies must be signed by the customer as an acknowledgement of the following:
sub: A 7-day grace period of exchange is provided for any medical or behavior problems.
If the animal is not spayed or neutered, 24 hours is provided for pick-up after veterinary staff notification to the owner.
Financial expenses incurred after adoption are the sole responsibility of the customer.

steps: If the dog or cat is certified to go home without being spayed or neutered, print two copies of the Spay/Neuter contract report from the drop-down menu for veterinary staff to review with the purchaser and obtain a signature.
Press Enter to bring the receipt number back to the outcome Receipt No field, then press F8 to update the Kennel window.

related.name: Kennel window (Outcome Type ADOPTION, Sub-type CONTRACT)
Spay/Neuter Contract (Chameleon Crystal report: Sterilization contract.rpt)

appendices: Appendix A: Sample Spay/Neuter Contract (source document, dated October 5, 2009; states the spay/neuter deposit covers the cost of the procedure up to $40 for a dog or $25 for a cat, instructs the adopter to contact the Riverside Spay/Neuter Clinic for an appointment in 7 to 10 days or when medication is completed, and directs that changes in the animal's condition be reported to shelter veterinary staff at (951) 358-7373, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM)
Appendix B: Tag/Link window screenshot (source document)

documents.title: Treatable vs Un-Treatable Medical-Behavior Subtype
documents.purpose: To define, for purposes of counting euthanasia of treatable medical/behavior versus un-treatable medical/behavior animals, which animals are considered untreatable, and to standardize the Chameleon outcome subtypes used to record them.
documents.authority: Chief Veterinarian, Veterinary Services.
documents.scope: All veterinary and kennel/adoptions staff.
documents.supersedes: Memorandum to All Veterinary and Kennel/Adoptions Staff, Treatable vs. Un-Treatable Medical/Behavior Subtype, February 16, 2017
references: California State Law definitions of potentially dangerous dog, vicious dog, and aggressive animal incident (as applied during legal hearing processes)

definitions.def: Any dog which has been declared a potentially dangerous dog, has been deemed vicious, or has an aggressive animal incident as defined by California State Law during any legal hearing process.
Any animal eligible for euthanasia based on behavior considerations that has a bite history.
In a manner, or to a degree, that prevents remedy, cure, or correction.
In the state of dying, approaching death, being in a state of inactivity or obsolescence.

sections.heading: Classification Rule
steps: Effective immediately, for purposes of counting euthanasia done on treatable medical/behavior versus un-treatable medical/behavior animals, consider all animals euthanized to be treatable medical or treatable behavior unless they meet one of the four untreatable categories: Declared Dangerous/Vicious/AAI, Bite History, Irremediably Suffering, or Moribund.
Only animals meeting one of the four definitions will be considered untreatable. Animals not meeting the criteria are to be labeled treatable-medical/behavior.
All staff shall print Attachment A (Untreatable Definitions) and Attachment B (Chameleon SOP for Animals Meeting the Criteria for Untreatable) as a reference for the euthanasia room.

steps.step: In the Kennel window, use the outcome Type EUTH and select Subtype UNTRT-BEHAVIOR for animals meeting either of the following:
sub: Declared Dangerous/Vicious/AAI: any dog which has been declared a potentially dangerous dog, has been deemed vicious, or has an aggressive animal incident as defined by California State Law during any legal hearing process.
Bite History: any animal eligible for euthanasia based on behavior considerations that has a bite history.

steps.step: In the Kennel window, use the outcome Type EUTH and select Subtype UNTRT-MEDICAL for animals meeting either of the following:
sub: Irremediably Suffering: in a manner, or to a degree, that prevents remedy, cure, or correction.
Moribund: in the state of dying, approaching death, being in a state of inactivity or obsolescence.

related.name: Kennel window (Outcome Type EUTH; Subtypes UNTRT-BEHAVIOR, UNTRT-MEDICAL)

appendices: Appendix A: Untreatable Definitions (Attachment A of source memorandum; content inlined below)
Appendix B: Chameleon SOP for Animals Meeting the Criteria for Untreatable (Attachment B of source memorandum; Kennel window screenshots in source PDF)

documents.title: Vaccination Certificate Notice to Comply
documents.purpose: To establish a uniform guideline for creating a Notice to Comply with pet licensing based on a current rabies vaccination certificate.
documents.authority: Department of Animal Services; the Chameleon administrator or backup runs notice to comply reports and generates administrative citations.
documents.scope: Staff who process rabies vaccination certificates and pet licensing records.
documents.supersedes: SOP Vaccination Certificate Notice to Comply, effective October 29, 2019 (revised from September 14, 2016; September 12, 2016; March 1, 2009)
references: Policy 100-1, Pet Licensing
California Code of Regulations, Title 17 §2606.4
Health and Safety Code 121690
SOP Issuing New Licenses

definitions.def: A Chameleon tag type used to record rabies vaccination certificate information for an unlicensed dog. A VACCERT is not a license.
Notice to Comply, a mailed notice requesting license compliance with applicable fees.

sections.heading: Requirements
steps: When a rabies vaccination certificate is received from a veterinary clinic and the pet owner does not have a current license for the dog, send the owner a Notice to Comply (NTC) for licensing.
Perform a search first to determine that the dog is not licensed.
Perform searches by the address, owner name, and phone number for each rabies vaccination certificate.
Verify that the dog owner resides in a jurisdiction that has a licensing contract with the Department of Animal Services.
The dog must be four (4) months of age or older, or of undetermined age.
The rabies vaccine must be approved for use in the State of California and signed by a veterinarian licensed in the State.
Use the tag type VACCERT.
The default tag price is $0.00.
Because a VACCERT is not a license, the due date to comply is 30 days from when the dog is four months old.

Prior to creating a new Vet ID (group) number, perform a search. It is preferable to assign the Vet ID to a clinic or animal hospital rather than to a specific veterinarian, as veterinarians can perform vaccines under multiple clinics.

sections.heading: Chameleon Image Entry
steps: When a system search results in an unlicensed dog, and the copy of the rabies certificate provides vaccination and owner information for an individual animal, set the rabies certificate aside until license searches are completed.
Deliver the stack of rabies certificates for individual unlicensed dogs in contracted cities and unincorporated areas to the Chameleon administrator for image entry.
The Chameleon administrator launches image entry to scan each rabies certificate based on pre-programmed resolutions and size.
On a daily basis, the package is uploaded to HLP through KnowledgeRocket.
When the licensing supervising office assistant processes lockbox in the morning, the data from the rabies certificates are downloaded to Chameleon and VACCERT records are created by the program.
These records have a tag subtype of HLP SCAN.
A notice to comply is mailed to the address on file.

sections.heading: Creating a Vaccination Certificate Notice to Comply (Manually)
steps: In the Tag/Link window, create a Person ID if the pet owner is not identified in Chameleon (refer to General Chameleon Procedures).
Create an Animal ID for the dog.
Leave the Tag No box empty. Chameleon will generate a temporary U00 number.
Tab to the Tag Type field, pop up (F4), and select VAC CERT. This signifies that the information for a license was acquired from a vaccination certificate.

steps.step: Tab to the Tag Subtype field and choose one of the following:
sub: Field: for field initiated records.
Counter: generated at the front counter.
Mail: received by mail.
Phone: received by telephone.

steps: The tag jurisdiction must conform to contract licensing jurisdictions only.
The status for a VACCERT shall always be Current, as it refers to the rabies vaccination and not the pet license.
If the rabies certificate also shows the dog's gender as female spayed or male neutered, make sure that the animal sex field is entered appropriately.
Enter today's correct date, or press F5, in the Tag Date field.
The Tag Term field must always be 12 months.

steps.step: Type the proposed license expiration date. It will be one year from the vaccination date or from when the dog attains the age of four months, whichever comes first. Examples:
sub: Example 1: If the dog was vaccinated for the first time on 1/1/2019 and the owner complies with licensing on 6/1/2019, the license will expire on 1/1/2020.

Example 2: If the dog is licensed for the first time on 4/1/2019, does not have existing rabies vaccination history, and presents a rabies vaccination certificate good for three years given on 1/1/2019, the license will expire on 1/1/2020.

Example 3: If the dog owner pays for a license on 1/1/2019 and produces proof of a rabies vaccination with a vaccination date of 4/1/2019, and it is a first time license, then the license expires on 4/1/2020.

steps: Enter the rabies vaccination date and term.
Enter the type of rabies vaccine used, the lot number, and the date the lot number expires.

Press F4 to pop up the Vet ID field and obtain the name of the vaccinating clinic, veterinarian, or animal hospital. Once the corresponding veterinarian information is located, press the Enter key to bring the information back to the Tag/Link window.
Tab to the Price field and enter the amount paid as $0.00.
Leave the Receipt field empty.
Press the F9 key to store the VACCERT record. Chameleon will issue a temporary tag number that starts with U plus the last two digits of the year issued (for example, U00-000001).
Scan the rabies certificate to the tag number (refer to SOP Uploading Images to Chameleon).
The pet owner will receive a notice to comply in the mail requesting license compliance with applicable fees.

sections.heading: Clearing a VACCERT Record
steps: Refer to the Issuing New Licenses policy for requirements of licensing.
Perform a query based on the U number in the Tag Number field.
Renew, or use ALT+P+W, to renew the record (HLP recommends renewing VACCERTs for tracking and statistical reports, such as how many VACCERTs were renewed).
In the Tag/Link window, highlight the U number in the Tag Number field.
Enter the license number as per policy and procedure. If license requirements are missing, delete the current U number to allow Chameleon to store a new U number.

Tab to the Tag Type field, enter LIC, then pop up (F4) and select the applicable LIC type. This signifies that the information for a license was acquired from a vaccination certificate. This action will also automatically populate the applicable fee in the Tag Price field.

steps.step: Tab to the Tag Subtype field and choose one of the following:
sub: Field: for field initiated records.
Counter: generated at the front counter.
Mail: received by mail.

steps: The tag jurisdiction must conform to contract licensing jurisdictions only.
The status stays Current unless license requirements are still missing (refer to SOP Issuing New Licenses).
Enter today's date, or press F5, in the Tag Date field.
The Tag Term field stays the same, 12 months.

The license expiration date is the same as the previous VACCERT expiration date unless the owner paid for a 1-year, 2-year, or 3-year license. The tag expiration should not exceed the vaccination expiration date.
The rabies vaccination date and term stay the same unless proof of a new rabies vaccination certificate is provided.
Vaccination information stays the same unless proof of a new rabies vaccination certificate is provided.
Based on the tag type used, the tag price is automatically entered by Chameleon. You can change the defaulted fee if you are adding the late fee to the license fee.
Press the F9 key to store the LIC record. This means that based on our notification, compliance was met.
Place the cursor on the Receipt Number field, then pop up (F4).
The Receipt window is now up and displays the LIC item on the grid.
If the price includes a penalty fee, display a line item for LIC and a line item for LIC LATE FEE.
Enter the amount paid according to Cash Handling policies and procedures.
Press the F9 key to store the receipt and print the receipt.
Press Enter to go back to the Tag/Link window.
Press the F12 key to refresh the record. A VACCERT with status Renewed should exist with a LIC tag that is Current or Temporary.

sections.heading: Final Notice to Comply
steps: In compliance with Health and Safety Code 121690 and the California Code of Regulations, Title 17, §2606.4, if a valid license has not been obtained no later than 30 days after the dog attains the age of four months old, a late fee is owed.

The Chameleon administrator or backup will run the notice to comply (N1) report with the same dates on the 31st day, or one month after the VACCERT was entered in Chameleon, to send a final notice to comply that includes the late fee.

On the 61st day, or two months after the VACCERT was entered in Chameleon, the Chameleon administrator or backup will generate administrative citations for failure to license, failure to microchip (if applicable), and spay/neuter violation.

related.name: Tag/Link window (tag type VACCERT, subtype HLP SCAN/Field/Counter/Mail/Phone)

documents.searchText: to establish a uniform guideline for creating a notice to comply with pet licensing based on a current rabies vaccination certificate. staff who process rabies vaccination certificates and pet licensing records. department of animal services; the chameleon administrator or backup runs notice to comply reports and generates administrative citations. vaccert a chameleon tag type used to record rabies vaccination certificate information for an unlicensed dog. a vaccert is not a license. ntc notice to comply, a mailed notice requesting license compliance with applicable fees. requirements when a rabies vaccination certificate is received from a veterinary clinic and the pet owner does not have a current license for the dog, send the owner a notice to comply (ntc) for licensing. perform a search first to determine that the dog is not licensed. perform searches by the address, owner name, and phone number for each rabies vaccination certificate. verify that the dog owner resides in a jurisdiction that has a licensing contract with the department of animal services. the dog must be four (4) months of age or older, or of undetermined age. the rabies vaccine must be approved for use in the state of california and signed by a veterinarian licensed in the state. use the tag type vaccert. the default tag price is $0.00. because a vaccert is not a license, the due date to comply is 30 days from when the dog is four months old. prior to creating a new vet id (group) number, perform a search. it is preferable to assign the vet id to a clinic or animal hospital rather than to a specific veterinarian, as veterinarians can perform vaccines under multiple clinics. chameleon image entry when a system search results in an unlicensed dog, and the copy of the rabies certificate provides vaccination and owner information for an individual animal, set the rabies certificate aside until license searches are completed. deliver the stack of rabies certificates for individual unlicensed dogs in contracted cities and unincorporated areas to the chameleon administrator for image entry. the chameleon administrator launches image entry to scan each rabies certificate based on pre-programmed resolutions and size. on a daily basis, the package is uploaded to hlp through knowledgerocket. when the licensing supervising office assistant processes lockbox in the morning, the data from the rabies certificates are downloaded to chameleon and vaccert records are created by the program. these records have a tag subtype of hlp scan. a notice to comply is mailed to the address on file. creating a vaccination certificate notice to comply (manually) in the tag/link window, create a person id if the pet owner is not identified in documents.title: Veterinary Animal Intake Processing
documents.purpose: To establish a consistent animal processing procedure upon intake of a dog or cat for RCDAS Veterinarians, Registered Veterinary Technicians, and Veterinary Assistants.
documents.authority: RCDAS Veterinary Services; medical issues outside protocol are addressed in consultation with the shelter Veterinarian.
documents.scope: RCDAS Veterinarians, Registered Veterinary Technicians, and Veterinary Assistants processing dogs and cats at intake.
documents.supersedes: SOP Veterinary Animal Intake Processing, effective October 29, 2014, revised February 20, 2026

definitions.def: An intake category used in this SOP for animals whose reunification with an owner is anticipated; rabies vaccination and microchip are provided upon reunification rather than at intake.
Community cat programming; impressions of ownership documented at intake can exclude a cat from CCP.

sections.heading: Processing Dogs
steps: Perform a cursory examination to determine age, weight, gender, and breed. Check for any obvious medical abnormalities, including but not limited to hair or dental loss, skin growths, matted hair coat, sores, abnormal eye issues, etc. Enter appropriate information and abnormal observations into the Chameleon Treatment screen.
Scan the dog for a microchip.
Make the dog a paper collar, blue for males and white for females. With a marker, write the Animal ID, impound, and due-out date legibly on the collar.

steps.step: Administer vaccines according to protocol, including rabies. Dogs receive flea and tick treatment according to protocol. Puppies under 6 months of age receive deworming agents according to protocol.
sub: If a dog qualifies as known wait, rabies vaccinations will be provided upon reunification.

steps.step: Implant a microchip in dogs over one month of age that do not already have one. Dogs under one month of age may be microchipped at the discretion of veterinary staff if deemed medically appropriate and safe.
sub: If a dog qualifies as known wait, microchips will be provided upon reunification.

steps: Address any potential medical issues according to protocol or consult with the shelter Veterinarian.
Take a picture that shows as much of the dog as possible and attach it to the Animal ID. Blur or omit any severe or potentially distressing visual injuries.
For dogs who do not qualify as known wait, input a name (new, or known if prior information is available) and standard health and pet details into Chameleon according to protocol.

sections.heading: Processing Cats
steps: Perform a cursory examination to determine age, gender, weight, and breed. Check for any obvious medical abnormalities, including but not limited to hair or dental loss, skin growths, matted hair coat, sores, abnormal eye issues, etc. Enter appropriate information and abnormal findings into the Chameleon Treatment screen.

steps.step: Scan the animal for a microchip.
sub: Document results in "Scanned for Microchip" in the INITIAL EXAM Chameleon notes.

Document any impressions of ownership that exclude the cat from CCP programming, including a collar, an indent indicating a collar, signs of recent or past veterinary care, a surgical scar, or shaved fur, beneath the "Scanned for Microchip" text line.

steps: Make the animal a paper collar, blue for males and white for females. With a permanent marker, write the Animal ID and the due-out date legibly on the collar.

steps.step: Create a name for the cat in Chameleon.
sub: When possible, ensure the name is not a duplicate of an existing, active RCDAS animal.

steps.step: Administer vaccines according to protocol, including rabies. Cats will receive flea and tick treatment according to protocol. Kittens under 6 months of age will receive deworming agents according to protocol.
sub: If a cat qualifies as known wait, rabies vaccinations will be provided upon reunification.

steps.step: Implant a microchip in cats over one month of age. Cats under one month may be microchipped at the discretion of veterinary staff if safe to do so.
sub: If a cat qualifies as known wait, microchips will be provided upon reunification.

steps: Address any potential medical issues according to protocol or consult with the shelter Veterinarian.
Take a picture that shows as much of the cat as possible and attach it to the Animal ID. Blur or omit any severe or potentially distressing visual injuries.
Input information and the picture into Chameleon according to protocol.

sections.heading: Record Keeping Requirement
steps: Always place all information into the Chameleon medical screen. It is important for the animals that each person who comes across the record can open the computer and see all they need to know. It is also important for the shelter legally. Chameleon is the single source for medical record keeping. Many animals are impounded by officers because of poor husbandry, neglect, and abuse, and medical records are vital in helping officers prosecute these cases.

revisions.desc: Revision (known wait provisions, CCP documentation, photo guidance)
